words.definition_en: adj. habitually complaining
words.example_en: One gets unsettled, depressed, and inclined to be querulous.
words.example_zh: 有的人变得心绪不宁，神情沮丧，动不动就爱发牢骚。

words.definition_en: n. mechanical or unthinking routine or repetition
words.example_en: He is very skeptical about the value of rote learning.
words.example_zh: 他非常怀疑死记硬背是否有用。

words.definition_en: v. to plan with cleverness or ingenuity
words.example_en: They somehow contrived to lose tens of thousands of applications.
words.example_zh: 他们精心策划故意遗失了数万份申请书。

words.definition_en: adj. dealing with or affecting a great variety of different things
words.example_en: The negotiations have a wide-ranging scope with emphasis on trade and investment liberalization and facilitation.
words.example_zh: 谈判涵盖的范畴广泛，重点是贸易和投资的自由化及便利化。

words.definition_en: v. to confuse completely
words.example_en: An apple tree producing square fruit is baffling experts.
words.example_zh: 一棵结方形苹果的树令专家大惑不解。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a symbolic representation (2)n. a story in which the characters and events are symbols that stand for ideas about human life or for a political or historical situation
words.example_en: The contrast in their personalities reminded me of the biblical allegory of Cain and Abel.
words.example_zh: 二人性格的反差让我想起来《圣经》里该隐和亚伯的寓言。

words.definition_en: adj. directly forward
words.example_en: I admire her forthright way of dealing with people.
words.example_zh: 我钦佩她那直率待人的方式。

words.definition_en: v. to say in a very strong and definite way
words.example_en: In spite of all you say, I still aver that his report is true.
words.example_zh: 不管你怎么说, 我还是断言他的报告是真实的。

words.definition_en: adj. flagrantly wicked or impious
words.example_en: The EU reckons that Intel's use of such rebates was nefarious.
words.example_zh: 欧盟认为，英特尔利用返点促销用意恶毒。

words.definition_en: v. to recruit someone to join one's party, institution, or cause
words.definition_zh: 极力劝诱某人加入（政党或宗教）
words.example_en: Do not proselytize your work or ideas.
words.example_zh: 不要极力向别人宣扬你的工作或观点。

words.definition_en: v. to feel or express annoyance or ill will at
words.example_en: Many conscripts resent having to do their military service.
words.example_zh: 许多应征入伍者对被迫服兵役愤愤不平。

words.definition_en: v. to increase or improve
words.example_en: BBC backing for the scheme will enhance its credibility.
words.example_zh: 英国广播公司对这一计划的支持将提高其可信度。

words.definition_en: n. dissent or deviation from a dominant theory, opinion, or practice
words.example_en: He was burned at the stake for heresy.
words.example_zh: 他因为信奉异端思想而以火刑处死。

words.definition_en: adj. done or said in a natural and often sudden way and without a lot of thought or planning
words.example_en: The audience burst into spontaneous applause.
words.example_zh: 观众自发地鼓起掌来。

words.definition_en: adj. having no personal reference or connection
words.example_en: We must be as impersonal as a surgeon with his knife
words.example_zh: 我们必须像外科医生拿手术刀时一样冷静客观，不受任何感情影响。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not usual (2)adj. much better than average
words.example_en: He showed exceptional musical ability.
words.example_zh: 他显示出特殊的音乐才能。

words.definition_en: adj. turbulent, stormy
words.example_en: Somehow the marriage lasted for eight tempestuous months.
words.example_zh: 不管怎么说，那场婚姻还是维持了 8 个月，其间双方的关系一直很紧张。

words.definition_en: adj. extremely or excessively particular, exacting, or meticulous in taste or standards
words.example_en: Even the most finicky eater will find something appetizing here.
words.example_zh: 即使是最挑剔的食客在这里也会找到让人胃口大开的美食。

words.definition_en: v. to come together
words.example_en: The capitalists amass great wealth by exploiting workers.
words.example_zh: 资本家剥削工人而积累了巨额财富。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to adopt an air of condescension toward (2)v. provide aid or support for
words.definition_zh: 以高人一等的态度去对待支持
words.example_en: Don't you patronize me!
words.example_zh: 别在我面前摆出一副屈尊俯就的样子！

words.definition_en: (1)v. to turn away or aside (2)v. to prevent from happening
words.example_en: Efforts are being made to avert war and find a diplomatic solution.
words.example_zh: 各方正在为避免战争、寻求外交解决途径而作出积极的努力。

words.definition_en: v. torment, vex
words.example_en: Todd could take it, whereas I'm harrowed by it.
words.example_zh: 托德可以忍受，但是我却感到很痛苦。

words.definition_en: v. to compose out of materials from other documents
words.example_en: The album was compiled from live recordings from last year's tour.
words.example_zh: 这套唱片选辑由去年巡回演出的实况录音汇编而成。

words.definition_en: v. to make a ritual of
words.example_en: Ritualize your transition at the close of the workday.
words.example_zh: 以仪式化的方式去结束这一天的工作。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. of or relating to trees (2)adj. living in or often found in trees
words.example_en: Squirrels are arboreal creatures.
words.example_zh: 松鼠是树栖动物。

words.definition_en: n. a way of using humor to show that someone or something is foolish, weak, bad, etc.
words.example_en: The novel is a stinging satire on American politics.
words.example_zh: 这部小说是对美国政治的尖锐讽刺。

words.definition_en: n. the power of choosing or determining
words.example_en: We like to think that everything we do and everything we think is a product of our volition.
words.example_zh: 我们愿意认为我们所做和所想的一切都是出于自己的意志。

words.definition_en: adj. existing from the time a person or animal is born
words.example_en: Correct ideas are not innate in the mind.
words.example_zh: 人的正确思想不是自己头脑中固有的。

words.definition_en: adj. expressing praise or admiration for someone or something
words.example_en: Meg's mother Susan Jordan was not complimentary about her daughter in the biography she wrote.
words.example_zh: 梅格的母亲苏珊·乔丹在她写的传记中对女儿评价并不高。

words.definition_en: v. to dislike greatly and often with disgust or intolerance
words.example_en: You loathe the smell of greasy food when you are seasick.
words.example_zh: 当你晕船时，你会厌恶油腻的气味。

words.definition_en: adj. very thorough
words.example_en: He has made an exhaustive study of the problem.
words.example_zh: 他对这个问题进行了彻底的研究。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. painfully affecting the feelings (2)adj. designed to make an impression
words.example_en: In fact, they are probably the most poignant and important experience.
words.example_zh: 事实上，它们可能是你最深刻最重要的人生阅历。

words.definition_en: v. to attract and hold the attention of by being interesting, pretty, etc.
words.example_en: For 40 years she has captivated the world with her radiant looks.
words.example_zh: 40年来，她那令人惊艳的美貌让全世界为之痴迷。

words.definition_en: v. to put on
words.example_en: Today's bikini offers more support, and the body-conscious woman is able to don a bikini in confidence.
words.example_zh: 今天的比基尼给人体多一些支撑，让担心自己身材的女士有自信穿上它。

words.definition_en: n. the physical or social setting in which something occurs or develops
words.example_en: They stayed, safe and happy, within their own social milieu.
words.example_zh: 他们幸福无忧地呆在自己的社交圈子里

words.definition_en: v. to give an omen or anticipatory sign of
words.example_en: The change did not portend a basic improvement in social conditions.
words.example_zh: 该变化没有预示社会状况的基本改观。

words.definition_en: n. a systematic plan for therapy (often including diet)
words.example_en: Life in a nursery provides children with a strict daily regimen.
words.example_zh: 孩子在托儿所饮食起居都有规律。

words.definition_en: adj. having no plan, order, or direction
words.example_en: The books had been piled on the shelves in a haphazard fashion.
words.example_zh: 书架上的书堆放得杂乱无序。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing a lack of intelligence or serious thought
words.example_en: When you feel vacuous and unhappy, you can do some labour, such as cleaning the house, tables and windows .
words.example_zh: 感到空虚不快乐的时候你可以劳动一下，比如打扫房子，抹抹桌子，窗子啥的。

words.definition_en: adj. coming from outside
words.example_en: For many people the Church was a symbol of external authority.
words.example_zh: 对许多人来讲，教堂是外来权威的象征。

words.definition_en: adj. capable of being easily led, taught, or controlled
words.example_en: He could easily manage his tractable and worshipping younger brother.
words.example_zh: 他能轻而易举地管住听话并且崇拜自己的弟弟。

words.definition_en: adj. of very great weight
words.example_en: He's slow and ponderous, and can pick up and put down objects nearby.
words.example_zh: 它行动迟缓笨重，可以拿起或者放下旁边的物件。

words.definition_en: v. to make impossible by necessary consequence: rule out in advance
words.example_en: We try to preclude any possibility of misunderstanding.
words.example_zh: 我们努力排除任何误解的可能性。

words.definition_en: v. to assign value or merit to
words.example_en: But a curious thing has happened in the half-century since his death — those who opposed him now valorize him.
words.example_zh: 但是自从他去世半个世纪以来，一件奇怪的事情发生了—那些反对他的人现在对他评价很高。

words.definition_en: n. devotion or loyalty to a person, group, or cause
words.example_en: The constitution requires members of parliament to take an oath of allegiance.
words.example_zh: 宪法规定，议员必须宣誓效忠。

words.definition_en: v. to say in a strong and definitive way that someone or something is bad or wrong
words.example_en: Political leaders united yesterday to condemn the latest wave of violence.
words.example_zh: 政界领袖昨日联合起来对最近激增的暴力事件予以谴责。

words.definition_en: v. to give up or leave entirely
words.example_en: To forsake good for the sake of gold is not for me.
words.example_zh: 我不干这种见利忘义的事。

words.definition_en: adj. shockingly unfair or unjust
words.example_en: It is unconscionable to leave the next generation with a crushing burden of debt and a nation in decline.
words.example_zh: 留给下一代巨额债务负担和一个衰败的国家是不合理的。

words.definition_en: adj. existing or occurring at the same time
words.example_en: The two simultaneous shots sounded like one.
words.example_zh: 那同时发出的两声枪响听起来像是一响.

words.definition_en: n. a heavy burden
words.example_en: For today's politicians, the treaty is becoming a millstone.
words.example_zh: 对今天的政治家而言，该条约正成为一大包袱。

words.definition_en: adj. too important and respected to be changed, criticized, etc.
words.example_en: Freedom of the press is sacrosanct.
words.example_zh: 新闻自由是神圣不可侵犯的。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to prevent from doing something (2)v. to keep under control
words.example_en: he was unable to restrain her desperate anger.
words.example_zh: 她无法控制自己极度的愤怒。

words.definition_en: adj. creating disunity or dissension
words.example_en: Abortion has always been a divisive issue.
words.example_zh: 人工流产一直是个引发争议的问题。

words.definition_en: adj. not wise or sensible
words.example_en: We deplore this lamentably ill-advised decision.
words.example_zh: 我们强烈反对这个拙劣的、欠考虑的决定。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to express sorrow, mourning, or regret for often demonstratively (2)n. complaint
words.example_en: The royal members lamented the passing of aristocratic society.
words.example_zh: 王室家族成员悲叹贵族社会的消失。

words.definition_en: adj. having qualities appropriate to or usually associated with a man
words.example_en: He looked masculine and masterful.
words.example_zh: 他看起来很有男子汉气概和驾驭能力。

words.definition_en: adj. of a commonplace or repetitious character
words.example_en: So many days are routine and uninteresting, especially in winter.
words.example_zh: 太多的日子都是平淡和无趣，尤其是在冬季。

words.definition_en: adj. done in a quiet and secret way to avoid being noticed
words.example_en: His furtive behavior aroused our suspicion.
words.example_zh: 他鬼鬼祟祟的行为引起了我们的怀疑。

words.definition_en: adj. causing someone to feel sad or without hope
words.example_en: All in all, it appeared that a pretty depressing summer awaited Jones.
words.example_zh: 总之，等待琼斯的似乎是一个相当令人沮丧的夏天。

words.definition_en: adj. very strong, clear, and effective
words.example_en: His comment was trenchant and perceptive.
words.example_zh: 他的评论既一针见血又鞭辟入里。

words.definition_en: v. to oppose or challenge especially in a direct and forceful way
words.example_en: This was the first time he had confronted an armed robber.
words.example_zh: 这是他第一次面对一个武装劫匪。

words.definition_en: v. to change level, strength, or value frequently
words.example_en: Body temperature can fluctuate if you are ill.
words.example_zh: 人患病后体温可能会上下波动。

words.definition_en: n. luck that takes the form of finding valuable or pleasant things that are not looked for
words.example_en: Some of the best effects in my garden have been the result of serendipity.
words.example_zh: 我园子里最珍贵的几件物品是机缘巧合之下意外所得。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. affecting or harming many people or things without careful distinction (2)adj. not careful in making choices
words.definition_zh: (1)不加区别的；任意的 (2)不加选择的
words.example_en: Doctors have been criticized for their indiscriminate use of antibiotics.
words.example_zh: 医生被指责滥用抗生素。

words.definition_en: v. to take on an aggressively defensive attitudes
words.example_en: His hair bristled with anger.
words.example_zh: 他怒发冲冠。

words.definition_en: n. the beginning of something
words.example_en: Most of the passes have been closed with the onset of winter.
words.example_zh: 随着冬天的来临，大多数的关卡都已经关闭。

words.definition_en: adj. of basic importance
words.example_en: As a salesman, your cardinal rule is to do everything you can to satisfy a customer.
words.example_zh: 作为推销员，最基本的原则是竭尽全力让顾客满意。

words.definition_en: adj. not focusing the mind on a matter
words.example_en: The government has said it was an inadvertent error.
words.example_zh: 政府声称那是因疏忽造成的过失。

words.definition_en: v. to bring legal action against for redress or punishment of a crime or violation of law
words.example_en: The police decided not to prosecute.
words.example_zh: 警方决定不予起诉。

words.definition_en: adj. showing or expressing deference
words.example_en: She gets excessively deferential to authorities, even when rejecting their views.
words.example_zh: 即便当她想反对权威的观点的时候，她也表现得过度顺从于权威。

words.definition_en: v. bring about, cause
words.example_en: Something vital must have occasioned this visit.
words.example_zh: 肯定有什么重大的事导致了这次访问。

words.definition_en: adj. not healthy or normal
words.example_en: It's morbid to dwell on cemeteries and such like.
words.example_zh: 不厌其烦地谈论墓地以及诸如此类的事是一种病态。

words.definition_en: adj. brilliantly lively, stimulating, or witty
words.example_en: You can hardly expect scintillating conversation from a kid that age.
words.example_zh: 你几乎想不到那么点儿的孩子会说出妙趣横生的话来。

words.definition_en: n. a diverse assortment or mixture
words.example_en: Japan won the Men's 200 metre individual medley.
words.example_zh: 日本赢得了男子200米个人混合泳比赛。

words.definition_en: adj. used to describe someone who allows things to happen or who accepts what other people do or decide without trying to change anything
words.example_en: His passive attitude made things easier for me.
words.example_zh: 他的被动态度使我办起事来容易多了。

words.definition_en: v. to keep from doing what he or she wants to do
words.example_en: Wine or sugary drinks inhibit digestion.
words.example_zh: 葡萄酒或含糖饮料抑制消化。

words.definition_en: v. to be better than
words.example_en: Sometimes small firms can outdo big business when it comes to customer care.
words.example_zh: 在顾客服务方面，有时小企业可能优于大企业。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to vibrate in a definite pattern
words.example_en: Missile deployment did much to further polarize opinion in Britain.
words.example_zh: 在英国，导弹部署极大地导致了意见进一步分化。

words.definition_en: n. a sharp cleavage
words.example_en: There is a sharp disjunction between the government's promises and its actions.
words.example_zh: 政府的承诺与行动之间存在明显脱节。

words.definition_en: adj. conflicting with accepted standards of good conduct or good taste
words.example_en: Carrie could not ignore his indecorous behavior though she loved him very much.
words.example_zh: 尽管凯莉非常爱他却无法忽视他那有失体统的行为。

words.definition_en: v. to completely do away with
words.example_en: Slavery was abolished in the United States in 1865.
words.example_zh: 美国1865年废除了奴隶制度。

words.definition_en: n. a great success or achievement
words.example_en: The winning team returned home in triumph .
words.example_zh: 球队凯旋而归。

words.definition_en: adj. able to be discussed and changed before an agreement or decision is made
words.example_en: He warned that his economic program for the country was not negotiable.
words.example_zh: 他告诫说他为国家制订的经济计划是不容商榷的。

words.definition_en: n. an opinion or idea formed without proof or sufficient evidence
words.example_en: That was a conjecture, not a fact.
words.example_zh: 那只是猜测，不是事实。

words.definition_en: v. to give support or maintain loyalty
words.example_en: Sikhs were expected to adhere strictly to the religious rules concerning appearance.
words.example_zh: 锡克教徒应当严格遵守该教对外表的规定。

words.definition_en: n. a disastrous event marked by great loss and lasting distress and suffering
words.example_en: The recent flooding in the south was a calamity.
words.example_zh: 最近南方的洪水是一场灾难。

words.definition_en: adj. having an air of easy unconcern or indifference
words.example_en: Denis tried to look nonchalant and uninterested.
words.example_zh: 丹尼斯竭力表现出漠不关心、不感兴趣的样子。

words.definition_en: v. to search for food
words.example_en: Gregarious species forage in flocks from colonies or roosts.
words.example_zh: 群居动物从聚居地或栖息地成群结队地外出觅食。

words.definition_en: adj. interchangeable
words.example_en: Financial innovation and globalisation have made capital fungible.
words.example_zh: 金融创新与全球化已使得资本变得可以替代。

words.definition_en: v. to actively second and encourage
words.example_en: You've done enough to be charged with attempt to aid and abet.
words.example_zh: 你所作的一切足以被控教唆犯罪。

words.definition_en: n. a feeling of great happiness and excitement
words.example_en: There was euphoria after the elections
words.example_zh: .选举后一片兴高采烈。

words.definition_en: v. to reduce to minute particles or to a fine spray
words.example_en: For proper combustion it is necessary to atomize the propellants.
words.example_zh: 为了有效地进行燃烧，推进剂必须雾化。

words.definition_en: v. take back, cancel
words.example_en: Trade Union leaders have demanded the government rescind the price rise.
words.example_zh: 工会领导人已要求政府取消这次涨价。

words.definition_en: n. a deserter from one faith, cause, or allegiance to another
words.example_en: When he was doing underground work he was arrested because a renegade informed against him.
words.example_zh: 他做地下工作时，曾因叛徒告密而被捕。

words.definition_en: n. personal belongings or the equipment used for a particular activity
words.definition_zh: 随身用品；（特定活动所需的）装备
words.example_en: All my fishing paraphernalia is in the car.
words.example_zh: 我的钓鱼用品都在汽车里。

words.definition_en: n. a feeling of contempt for someone or something regarded as unworthy or inferior
words.example_en: She regarded the proposal with disdain.
words.example_zh: 她以轻蔑的态度看待这项提议。

words.definition_en: v. to advance beyond the usual or proper limits
words.example_en: We will never allow anybody to encroach upon China's territorial integrity and sovereignty.
words.example_zh: 中国的领土和主权绝不允许任何人侵犯。

words.definition_en: v. to make noble
words.example_en: Hard work ennobles human spirit.
words.example_zh: 艰苦的工作使人精神变得高尚。

words.definition_en: v. to fall or drop suddenly in amount, value, etc. （
words.example_en: Mengniu and Yili have seen their shares plummet since the incident broke.
words.example_zh: 自事件发生以来，蒙牛和伊利的股票大幅下跌.

words.definition_en: adj. able to be understood in more than one way
words.example_en: This agreement is very ambiguous and open to various interpretations.
words.example_zh: 这份协议非常含糊，可以有多种解释。

words.definition_en: v. to coax with flattery
words.example_en: They find means to approach to me and blandish to me.
words.example_zh: 他们总是想尽一切办法来接近我,讨好我。

words.definition_en: n. a show of intention to inflict harm
words.example_en: In my view you are a menace to the public.
words.example_zh: 在我看来，你对公众是个威胁。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to move on unsteadily (2)v. to cause to doubt or hesitate
words.example_en: It was sad to watch the old woman stagger out with the big bundle and disappear.
words.example_zh: 目送老妇人扛着一大包衣服蹒跚而去，实在令人心酸。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. strong and healthy (2)adj. successful or impressive and not likely to fail or weaken
words.example_en: The Secretary of State has made a robust defence of the agreement.
words.example_zh: 国务卿对这项协议进行了强有力的辩护。

words.definition_en: v. to pass or flow slowly through or as if through small openings or interstices
words.example_en: He saw there was a big hole in the back of the man's head, blood was still oozing from it.
words.example_zh: 他看到这个男人后脑勺上有道大口子，血还在往外渗。

words.definition_en: n. matters relating to the life or culture of ancient times
words.example_en: The museum contains the remains of Chinese antiquity.
words.example_zh: 博物馆藏有中国古代的遗物。

words.definition_en: n. a cooperative relationship (as between two persons or groups)
words.example_en: One consequence for coral reefs is that the symbiosis between the corals and algae that constitute a living reef is breaking down.
words.example_zh: 珊瑚礁面临的一个后果是，构成活珊瑚礁的珊瑚和藻类之间的共生关系正在瓦解。

words.definition_en: v. to give energy or excitement to
words.example_en: Your enthusiasm will energize not just you, but also other people around you.
words.example_zh: 而且你的热情不仅会点燃你自己，还包括你身边的其他人。

words.definition_en: adj. not easily apprehended or understood
words.example_en: The chaplain tingled with a weird, occult sensation of having experienced the identical situation before in some prior time or existence.
words.example_zh: 牧师心头猛地一动，出现了一种神秘古怪的感觉，好象从前某个时候曾经经历过同样的情景似的。

words.definition_en: adj. edible
words.example_en: Ethyl lactate is one kind of comestible synthetic spicery.
words.example_zh: 乳酸乙酯是一种食用合成香料。

words.definition_en: v. to place or set firmly in something else
words.example_en: They used to kind of embed it in the glass.
words.example_zh: 他们往往把它嵌在玻璃里。

words.definition_en: n. someone or something that washes or protects something
words.example_en: Farmers should be guardians of the countryside.
words.example_zh: 农民应是乡村的保卫者。

words.definition_en: n. a product of one’s creative effort
words.example_en: The record was the brainchild of rock star Bob Geldof.
words.example_zh: 这张唱片是摇滚歌星鲍勃·格尔多夫的心血之作。

words.definition_en: v. to agree to or approve of something
words.example_en: The board assented to the revised proposal.
words.example_zh: 董事会同意了修订后的提案。

words.definition_en: adj. experienced or realized through imaginative or sympathetic participation in the experience of another
words.definition_zh: 间接体验他人感受的，产生共鸣的
words.example_en: Lots of people use television as their vicarious form of social life.
words.example_zh: 许多人从电视中间接感受社会生活。

words.definition_en: adj. merciless or cruel
words.example_en: The President was ruthless in dealing with any hint of internal political dissent.
words.example_zh: 在处理任何内部政治分歧的苗头时，总统都毫不留情。

words.definition_en: adj. having or wielding force, authority, or influence
words.example_en: The World Health Organization WHO is warning about resistance to drugs which were once so potent. WHO
words.example_zh: 对那些曾经很有效的抗生素的耐药性发出了警告。

words.definition_en: n. complete and confident composure or self-assurance
words.example_en: The whole cast executed the production with truly professional aplomb.
words.example_zh: 全体演职人员以一种真正的专业精神，从容不迫地开始了影片的制作。

words.definition_en: v. to utter slanderous and abusive statements against
words.example_en: He was vilified, hounded, and forced into exile by the FBI.
words.example_zh: 他遭到了联邦调查局的诬蔑和追捕，被迫流亡国外。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to agree to a request or a demand (2)v. to become a party (as to an agreement)
words.example_en: Any country of the Union which has signed this Act may ratify it, and, if it has not signed it, may accede to it.
words.example_zh: 本联盟任何国家已在本议定书上签字者，可以批准本议定书，未签字者可以加入本议定书。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to represent in a clear and obvious way (2)v. to include as a part of feature
words.example_en: Toyota, a company that used to embody Japan's reputation for quality, is enmeshed in a safety and public relations nightmare.
words.example_zh: 曾作为日本质量象征的丰田汽车（Toyota），如今陷入了一场安全与公关梦魇。

words.definition_en: n. a strong support or protection
words.example_en: The House of Lords is the only bulwark of democracy in this country.
words.example_zh: 在这个国家，上议院是唯一的民主堡垒。

words.definition_en: v. to make ill-humored or discontented
words.example_en: He was disgruntled at their absence.
words.example_zh: 他对他们的缺席不满。

words.definition_en: v. to spread apart
words.example_en: A gust of wind dispersed the smoke from the stove.
words.example_zh: 一阵风把炉子里产生的烟吹散了。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. in a position of less power or authority than someone else (2)adj. less important than someone or something else (3)n. someone who has less power or authority than someone else
words.example_en: Sixty of his subordinate officers followed his example.
words.example_zh: 他的60个下级官员都以他为榜样。

words.definition_en: adj. guided by materialism and disdainful of intellectual or artistic values
words.example_en: The method borrowed from the Chinese pharmacy, which many of our comrades are very fond of, is really the most crude, infantile and philistine of all.
words.example_zh: 现在许多同志津津有味于这个开中药铺的方法，实在是一种最低级、最幼稚、最庸俗的方法。

words.definition_en: n. a person who does not behave the way most people behave
words.example_en: Victoria stood out as a dazzling non-conformist.
words.example_zh: 维多利亚以令人惊叹的打破陈规者的形象而引人注目。

words.definition_en: n. secret or dishonest behavior or activity
words.example_en: Ask any student about academic skullduggery and the response is startlingly nonchalant.
words.example_zh: 随便问一位学生对于学术欺骗的看法,其若无其事的态度会让你非常震惊.

words.definition_en: adj. limiting or limited to possession, control, or use by a single individual or group
words.example_en: We ask to be the exclusive agency for your computers in Taiwan.
words.example_zh: 我们公司请求作为贵公司电脑在台湾的独家代理商。

words.definition_en: v. to watch, observe, listen to, or check for a special purpose over a period of time
words.example_en: The agency will continue to monitor the situation closely.
words.example_zh: 该机构将继续密切监测事态发展。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by a lack of order or plan
words.example_en: He now faces another crisis in his helter-skelter existence.
words.example_zh: 他乱糟糟的生活中现在又面临着另一个危机。

words.definition_en: adj. unwise
words.example_en: It would have been impolitic to refuse his offer.
words.example_zh: 当时若拒绝了他的好意当然是不明智的。

words.definition_en: adj. stubbornly disobedient
words.example_en: The contumacious mob shouted defiantly at the police.
words.example_zh: 反抗权威的暴徒目空一切的对警察大喊。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not well-known (2)adj. difficult to understand (3)v. to make difficult to understand or know (4)v. to hide or cover
words.definition_zh: 不出名的费解的使...费解遮盖
words.example_en: The contracts are written in obscure language.
words.example_zh: 这些合同的语言晦涩难懂。

words.definition_en: v. to prove that someone is not guilty of a crime or responsible for a problem, bad situation, etc.
words.example_en: The report on the accident exonerates the bus driver from any responsibility.
words.example_zh: 事故的报告认为公共汽车司机是没有任何责任的。

words.definition_en: adj. never stopping
words.example_en: Human society makes unceasing progress.
words.example_zh: 人类社会总是不断进步的。

words.definition_en: v. to have a mental picture of especially in advance of realization
words.example_en: It’s hard to envisage a more disruptive business model.
words.example_zh: 很难想像一个更具有破坏性的商业模式。

words.definition_en: n. a simple object that was made by people in the past
words.example_en: Every artifact you own should have meaning in your life.
words.example_zh: 你的每件手工品都应对你的生活有意义。

words.definition_en: adj. fought or conducted from the last ditch
words.definition_zh: 孤注一掷的，最后一搏的
words.example_en: It's a last-ditch attempt to prevent civil war.
words.example_zh: 这是阻止内战发生的最后一搏。

words.definition_en: adj. standing at right angles to the plane of the horizon
words.example_en: The sides of the loch are almost perpendicular.
words.example_zh: 湖岸几乎都是垂直的。

words.definition_en: n. one that serves as a substitute
words.example_en: Arms control should not be made into a surrogate for peace.
words.example_zh: 军备控制不应变成和平的替代物。

words.definition_en: v. sparkle
words.example_en: The clean and elegant color and structure coruscate the unique expressive force and permanent life.
words.example_zh: 清雅的色彩与结构，在纷繁的世界里，焕发出独特的表现力和持久的生命力。

words.definition_en: n. a state of very great happiness
words.example_en: My father was in ecstasy when I won my scholarship.
words.example_zh: 我获得奖学金时父亲欣喜若狂。

words.definition_en: adj. comprehensive, universal
words.example_en: He was a man of catholic tastes, a lover of grand opera, history and the fine arts.
words.example_zh: 他这个人兴趣广泛，大歌剧、历史、美术，无所不好。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to keep from being known or noticed (2)n. a cover or partial cover for the face used for disguise
words.example_en: This perfume won't mask the unpleasant smell.
words.example_zh: 这种香水遮不住那股难闻的气味。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. said or done in such a way that others know exactly what you mean (2)adj. not likely to change
words.example_en: It's too soon to give a definite answer.
words.example_zh: 要给出一个明确的答复，现在还为时过早。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by forceful and fluent expression
words.example_en: I heard him make a very eloquent speech at that dinner.
words.example_zh: 在那次晚宴上，我听到他作了一番非常有说服力的陈词。

words.definition_en: v. to infer from a general principle
words.example_en: Alison had cleverly deduced that I was the author of the letter.
words.example_zh: 艾莉森已经聪明地推断出那封信就是我写的。

words.definition_en: v. to set a high value on
words.example_en: Her colleagues esteem her for her integrity.
words.example_zh: 同事们因她为人正直而敬重她。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by exceptional commonplaceness
words.example_en: A bathetic novel will be detrimental to your mind to some extent.
words.example_zh: 一本陈腐的小说会从某种程度上荼毒你的思想。

words.definition_en: v. to think about or consider carefully
words.example_en: We can ponder over the ways and means for raising funds before the construction work starts.
words.example_zh: 我们可以在建筑工程开工之前详细考虑各种筹款集资的措施。

words.definition_en: adj. staying strong and determined even when things are difficult
words.example_en: The ancient people who was successful, not only had exceptional talent but also had unflinching will.
words.example_zh: 古人立大事者，不惟有超世之才,亦必有望忍不拔之志。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. combining characteristics of more than one kind (2)adj. including or accompanied by inconsistent, incompatible, or contrary elements
words.example_en: Girls who have always been at a mixed school know how to stand up for themselves.
words.example_zh: 一直在男女混合学校上学的女孩子们知道如何自我保护。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a strong belief (2)n. a convicting or being convicted
words.example_en: There was not sufficient evidence to secure a conviction.
words.example_zh: 没有足够的证据来定罪。

words.definition_en: n. an erratic, unpredictable, or extravagant manifestation, action, or notion
words.example_en: The style of vagary and ghastfulness and the sense of mystery are full of thestory of Allan.
words.example_zh: 爱伦的小说风格奇特恐怖,充满了神秘感.

words.definition_en: adj. being both relevant and opportune
words.example_en: Such people are ones that you turn to for guidance, knowledge and apropos wisdom about life.
words.example_zh: 这些人是良师益友，他们指引你成为全知并充满适当的智慧去了解生命的意义。

words.definition_en: adj. deserving to be admired
words.example_en: The soldiers showed admirable bravery.
words.example_zh: 士兵们表现出了令人钦佩的勇敢。

words.definition_en: n. an intimate and often exclusive group of persons with unifying common interest or purpose
words.example_en: Mary and her coterie gave a party to which we were not invited.
words.example_zh: 玛利和她的圈内朋友举行派对，我们没被邀请。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to adhere or become attached
words.example_en: The collection of art he had accreted was to be sold.
words.example_zh: 他经年积累的艺术收藏品将被出售。

words.definition_en: n. the state or an instance of disagreement
words.example_en: It could explain the odd discordance of diseases among identical twins.
words.example_zh: 它可以解释同卵双胞胎不一致的发病现象。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by trouble or unhappiness
words.example_en: That's the plan—unless anything untoward happens.
words.example_zh: 计划就这么定了—除非出现异常情况。

words.definition_en: adj. capable of being explained
words.example_en: Differences in schools were not explicable in terms of differences in intake.
words.example_zh: 学校之间的差别不能用新生的差异来解释。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to birds
words.example_en: At least one avian disease pathogen has been found.
words.example_zh: 已经发现至少有一种禽病的病原体。

words.definition_en: adv. by force of circumstances or of necessity
words.example_en: The war in 1939 perforce ushered in an era of more grime and drabness.
words.example_zh: 1939年的战争必然导致一个更加肮脏、更加灰暗的时代的到来。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. dividing into two parts (2)adj. relating to, involving, or proceeding from dichotomy
words.example_en: His recent works involves the dichotomous elements in which each painting is in search of a new sense of harmony.
words.example_zh: 近期作品融合原本二元对立的元素，每幅画作都试图寻求一种新的和谐感。

words.definition_en: v. to give life, action, or spirit to
words.example_en: The dreary book is enlivened by some very amusing illustrations.
words.example_zh: 这本枯燥乏味的书加了一些精彩的插图才变得生动有趣。

words.definition_en: v. to stop something (especially blood) from flowing
words.example_en: Cuttlebone can be used as a medicine to stanch bleeding.
words.example_zh: 海螵蛸可以入药，用来止血。

words.definition_en: n. a joyous song or hymn of praise, tribute, thanksgiving, or triumph
words.example_en: The song is a paean to solitude and independence.
words.example_zh: 这首歌赞美孤寂和独立。

words.definition_en: n. a grave crime
words.example_en: He pleaded guilty to six felonies.
words.example_zh: 他承认犯有 6 项重罪。

words.definition_en: v. to banish or expel from one's own country or home
words.example_en: The regime exiled its political opponents to remote islands.
words.example_zh: 该政权把政治反对者流放到偏远岛屿。

words.definition_en: adj. failing to show proper respect and courtesy
words.example_en: The teacher threatened to kick the impudent pupil out of the room.
words.example_zh: 老师威胁着要把这放肆无礼的小学生撵出教室。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to satisfy (thirst) (2)v. to extinguish or suppress
words.example_en: He stopped to quench his thirst at a stream.
words.example_zh: 他停了下来，在小溪边饮水止渴。

words.definition_en: v. to settle down for rest or sleep
words.example_en: The peacocks roost in nearby shrubs.
words.example_zh: 孔雀栖息在附近的灌木丛里。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to relieve of a charge, load, or burden (2)v. to dismiss from employment
words.example_en: He was discharged from the police force for bad conduct.
words.example_zh: 他因行为不轨被清除出警察队伍。

words.definition_en: (1)n. an inexperienced person (2)n. a person who learns a job or skill by working for a fixed period of time for someone who is very good at that job or skill
words.example_en: My son is an apprentice in a furniture maker's workshop.
words.example_zh: 我的儿子在一家家具厂里当学徒工。

words.definition_en: adj. recklessly and often ostentatiously daring
words.example_en: A daredevil parachutist jumped from the top of Tower Bridge today.
words.example_zh: 一位大胆鲁莽的跳伞者今天从伦敦塔桥的桥顶跳了下去。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. marked by outstanding strength and vigor of body, mind, or spirit (2)adj. very loyal and dedicated
words.example_en: I knew I was never in any danger with my stalwart bodyguard around me.
words.example_zh: 我知道有强壮的保镖在我的周围，我永远不会有什么危险

words.definition_en: adj. lacking in freshness or originality
words.example_en: That's the old hackneyed phrase, but it's true.
words.example_zh: 这虽是老掉牙的套话，但却是真理。

words.definition_en: v. merge, blend
words.example_en: The Senate must pass its own bill and meld it with the House bill before sending it to president's desk.
words.example_zh: 参议院必须先通过自己的法案并与众议院的法案融合为一，之后才能把法案提交给总统签署成法律。

words.definition_en: v. to withdraw or shrink from or as if from pain
words.example_en: Murat had looked into the eyes of the firing squad without flinching.
words.example_zh: 穆拉特毫不退缩地直视着执行枪决的行刑队员们的眼睛。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to include as part of something else (2)v. to blend or combine
words.example_en: We had to incorporate the company for tax reasons.
words.example_zh: 由于纳税的原因，我们不得不把那家公司合并了。

words.definition_en: n. something offensive to view
words.example_en: That new multi-story carpark is a real eyesore.
words.example_zh: 那座新的多层停车场实在难看。

words.definition_en: adj. free from all elaboration and superfluous detail
words.example_en: The explanation in this dictionary is concise and to the point.
words.example_zh: 这部词典里的释义简明扼要。

words.definition_en: n. an act of approving formally or officially
words.example_en: The proposal met his approbation.
words.example_zh: 这个提议获得了他的赞许。

words.definition_en: n. a feeling of being frustrated or annoyed because of failure or disappointment
words.example_en: But to the chagrin of many of its trading partners, the stabilization of the Chinese renminbi is most unwelcome.
words.example_zh: 但对中国的贸易伙伴来说，人民币汇率趋稳可不是好事，令它们失望。

words.definition_en: adj. done to trick someone for the purpose of getting something valuable
words.example_en: The investigation has laid bare their fraudulent scheme.
words.example_zh: 这个调查把他们的骗局暴露无遗.

words.definition_en: adj. marked by an often ill-natured inclination to stress faults and raise objections
words.example_en: There is no captious client but faulty product.
words.example_zh: 只有不完美的产品，没有挑剔的客户。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking adult sophistication
words.example_en: He was a callow youth when he joined the newspaper.
words.example_zh: 他进报社时还是个乳臭未干的小子。

words.definition_en: v. to become weaker
words.example_en: The doctor gave him some medicine to abate the pain.
words.example_zh: 那医生给他一些药以减轻痛苦。

words.definition_en: v. to refuse in a rude way
words.example_en: She rebuffed his offer of help.
words.example_zh: 她断然拒绝了他的帮助。

words.definition_en: adj. carefully considered or prepared
words.example_en: There's something seriously wrong with you. You should be studied.
words.example_zh: 你一定有什么问题，你应该是故意的。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. lacking in strength or vitality (2)adj. lacking a spinal column
words.definition_zh: 无骨气的，软弱的无脊椎的
words.example_en: So invertebrate is today's Congress regarding foreign policy responsibilities.
words.example_zh: 当今国会在外交政策责任方面是如此软弱无能。

words.definition_en: adj. not lasting forever
words.example_en: We are reminded just how small and how impermanent we are.
words.example_zh: 我们意识到自己有多么渺小，我们的生命是何其短暂。

words.definition_en: v. to declare to be evil or detestable
words.example_en: It is not polite to execrate others in the public.
words.example_zh: 在公共场合对他人咒骂很不礼貌。

words.definition_en: v. to indicate and set apart for a specific purpose, office, or duty
words.example_en: Some of the rooms were designated as offices.
words.example_zh: 其中一些房间被指定用作办公室。

words.definition_en: adj. given to tears or weeping
words.example_en: Julia Moreno plays the lachrymose heroine of the film.
words.example_zh: 朱莉亚莫雷诺在片中扮演爱哭的女主角。

words.definition_en: adj. causing difficulty or worry
words.example_en: The load was too burdensome.
words.example_zh: 工作量过于繁重。

words.definition_en: adj. having the ability to make a person feel strong or healthy again
words.example_en: Sure I thought of rest as refreshing, restorative and necessary.
words.example_zh: 当然，我认为休息可以使人振作，恢复健康，并且是必要的。

words.definition_en: v. to let out or release
words.example_en: The combine disgorged a steady stream of grain.
words.example_zh: 收割机不断吐出谷粒。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. tired and ready to fall asleep (2)adj. causing a person to fall asleep
words.example_en: The sedative makes people very somnolent.
words.example_zh: 这种镇静剂会让人瞌睡。

words.definition_en: n. an underground storage compartment
words.example_en: Most of the money was in storage in bank vaults.
words.example_zh: 这笔钱的一大部分是存放在银行保险库中的。

words.definition_en: adj. shared or done by a group of people
words.example_en: It was a collective decision.
words.example_zh: 这是集体的决定。

words.definition_en: v. to gather or collect in a gradual way
words.example_en: At present we are gleaning information from all sources.
words.example_zh: 目前，我们正从各种渠道收集信息。

words.definition_en: (1)n. something that happens or appears in many places and is not unusual (2)n. an obvious or trite comment
words.example_en: Street begging has become a commonplace in London.
words.example_zh: 街头乞讨在伦敦已成了常见现象。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. ample to satisfy and nourish (2)adj. not imaginary or illusory
words.example_en: Substantial numbers of people support the reforms.
words.example_zh: 相当多的人支持这些改革措施。

words.definition_en: v. to feel a strong desire or craving especially for something not likely to be attained
words.example_en: I'm longing to see you again.
words.example_zh: 我渴望再次见到你。

words.definition_en: adj. introducing or using new ideas or methods
words.example_en: Japanese companies have been pumping out plenty of innovative products.
words.example_zh: 日本公司一直在生产大量富有创意的产品。

words.definition_en: adj. inclined by nature to companionship with others of the same species
words.example_en: She's a sociable child who'll talk to anyone.
words.example_zh: 她是个合群的孩子，跟谁都有话说。

words.definition_en: adj. expressed or carried on without words or speech
words.example_en: By tacit agreement , the subject was never mentioned again.
words.example_zh: 根据达成的默契，这个话题从未再提起过。

words.definition_en: adj. exceeding the usual or expected (as in size, force, or prominence)
words.example_en: The earth may be thought of as a gigantic magnet.
words.example_zh: 整个地球可以想像为一块硕大的磁石。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to grasp with the understanding (2)v. to anticipate especially with anxiety, dread, or fear (3)v. arrest, seize
words.example_en: I would try to understand the conversation by watching the captions, so as to apprehend it better.
words.example_zh: 在看的过程中，我会努力的去听里面人物的对话，去看屏幕上的字幕，以便能够更好的理解它。

words.definition_en: n. an imperfection or lack that detracts from the whole
words.example_en: Not being punctual is his greatest shortcoming.
words.example_zh: 不守时间是他的最大缺点。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to pass on from one person or entity to another (2)v. to gradually go from an advanced state to a less advanced state
words.example_en: Additional powers will devolve to the regional governments.
words.example_zh: 将向地方政府下放更多的权力。

words.definition_en: v. to represent (an abstraction) by a concrete instance
words.example_en: In his paper, there is a study of two groups who seemed to instantiate productive aspects of this.
words.example_zh: 他的论文里有关于这两个组的研究，研究似乎能似乎能举例说明这东西有效性。

words.definition_en: n. the path of a point in a plane moving around a central point while continuously receding from or approaching it
words.example_en: The process is not a circle but rather a spiral
words.example_zh: 这个过程不是一个圆周运动而是螺旋上升型的。

words.definition_en: adj. arising from annoyance or irascibility
words.example_en: She was often snappish with the children.
words.example_zh: 她经常对孩子们凶巴巴的。

words.definition_en: n. an outline or synopsis of a play
words.example_en: In the worst-case scenario, you could become a homeless person.
words.example_zh: 最坏的情况是你可能会无家可归。

words.definition_en: adj. causing or tending to cause sleep
words.example_en: The warmth of the room and the monotony of the speaker's voice grew soporific.
words.example_zh: 房间里的热气和发言人单调的声音让人昏昏欲睡。

words.definition_en: v. to say publicly and forcefully that you regard as bad, wrong, etc.
words.example_en: People decried the campaign as a waste of money.
words.example_zh: 人们谴责这一活动是浪费金钱。

words.definition_en: adj. plain to the understanding especially because of clarity and precision of presentation
words.example_en: The analysis of concepts is a matter of finding more perspicuous and complexity-revealing words.
words.example_zh: 对概念进行分析是一项有关寻找更加明白易懂和能揭示复杂性的词汇的问题。

words.definition_en: n. the implicit or metaphorical meaning (as of a literary text)
words.example_en: Europe's divisions are the subtext of a new movie thriller called Zentropa.
words.example_zh: 新上映的惊悚电影《欧罗巴》背后的潜台词是欧洲的分裂。

words.definition_en: n. a body of people entitled to vote
words.example_en: The government largely misread the mood of the electorate.
words.example_zh: 政府在很大程度上没有弄清楚全体选民的情绪。

words.definition_en: n. skill in planning, making, or executing
words.example_en: Nowadays, people tend to write letter by computers, there was a time, not so long ago, when letter writing was a real craft.
words.example_zh: 现在，人们更愿意用电脑写信了，不久的以前，那时候手写书信也算是一种手艺。

words.definition_en: n. action that is quickest or most effective in a particular situation
words.example_en: This was a matter less of morals than of expediency.
words.example_zh: 此举只是权宜之计，不关乎道德问题。

words.definition_en: n. a contrary or opposing force
words.example_en: Currently, the only counterforce to the power of the capital is the supreme political power.
words.example_zh: 目前在中国能够对抗资本强大力量的只有最高的政治权利。

words.definition_en: v. to adapt (someone) to a new temperature, altitude, climate, environment, or situation
words.example_en: Jim has acclimated to the weather since he moved here.
words.example_zh: 吉姆搬来后已经适应了这里的天气。

words.definition_en: adj. strongly marked
words.example_en: The early phase of Roman affairs was an aristocracy of a very pronounced type.
words.example_zh: 早期罗马的事务是属于显著的贵族政治类型。

words.definition_en: adj. successful, well-known and respected
words.example_en: He is one of the three eminent professors in the college.
words.example_zh: 他是这所大学三名杰出教授之一。

words.definition_en: n. a place of refuge and protection
words.example_en: There was a sanctuary of political refugees behind the hospital.
words.example_zh: 医院后面有一个政治难民的避难所。

words.definition_en: n. a practice or interest followed for a time with exaggerated zeal
words.example_en: Rap music proved to be more than just a passing fad.
words.example_zh: 事实证明，说唱音乐并不是昙花一现。

words.definition_en: adj. commonplace, unimaginative
words.example_en: His style is so pedestrian that the book becomes a real bore.
words.example_zh: 他的文风十分平庸，那本书乏味至极。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. likely to cause people to argue or disagree (2)adj. likely or willing to argue
words.example_en: Sanctions are expected to be among the most contentious issues.
words.example_zh: 制裁可能会是争议最大的问题之一。

words.definition_en: v. to cause or aid the development of
words.example_en: The birds returned to their nests and continued to incubate the eggs.
words.example_zh: 鸟儿回到巢里继续孵卵。

words.definition_en: n. the forefront of an action or movement
words.example_en: In a way they played a vanguard role.
words.example_zh: 在某种意义上说，他们起了先锋的作用。

words.definition_en: n. something pleasant or helpful
words.example_en: Low-cost Chinese imports have been a boon to European consumers, retailers and producers.
words.example_zh: 低成本的中国进口产品给欧洲消费者、零售商和生产商带来了实惠。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing great skills or cleverness
words.example_en: As people grow older they generally become less dexterous.
words.example_zh: 随着年龄的增长，人通常会变得不再那么手巧。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking life, spirit, or zest
words.example_en: My problem is, that when we get a few wins, we become lackadaisical .
words.example_zh: 我的问题是，当取得一点点地胜利时，我们就会变得懒散。

words.definition_en: adj. acting, moving, or changing in ways that are not expected or usual
words.example_en: Businessmen are displeased with erratic economic policy-making.
words.example_zh: 商界人士对变化无常的经济决策很是恼火。

words.definition_en: adj. of little or no consequence
words.example_en: He believed all of their conclusion comes from a nugatory and pointless observation.
words.example_zh: 他相信他们的结论来自毫无价值和毫无意义的观察。

words.definition_en: adj. belonging to the basic nature of someone or something
words.example_en: Industry and frugality are the inherent qualities of the Chinese nation.
words.example_zh: 勤劳俭朴是中华民族的本色。

words.definition_en: n. a rash or reckless act
words.example_en: They marveled at his temerity.
words.example_zh: 她们对他的轻率鲁莽感到惊异。

words.definition_en: n. a system of government or business that has many complicated rules and ways of doing things
words.definition_zh: 官僚机构，规矩复杂的机构
words.example_en: State bureaucracies can tend to stifle enterprise and initiative.
words.example_zh: 国家的官僚机构往往会扼杀人们的事业心和积极性。

words.definition_en: adj. different from what is usually done or accepted
words.example_en: Many doctors don't approve of unorthodox medicine.
words.example_zh: 许多医生不赞同用非正统的药品。

words.definition_en: v. interfere without right or propriety
words.example_en: He is inclined to meddle in other people's affairs.
words.example_zh: 他总喜欢干涉别人的事。

words.definition_en: v. to show
words.example_en: The president alone betokened the national identity.
words.example_zh: 总统本身代表着一个国家的形象。

words.definition_en: adj. good at doing something
words.example_en: A great number of Egyptians are proficient in foreign languages.
words.example_zh: 很多埃及人都精通外语。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking in foresight or discernment
words.example_en: The government still has a myopic attitude to spending.
words.example_zh: 政府在开支方面仍然缺乏远见。

words.definition_en: v. to twist or weave together
words.example_en: The program successfully interweaves words and pictures.
words.example_zh: 该节目将文字和画面成功地交织在一起。

words.definition_en: adj. not done or experienced before
words.example_en: Such a move is rare, but not unprecedented
words.example_zh: 这种做法很罕见，但也不是没有先例的。

words.definition_en: n. a coming into being or use
words.example_en: Until the advent of the railways, the camel was the main form of transport throughout north China and into Central Asia.
words.example_zh: 在铁路出现之前，骆驼是中国北部去往中亚的主要运输工具。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to become visible (2)v. to begin to happen or exist
words.example_en: A rebellion by radicals failed to materialize.
words.example_zh: 激进分子的一次叛乱未能实现。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to fail to see or notice (2)v. inspect (3)v. to look down upon from above
words.example_en: He seems to have overlooked one important fact.
words.example_zh: 他好像忽略了一个重要的事实。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to prevent or keep from developing freely (2)v. to become narrower, smaller or tighter
words.example_en: Severe migraine can be treated with a drug which constricts the blood vessel.
words.example_zh: 严重的偏头疼可以用一种收缩血管的药物进行治疗。

words.definition_en: adj. increasing or restoring physical or mental tone
words.example_en: Ginseng is well known for its tonic effect.
words.example_zh: 人参是滋补身体的名药。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to social events where people can eat, drink and talk in a friendly way with others
words.example_en: The atmosphere was quite convivial at the reunion..
words.example_zh: 团聚时的气氛非常轻松愉快。

words.definition_en: v. to prove that something is real, true or genuine
words.example_en: An expert was needed to authenticate the original Van Gogh painting from his imitation.
words.example_zh: 这幅画是凡·高的真迹还是赝品，需由专家来鉴定。

words.definition_en: v. to begin to do or use
words.example_en: The government has agreed to implement the recommendation in the report.
words.example_zh: 政府已同意实施报告中的建议。

words.definition_en: adj. not outstanding in quality or rarity
words.example_en: For many they clearly represent an alternative to run-of-the-mill estate cars.
words.example_zh: 对于很多人来说，他们显然可以作为普通旅行车的替代品。

words.definition_en: adj. being a perfect example of something
words.example_en: His own career was an archetypical American success story.
words.example_zh: 他的职业生涯,可说是典型的美国成功故事。

words.definition_en: n. the quality or state of being self-governing
words.example_en: Activists stepped up their demands for local autonomy last month.
words.example_zh: 上个月激进分子对地方自治的呼声更高了。

words.definition_en: v. cut down, reduce
words.example_en: Shortly afterwards, cuts in defence spending forced the aerospace industry to retrench.
words.example_zh: 此后不久，国防开支的削减迫使航空航天业紧缩开支。

words.definition_en: n. something that incites or has a tendency to incite to determination or action
words.example_en: Many companies in Britain are keen on the idea of tax incentives for R& D.
words.example_zh: 英国许多公司都热衷于用税收优惠的方式来鼓励研发。

words.definition_en: n. victualler, caterer
words.example_en: Over the past few years, the company has been shifting from its roots as a hardware and on-premise software vendor, toward a future as a cloud and services purveyor.
words.example_zh: 过去几年，这家公司一直在努力从硬件和内部部署软件提供商转型成云计算和服务供应商。

words.definition_en: adj. present and capable of emerging or developing but not now visible, obvious, active, or symptomatic
words.example_en: Advertisements attempt to project a latent meaning behind an overt message.
words.example_zh: 广告试图传达一种隐藏在公开信息里的潜在意义。

words.definition_en: v. to associate with another or others for mutual benefit
words.example_en: The two airlines will cooperate on ticketing and schedules.
words.example_zh: 两家航空公司将在票务和航班安排上展开合作。

words.definition_en: adj. not readily ruled, disciplined, or managed
words.example_en: It's not good enough just to blame the unruly children.
words.example_zh: 对难管教的孩子只是责备是不够的。

words.definition_en: adj. unexciting
words.example_en: I did get a bit cheesed off with the movie's rather plodding pace.
words.example_zh: 电影的节奏相当缓慢，确实让我有点不耐烦。

words.definition_en: adj. impaired by damp or mildew
words.example_en: The musty aroma of incense made her head swim.
words.example_zh: 熏香发霉的气味令她感到头晕。

words.definition_en: adj. having a decisive influence on the character or development of something
words.definition_zh: 起决定性作用的；界定性的
words.example_en: For me, that was the defining moment of what would become the next six years of our lives together.
words.example_zh: 对我而言，那个时刻注定了接下来的六年我们四个人的生活将变成什么样子。

words.definition_en: v. to create an equal balance between two things
words.example_en: Prices have risen in order to offset the increased cost of materials.
words.example_zh: 为补偿原料成本的增加而提高了价格。

words.definition_en: adj. harmful to living things
words.example_en: Many household products give off noxious fumes.
words.example_zh: 很多家用产品散发有害气体。

words.definition_en: adj. lasting a very short time
words.example_en: The evanescent post-war economic boom was quickly followed by a deep recession.
words.example_zh: 大萧条紧随短暂的战后经济繁荣而来。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. unable to move (2)adj. very slow to move or act
words.example_en: He lay inert with half-closed eyes.
words.example_zh: 他半睁着双眼一动不动地躺着。

words.definition_en: adj. lasting only for a short time
words.example_en: In the final analysis, most of life's joys are transitory.
words.example_zh: 归根结底，尘世间的欢乐大多是过眼烟云，转瞬即逝。

words.definition_en: adj. favoring or inclined to start quarrels or wars
words.example_en: His bellicose disposition alienated his friend.
words.example_zh: 他生性好斗，他的朋友们都疏远了他。

words.definition_en: n. an estate inherited from one's father or ancestor
words.example_en: I left my parents 'house, relinquished my estate and my patrimony.
words.example_zh: 我离开了父母的家，放弃了我的房产和祖传财产。

words.definition_en: adj. appearing ready to collapse
words.example_en: They entered the shop, which was a curious ramshackle building.
words.example_zh: 他们进了商店，那是幢破烂不堪的奇特建筑。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking flavor, zest, interest, animation, or spirit
words.example_en: She made a vapid comment about the weather.
words.example_zh: 她对天气作了一番平淡无奇的评论。

words.definition_en: n. the quality or state of not thinking you are better than other people
words.example_en: Defeat and failure cause humility.
words.example_zh: 挫折与失败使人谦卑。

words.definition_en: v. to do away with as completely as if by pulling up by the roots
words.example_en: These insects are very difficult to eradicate.
words.example_zh: 这些昆虫很难根除。

words.definition_en: adj. talking or writing about many different things in a way that is not highly organized
words.example_en: His own toast was discursive and overlong, though rather touching.
words.example_zh: 他自己的祝酒词虽然也颇为动人,但是比较松散而冗长。

words.definition_en: adj. free, unrestrained
words.example_en: I want to be an unfettered bird.
words.example_zh: 想要做一只无拘无束的小鸟。

words.definition_en: n. someone or something that is very strongly disliked
words.example_en: Racial prejudice is an anathema to me.
words.example_zh: 对我来说，种族歧视非常可恶。

words.definition_en: adj. morally crude, undeveloped, or unregenerate
words.example_en: Certain words are vulgar and not acceptable in polite society.
words.example_zh: 有些字眼较粗俗，不为上流社会所接受。

words.definition_en: adj. easily notice or seen
words.example_en: It is important that China play an increasingly prominent role on climate change.
words.example_zh: 中国在气候变化问题上发挥日益突出的重要作用。

words.definition_en: n. insulting or abusive language
words.example_en: Crowley maintained a stream of invective and abuse against Waite.
words.example_zh: 克劳利连珠炮似的对韦特骂个不停。

words.definition_en: adj. supple enough to bend freely or repeatedly without breaking
words.example_en: As your baby grows bigger, his bones become less pliable.
words.example_zh: 随着宝宝的成长，其骨骼的柔韧性会降低。

words.definition_en: v. to speak or write in high praise of
words.example_en: Leaders from around the world eulogized the Egyptian president.
words.example_zh: 全世界的领导人都致悼词称颂那位埃及总统。

words.definition_en: n. the quality or state of being kin
words.example_en: In different societies and cultures, there must be different systems of kinship terms.
words.example_zh: 在不同的社会和文化中，有不同的亲属关系称谓。

words.definition_en: n. a person who is very strict and demands obedience from others
words.example_en: They discover that the new teacher is a martinet.
words.example_zh: 他们发现新来的老师非常严格。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by keen caution, cunning, and watchfulness especially in detecting and escaping danger
words.example_en: Bankers are keeping a wary eye on the outcome.
words.example_zh: 银行家们对结果保持高度谨慎。

words.definition_en: n. practical knowledge, understanding, or shrewdness
words.example_en: He is known for his political savvy and strong management skills.
words.example_zh: 他以政治悟性和强势的管理手腕出名。

words.definition_en: n. a chaotic situation
words.example_en: Pandemonium broke out as they ran into the street shouting.
words.example_zh: 他们叫嚷着冲到大街上，大街上顿时乱作一团。

words.definition_en: v. to damage the reputation of
words.example_en: They spread disinformation in order to discredit politicians.
words.example_zh: 他们散布假消息，企图败坏政治家的名声。

words.definition_en: v. to speak or perform without preparation
words.example_en: Uncle Richard intoned a chapter from the Bible and improvised a prayer.
words.example_zh: 理查德叔叔吟诵了《圣经》中的一章，又即兴作了一段祈祷文。

words.definition_en: adj. not having all that is needed or expected
words.example_en: He analyzed his game and found it wanting.
words.example_zh: 他分析了他的比赛，发现还有待改进。

words.definition_en: v. to criticize severely
words.example_en: His first high-budget movie, called 'Brain Donors', was panned by the critics.
words.example_zh: 他的第一部高成本制作电影《撞板三舞男》遭到恶评。

words.definition_en: adj. not clear in meaning
words.example_en: He looked at her vague shape through the frosted glass.
words.example_zh: 他透过雾蒙蒙的眼镜注视着她模糊的身形。

words.definition_en: n. an offensive exhalation or smell
words.example_en: The sources, components and output of effluvium derived from municipal wastewater treatment plants were introduced in this paper.
words.example_zh: 这篇论文介绍了城市污水处理厂臭气来源、成分及产量。

words.definition_en: adj. messy or untidy
words.example_en: The old beggar was dirty and unkempt.
words.example_zh: 那个老乞丐蓬头垢面。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to damage by rubbing, grinding or scraping (2)v. to wear down in spirit
words.example_en: Their gossips abraded her into restlessness.
words.example_zh: 他们的流言蜚语使她心烦意乱。

words.definition_en: n. a person who predicts the future by magical, intuitive, or more rational means
words.example_en: A soothsayer in my village says I'll find a sword.
words.example_zh: 村里的一个占卜者说，我会找到一把剑。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. of or relating to the theater or the presentation of plays (2)adj. marked by extravagant display or exhibitionism
words.example_en: These are the prizes given for the most outstanding British theatrical performances of the year.
words.example_zh: 这些奖项将要颁发给本年度英国最杰出的戏剧表演作品。

words.definition_en: n. a person whom one knows but who is not a particularly close friend
words.example_en: She is not my friend, just an acquaintance.
words.example_zh: 她不是我的朋友，只是一个熟人。

words.definition_en: v. to become unsteady, feeble, or spiritless
words.example_en: Her confidence had never flagged.
words.example_zh: 她的信心从未减弱。

words.definition_en: adj. damaging or harmful
words.example_en: Petty crime is having a deleterious effect on community life.
words.example_zh: 轻微罪行正对社区生活产生有害影响。

words.definition_en: n. a thing done with ease
words.definition_zh: 有把握的事，很容易的事
words.example_en: For me, the GRE was really a cinch.
words.example_zh: 对我来说，GRE考试真是小菜一碟。

words.definition_en: v. to cancel (an order) especially by giving a new order
words.example_en: The general countermand the orders issued in his absence.
words.example_zh: 将军撤销了他不在时所发布的命令。

words.definition_en: n. a strong feeling of not liking something
words.example_en: He had an aversion to getting up early.
words.example_zh: 他十分讨厌早起。

words.definition_en: v. to end or stop by force
words.example_en: The regime ruthlessly suppresses all dissent.
words.example_zh: 这个政权残酷压制所有不同意见。

words.definition_en: n. something that is made up of different things
words.example_en: The low mountains were a patchwork of green and brown.
words.example_zh: 低矮的群山上，绿色和棕色混杂着。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to move with a violent twist (2)v. to distort
words.example_en: She wrenched her knee when she fell.
words.example_zh: 她跌倒时把膝盖扭伤了。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. spread out over a large space (2)v. to spread out (3)adj. being at once verbose and ill-organized
words.example_en: His talk was so diffuse that I missed his point.
words.example_zh: 他的谈话漫无边际, 我抓不住他的要点。

words.definition_en: adj. too evident to be doubted
words.example_en: His brilliance renders this film an indubitable classic.
words.example_zh: 他的出色表现让这部电影成为了毋庸置疑的经典。

words.definition_en: v. to bring to maturity or self-sufficiency usually through nurturing care
words.example_en: It's really not easy for a widowed mother to rear up four children.
words.example_zh: 一个寡母要抚养4个孩子成人确实不易。

words.definition_en: adj. serving one's own interests often in disregard of the truth or the interests of others
words.example_en: They suggest that my motives for proposing reform are self-serving and mercenary.
words.example_zh: 他们暗示说我之所以提议改革是贪图金钱，想谋取私利。

words.definition_en: v. to give permission or consent to
words.example_en: The new drug has not yet been licensed in the US.
words.example_zh: 这种新药尚未在美国获得许可。

words.definition_en: adj. having a sharp or bitter quality
words.example_en: They directed their most astringent attacks at me.
words.example_zh: 他们对我作最为严厉的攻击

words.definition_en: adj. expressing personal opinions or beliefs as if they are certainly correct and cannot be doubted
words.example_en: I am no pedant and avoid being dogmatic concerning English grammar and expression.
words.example_zh: 我不是书呆子，不想在英语语法和表达方式上过于教条主义。

words.definition_en: v. to excite or elate to the point of enthusiasm or frenzy
words.example_en: He does not drink at all; a little wine will intoxicate him.
words.example_zh: 他根本不喝酒，因为一点酒就能使他醉。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. likely to do something (2)adj. appropriate or suitable (3)adj. keenly intelligent and responsive
words.example_en: When he speaks on the air, listeners are apt to mistake him for a white man.
words.example_zh: 他在广播中的说话声会让观众以为他是白人。

words.definition_en: v. to fight or speak publicly in support of
words.example_en: He has always championed the cause of human rights.
words.example_zh: 他一直在为支持人权而斗争。

words.definition_en: n. one that assumes false identity or title for the purpose of deception
words.example_en: That he was not a God at all, but merely an imposter.
words.example_zh: 他只不过是一个伪装者，根本就不是神。

words.definition_en: adj. awkward, clumsy
words.example_en: Mark Cuban's best player is still a tall gawky German guy.
words.example_zh: 马克库班的最好的球员依然是那个迟钝的德国大个儿.

words.definition_en: v. to accept readily or gladly
words.example_en: any users will embrace the new portal technology and agree that the migration will pay dividends over time.
words.example_zh: 许多用户会欣然接受新的门户技术，并相信迁移总有一天会显出它的好处。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to exist or to develop
words.example_en: The issue engendered continuing controversy.
words.example_zh: 这个问题引起了不断的争论。

words.definition_en: adj. requiring much time, attention, or effort from someone
words.example_en: Products should be designed to meet the exacting standards of today's marketplace.
words.example_zh: 产品的涉及应当符合当今市场严格的标准。

words.definition_en: v. to secretly damage or destroy equipment, vehicles ,etc…
words.example_en: He accused the opposition of trying to sabotage the election.
words.example_zh: 他谴责反对派企图暗中破坏选举的进行。

words.definition_en: v. to want very much
words.example_en: Never covet wealth and power.
words.example_zh: 切勿贪图财富和权力。

words.definition_en: n. dull, irksome, and fatiguing work
words.example_en: He spent his life in pointlessly tiresome drudgery.
words.example_zh: 他的一生都在做毫无意义的烦人的苦差事。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. having many twists and turns (2)adj. complicated, long, and confusing
words.example_en: The Lot river follows a winding and tortuous course.
words.example_zh: 洛特河的河道蜿蜒曲折。

words.definition_en: (1)n. deliberate purposive planning (2)n. the process of planning how something will look, happen, be made
words.example_en: The machine's design is both attractive and practical.
words.example_zh: 这台机器的设计既美观又实用。

words.definition_en: adj. involving a lot of violence, confusion, or disorder
words.example_en: It's been a tumultuous day at the international trade negotiations in Brussels.
words.example_zh: 那是布鲁塞尔国际贸易谈判中纷乱的一天。

words.definition_en: (1)n. the act of giving or delivering something to people (2)n. the position, arrangement, or frequency of occurrence over an area or through a space or unit of time
words.example_en: Inflation is considered to be undesirable because of its adverse effects on income distribution.
words.example_zh: 通货膨胀被认为是有害的，因为它会对收入分配产生不利影响。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to agree to stop fighting, hiding, resisting, etc., because you know that you will not win or succeed (2)v. to give the control or use of to someone else (3)v. to allow something to influence or control you
words.example_en: The rebel soldiers were forced to surrender.
words.example_zh: 叛军被迫投降。

words.definition_en: n. a temporary madness
words.example_en: The country was gripped by a frenzy of nationalism.
words.example_zh: 这个国家笼罩在民族主义的狂热中。

words.definition_en: adv. side by side and facing or moving in the same direction
words.example_en: Mary ran abreast of him toward the river.
words.example_zh: 玛丽和他并肩朝河边跑去。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to stop short and refuse to proceed (2)v. to check or stop by or as if by an obstacle
words.example_en: We get strong indications that his agent would balk at that request.
words.example_zh: 我们得到的强烈暗示是他的经纪人会回避那个要求。

words.definition_en: v. to strike out, obliterate, or mark for deletion
words.example_en: His name was expunged from the record books.
words.example_zh: 他的名字被从档案簿中删去了。

words.definition_en: adj. very unwilling to spend money
words.example_en: Even the parsimonious Littler paid for drinks all round.
words.example_zh: 即便是吝啬鬼利特勒，无论在哪里喝酒也都是付钱的。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to use for a particular purpose or to do something (2)v. to use or get the services of to do a particular job
words.example_en: The firm employs about 100 men.
words.example_zh: 这家公司大约雇了100人。

words.definition_en: n. the state of being near
words.example_en: What drew him to the area was its proximity to central London.
words.example_zh: 吸引他到该地区的原因是那里靠近伦敦市中心。

words.definition_en: adj. easily annoyed or angered and likely to argue
words.example_en: He was seen as truculent, temperamental, too unwilling to tolerate others.
words.example_zh: 他们认为他性格好斗, 性情暴躁, 不大能容人。

words.definition_en: n. one who excels in the technique of an art
words.example_en: He was gaining a reputation as a remarkable virtuoso.
words.example_zh: 作为一位技艺非凡的大师，他声名远扬。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to deal with or manage wrong or ignorantly (2)v. maltreat
words.example_en: The equipment could be dangerous if mishandled.
words.example_zh: 这套设备如果使用不当会有危险。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to petition for help or support (2)v. to put into effect or operation (3)v. to appeal to or cite as authority
words.example_en: The judge invoked an international law that protects refugees.
words.example_zh: 法官援用了一项保护难民的国际法律。

words.definition_en: adj. exaggeratedly or childishly emotional
words.example_en: The most common writing theme in the western newspaper is obituary report which is rich in content, varied in style, and sentimental in expression but not mawkish as well.
words.example_zh: 西方报纸上常见的讣闻，内容丰富、形式多样，哀而不伤。

words.definition_en: v. to present as evil or diabolic
words.example_en: Each side began to demonize the other.
words.example_zh: 双方都开始把对方妖魔化。

words.definition_en: v. to instill with boldness or courage
words.example_en: But the union's stance could embolden other critics.
words.example_zh: 但工会的姿态可能会为其他批评人士壮胆。

words.definition_en: n. a cause of trouble
words.example_en: Some people blame all his vexation for bad luck rather than lacking of competence.
words.example_zh: 有些人把自己所有的苦恼都归咎于运气不佳, 而不认为是缺乏能力。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. very interesting (2)adj. capable of causing someone to believe or agree (3)adj. strong and forceful
words.definition_zh: 激发兴趣的令人信服的强大的
words.example_en: There is no compelling reason to believe him.
words.example_zh: 没有令人信服的理由让人相信他。

words.definition_en: (1)n. respect that is given to someone who is admired (2)v. to regard or treat with respect and admiration
words.example_en: The Chinese make a point of their personal honor.
words.example_zh: 中国人特别重视个人荣誉。

words.definition_en: n. a departure from or rejection of something
words.example_en: A breakaway faction of the rebel group has claimed responsibility for the killing.
words.example_zh: 一支从反叛团体分裂出来的派别已宣称对那次凶杀负责。

words.definition_en: v. to see beforehand
words.example_en: No one can foresee what will happen in the future.
words.example_zh: 谁也无法预见到将来会发生什么事。

words.definition_en: v. support, substantiate
words.example_en: The report is underpinned by extensive research.
words.example_zh: 这份报告以广泛的研究为基础。

words.definition_en: v. to provoke impatience, anger, or displeasure in
words.example_en: Their attitude irritates me.
words.example_zh: 他们的态度激怒了我。

words.definition_en: v. to bring into harmony
words.example_en: He became very attuned to all the suffering around him.
words.example_zh: 他变得对周围的苦难有强烈的共鸣。

words.definition_en: adj. sure to happen
words.example_en: Most unions see privatization as an inevitable prelude to job losses.
words.example_zh: 大多数工会认为私有化会不可避免地导致失业。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. marked by simplicity, frugality, or avoidance of luxury and comfort (2)n. a person of great courage and self-discipline
words.definition_zh: 简朴的勇敢守纪律的人
words.example_en: Even expedition ships, where spartan accommodation is almost a point of pride, are offering creature comforts.
words.example_zh: 即使是以简朴为傲的探险船上也能提供舒适性。

words.definition_en: v. to reduce in size, quantity, or volume as if by squeezing
words.example_en: The four debates will be compressed into an unprecedentedly short eight-day period
words.example_zh: 这4场辩论将被压缩到短短8天的时间，这是前所未有的。

words.definition_en: v. to be earlier or older than
words.example_en: That event antedated World War Ⅱ.
words.example_zh: 那事件发生在第二次世界大战之前。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to fields or lands or their tenure
words.example_en: Since the agrarian reform polarization has taken place among the peasants.
words.example_zh: 在土地改革后，农民发生了分化。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. easy or possible to get or use (2)adj. present and able or willing to talk to someone
words.example_en: There are three small boats available for hire.
words.example_zh: 有3艘小船可供出租。

words.definition_en: v. to feel or express sympathy
words.example_en: When I lost, he commiserated with me.
words.example_zh: 我落败的时候，他向我表示同情。

words.definition_en: v. to make happy or satisfied
words.example_en: Jay Gatsby did that to gratify his girlfriend's vanity.
words.example_zh: 盖茨比做那事是要满足他女友的虚荣心。

words.definition_en: v. to name or dedicate (something, such as a ship) by a ceremony
words.example_en: She was born in March and christened in June.
words.example_zh: 她3月份出生，6月洗礼时取了名。

words.definition_en: v. to take intense pleasure or satisfaction
words.example_en: They reveled in the success of the project.
words.example_zh: 他们陶醉于项目的成功。

words.definition_en: adv. immediately
words.example_en: The agreement between us is terminated forthwith.
words.example_zh: 我们之间的协议立即终止。

words.definition_en: adj. difficult and unpleasant to do or deal with
words.example_en: My household duties were not particularly onerous.
words.example_zh: 我的家务活并不繁重。

words.definition_en: v. to add notes or comments to
words.example_en: Historians annotate, check and interpret the diary selections.
words.example_zh: 历史学家们对这本日记选集进行了注释、检查和阐释。

words.definition_en: adj. extraordinary in bulk, quantity, or degree
words.example_en: This business generates cash in prodigious amounts.
words.example_zh: 这生意带来大量现金。

words.definition_en: v. to stop from moving through or going by
words.example_en: For years the country has tried to block imports of various cheap foreign products.
words.example_zh: 多年来，这个国家一直竭力阻挠各种廉价外国产品的进口。

words.definition_en: v. to read and mark corrections in (something, such as a proof)
words.example_en: I didn't even have the chance to proofread my own report.
words.example_zh: 我甚至没有机会校对自己的报告。

words.definition_en: n. a sudden overpowering fright
words.example_en: Office workers fled in panic as the fire took hold.
words.example_zh: 火势起来时，办公室人员惊慌逃出。

words.definition_en: v. to get revenge
words.example_en: Christie retaliated by sending his friend a long letter detailing Carl's utter incompetence.
words.example_zh: 为了报复，克里斯蒂给他朋友寄了一封长信，细述卡尔完全不称职。

words.definition_en: adj. spirited, brave
words.example_en: Huang Jiguang's gallant deed is known by all Chinese.
words.example_zh: 中国人皆知黄继光的英勇事迹。

words.definition_en: v. be sufficient
words.example_en: One example will suffice to illustrate the point.
words.example_zh: 举一个例子就足以说明这一点。

words.definition_en: v. to make major changes or improvements to
words.example_en: They have tried to reinvent their retail stores.
words.example_zh: 他们一直在尝试改造他们的零售店。

words.definition_en: v. to make less painful, severe, etc.
words.example_en: To assuage his wife's grief, James took her on a tour of Europe.
words.example_zh: 为了缓和妻子的悲痛，杰姆斯带她去了欧洲旅游。

words.definition_en: adj. very devoted or loyal to a person, belief, or cause
words.example_en: He's a staunch supporter of controls on government spending.
words.example_zh: 他坚决支持控制政府开支。

words.definition_en: n. a difficult, perplexing, or trying situation
words.example_en: Hank explained our predicament.
words.example_zh: 汉克说明了我们的艰难处境。

words.definition_en: v. to promise to give up or to stop doing
words.example_en: Wells was not likely to forswear the faith of a lifetime.
words.example_zh: 威尔斯决不会背弃他毕生的信仰。

words.definition_en: adj. matchless, incomparable
words.definition_zh: 无与伦比的，出类拔萃的
words.example_en: People say: “ Guilin's scenery is peerless in the world. ” It was not until I visited the place that I found it really worthy of the reputation.
words.example_zh: 人们都说“桂林山水甲天下”，到桂林一看，果然名不虚传。

words.definition_en: v. to show or suggest something that will happen or exist at a future time
words.example_en: The wall through Berlin was finally ruptured, prefiguring the reunification of Germany.
words.example_zh: 柏林墙终于倒塌了，预示着德国的重新统一。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. causing death (2)adj. determining one's fate
words.definition_zh: 致命的重大的，决定命运的
words.example_en: There was a fatal flaw in the plan.
words.example_zh: 计划中有一个致命的缺陷。

words.definition_en: adj. likely to change in a very sudden or extreme way
words.example_en: He has a volatile temper.
words.example_zh: 他脾气反复无常。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. marked by, responding to, or expressing the softer emotions (2)v. to present for acceptance
words.example_en: Her voice was tender, full of pity.
words.example_zh: 她的声音很温柔，充满了怜爱。

words.definition_en: v. to reduce drastically especially in number
words.example_en: The pollution could decimate the river's thriving population of kingfishers.
words.example_zh: 污染可能会造成河边大量繁殖的翠鸟大批死亡。

words.definition_en: adj. excessively acquisitive especially in seeking to hoard riches
words.example_en: They are avaricious and will do anything for money.
words.example_zh: 他们很贪婪，愿意为了钱做任何事情。

words.definition_en: adj. simple and plain
words.example_en: My grandma lives a frugal life.
words.example_zh: 我奶奶一向生活俭朴。

words.definition_en: n. an area within which someone or something has authority, influence, or knowledge
words.example_en: These are questions that lie outside / that do not come within the purview of our inquiry.
words.example_zh: 这些问题不在我们的调查范围之内.

words.definition_en: v. to make (someone) ashamed or embarrassed
words.example_en: She had been beaten and humiliated by her husband.
words.example_zh: 她遭到丈夫的殴打和羞辱。

words.definition_en: adj. calm and in control of your emotions
words.example_en: Laura was very calm and composed.
words.example_zh: 劳拉看上去十分沉着冷静。

words.definition_en: adj. markedly short and abrupt
words.example_en: They received a characteristically brusque reply from him.
words.example_zh: 他们从他那里得到的是简短的答复，他一贯都是那样的。

words.definition_en: n. the cooperative relationship of colleagues
words.example_en: His collegiality also signals an end to the imperial premierships of recent decades.
words.example_zh: 他的共同掌权方式也标志着近几十年帝国主义首相制的终结。

words.definition_en: v. deprive of strength
words.example_en: That bout of pneumonia enfeebled her.
words.example_zh: 那次肺炎的发作使她虚弱了。

words.definition_en: v. to display clearly
words.example_en: His conversation evinces great courage.
words.example_zh: 他的谈话显示了很大的勇气。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. occurring every day (2)adj. commonplace, ordinary
words.example_en: Television has become part of our quotidian existence.
words.example_zh: 电视已成为我们日常生活的一部分。

words.definition_en: n. branch, offshoot
words.example_en: Public relation of police is a ramification of modern public relations.
words.example_zh: 警察公共关系是现代公共关系的衍生物。

words.definition_en: adj. following one’s own capricious, wanton, or depraved inclinations
words.example_en: Wayward students are always getting into trouble.
words.example_zh: 任性的学生老是惹麻烦。

words.definition_en: n. culminating point
words.example_en: He has reached the zenith of his career.
words.example_zh: 他已经达到了他事业的顶峰。

words.definition_en: adj. meant to be humorous or funny
words.example_en: Some people are inherent and serious, have a few people additionally inherent facetious.
words.example_zh: 有些人天生严肃，另有一些人天生爱开玩笑。

words.definition_en: v. to make weaker or less effective
words.example_en: Some members of the board were trying to undercut the chairman's authority.
words.example_zh: 委员会的某些成员试图削弱主席的权力。

words.definition_en: n. someone or something that is closely connected with a particular quality
words.example_en: The soldier's name was a byword for bravery.
words.example_zh: 那个战士的名字原是勇敢的代名词。

words.definition_en: v. to hurt the reputation of especially by saying things that are false or unfair
words.example_en: Sgt Norwood complained that the article defamed him.
words.example_zh: 诺伍德中士申诉说这篇文章对他进行了诽谤。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by or tending to promote optimism
words.example_en: That dream does not seem so rosy now.
words.example_zh: 如今那种梦想似乎也不那么乐观了。

words.definition_en: v. to endure without protest or reaction
words.example_en: I may be poor, but I can't accept his lies, his cheating, his bribes and all the rest of it.
words.example_zh: 我虽贫穷，可我不能忍受他的谎言、他的欺诈行为、他的贿赂和其他种种劣行。

words.definition_en: v. to find the correct explanation for
words.example_en: The discovery will help scientists unravel the mystery of the Ice Age.
words.example_zh: 这一发现将有助于科学家揭开冰川时代的奥秘。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. close in value or amount but not precise (2)v. to bring near or close
words.example_en: The cost of the project will approximate to 10, 000 pounds.
words.example_zh: 这项工程的成本约需一万英镑。

words.definition_en: n. a usually short narrative of an interesting, amusing, or biographical incident
words.example_en: He introduced his speech with a humorous anecdote.
words.example_zh: 他讲了一则幽默轶事作为讲演的引子。

words.definition_en: v. attract actively and strongly
