words.example_en: The child was lured into a car but managed to escape.
words.example_zh: 小孩被诱骗到汽车里但还是设法逃脱了。

words.definition_en: n. a belief that considers human beings as the most significant entity of the universe
words.example_en: Thoreau's thought exceeds the period in which he lives and accomplishes the springing from Anthropocentrism to Ecocentrism.
words.example_zh: 梭罗超越了他所处的时代，完成了从人类中心主义到生态中心主义的飞跃。

words.definition_en: n. a set of fundamental beliefs
words.example_en: “ Honesty is the best policy ” was his creed in all his business dealings.
words.example_zh: “诚实乃上策”是他做生意的信条。

words.definition_en: adj. having been someone or something specified in the past
words.example_en: The legislative body had voted to oust the country's onetime rulers.
words.example_zh: 立法机构通过投票将该国的前度领导人赶下了台。

words.definition_en: n. the act of praising or approving of someone or something
words.example_en: His conduct merits commendation.
words.example_zh: 他的行为应该受到称赞。

words.definition_en: n. a device that is used to remove something unwanted from a liquid or gas that passes through it
words.example_en: You might use a yellow filter to improve the clarity of a hazy horizon.
words.example_zh: 可以使用黄色滤光器让模糊的视野更加清晰。

words.definition_en: n. a state of mental and motor inactivity with partial or total insensibility
words.example_en: He had slumped into a state of torpor from which nothing could rouse him
words.example_zh: 他陷入麻木状态，任何事都不能令他振奋。

words.definition_en: v. to make denser or more compact
words.example_en: We have learnt how to condense serious messages into short, self-contained sentences
words.example_zh: 我们已经学会了如何把重要的信息压缩成简短独立的句子。

words.definition_en: adj. not correct
words.example_en: The newspaper published a retraction of the erroneous report.
words.example_zh: 那家报纸声明撤回那篇错误的报道。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to be grateful for (2)v. to understand the worth or importance of
words.example_en: In time you'll appreciate the beauty and subtlety of this language.
words.example_zh: 总有一天你会体会到这门语言的优美和微妙之处。

words.definition_en: v. to pull, force, or move by violent wringing or twisting movements
words.example_en: They attempted to wrest control of the town from government forces.
words.example_zh: 他们企图从政府军手中夺取对这个城镇的控制权。

words.definition_en: v. to get worse
words.example_en: When the tide ebbs it's a rock pool inhabited by crustaceans.
words.example_zh: 退潮时，它便成为甲壳动物居住的岩石区潮水潭。

words.definition_en: (1)n. the act of making fun of someone or something in a cruel or harsh way (2)v. to laugh at and make jokes about in a cruel or harsh way
words.example_en: She doesn't ridicule my timidity.
words.example_zh: 她不会嘲笑我的羞怯。

words.definition_en: n. one who travels to a shrine or holy place as a devotee
words.example_en: So Muhammad established the One True God in Mecca without injuring its pilgrim traffic.
words.example_zh: 穆罕默德就这样在麦加创立了惟一神，丝毫没有影响麦加朝圣者往来的数量。

words.definition_en: adj. elegant, stylish
words.example_en: Lord White is as sleek and elegant as any other millionaire businessman.
words.example_zh: 怀特勋爵像其他百万富商一样时尚高雅。

words.definition_en: v. to deal shrewdly or fraudulently with
words.example_en: He jockeyed the poor woman out of her savings.
words.example_zh: 他骗去了这可怜的妇人的储蓄。

words.definition_en: adj. respected and dignified
words.example_en: Jack is an august soldier.
words.example_zh: 杰克是一个威严的士兵。

words.definition_en: v. to become quiet or less
words.example_en: I took an aspirin and the pain gradually subsided.
words.example_zh: 我服了一片阿司匹林，疼痛逐渐缓解了。

words.definition_en: v. to credit or ascribe (something) to a person or a cause
words.example_en: It is grossly unfair to impute blame to the United Nations.
words.example_zh: 把责任归咎于联合国极其不公。

words.definition_en: n. deceitful cunning
words.example_en: His cunning and guile were not attributes I would ever underestimate.
words.example_zh: 我从未对他的狡猾和奸诈掉以轻心。

words.definition_en: adj. not rigidly fastened or securely attached
words.example_en: Check that the plug has not come loose .
words.example_zh: 检查一下别让插头松脱了。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not interesting (2)adj. too simple
words.example_en: They were of great service in correcting my jejune generalizations.
words.example_zh: 他们纠正了我不成熟的泛泛之论，帮了我大忙。

words.definition_en: n. excessive use of the first person singular personal pronoun
words.example_en: Egotism produces disrespectful attitudes toward others.
words.example_zh: 自我中心会产生对别人不尊重的态度。

words.definition_en: n. suffering or death caused by having nothing to eat or not enough to eat
words.example_en: Over three hundred people have died of starvation since the beginning of the year.
words.example_zh: 从年初起，已有 300 多人饿死。

words.definition_en: v. to gradually become smaller
words.example_en: Exports are dwindling and the trade deficit is swelling.
words.example_zh: 出口在缩减，贸易赤字在增长。

words.definition_en: adj. not based on facts
words.example_en: A ministry official described the report as groundless.
words.example_zh: 一位部级官员称这篇报道纯属子虚乌有。

words.definition_en: n. the state of looking or being like someone or something else
words.example_en: There was a remarkable resemblance between him and Pete.
words.example_zh: 他和皮特之间有着惊人的相似之处。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by harsh, insistent, and discordant sound
words.example_en: She tried to laugh, and the sound was harsh and strident.
words.example_zh: 她想笑，但声音听起来尖锐刺耳。

words.definition_en: adj. refusing to obey something or someone
words.example_en: Despite pulling back its missiles, the government maintained a defiant posture for home consumption.
words.example_zh: 尽管撤回了导弹，该政府在国内消费问题上仍旧保持蔑视的姿态。

words.definition_en: (1)n. the state of being simple, uncomplicated, or uncompounded (2)n. lack of subtlety or penetration
words.example_en: A pair of jeweled earrings will liven up this dress without detracting from its simplicity.
words.example_zh: 一对宝石耳环会让这件礼服更出彩而又不失简洁。

words.definition_en: v. to deprive of possessions
words.example_en: It is difficult to strip away the greasy dirt on the table.
words.example_zh: 要除去桌上的油污太难了。

words.definition_en: v. emphasize, stress
words.example_en: We underscore several strengths and limitations of the study.
words.example_zh: 我们强调了这项研究的几个优点和缺陷之处。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. sudden and extreme (2)adj. attracting attention
words.example_en: The Liberals have scored a dramatic victory in this by-election.
words.example_zh: 自由党在本次补选中取得引人注目的胜利。

words.definition_en: n. honor or respect that is felt for or shown to
words.example_en: The poem conveys his deep reverence for nature.
words.example_zh: 这首诗表达了他对大自然的深深崇敬之情。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to make by mixing different things together (2)v. to invent or develop
words.example_en: Nancy concocted some elaborate story to explain her absence.
words.example_zh: 南希精心编造了解释自己不在场的一些谎言。

words.definition_en: v. to impede or hamper the function or activity of
words.example_en: They can't encumber us on the road.
words.example_zh: 他们不会在路上拖累大家。

words.definition_en: adj. extraordinary, remarkable
words.example_en: Exports of Australian wine are growing at a phenomenal rate.
words.example_zh: 澳大利亚葡萄酒出口正在以惊人的速度增长。

words.definition_en: adj. excitingly attractive
words.example_en: Smoking is still attractive to many young people who see it as glamorous.
words.example_zh: 吸烟对很多年轻人还是很有诱惑力，他们觉得这让自己显得魅力十足。

words.definition_en: adj. having a tendency or inclination
words.example_en: First, it's possible that people already prone to hallucinations turn to caffeine as a way to self-medicate.
words.example_zh: 首先，可能是有幻觉倾向的人把咖啡因当做一种自我疗法的方式。

words.definition_en: v. to declare to be untrue or invalid
words.example_en: Nobody can gainsay his claims.
words.example_zh: 没人能够反驳他的说法。

words.definition_en: adj. sad or depressed
words.example_en: The short-term outlook for employment remains gloomy.
words.example_zh: 近期的就业前景依然暗淡。

words.definition_en: n. importance
words.example_en: Who leads Canada is also of some import to the rest of the world.
words.example_zh: 谁领导加拿大对世界来讲也具有一定的重要性。

words.definition_en: n. a feeling of good friendship among the people in a group
words.example_en: Everyone in our class is permeated with the spirit of camaraderie.
words.example_zh: 我们班贯穿着友爱的精神。

words.definition_en: adj. very large in amount or number
words.example_en: She took copious notes of the professor's lecture.
words.example_zh: 上教授的课她记了大量的笔记。

words.definition_en: v. to publicly say that you no longer have an opinion or belief that you once had
words.example_en: White House officials ordered Williams to recant.
words.example_zh: 白宫官员命令威廉斯公开宣布放弃自己的观点。

words.definition_en: n. great happiness or joy
words.example_en: The goal was greeted by jubilation from the home fans.
words.example_zh: 主场球迷为进球欢呼。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. having small holes that allow air or liquid to pass through (2)adj. easy to pass or get through
words.example_en: Oxford is a rather soft and porous cotton fabric.
words.example_zh: 牛津布是一种颇为柔软，多孔的棉织物。

words.definition_en: adj. trying to teach something in a way that is annoying or unwanted
words.example_en: The novel was preachy but sugared heavily with jokes.
words.example_zh: 这部小说虽然有说教性质，但却搀有大量笑话，使人易于接受。

words.definition_en: adj. subsisting or feeding on animal tissues
words.example_en: Tigers, lions and leopards are all ferocious carnivorous animals.
words.example_zh: 老虎、狮子和豹都是凶猛的食肉动物。

words.definition_en: adj. large and impressively beautiful
words.example_en: She was attracted by the majestic scenery.
words.example_zh: 她被这壮丽的景色所吸引。

words.definition_en: n. the action or process of increase especially in quantity or value
words.example_en: The average yearly increment in labour productivity in industry was 4.5 percent.
words.example_zh: 工业劳动生产率年平均增长4.5%。

words.definition_en: v. to respect greatly
words.example_en: The Ganges is hallowed as a sacred, cleansing river.
words.example_zh: 恒河被奉为有净化作用的圣河。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. pleasant and harmonious (2)adj. having the same nature, disposition or tastes
words.definition_zh: 令人愉快的性格相同的
words.example_en: She and he were congenial companion in youth.
words.example_zh: 他和她是竹马青梅，志趣相投。

words.definition_en: v. to prevail over
words.example_en: I realized I had to surmount the language barrier.
words.example_zh: 我意识到我不得不克服语言障碍。

words.definition_en: n. sphere, domain
words.example_en: In the realm of sculpture too, the technical skill of foreign artists was long recognized.
words.example_zh: 在雕塑领域，外国艺术家的技艺也早已得到认可。

words.definition_en: adj. repellent, disconcerting
words.definition_zh: 令人不愉快的，令人讨厌的
words.example_en: However, many customers found the smell of this product distinctly off-putting.
words.example_zh: 然而，很多顾客觉得该产品有一股异味，非常难闻。

words.definition_en: (1)adv. not working correctly or happening in the expected way (2)adj. not straight or neat
words.example_en: Things started to go awry just after you left the company.
words.example_zh: 你刚离开公司，事情就出了岔子。

words.definition_en: v. to destroy or interrupt the orderly structure or function of
words.example_en: I think that man has a spite against us, he is always trying to disorganize our plans.
words.example_zh: 我看那人对我们不怀好意，他老是企图破坏我们的计划。

words.definition_en: n. a lack of order or sequence
words.example_en: The nation is in disarray following rioting led by the military.
words.example_zh: 军队发动暴乱之后国家陷入混乱中。

words.definition_en: adj. done with thought and care
words.example_en: Her more measured response will appeal to voters.
words.example_zh: 她那经过斟酌的答复会受到选民的欢迎。

words.definition_en: adj. not true and is not based on facts
words.example_en: The charges against her are baseless.
words.example_zh: 对她的指控毫无根据。

words.definition_en: v. to dispute angrily or noisily
words.example_en: The neighbors altercated noisily over the boundary.
words.example_zh: 邻居们为边界问题大声争吵。

words.definition_en: adj. breaking out again
words.example_en: The patient presented with a case of recrudescent gastralgia.
words.example_zh: 患者的症状是复发性胃痛。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to weaken, damage, or destroy suddenly or in a way that causes surprise or embarrassment (2)v. to make a hole in with a sharp point
words.example_en: The sharp bone punctured his lung.
words.example_zh: 尖锐的骨头刺穿了他的肺。

words.definition_en: v. to elevate by praise or in estimation
words.example_en: His work exalts all those virtues that we, as Americans, are taught to hold dear.
words.example_zh: 他的作品颂扬了所有那些身为美国人应该珍视的美德。

words.definition_en: adj. mentally sharp or clever
words.example_en: He made a series of astute business decisions.
words.example_zh: 他作出了一系列精明的商业决策。

words.definition_en: v. to exhibit in a ostentatious or aggressive manner
words.example_en: As a modest scholar, my professor never brandishes his intellect.
words.example_zh: 作为一名谦虚的学者，我的教授从不吹嘘自己的才智。

words.definition_en: v. to stop from doing something or to stop from happening
words.example_en: Relief efforts have been stymied in recent weeks by armed gunmen.
words.example_zh: 最近几周的救援工作一直受到武装分子的阻挠。

words.definition_en: v. persist, continue
words.example_en: Old people often subsist on very small incomes.
words.example_zh: 老人往往靠十分微薄的收入艰难度日。

words.definition_en: n. public violence, tumult, or disorder
words.example_en: Twelve inmates have been killed during a riot at the prison.
words.example_zh: 12名犯人在一场监狱暴乱中被杀害。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. hard to handle or manage because of size or weight (2)adj. complicated and hard to do
words.example_en: Although the machine looks cumbersome, it is actually easy to use.
words.example_zh: 尽管这台机器看上去很笨重，操作起来却很容易。

words.definition_en: v. to vacillate irresolutely between choices
words.example_en: She never wavered in her determination to succeed.
words.example_zh: 她要取得成功的决心从未动摇过。

words.definition_en: adj. deserving to be laughed at
words.example_en: The entire proposal is risible.
words.example_zh: 这个建议完全是荒唐可笑的.

words.definition_en: v. to change from one form or function to another
words.example_en: Innovators rarely come up with new ideas; instead, they convert old ideas into new ones, adapting them from one context to another.
words.example_zh: 创新者很少产生新创意。他们把老的创意变成新创意，让其从一个环境转换到另一个环境。

words.definition_en: adj. irritably sullen and churlish in mood or manner
words.example_en: He is too surly to reason with.
words.example_zh: 他态度强横，没法跟他讲理。

words.definition_en: n. a sudden increase in knowledge, understanding, etc.
words.example_en: Scientists have made a breakthrough in their treatment of that disease.
words.example_zh: 科学家在治疗那种疾病方面已有突破。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a very smart or talented person (2)n. great natural ability
words.example_en: This is the mark of her real genius as a designer.
words.example_zh: 这展现了她作为设计师的真正天赋。

words.definition_en: v. to remove from power
words.example_en: The general is accused of conniving in a plot to topple the government.
words.example_zh: 将军被指控纵容一个颠覆政府的阴谋。

words.definition_en: v. to utter or send out with denunciation
words.example_en: They all fulminated against the new curriculum.
words.example_zh: 他们都对新课表表示强烈不满。

words.definition_en: v. to convey as a gift
words.example_en: The Queen has bestowed a knighthood on him.
words.example_zh: 女王已经授予他爵士头衔。

words.definition_en: adj. difficult to handle, control, or deal with because of being large, heavy, or complex
words.example_en: They came panting up to his door with their unwieldy baggage.
words.example_zh: 他们提着笨重的行李气喘吁吁地来到他门口。

words.definition_en: v. extinguish
words.example_en: Men came with buckets of water and began to douse the flames.
words.example_zh: 男人们提来一桶桶水灭火。

words.definition_en: v. to free of unwanted or inferior elements
words.example_en: Administration officials have winnowed the list of candidates to three.
words.example_zh: 行政官员将候选名单筛减至3人。

words.definition_en: v. to make more acute, intense, or effective
words.example_en: She honed her debating skills at college.
words.example_zh: 她在大学时便练就了辩论技巧。

words.definition_en: n. a leading element, force, or influence in an undertaking or development
words.example_en: The marines went ashore as a spearhead this morning to capture key targets.
words.example_zh: 今天早晨，海军陆战队作为先头部队率先登陆以占领主要目标。

words.definition_en: v. confuse
words.example_en: I'm often flummoxed by current slang.
words.example_zh: 我一直听不太懂这种流行语。

words.definition_en: adj. evoking or tending to evoke an especially emotional response
words.example_en: Those old toys are evocative of my boyhood.
words.example_zh: 这些旧玩具唤起我对童年的回忆。

words.definition_en: v. to produce or cause to be produced
words.example_en: To generate new money the sport needs to be more entertaining.
words.example_zh: 要想创造更多的收入，这项体育活动需要变得更加有趣味性。

words.definition_en: n. a place where events of a specific type are held
words.example_en: The International Convention Centre is the venue for a three-day arts festival.
words.example_zh: 国际会议中心是为期3天的艺术节的举办场所。

words.definition_en: n. a magnificent or impressive array
words.example_en: In a few months, the British royal family will be yet again rebranded and relaunched in the panoply of a wedding.
words.example_zh: 再过几个月，英国王室将再次集体亮相，再现一场盛大的王室婚礼。

words.definition_en: adj. unlawful
words.example_en: Dante clearly condemns illicit love.
words.example_zh: 丹蒂旗帜鲜明地谴责不正当恋情。

words.definition_en: adj. normally used up or consumed in service
words.example_en: We need such expendable supplies as pencils, ink and paper.
words.example_zh: 我们需要像铅笔、墨水和纸张这样的消费品。

words.definition_en: v. to be slow to act
words.example_en: We like to linger in the change phases that we enjoy most.
words.example_zh: 我们习惯徘徊在自己最喜欢的改变阶段。

words.definition_en: n. a careful preservation and protection of something
words.example_en: The zoo is doing major conservation work, both in captivity and in the field.
words.example_zh: 无论在圈养场还是在野外，动物园在动物保护方面都承担了主要的工作。

words.definition_en: n. a place or situation regarded as isolated, stagnant or backward
words.definition_zh: 停滞不前的地方或状态
words.example_en: Quebec remained a backwater until the 1960s.
words.example_zh: 魁北克直到20世纪60年代还是一片死气沉沉。

words.definition_en: v. to make more serious or severe
words.example_en: South Korean media said the move will further aggravate the tense situation in the peninsula.
words.example_zh: 韩国媒体称，此举将进一步加剧半岛紧张局势。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by or given to reminiscence
words.example_en: Her writing style was reminiscent of ancient classical writers .
words.example_zh: 她的写作风格使人联想到古代的古典作家。

words.definition_en: adj. humorous
words.example_en: It has the waggish adorableness and the tripping-and-falling roughhouse of other animated films, but it's also a work of tragic nostalgia.
words.example_zh: 这部电影不但像其他动画作品一样有着滑稽可爱的动画角色，和跌宕起伏的嬉闹场面，同时，它也不失为一部充满悲剧色彩得感伤作品。

words.definition_en: adj. very polite in a way that shows respect
words.example_en: The staff is well trained in courteous and attentive service to each and every guest.
words.example_zh: 员工训练有素，为每一位客人提供礼貌周到的服务。

words.definition_en: adj. open to view
words.example_en: The ostensible reason wasn’t the real reason.
words.example_zh: 表面上的理由并不是真正的理由。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. causing very great excitement or interest with shocking details (2)adj. very excellent or great
words.example_en: Papers of this kind are full of sensational news reports.
words.example_zh: 这类报纸满是耸人听闻的新闻报道。

words.definition_en: adj. not important or significant
words.example_en: Whether we like him or not is immaterial.
words.example_zh: 我们是否喜欢他无关紧要。

words.definition_en: adj. causing fear or alarm
words.example_en: She was a redoubtable fighter, and strange cats were vanquished in one round.
words.example_zh: 她是个令人敬畏的斗士，陌生的猫一轮就会被她击败。

words.definition_en: n. edge, periphery
words.example_en: It was easier to move about on the fringe of the crowd.
words.example_zh: 在人群外围移动比较容易。

words.definition_en: adj. separated by different opinions
words.example_en: The profession is divided on the issue.
words.example_zh: 对于这一问题，从业人士意见有分歧。

words.definition_en: adj. coming from or belonging to a time in the past
words.example_en: The dated machinery is still housed in one section of the factory, but it is being phased out.
words.example_zh: 这些陈旧的机器如今仍然待在工厂一角，但正在被逐渐淘汰。

words.definition_en: v. to describe, portray, or set forth with accuracy or in detail
words.example_en: Biography must to some extent delineate characters.
words.example_zh: 传记必须在一定程度上描绘人物。

words.definition_en: n. the way in which linguistic elements (such as words) are put together to form constituents
words.example_en: Each computer language has its own syntax and vocabulary.
words.example_zh: 每种计算机语言都有自己的语法和词汇。

words.definition_en: adj. prosaic
words.example_en: The prose details of the budget bored the audience.
words.example_zh: 预算中平淡乏味的细节让听众感到厌烦。

words.definition_en: adj. exceeding or violating propriety or fitness
words.example_en: This would help the families to survive the drought without undue suffering.
words.example_zh: 这将有助于这些家庭免受过多痛苦来度过这次旱灾。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to swell or distend with air or gas (2)v. to think or say that is larger or more important than it really is
words.example_en: They inflated clients' medical treatment to defraud insurance companies.
words.example_zh: 他们夸大客户伤势的严重性和治疗情形以欺骗保险公司。

words.definition_en: adj. being under obligation for a favor or gift
words.example_en: He was made beholden to the Mafia.
words.example_zh: 他受过黑手党的恩惠。

words.definition_en: adj. lasting forever
words.example_en: Eternal glory to the people's heroes.
words.example_zh: 人民英雄永垂不朽。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. marked by or indicating a liking for companionship (2)adj. growing in a cluster or colony
words.example_en: Holmes was gregarious, a great wit, a man of wide interests.
words.example_zh: 福尔摩斯爱交际，非常风趣，兴趣广泛。

words.definition_en: adj. causing distress or worry
words.example_en: Water pollution is among the most worrisome byproducts of China's rapid economic growth.
words.example_zh: 水污染是中国经济高速发展最令人不安的副产品之一。

words.definition_en: adj. relating to the statistical characteristics of populations
words.definition_zh: 人口统计学的；人口结构的
words.example_en: There have been monumental social and demographic changes in the country.
words.example_zh: 国家已有了巨大的社会和人口状况变化。

words.definition_en: v. to goad or urge forward
words.example_en: His object was to instigate a little rebellion on the part of the bishop.
words.example_zh: 他的目的是,在主教方面煽起一场小小的造反。

words.definition_en: v. to control or direct with or as if with reins
words.example_en: The government would try to rein back inflation.
words.example_zh: 政府将努力控制通货膨胀。

words.definition_en: v. to make better or more tolerable
words.example_en: Nothing can be done to ameliorate the situation.
words.example_zh: 做什么也无法使情况好转。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to form by heating and hammering (2)v. to make or imitate falsely especially with intent to defraud
words.example_en: He's getting good at forging his mother's signature.
words.example_zh: 他把母亲的签名伪造得越来越惟妙惟肖了。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to produce or provide (2)v. to surrender or submit to another
words.example_en: He reluctantly yielded to their demands.
words.example_zh: 他不情愿地屈从于他们的要求。

words.definition_en: adj. having the same value, use, meaning, etc.
words.example_en: The cost of even the cheapest car is equivalent to seventy years' salary for a government worker.
words.example_zh: 即使最便宜的汽车，其价格也相当于一名公务员七十年的工资。

words.definition_en: adj. depending on something else that might or might not happen
words.example_en: The role they play is contingent on their political ability and zeal.
words.example_zh: 他们起的作用取决于他们的政治能力和热情。

words.definition_en: v. to begin to understand or deal with something in a direct or effective way
words.example_en: Many investigators had grappled with the problem, usually with poor results.
words.example_zh: 许多研究人员曾设法处理这个问题，但往往成效不佳。

words.definition_en: n. something that is incongruous
words.example_en: There is an absurd incongruity between her wealth and her lifestyle.
words.example_zh: 她的财富与生活方式之间几近荒谬的不相称。

words.definition_en: adj. using or expressed in lofty, pompous language
words.definition_zh: 措辞浮夸的；辞藻华丽的
words.example_en: He was a performer who loved making grandiloquent gesture.
words.example_zh: 他是一个喜欢打夸张手势的演员。

words.definition_en: v. to picture to oneself
words.example_en: She envisioned the admiring glances of guests seeing her home.
words.example_zh: 她想像着客人们看到她家时的那种羡慕的目光。

words.definition_en: n. the feeling that people express when they criticize and laugh at someone or something in an insulting way
words.example_en: Americans speak with derision of those who are lazy.
words.example_zh: 美国人总是带着嘲笑的口气谈论那些懒惰的人。

words.definition_en: adj. disgusting or distasteful by reason of excess
words.example_en: Her cheap, cloying scent enveloped him.
words.example_zh: 她那廉价香水的甜腻气味一下子包围了他。

words.definition_en: v. to express disapproval by shouting 'boo'
words.example_en: The audience booed as she started her speech.
words.example_zh: 她一开始讲话，听众便发出一阵嘘声。

words.definition_en: adj. varying in pitch from the proper tone of a melody
words.example_en: Moe was having fun banging the drums and singing off-key.
words.example_zh: 莫自得其乐地一边敲着鼓，一边不成调地唱着歌。

words.definition_en: v. to talk boastfully
words.example_en: The player is always bragging about his prowess as a cricketer
words.example_zh: 这名运动员总是吹嘘自己板球球技的高超。

words.definition_en: v. to happen as a result
words.example_en: Trouble will ensue if wind of this scandal gets out.
words.example_zh: 这件丑事一露出风声便会招来麻烦。

words.definition_en: n. the quality or state of not supporting either side in an argument, fight, war, etc.
words.example_en: They had thrown their neutrality overboard in the crisis.
words.example_zh: 他们在这场危机中丢弃了中立立场。

words.definition_en: adj. discreetly cautious
words.example_en: I am rather chary of making too many idiotic mistakes.
words.example_zh: 我小心翼翼避免犯太多的愚蠢错误。

words.definition_en: v. to provide with what is needed
words.example_en: She furnished him with the facts surrounding the case.
words.example_zh: 她向他提供了与案件有关的事实。

words.definition_en: adv. into parts
words.example_en: The debate is tearing Wall Street asunder.
words.example_zh: 这场争论令华尔街分崩离析。

words.definition_en: adj. tending or intended to evade: equivocal
words.example_en: They decided to take evasive action.
words.example_zh: 他们决定采取回避行动。

words.definition_en: adj. following one after the other in a series
words.example_en: Jackson was the winner for a second successive year.
words.example_zh: 杰克逊已经是连续第二年获胜了。

words.definition_en: adj. causing damage or injury
words.example_en: Poor eating habits are detrimental to health.
words.example_zh: 不良的饮食习惯对健康有害。

words.definition_en: n. a small piece of paper that allows one to get a service or product for free or at a lower price
words.example_en: Bring the coupon below to any Tecno store and pay just £ 10.99.
words.example_zh: 持下面的优惠券到任意一家Tecno专卖店，只需付10.99英镑。

words.definition_en: adj. putting an end to debate or question especially by reason of irrefutability
words.example_en: Her attorneys claim there is no conclusive evidence that any murders took place.
words.example_zh: 她的律师声称并无确凿证据表明发生了谋杀。

words.definition_en: v. scream
words.example_en: The phone shrilled, making her jump.
words.example_zh: 电话铃声刺耳地响起，惊得她跳了起来。

words.definition_en: n. friendly social atmosphere
words.example_en: The comity needs sincerity to measure, but the sincerity is offer by comity.
words.example_zh: 友谊需真诚来衡量，而真诚由友谊来奉献。

words.definition_en: v. to hinder from passage, action, or operation
words.example_en: A number of local people have been arrested for trying to obstruct lorries loaded with logs.
words.example_zh: 一些当地人因试图阻挡满载原木的卡车通行而被逮捕。

words.definition_en: adj. showing or characterized by a superior attitude towards others
words.example_en: The Chinese court assumed an aloof and patronizing attitude toward these Westerners, keeping them confined to the southern port cities of Macao and later, Canton.
words.example_zh: 中国朝廷对这些西方人采取的是冷漠和施恩的态度，把他们限制在南部的港口城市：澳门以及和后来的广州。

words.definition_en: adj. done, made, or accomplished piece by piece or in a fragmentary way
words.example_en: Instead of the government's piecemeal approach, what is needed is a radical shake-up of 16-19 education.
words.example_zh: 需要的不是政府零敲碎打的措施，而是要对16至19岁年龄段的教育进行彻底变革。

words.definition_en: adj. of moderate or low quality, value, ability, or performance
words.example_en: His school record was mediocre.
words.example_zh: 他在学校成绩平平。

words.definition_en: adj. causing or tending to cause sadness or depression of mind or spirit
words.example_en: The general watched the process with an air of melancholy.
words.example_zh: 将军神情忧郁地望着队列经过。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. roughly outlined (2)adj. wanting in completeness, clearness, or substance: slight, superficial
words.example_en: He gave us a very sketchy account of his visit.
words.example_zh: 他跟我们非常粗略地讲了他参观的情况。

words.definition_en: n. the principle or practice of complete abstinence from alcoholic drinks
words.example_en: Teetotalism is against nature.
words.example_zh: 滴酒不沾是违背天性的。

words.definition_en: adj. broodingly morose
words.example_en: The princess looked glum but later cheered up.
words.example_zh: 公主看起来闷闷不乐，但后来又振作起来。

words.definition_en: v. to exceed in number
words.example_en: The demonstrators were heavily outnumbered by the police.
words.example_zh: 示威者人数远不及警察人数。

words.definition_en: v. to refuse to acknowledge as one's own
words.example_en: Those comments were later disowned by an official army spokesman.
words.example_zh: 这些话随后遭到了军方一位官方发言人的否认。

words.definition_en: v. to praise in a very strong and enthusiastic way
words.example_en: Critics acclaimed Angela Bassett for her excellent performance.
words.example_zh: 评论家盛赞安吉拉·贝塞特的出色表演。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. marked by unruly or aggressive noisiness (2)adj. stubbornly resistant to control
words.example_en: Ms Chen finds it hard to manage a class of obstreperous students.
words.example_zh: 面对一班吵闹的学生，陈老师感到难以管束。

words.definition_en: adj. relating to a subject in an appropriate way
words.example_en: He asks questions that are germane and central to the issue.
words.example_zh: 他问了一些与论点有密切关系的重要问题。

words.definition_en: n. a person who falsely pretends to know or be something in order to deceive people
words.example_en: He was exposed as a charlatan.
words.example_zh: 他被揭穿，原来是个骗子。

words.definition_en: adj. relaxed and casual in style or manner
words.example_en: I noticed that man who is regarded as easygoing and agreeable.
words.example_zh: 我注意到那个男人，大家认为他是个看起来逍遥自在和惬意的人。

words.definition_en: adj. childish, silly
words.example_en: The story is simple, even puerile.
words.example_zh: 故事很简单，甚至有些幼稚。

words.definition_en: v. to include or consist of
words.example_en: The task force is comprised of congressional leaders, cabinet heads and administration officials.
words.example_zh: 工作小组由国会领导人、内阁首脑和行政官员组成。

words.definition_en: n. a principle, belief, or doctrine generally held to be true
words.example_en: Non-violence and patience are the central tenets of their faith.
words.example_zh: 非暴力和忍耐是他们的信仰的核心原则。

words.definition_en: v. to make less angry
words.example_en: He tried to find ways of mollifying her.
words.example_zh: 他想方设法安慰她。

words.definition_en: n. the state or condition of not having enough of something
words.example_en: There was a dearth of reliable information on the subject.
words.example_zh: 关于这个课题缺乏可靠资料。

words.definition_en: adj. attractive, tempting
words.example_en: The mellow wine and fruit are so inviting.
words.example_zh: 醇酒和熟透了的水果是这样诱人。

words.definition_en: adj. becoming angry very easily
words.example_en: He had an irascible temper.
words.example_zh: 他脾气易怒。

words.definition_en: adj. more interested in reading books and studying than doing more physical activities
words.example_en: He had a bookish and rather shy look.
words.example_zh: 他外表有点学究气，相当害羞。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by display of feeling
words.example_en: We came from the English tradition of not being demonstrative.
words.example_zh: 我们继承了英国人轻易不表露感情的传统。

words.definition_en: adj. in a low position, manner, or degree
words.example_en: He was irked by his lowly status.
words.example_zh: 他为自己地位卑微而恼恨。

words.definition_en: adj. following the current fashion or style
words.example_en: She is a dainty, chic woman with wide-set eyes and the neck of a ballerina.
words.example_zh: 她是一个秀丽时髦的女人，有一对大眼睛，同样有着芭蕾舞女演员一样的脖子。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to make go away or end (2)v. to drive away by or as if by scattering
words.example_en: The President is attempting to dispel the notion that he has neglected the economy.
words.example_zh: 有人认为总统忽视了经济，总统正试图消除这种看法。

words.definition_en: adj. causing unnecessary fear or anxiety that something unpleasant or dangerous is going to happen
words.example_en: Some people are inclined to dismiss such questions as alarmist.
words.example_zh: 有些人倾向于将这样的问题斥为耸人听闻.

words.definition_en: v. to dissolve out by the action of a percolating liquid
words.example_en: The water percolating through the soil may leach out minerals.
words.example_zh: 经土壤过滤的水会滤掉矿物质。

words.definition_en: v. to give, convey, or grant from or as if from a store
words.example_en: The ability to impart knowledge and command respect is the essential qualification for teachers.
words.example_zh: 具备传授知识并赢得尊敬的能力是对教师的基本要求。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to lower in grade, rank, or status (2)v. to make the quality of worse (3)v. to wear down by erosion
words.example_en: A dishonest action like that will degrade you.
words.example_zh: 像这样不诚实的行为会使你降低身份。

words.definition_en: v. to make necessary
words.example_en: A prolonged drought had necessitated the introduction of water rationing.
words.example_zh: 一场持续的干旱使定量供水的引进成为必需。

words.definition_en: adj. too bad to be corrected or repaired
words.example_en: The move would cause irreparable harm to the organization.
words.example_zh: 这一举动将给该组织带来不可补救的损害。

words.definition_en: v. to wander from the direct course or way
words.example_en: Her grey hair straggled in wisps about her face.
words.example_zh: 她那灰白的头发一缕缕地披散在脸旁。

words.definition_en: adj. hard, harsh, or severe in manner or character
words.example_en: The police are planning sterner measures to combat crime.
words.example_zh: 警方正制订更严厉的措施打击犯罪活动。

words.definition_en: n. a retaliatory act
words.example_en: They did not want to give evidence for fear of reprisals.
words.example_zh: 他们因为害怕报复而不想作证。

words.definition_en: adj. not polite
words.example_en: It would seem churlish to refuse.
words.example_zh: 拒绝似乎显得无礼。

words.definition_en: n. a person or thing that receives help or an advantage from something
words.example_en: The main beneficiaries of pension equality so far have been men.
words.example_zh: 迄今为止，退休金均等政策的主要受益者一直是男性。

words.definition_en: prep. except for; unless something happens
words.example_en: Barring accidents, I believe they will succeed.
words.example_zh: 不出意外的话，我相信他们会成功的。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to be very interested in
words.example_en: A new generation of scientists became fascinated by dinosaurs.
words.example_zh: 新一代科学家对恐龙研究着了迷。

words.definition_en: v. to bring to bear especially with sustained effort or lasting effect
words.example_en: He exerted considerable influence on the thinking of the scientific community on these issues.
words.example_zh: 他极大地影响了科学界对这些问题的看法。

words.definition_en: n. a checking of progress
words.example_en: The breakdown in talks represents a temporary setback in the peace process.
words.example_zh: 谈判破裂意味着和平进程暂时受阻。

words.definition_en: n. a sense of common purpose with respect to a group
words.example_en: Morale perked up immediately at the good news.
words.example_zh: 这个好消息立刻振作起大家的士气。

words.definition_en: adj. in a badly damaged or deteriorated condition
words.example_en: The blighted roses never recovered from the frost.
words.example_zh: 这些枯萎的玫瑰始终未能从霜冻中恢复过来。

words.definition_en: adj. having or preceding from authority
words.example_en: Amanda launched into an authoritative disquisition about contracts.
words.example_zh: 阿曼达就合约给出了权威性的阐述。

words.definition_en: n. a fundamental and inevitably true value
words.example_en: This book reveals some verities of human nature.
words.example_zh: 这本书揭示了关于人性的一些真理。

words.definition_en: v. to bring about or carry out (something, such as a crime or deception)
words.example_en: You begin to ask yourself what kind of person perpetrated this crime.
words.example_zh: 你开始自问是什么样的人犯下这样的罪行。

words.definition_en: n. an interpretation of what is not human or personal in terms of human or personal characteristics
words.example_en: One uses the language of anthropomorphism in saying that God sees, hears, knows, and loves.
words.example_zh: 我们说上帝能见，能听，能知，能爱，就是在使用神人拟人化的言语。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. working closely and well together (2)adj. able to talk or express yourself in a clear way that can be easily understood
words.example_en: Little attempt was made to integrate the parts into a coherent whole.
words.example_zh: 在把各部分结合成一个连贯的整体方面几乎未作任何尝试。

words.definition_en: adj. free from agitation of mind or spirit
words.example_en: The tranquil atmosphere of the inn allows guests to feel totally at home.
words.example_zh: 这家客栈的宁静氛围让客人们感到宾至如归。

words.definition_en: adj. astonishing, overwhelming
words.example_en: The results from this unit are staggering.
words.example_zh: 这个团队得出的结果令人吃惊。

words.definition_en: v. to promise the performance of by a pledge
words.example_en: Mr Dudley has pledged to give any award to charity.
words.example_zh: 达德利先生已承诺将任何所获的损害赔偿额都捐给慈善机构。

words.definition_en: adv. it can be argued
words.example_en: Arguably, 1932 was the year of greatest achievement at the Laboratories.
words.example_zh: 可以说1932年是该实验室最有收获的一年。

words.definition_en: v. to say that is larger or greater than it really is
words.example_en: The authors no doubt overstated their case with a view to catching the public's attention.
words.example_zh: 作者们毫无疑问地夸大了他们的情形以吸引公众的注意力。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to drive danger or attack away from (2)v. to maintain or support in the face of argument or hostile criticism
words.example_en: He has hired a lawyer to defend him against the allegations.
words.example_zh: 他已聘请了一位律师针对这些指控为自己辩护。

words.definition_en: adj. being, living, or going alone or without companions
words.example_en: He led a solitary life.
words.example_zh: 他过着独居生活。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not to be avoided or evaded (2)adj. having power to restrain, control, and direct
words.definition_zh: 必要的命令的，势在必行的
words.example_en: It is imperative that the government create more jobs.
words.example_zh: 政府必须创造更多就业机会。

words.definition_en: v. to express scornfully one's low opinion of
words.example_en: To assert this is to denigrate the effectiveness of the police.
words.example_zh: 坚持这一点就是贬低警方的办事能力。

words.definition_en: adj. strikingly out of the ordinary
words.example_en: His fast-paced novels are full of bizarre situations and madcap antics.
words.example_zh: 他那些快节奏的小说里充满了怪诞的场景和荒唐的行为。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a large amount of money and possessions (2)n. a large amount or number
words.example_en: The purpose of industry is to create wealth.
words.example_zh: 工业的目的是创造财富。

words.definition_en: adj. very complicated
words.example_en: The operations can be quite involved, requiring many procedures in order to restructure the anatomy.
words.example_zh: 手术可能会十分复杂，需要许多步骤来重建结构。

words.definition_en: v. to control and use the natural force or power of something
words.example_en: Harness your nervous energy and transform it into vitality and enthusiasm.
words.example_zh: 要控制你的紧张情绪并把它变成活力和热情。

words.definition_en: n. a chief support
words.example_en: As the US economy has slowed, Asian exporting firms - a mainstay of the region's economies - have been hard hit.
words.example_zh: 美国经济的放缓，对于作为该地区经济支柱的亚洲出口公司来说是一个沉重的打击。

words.definition_en: adj. restricted or peculiar to a locality or region
words.example_en: Street crime is virtually endemic in large cities.
words.example_zh: 街头犯罪近乎是大城市的通病。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. out of date or old-fashioned (2)adj. something that is mistakenly placed in a time where it does not belong in a story, movie, etc.
words.example_en: It is a shame such things have come to seem so anachronistic.
words.example_zh: 遗憾的是这类事物最终流于不合时宜了。

words.definition_en: v. to assail by words or argument
words.example_en: All that Cheney has done was to impugn Obama's policies.
words.example_zh: 切尼所做的一切都是指责奥巴马的政策

words.definition_en: adj. existing or being something in name or form only
words.example_en: As he was still not allowed to run a company, his wife became its nominal head.
words.example_zh: 因为他仍未获准经营公司，他妻子成了名义上的老板。

words.definition_en: adj. complacently or inanely foolish
words.example_en: Why did the fatuous King send Qu Yuan into exile?
words.example_zh: 昏庸的楚王为什么把屈原流放了?

words.definition_en: n. the act of maintaining in good condition
words.example_en: Even if expansion plans are put on hold, existing grids will require repair and constant upkeep.
words.example_zh: 即使（电力）扩张计划被搁置，现有电网将需要不断保养和维修。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. required to be responsible for something (2)adj. capable of being explained
words.example_en: President Barack Obama yesterday promised that BP would be held accountable if it had broken US laws.
words.example_zh: 美国总统巴拉克-奥巴马昨日许诺，如果BP触犯了美国法律，将追究其责任。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking distinctive or interesting qualities
words.example_en: She lived in a nondescript suburban apartment block.
words.example_zh: 她住在一个毫无特征的市郊公寓楼里。

words.definition_en: n. a source of relief or consolation
words.example_en: I found solace in writing when my father died three years ago.
words.example_zh: 我父亲3年前去世的时候，我从写作中找到了安慰。

words.definition_en: adj. physiologically immature or undeveloped
words.example_en: We can curb juvenile delinquency by education.
words.example_zh: 我们可以通过教育来控制少年犯罪。

words.definition_en: adj. base one something that is not true or real
words.example_en: It seemed an idealistic and illusory dream.
words.example_zh: 那似乎是个不切实际的幻梦。

words.definition_en: v. to ask humbly and earnestly of
words.example_en: She supplicated the judge for protection.
words.example_zh: 她恳求法官保护。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to speak to in a way that expresses disapproval or criticism (2)v. to tell or urge to do something
words.example_en: My wife admonished me for taking risks with my health.
words.example_zh: 我的妻子责备我不应拿自己的健康冒险。

words.definition_en: adj. notably polite or polished in manner
words.example_en: He tried hard to be urbane.
words.example_zh: 他极力作出彬彬有礼的神态。

words.definition_en: adj. causing the body or part of the body to become weaker or less able to function as time passes
words.example_en: Parkinson's disease is a degenerative disorder, which attacks parts of the brain that control movement.
words.example_zh: 帕金森病是退化性疾病，它袭击大脑中控制运动的部分。

words.definition_en: adj. easily affected, influenced, or harmed by something
words.example_en: Some of these plants are more susceptible to frost damage than others.
words.example_zh: 这些植物中有一些较其他的易受霜冻危害。

words.definition_en: v. to engage in contemplation or reflection
words.example_en: That's when I try to meditate or do some deep-breathing exercises.
words.example_zh: 这时我就会试着沉思或者做些深呼吸练习。

words.definition_en: adj. made, shown, or done in a way that is not easily seen or notices
words.example_en: They have been supplying covert military aid to the rebels.
words.example_zh: 他们一直在暗地里向叛乱者提供军事支援。

words.definition_en: adj. important, consequential
words.example_en: I am deeply honored to be invited to this momentous occasion.
words.example_zh: 能应邀出席如此重要的场合，我深感荣幸。

words.definition_en: adj. loving peace
words.example_en: The Liberals were traditionally seen as the more pacific party.
words.example_zh: 传统上，自由党人被认为更加爱好和平。

words.definition_en: adj. not genuine, sincere, or authentic
words.example_en: He had managed to create the entirely spurious impression that the company was thriving.
words.example_zh: 他设法制造出一种彻头彻尾的假象，让人误以为公司一派兴旺。

words.definition_en: adj. in the wrong way
words.example_en: Their instincts warned them something was amiss.
words.example_zh: 他们直觉上感到有什么地方不对劲儿。

words.definition_en: adj. used to describe things that touch each other or are immediately next to each other
words.example_en: Its vineyards are virtually contiguous with those of Ausone.
words.example_zh: 其葡萄园差不多与着欧颂堡的葡萄园相邻。

words.definition_en: n. an untrue statement that is made to damage someone’s reputation
words.example_en: This is a malicious calumny.
words.example_zh: 这完全是恶意诽谤。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to execute tactical movements
words.example_en: The pilot was able to maneuver the crippled aircraft out of the hostile area.
words.example_zh: 飞行员成功地操纵严重损坏的飞机驶出敌区。

words.definition_en: n. the quality or state of being dual or of having a dual nature
words.example_en: The nature of a state is a social man rather than an economic man, with a dualism of being both self-interested and altruistic.
words.example_zh: 国家的本性不是经济人，而是社会人，具有利己和利他的双重性。

words.definition_en: v. to make no longer necessary
words.example_en: This deferral would obviate pressure on the rouble exchange rate.
words.example_zh: 这一延期将消除卢布汇率面临的压力。

words.definition_en: adj. irreproachable
words.example_en: We deliver faultless products on time.
words.example_zh: 我们按时交付无缺陷的产品。

words.definition_en: adj. incapable of being taken by assault
words.example_en: The Bundesbank's seemingly impregnable position has begun to weaken.
words.example_zh: 德意志联邦银行看似不可撼动的地位开始削弱了。

words.definition_en: n. a strong feeling of excitement and enthusiasm
words.example_en: The speech aroused nationalist fervor.
words.example_zh: 这个演讲喚起了民族主义热情。

words.definition_en: n. something that prevents or blocks movement from one place to another
words.example_en: The Yangtze river is a natural barrier to the north-east.
words.example_zh: 长江是东北方向的一道天然屏障。

words.definition_en: adj. threadbare and faded from wear
words.example_en: The old man was shabby and unkempt.
words.example_zh: 老头蓬头垢面，衣衫褴褛。

words.definition_en: n. the state of being joined in some activity or effort
words.example_en: What will be the effect of the alliance between IBM and Apple?
words.example_zh: 若IBM公司和苹果公司联手将会有什么效果呢？

words.definition_en: adj. not having or showing a desire to be noticed, praised, etc.
words.example_en: He's a man of few words, very polite and unassuming.
words.example_zh: 他话不多，很有礼貌，为人谦和。

words.definition_en: adj. imperfectly formed or formulated
words.example_en: The plan remained inchoate and lacked clear details.
words.example_zh: 这个计划仍未成形，缺乏明确细节。

words.definition_en: adj. degraded, sordid
words.example_en: It’s well known that the world of entertainment has its seamy side: drug abuse, corruption, alcoholism.
words.example_zh: 众所周知，演艺界有其肮脏的一面：滥用毒品、堕落腐化、酗酒等等。

words.definition_en: n. nimbleness, dexterity
words.example_en: His intellect and mental agility have never been in doubt.
words.example_zh: 他的才智和机敏从未受到怀疑。

words.definition_en: (1)n. unlimited power over a country (2)n. a country’s independent authority and the right to govern itself
words.example_en: These people cherish their independence and sovereignty.
words.example_zh: 这些人民珍视他们的独立和主权。

words.definition_en: n. extreme hatred or dislike
words.example_en: People always hold this abominable behavior in detestation.
words.example_zh: 这种恶劣作风一向叫人厌恶。

words.definition_en: adj. very excited about and interested in something
words.example_en: We are keen that our school should get involved too.
words.example_zh: 我们盼着自己的学校也参与其中。

words.definition_en: adj. having knowledge or experience
words.example_en: Those in business are not, on the whole, conversant with basic scientific principles.
words.example_zh: 总的来说，那些从商的人并不太熟悉基本的科学原理。

words.definition_en: adj. showing or feeling very strong emotions
words.example_en: He made an impassioned appeal for peace.
words.example_zh: 他慷慨陈词，呼吁和平。

words.definition_en: v. to refuse to admit or approve
words.example_en: The president vetoed the economic package passed by Congress.
words.example_zh: 总统否决了国会通过的一揽子经济计划。

words.definition_en: adj. of a kind that can be remitted
words.example_en: Her poverty had been a venial fault for two gallant gentlemen.
words.example_zh: 她的贫穷对那两位殷勤的绅士而言，只是一个微不足道的缺点。

words.definition_en: adj. of a timid disposition
words.example_en: He is a reclusive, timorous creature.
words.example_zh: 他是个离群索居、胆小羞怯的家伙。

words.definition_en: adj. coming and going at intervals
words.example_en: After three hours of intermittent rain, the game was cancelled.
words.example_zh: 雨断断续续地下了3个小时之后，比赛被取消了。

words.definition_en: n. a person who has previously occupied a position or office to which another has succeeded
words.example_en: He maintained that he learned everything he knew from his predecessor Kenneth Sisam.
words.example_zh: 他坚称他所知道的一切都获自前任肯尼思·赛瑟姆。

words.definition_en: adj. free from care, anxiety, or seriousness
words.example_en: I wanted to be brave, eager, confident, at ease, and lighthearted.
words.example_zh: 我想变得勇敢，充满渴望，自信，自在，无忧无虑。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to the kitchen or cookery
words.example_en: She was keen to acquire more advanced culinary skills.
words.example_zh: 她热切渴望学到更高超的烹饪技巧。

words.definition_en: (1)n. the condition of having a healthy mind (2)n. the condition of being based on reason or good judgment
words.example_en: His behavior was so strange that I began to doubt his sanity.
words.example_zh: 他行为怪异，我有点怀疑他是否神智正常。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by flawless craftsmanship or by beautiful, ingenious, delicate, or elaborate execution
words.example_en: The Indians brought in exquisite beadwork to sell.
words.example_zh: 印第安人带了精美的珠饰来卖。

words.definition_en: adj. happening completely by chance without being planned
words.example_en: Any resemblance to actual persons, places or events is purely coincidental.
words.example_zh: 如与真人、真实地点或真实事件有雷同之处，纯属巧合。

words.definition_en: n. the act or process of becoming worse
words.example_en: The recent deterioration has been caused by an apparently endless recession.
words.example_zh: 看似永无止境的经济衰退导致了最近的恶化。

words.definition_en: adj. extremely costly, rich, luxurious, or magnificent
words.example_en: We dined in sumptuous surroundings.
words.example_zh: 我们在富丽堂皇的环境中用餐。

words.definition_en: adj. having a shy or sweetly innocent quality
words.example_en: He was charmed by the young woman's coy smile.
words.example_zh: 他被这名年轻女子的娇羞笑容迷住了。

words.definition_en: v. to call forth or draw out
words.example_en: The crime elicited an outcry against illicit drugs.
words.example_zh: 那起罪行引发了打击违禁药品的呼声。

words.definition_en: n. a group of illustrious or notable persons or things
words.example_en: This book records the pantheon of the all-time greats.
words.example_zh: 这本书记载了古往今来的名人。

words.definition_en: v. to throw a strong light on
words.example_en: The President highlights the importance of his visit to China.
words.example_zh: 总统强调访问中国的重要性。

words.definition_en: n. the establishment of boundaries or limits separating two areas, groups, or things
words.example_en: Talks were continuing about the demarcation of the border between the two countries.
words.example_zh: 关于两国之间边界划定问题的谈判仍在继续。

words.definition_en: adj. very old or old-fashioned
words.example_en: His ideas are positively antediluvian.
words.example_zh: 他的思想是纯粹的老古董。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not influenced by personal feelings, opinions, or concerns (2)adj. not interested
words.example_en: The current sole superpower is far from being a disinterested observer.
words.example_zh: 当前唯一的超级大国远不是一个公正的旁观者。

words.definition_en: n. an unusual part or feature of something
words.example_en: The book is a gem of Victorian idiosyncrasy.
words.example_zh: 这本书展现了维多利亚时代特质的精华。

words.definition_en: adj. attractively suitable
words.example_en: A becoming dress lends charm to a lady.
words.example_zh: 合身的服装会给女士增添魅力。

words.definition_en: adj. lasting, durable
words.example_en: Only Marilyn has proved as an enduring fashion icon.
words.example_zh: 只有玛丽莲是常青的时尚偶像。

words.definition_en: adj. often angry and annoyed
words.example_en: He met a crabbed, cantankerous director.
words.example_zh: 他碰上了一位坏脾气、易怒的主管。

words.definition_en: v. to conquer and bring into subjection
words.example_en: Troops were called in to subdue the rebels.
words.example_zh: 军队被调来镇压反叛者。

words.definition_en: n. rapidity of motion or action
words.example_en: We are able to deal with emergency cases with celerity, seeking the most economical solutions.
words.example_zh: 我所律师能迅速地处理突发案件，寻求最经济的解决方法。

words.definition_en: v. to keep in a position, job, etc.
words.example_en: The interior of the shop still retains a nineteenth-century atmosphere.
words.example_zh: 那家商店内部依然保持着19世纪的格调。

words.definition_en: n. moral integrity
words.example_en: You're a symbol of righteousness and rectitude in our godforsaken land.
words.example_zh: 在这个凄凉的土地上，你是正直和清廉的象征。

words.definition_en: v. to be in charge of something
words.example_en: The PM returned to Downing Street to preside over a meeting of his inner cabinet.
words.example_zh: 首相回到唐宁街去主持一场核心内阁会议。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking resilience or firmness
words.example_en: This exercise helps to flatten a flabby stomach.
words.example_zh: 这种锻炼能促使松弛的腹部变得平坦。

words.definition_en: adj. belonging to, consisting in, or dependent on circumstances
words.example_en: The reasons for the project collapsing were circumstantial.
words.example_zh: 工程垮掉的原因得依情况而定。

words.definition_en: v. to halt or retard the progress, operation, or growth of
words.example_en: Without painful surgery, there is a real danger that the arteries of finance may soon clog.
words.example_zh: 如果不实施痛苦的手术，金融动脉就有马上阻塞的真正风险。

words.definition_en: v. to suppress or block completely
words.example_en: The article was spiked for fear of legal action against the newspaper.
words.example_zh: 因担心被提起诉讼，报社阻止了那篇文章的发表。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to lose the sense of time, place, or identity
words.example_en: They were disorientated by the smoke and were firing blindly into it.
words.example_zh: 他们在烟雾中迷失了方向，向里面胡乱射击了一通。

words.definition_en: n. a mixture of different things
words.example_en: The gross national product were seen as a hodgepodge of buicks, chickens, shirts, and diaper services.
words.example_zh: 把国民生产总值看成是别克牌汽车，小鸡，衬衫和织补服务等的大杂烩。

words.definition_en: n. an outer margin of an object or structural part
words.example_en: He was on the verge of tears.
words.example_zh: 他差点儿哭了出来。

words.definition_en: adj. opposed to someone or something in a very angry or determined way that cannot be changed
words.example_en: She is implacable an adversary as a wife suing for alimony.
words.example_zh: 有如诉讼要求赡养费的妻子一样，她是个难以安抚的对手。

words.definition_en: n. lack of courage or firmness of purpose
words.example_en: He openly accused his opponents of cowardice.
words.example_zh: 他公然指责对手胆小懦弱。

words.definition_en: v. to make simpler or more efficient
words.example_en: They're making efforts to streamline their normally cumbersome bureaucracy.
words.example_zh: 他们正努力精简本来繁冗复杂的官僚体制。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not familiar or like other things you have known (2)adj. from another country
words.example_en: He quickly adjusts to the alien environment.
words.example_zh: 他很快适应陌生的环境。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to give to someone or something (2)v. to discuss something important in order to make a decision
words.example_en: An honorary degree was conferred on him by Oxford University in 2001.
words.example_zh: 牛津大学于 2001 年授予他荣誉学位。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a medicine or treatment that relieves pain or cures a usually minor illness (2)n. a way of solving or correcting a problem (3)v. to solve, correct, or improve
words.definition_zh: 治疗药物改进措施纠正，弥补
words.example_en: There is no simple remedy for unemployment.
words.example_zh: 失业问题没有简单的解决办法。

words.definition_en: adj. useless, unprofitable
words.example_en: In the absence of exact data, these discussions are bootless.
words.example_zh: 由于缺乏确切的资料，这些讨论是无用的。

words.definition_en: v. to include different areas of activity, groups, time etc
words.definition_zh: 包括，跨越（不同的活动领域、团体、时间等）
words.example_en: The mountains straddle the French-Swiss border.
words.example_zh: 这座山脉横跨法国和瑞士边界。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking foresight
words.example_en: I think their approach to the problem is very shortsighted.
words.example_zh: 我认为他们解决问题的方法缺乏远见。

words.definition_en: n. calm emotions when dealing with problems or pressure
words.example_en: Nothing disturbs his equanimity.
words.example_zh: 没有任何事能扰乱他平静的心绪。

words.definition_en: v. to make stupid, groggy, or insensible
words.example_en: He was stupefied by the amount they had spent.
words.example_zh: 得知他们花了那么多钱，他都惊呆了。

words.definition_en: adj. based on facts rather than feelings or opinions
words.example_en: Historians try to be objective and impartial.
words.example_zh: 历史学家们力图做到客观公正。

words.definition_en: n. the act of giving away money or the quality of a person who gives away money
words.example_en: Our people are in no need of richer nations’ largesse.
words.example_zh: 我国人民不需要富国的施舍。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by lack of definite plan, regularity, or purpose
words.example_en: The constables made a desultory attempt to keep them away from the barn.
words.example_zh: 警察漫不经心地拦着不让他们靠近谷仓。

words.definition_en: adj. very well suited or expressed
words.example_en: Her prose style is not always felicitous; she tends to repetition.
words.example_zh: 她的行文风格并非总是恰到好处，往往爱重复

words.definition_en: n. a person who knows a lot about something, an expert in a particular subject.
words.example_en: Sarah tells me you're something of an art connoisseur.
words.example_zh: 萨拉告诉我你是一位艺术鉴赏家。

words.definition_en: n. a person who supplies the directing or creative intelligence for a project
words.example_en: He was the mastermind behind the plan to acquire the explosives.
words.example_zh: 他是获取炸药计划的幕后操纵者。

words.definition_en: adj. delayed beyond an appointed time
words.example_en: This car is overdue for a service.
words.example_zh: 这辆汽车早就该维修了。

words.definition_en: adj. hard to understand
words.example_en: The novel was too esoteric to sustain much attention.
words.example_zh: 这本小说太玄奥以至于没能引起多少注意。

words.definition_en: adv. up to this time
words.example_en: Heretofore, we sent out bills on the first of each month.
words.example_zh: 在此以前我们每个月的第一天发账单。

words.definition_en: adj. confident and hopeful
words.example_en: They are less sanguine about the company's long-term prospects.
words.example_zh: 他们对公司的远景不那么乐观。

words.definition_en: v. to go or pass around or about
words.example_en: He carefully skirted the issue of where they would live.
words.example_zh: 他小心地避开了他们将住在何处这个问题。

words.definition_en: v. to lie in wait in a place of concealment especially for an evil purpose
words.example_en: There is a suspicious man lurking in the shadows.
words.example_zh: 有一可疑的人躲在阴暗中。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. of, relating to, or characteristic of a past era (2)adj. outmoded
words.example_en: There are some traditional farmers left who still make cheese the old-fashioned way.
words.example_zh: 还有一些传统的农场主仍旧在用老式的方法制作奶酪。

words.definition_en: adj. being without exception or qualification
words.example_en: We don't know whether this was because he didn't want to take the chance or because he believed in the categorical moral principles.
words.example_zh: 我们不知道这是否是因为他不想冒这个险，还是因为他相信绝对的道德原则。

words.definition_en: v. bewilder, perplex n. a confusing intricate network of passages
words.example_en: We got lost in the maze.
words.example_zh: 我们在迷宫里迷失了方向。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by rigor, strictness, or severity especially with regard to rule or standard
words.example_en: He announced that there would be more stringent controls on the possession of weapons.
words.example_zh: 他宣布在武器的持有方面将会有更严格的控制。

words.definition_en: adj. offensive to the senses and especially to the sense of smell
words.example_en: Noisome vapors arise from the mud left in the docks.
words.example_zh: 难闻的蒸汽由船坞中余留的泥浆散发出来。

words.definition_en: adj. wanting to do something or likely to do something
words.example_en: I am inclined to agree with Alan.
words.example_zh: 我倾向于赞同艾伦的观点。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not bright or clear (2)v. to make less bright or to become less bright
words.example_en: In the dim light it was difficult to make out the illustration.
words.example_zh: 在昏暗的光线下很难看清那说明。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing a very clear understanding of something
words.example_en: She offered some really interesting, insightful observations.
words.example_zh: 她给出了一些非常有趣又富有洞察力的评论。

words.definition_en: v. to utter a deep moan indicative of pain, grief, or annoyance
words.example_en: We all groaned at his terrible jokes.
words.example_zh: 他讲的笑话很糟糕，我们都发出不满的抱怨声。

words.definition_en: adj. capable of working, functioning, or developing adequately
words.example_en: Their defense markets are too small to sustain economically viable production runs.
words.example_zh: 他们的国防市场太小，就经济可行性而言不足以维持生产。

words.definition_en: n. defamation
words.example_en: We cast no aspersion on the man or his program. Mr. Obama, in his short career, has not strongly associated himself with any policy idea.
words.example_zh: 我们无意诽谤这个人或他的计划，然而奥巴马在他短暂的职业生涯里，从未对任何政策主张表现过强烈支持的态度。

words.definition_en: v. to seek to gain or bring about
words.example_en: They wooed customers by offering low interest rates.
words.example_zh: 他们靠提供低息来招揽客户。

words.definition_en: v. to devise (a new word or phrase)
words.example_en: The term ‘cardboard city’ was coined to describe communities of homeless people living in cardboard boxes.
words.example_zh: 人们创造了cardboard city一词，用来指居住在纸板棚屋里无家可归者的居住区。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to allow to exist, happen, or be done (2)v. to put up with
words.example_en: There is a limit to what one person can tolerate.
words.example_zh: 一个人的容忍是有限度的。

words.definition_en: adj. deviating from an established or usual pattern or style
words.example_en: Geniuses are supposed to be eccentric and hopelessly impractical.
words.example_zh: 天才通常都被认为行为古怪而且毫无实践能力。

words.definition_en: adj. containing a mistake
words.example_en: Their main argument is fallacious.
words.example_zh: 他们的主要论点是错误的。

words.definition_en: v. to offer in sacrifice
words.example_en: I choose my career and immolate my time for health and family.
words.example_zh: 我选择了事业而牺牲了健康和家庭的时间。

words.definition_en: v. to drive or beat back
words.example_en: He spent most of his speech rebutting criticisms of his foreign policy.
words.example_zh: 他讲演的大部分时间都在反驳对他外交政策的批评。

words.definition_en: v. to put in danger
words.example_en: The dispute could jeopardize the negotiations.
words.example_zh: 那一争论可能会使谈判处于险境。

words.definition_en: v. to become split
words.example_en: The old tree behind our house was riven by lightning last night.
words.example_zh: 我们房后的古树昨天夜里被闪电劈了。

words.definition_en: v. to limit the size or amount of
words.example_en: His activities have been severely circumscribed since his illness.
words.example_zh: 自生病以来他的行动一直受到严格的限制。

words.definition_en: adj. continuing to happen or develop in the same way
words.example_en: To standardize the Front Office's approach to guest amenities service and workstations to ensure a consistent standard through the company.
words.example_zh: 使前厅对客房服务和工作岗位标准化，确保始终如一的工作水平。

words.definition_en: v. to deprive or dispossess especially of property, authority, or title
words.example_en: The army divested the king of all his power.
words.example_zh: 陆军剥夺了国王的全部权力。

words.definition_en: n. a valuable person or thing
words.example_en: From day one he's been a great asset to the company.
words.example_zh: 从入职第一天开始，他就是公司难得的人才。

words.definition_en: adj. bitterly scathing
words.example_en: There was a vicious and vitriolic attack on him in one of the Sunday newspapers two weeks ago.
words.example_zh: 两周前一份周日报纸刊登了一篇对他进行尖酸恶毒攻击的文章。

words.definition_en: adj. requiring a lot of effort, energy, etc.
words.example_en: This shouldn't be too taxing for you.
words.example_zh: 这对你来说不至于太费劲。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not interested in or concerned about something (2)adj. marked by impartiality
words.example_en: People have become indifferent to the suffering of others.
words.example_zh: 人们对别人的痛苦已经变得无动于衷。

words.definition_en: adj. deserved, appropriate
words.example_en: The public approved the condign punishment.
words.example_zh: 公众一致称赞这个罪判得很恰当。

words.definition_en: n. mettle, pluck
words.example_en: I admired her independence and spunk.
words.example_zh: 我敬佩她的独立精神和她的勇气。

words.definition_en: v. to eliminate the interrelationship of
words.example_en: The issue threatened to decouple Europe from the United States.
words.example_zh: 这一问题可能会割裂欧洲同美国的关系。

words.definition_en: adj. flowing in abundance
words.example_en: People's need for money has been increasing since they have been enjoying the many benefits brought by an affluent material life.
words.example_zh: 人们享受在丰富的物质生活带来的好处中，从而对金钱的需求越来越大。

words.definition_en: v. to disappear entirely without a clear explanation
words.example_en: The magician vanished in a puff of smoke.
words.example_zh: 魔术师在一股烟雾中突然不见了。

words.definition_en: adj. liking to talk and talking smoothly and easily
words.example_en: Would you guess women are more loquacious?
words.example_zh: 你会认为女人更健谈吗？

words.definition_en: v. to have and use (power, influence, etc.)
words.example_en: They wield enormous political power.
words.example_zh: 他们行使巨大的政治权力

words.definition_en: adj. caused by or indicating acute discernment
words.example_en: He is the most sagacious member of the committee.
words.example_zh: 他是委员会里最睿智的成员。

words.definition_en: adj. very appropriate
words.example_en: She thought in both languages, and selected the most apposite phrase from either.
words.example_zh: 她用两种语言思考，然后从中选择最贴切的一种表达。

words.definition_en: adj. not feeling or showing any concern about the problems or suffering of other people
words.example_en: The callous couple milked money from a hospital charity to fund a lavish lifestyle.
words.example_zh: 为了过上奢靡的生活，这对冷漠无情的夫妇竟然贪污医院的慈善基金。

words.definition_en: n. the state of being easily changed
words.example_en: The flexibility of distance learning would be particularly suited to busy managers.
words.example_zh: 远程学习的灵活性对于日理万机的经理人来说特别适合。

words.definition_en: adj. old-fashioned and no longer suitable for modern conditions
words.example_en: Many factories are so antiquated they are not worth saving.
words.example_zh: 很多工厂过于陈旧落后，已不值得挽救。

words.definition_en: adj. seeking to avenge
words.example_en: The morning after that combat, Hugo got up with a heart filled with vengeful purposes against the King.
words.example_zh: 在那次决斗后的早晨，雨果怀着对国王复仇的心起床了。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to signal the approach of (2)v. to greet especially with enthusiasm
words.example_en: These talks could herald a new era of peace.
words.example_zh: 这些谈判可能预示着新的和平时代的来临。

words.definition_en: v. to make beautiful with ornamentation
words.example_en: The door of the old church was embellished with decorations.
words.example_zh: 老教堂的门是用雕饰美化的。

words.definition_en: v. to stop from doing or expressing something
words.example_en: The government failed to stifle the unrest.
words.example_zh: 政府没有制止住动乱。

words.definition_en: n. a state of inaction or neutralization resulting from the opposition of equally powerful uncompromising persons or factions
words.example_en: They called for a compromise on all sides to break the deadlock in the world trade talks.
words.example_zh: 他们呼吁各方作出让步以打破世界贸易谈判中的僵局。

words.definition_en: adj. imitative
words.example_en: Both realism and naturalism are mimetic systems or practices of representation.
words.example_zh: 现实主义和自然主义都是一种模仿体系或表现性的做法。

words.definition_en: (1)n. an occurrence in which you fail to think or act in the usual or proper way for a brief time and make a mistake (2)n. an occurrence in which someone behaves badly or usually a short period of time (3)v. to stop for usually a brief time
words.example_en: A momentary lapse in the final set cost her the match.
words.example_zh: 她最后一盘稍有失误，输掉了整场比赛

words.definition_en: adj. unambiguous
words.example_en: The president declared that it was important to send a univocal message of support to the beleaguered country.
words.example_zh: 总统宣称对处于困境中的国家，明确表示支持是很重要的。

words.definition_en: v. to approve and sanction formally
words.example_en: The treaty was ratified by all the member states.
words.example_zh: 这个条约得到了所有成员国的批准。

words.definition_en: v. to imitate or mimic in an inept way
words.example_en: Modeling yourself on someone you admire is not the same as aping all they say or do.
words.example_zh: 以偶像为榜样并不是说要模仿他们的一言一行。

words.definition_en: adj. fully taking one’s attention
words.example_en: Children will find other exhibits equally absorbing.
words.example_zh: 孩子们会对其他展品一样感兴趣。

words.definition_en: n. a collection of selected literary pieces or passages or works of art or music
words.example_en: Despite some artful editing, the anthology is a weak one.
words.example_zh: 除了编辑上的一些巧妙之处，这本选集乏善可陈。

words.definition_en: v. to declare assuredly
words.example_en: The senator was forced to avow openly that he had received some money from that company.
words.example_zh: 那个参议员被迫公开声明曾经收过那家公司的一些钱。

words.definition_en: v. to make feel annoyed or angry
words.example_en: It galls me to have to apologize to her.
words.example_zh: 非得向她道歉，真让我恼火。

words.definition_en: v. to lower the value or reputation of
words.example_en: Politicians have debased the meaning of the word freedom.
words.example_zh: 政客们贬低了“自由”一词的意义。

words.definition_en: n. the money, skill, etc., that is needed to get or do something
words.example_en: China has the wherewithal to make good on its promises of massive economic stimulus.
words.example_zh: 中国有足够的资金实现其庞大的经济刺激计划。

words.definition_en: v. to impair the strength of
words.example_en: Prolonged strike action debilitated the industry.
words.example_zh: 长时间的罢工削弱了这个行业的活力。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by procrastination
words.example_en: The boss sacked a dilatory worker yesterday.
words.example_zh: 昨天老板开除了一个凡事都爱拖延的人。

words.definition_en: v. to criticize very harshly
words.example_en: Susan was excoriated for her mistakes.
words.example_zh: 苏珊因犯了错误而被指责。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing good judgment
words.example_en: At a key moment in his life, he made a judicious investment that was the foundation of his wealth.
words.example_zh: 在他一生的关键时刻, 他做出了明智的投资, 这便成了他以后富裕的基础。

words.definition_en: adj. having a wide range or effect
words.example_en: The economy is in danger of collapse unless far-reaching reforms are implemented.
words.example_zh: 如果不实施影响深远的改革，经济就面临崩溃的危险。

words.definition_en: n. the curved path of something
words.example_en: It is not difficult to sketch the subsequent trajectory.
words.example_zh: 很容易描绘出它们最终的轨迹。

words.definition_en: v. to be unwilling to give or admit
words.example_en: I grudge paying so much for such inferior goods.
words.example_zh: 我不愿花这么多钱买次品。

words.definition_en: v. to establish or order by appointment, decree, or law
words.example_en: Equal punishment was ordained for the two crimes.
words.example_zh: 规定对这两种罪行量刑相同。

words.definition_en: adj. weakly and effusively sentimental
words.example_en: Jimmy turned maudlin after three drinks.
words.example_zh: 三杯酒下肚后，吉米伤感起来。

words.definition_en: v. to stop developing, progressing, moving, etc.
words.example_en: Taiwan's economy is likely to stagnate for a long time to come.
words.example_zh: 台湾经济很可能会停滞很长一段时间。

words.definition_en: adj. very careful about doing something in a extremely accurate and exact way
words.example_en: The painting had been executed with meticulous attention to detail.
words.example_zh: 画这幅画的时候，画家对细节真是一丝不苟。

words.definition_en: n. evil reputation brought about by something grossly criminal, shocking, or brutal
words.example_en: He enjoyed exaggerating his infamy.
words.example_zh: 他喜欢夸大他的恶行。

words.definition_en: adj. fatigued by overwork
words.example_en: I felt terribly jaded after working all weekend.
words.example_zh: 整个周末工作之后我感到疲惫不堪。

words.definition_en: adj. unable to be changed
words.example_en: Our nature is not considered immutable, either socially or biologically.
words.example_zh: 无论是从社会学还是从生物学的角度来看，我们的本性都不是永远不变的.

words.definition_en: v. adorn, embellish
words.example_en: He tried to varnish over the facts, but it was useless.
words.example_zh: 他想粉饰事实，但那是徒劳的。

words.definition_en: v. to secure firmly
words.example_en: The roots anchor the plant in the earth.
words.example_zh: 根部使植物固定在土壤中。

words.definition_en: v. to enjoy or take pleasure in
words.example_en: He ate quietly, relishing his meal.
words.example_zh: 他安静地吃着，细细品味着食物。

words.definition_en: adj. having or expressing doubt about something
words.example_en: They are skeptical about how much will be accomplished by legislation.
words.example_zh: 他们对于立法成效心存疑虑。

words.definition_en: adj. capable of being easily changed or influenced
words.example_en: Silver is the most malleable of all metals.
words.example_zh: 银是所有金属中延展性最好的。

words.definition_en: v. to rate too low
words.example_en: He underrated the seriousness of William's head injury.
words.example_zh: 他低估了威廉头部伤势的严重性。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. fully revealed or expressed without vagueness, implication, or ambiguity (2)adj. fully developed or formulated
words.example_en: He was explicit about his intention to overhaul the party's internal voting system.
words.example_zh: 他直言自己的目的是改革党内的投票制度。

words.definition_en: adj. equal to something in value, meaning, or effect
words.example_en: If he resigned it would be tantamount to admitting that he was guilty.
words.example_zh: 他若辞职就等于承认自己有错。

words.definition_en: n. harsh or biting sharpness especially of words, manner, or feelings
words.example_en: This book review was written with acrimony.
words.example_zh: 这篇书评语调刻薄。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to idealists or idealism
words.example_en: This idealistic concept is at variance with reality.
words.example_zh: 这种空想主义的观念和现实相矛盾。

words.definition_en: n. extreme dislike or disgust
words.example_en: There was fear and hatred in his voice.
words.example_zh: 他的声音里透露着恐惧和仇恨。

words.definition_en: adj. stubbornly adhering to an opinion, purpose, or course in spite of reason, arguments, or persuasion
words.example_en: The trader was obstinate in the negotiation.
words.example_zh: 这个商人在谈判中强拗固执。

words.definition_en: adj. making usually unjustified or excessive claims
words.example_en: He is a talented but pretentious writer.
words.example_zh: 他是一个有才华但自以为是的作家。

words.definition_en: adj. penetrating only the easily or quickly perceived
words.example_en: The evening news is often criticized for being shallow.
words.example_zh: 晚间新闻常因其内容肤浅而受到批评。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to be loved or admired
words.example_en: He became enamored of Jenny.
words.example_zh: 他爱上珍妮了。

words.definition_en: n. the quality or state of being very determined to do something
words.example_en: The athletes displayed great tenacity throughout the contest.
words.example_zh: 运动员在比赛中表现出顽强的意志。

words.definition_en: adj. relating to the collection and study of valuable old things
words.example_en: The library is rich in historical and antiquarian books.
words.example_zh: 这家图书馆藏有大量历史书籍和古籍。

words.definition_en: v. to give (control, responsibility, authority, etc.) to someone
words.example_en: Officials have now been delegated to start work on a draft settlement.
words.example_zh: 现在已经委派官员着手起草解决方案。

words.definition_en: n. a member of the laity
words.example_en: How would you explain to the layperson how gene therapy works?
words.example_zh: 你会如何向外行人解释基因治疗法的工作原理呢？

words.definition_en: adj. having many parts
words.example_en: The plot of the novel is intricate and fascinating.
words.example_zh: 这部小说的情节错综复杂，引人入胜。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to experience failure (2)n. one founds or establishes
words.example_en: He was one of the founders of the university's medical faculty.
words.example_zh: 他是该大学医学院的创建人之一。

words.definition_en: n. an unfortunate, difficult, or precarious situation
words.example_en: He just wants to draw attention to the plight of the unemployed.
words.example_zh: 他仅仅是想引起人们对失业者困境的关注。

words.definition_en: n. something made up or contrived
words.example_en: The attack wasn't just a figment of my imagination.
words.example_zh: 袭击可不是我凭空想象出来的。

words.definition_en: n. a concluding section that rounds out the design of a literary work
words.example_en: Fortinbras speaks the epilogue in Shakespeare's Hamlet.
words.example_zh: 在莎士比亚的《哈姆雷特》中，由福丁布拉斯念收场白。

words.definition_en: adj. friendly, sociable, and congenial
words.example_en: He is an educated, amiable and decent man.
words.example_zh: 他是个有教养、和蔼可亲的正人君子。

words.definition_en: adj. tremendous in size, volume, or degree
words.example_en: That is a gargantuan challenge.
words.example_zh: 这是一个巨大的挑战。

words.definition_en: n. a feeling of unity between people who have the same interests, goals, etc.
words.example_en: The great friendship and militant solidarity between the peoples of our two countries are unbreakable.
words.example_zh: 我们两国人民的伟大友谊与战斗团结是坚不可摧的。

words.definition_en: adj. sincerely and honestly felt or experienced
words.example_en: Fake designer watches are sold at a fraction of the price of the genuine article .
words.example_zh: 伪造的设计师名牌手表以真品若干分之一的价格出售。

words.definition_en: adj. having only one side
words.example_en: Unpopular policies such as unilateral disarmament were ditched.
words.example_zh: 诸如单边裁军之类不得人心的政策已被废除了。

words.definition_en: adj. not exciting or interesting
words.example_en: I found the characters very two-dimensional, not to say dull.
words.example_zh: 我觉得那些人物形象即使不说是乏味无聊，至少也是毫无深度。

words.definition_en: adj. of the best and most characteristic
words.example_en: If you can buy only one case at auction, it should be vintage port.
words.example_zh: 如果你在拍卖会上只能买一箱酒，那就应该是优质的波尔图葡萄酒。

words.definition_en: adj. having or occurring in great variety
words.example_en: Spain is a composite of multifarious traditions and people.
words.example_zh: 西班牙是多种传统和多个民族的合成体。

words.definition_en: v. to give pain or trouble to
words.example_en: She was aggrieved at the insult.
words.example_zh: 她因被侮辱而觉得感情上受到伤害。

words.definition_en: n. friendly joking about or with somebody
words.example_en: Prince Charles does not escape raillery from others.
words.example_zh: 查尔斯王子也难逃人们善意的玩笑。

words.definition_en: adj. unable to understand or think clearly
words.example_en: People are confused about all the different labels on food these days.
words.example_zh: 人们如今被那些五花八门的食物标签搞得稀里糊涂。

words.definition_en: n. the quality or state of something that is not easily changed or likely to change
words.example_en: NATO had charted a new course for stability and cooperation in Europe.
words.example_zh: 北约制订了保持欧洲稳定与合作的新计划。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to come
words.example_en: Her screams drew passers-by to the scene.
words.example_zh: 她的惊叫声把过路人吸引到了现场。

words.definition_en: n. the doctrine that pleasure or happiness is the sole or chief good in life
words.example_en: The age of hedonism is being ushered out by a new era of temperance.
words.example_zh: 享乐主义时代正让位于一个注重节制的时代。

words.definition_en: v. to slow the movement, progress, or action of
words.example_en: The bad weather hampered rescue operations.
words.example_zh: 恶劣的天气阻碍了救援工作的进行。

words.definition_en: v. to become worse as time passes
words.example_en: The chops will fester and go to waste.
words.example_zh: 猪排会腐烂，给糟蹋掉。

words.definition_en: n. a sickness or illness
words.example_en: The pharmacist can assist you with the treatment of common ailments.
words.example_zh: 药剂师能帮你治疗一般的小病。

words.definition_en: n. the state of having identical cumulative distribution function or values
words.example_en: The government panicked into imposing a kind of cultural homogeneity.
words.example_zh: 政府在恐慌下开始强制实施某种文化统一政策。

words.definition_en: v. to demand an express term in an agreement
words.example_en: That is why the vehicle manufacturers stipulate regular engine oil changes.
words.example_zh: 这就是为什么汽车制造商规定定期发动机换油。

words.definition_en: v. to shine with or as if with subdued steady light or moderate brightness
words.example_en: Her black hair gleamed in the sun.
words.example_zh: 她的黑发在阳光下闪着光泽。

words.definition_en: adj. narrowly, stodgily, and often ostentatiously learned
