words.example_en: his is often cited as an example of the useless and pedantic degeneracy of medieval Catholic theology.
words.example_zh: 这经常被作为一个对中世纪神学无用和迂腐的例子。

words.definition_en: n. a feeling of not worrying about anything
words.example_en: She hid her worries behind an air of insouciance.
words.example_zh: 她掩饰着自己的烦恼，表现得无忧无虑。

words.definition_en: adj. intricately involved
words.example_en: The procedures surrounding legal redress are byzantine.
words.example_zh: 修改法律的程序是错综复杂的。

words.definition_en: n. a distinguishing quality
words.example_en: Creativity is a human trait.
words.example_zh: 创造力是人类的一种特性。

words.definition_en: adj. very noticeable
words.example_en: Her novels have just been reissued with eye-catching new covers.
words.example_zh: 她的小说刚进行了重印，新封面非常抢眼。

words.definition_en: v. to fill up the number of with new members
words.example_en: The police are trying to recruit more black and Asian officers.
words.example_zh: 警方正在试图招聘更多黑人和亚裔警官。

words.definition_en: v. entrance or enthrall
words.example_en: Like all great books, it has an enduring power to surprise and mesmerize.
words.example_zh: 像所有伟大的著作一样，它拥有持久的力量，能不断给人惊喜并使人着迷。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to show to be false or wrong (2)v. disguise (3)v. to present an appearance not in agreement with
words.definition_zh: 证明...是错误的掩饰与...不符
words.example_en: Her energy and youthful good looks belie her 65 years.
words.example_zh: 她的活力与年轻美貌使人看不出她有65岁了。

words.definition_en: adj. promoting health or well-being of mind or spirit
words.example_en: Water is a wholesome drink.
words.example_zh: 水是有益健康的饮料。

words.definition_en: n. a person who attempts to stir up or spread something that is usually petty or discreditable
words.example_en: Of course, once you've spotted the gossip-monger it might help to be very careful about the information you share with this employee.
words.example_zh: 当然，一旦你知道了谁是喜欢传闲话的人，那么跟这个员工分享信息的时候就要特别小心谨慎。

words.definition_en: adj. too ready to believe things
words.example_en: Mary is so credulous that she may readily accept any excuse you make.
words.example_zh: 玛丽很轻信，你随便找个借口她都可以相信。

words.definition_en: n. one who attends or assists a leader
words.example_en: Others say of course that he is an acolyte of the old regime, he's the one who has brought problem to Egypt.
words.example_zh: 当然也有人说，他是旧政权的支持者，埃及的问题就出在他身上。

words.definition_en: v. to help someone or something pay for the costs of
words.example_en: Many universities subsidize research and publications.
words.example_zh: 许多大学资助研究和出版。

words.definition_en: adj. not easily understood or explained
words.example_en: His equivocal response has done nothing to dampen the speculation.
words.example_zh: 模棱两可的回应丝毫没有消除人们的猜疑。

words.definition_en: v. to agree precisely
words.example_en: His practice does not square with his principles.
words.example_zh: 他的行为与主张不符。

words.definition_en: v. to represent as less than is the case
words.example_en: The government chooses deliberately to understate the increase in prices.
words.example_zh: 政府有意选择对价格的上涨轻描淡写。

words.definition_en: v. to manage prudently and economically
words.example_en: Husbanding precious resources was part of rural life.
words.example_zh: 节约地使用宝贵的资源是乡村生活的一部分。

words.definition_en: v. to ruin or spoil
words.example_en: His spirit was vitiated by pleasure seeking.
words.example_zh: 他的志气因追求吃喝玩乐而消磨殆尽。

words.definition_en: adj. experiencing or tending to bring about renewal or revival
words.example_en: That could harm exports and growth in the resurgent eurozone.
words.example_zh: 对于正在复苏的欧元区，这可能会危害到它的出口和增长。

words.definition_en: adj. providing something additional to a main part of function
words.example_en: The transport corps is ancillary to the infantry.
words.example_zh: 运输队是步兵的辅助部队。

words.definition_en: adj. changing often
words.example_en: Online players are a fickle bunch who move to new games quickly.
words.example_zh: 在线玩家是一群易变的人，他们很快就会转移到新游戏中。

words.definition_en: adj. organized to do good things for other people
words.example_en: The company has proved to be a most benevolent employer.
words.example_zh: 结果证明该公司是一个非常仁慈的雇主。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. of or relating to the brain (2)adj. intellectual and not emotional
words.example_en: His poetry is very cerebral.
words.example_zh: 他的诗富涵理性。

words.definition_en: adj. strongly desirous of acquiring and possessing
words.example_en: The most acquisitive firms tend to be engineering groups.
words.example_zh: 最贪得无厌的公司往往是工程队。

words.definition_en: n. product of the imagination
words.example_en: The story was certainly a favorite one, but it was undoubtedly pure invention.
words.example_zh: 这个故事的确是人们最喜欢的一个，但毫无疑问它纯粹是虚构的。

words.definition_en: adj. agreeing with a set of rules, standards, or requirements
words.example_en: She was much naughtier than her compliant brother.
words.example_zh: 她哥哥很听话，她却调皮得多。

words.definition_en: v. to take arbitrary or forcible possession of
words.example_en: All automobiles in the town were commandeered by the army.
words.example_zh: 城中所有的汽车均被部队征用了。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a person who argues for or supports a cause or policy (2)v. to support or argue for
words.example_en: Mr Williams is a strong advocate of free market policies and a multi-party system.
words.example_zh: 威廉斯先生是自由市场政策和多党派制度的坚决拥护者。

words.definition_en: v. to confuse very much
words.example_en: Don't perplex her with the question.
words.example_zh: 你不要以这个问题困惑她。

words.definition_en: adj. of outstanding spiritual, intellectual, or moral worth
words.example_en: Her sublime moral courage inspired the entire community.
words.example_zh: 她崇高的道德勇气鼓舞了整个社区。

words.definition_en: n. a person who has just started learning or doing something
words.example_en: In fact, in the world of complex software, everyone is effectively a novice.
words.example_zh: 事实上，在复杂的软件世界里，每个人实际上都是新手。

words.definition_en: adj. exhibiting or given to extreme fierceness and unrestrained violence and brutality
words.example_en: The ferocious panther is chasing a rabbit.
words.example_zh: 那只凶猛的豹子正追赶一只兔子。

words.definition_en: n. a natural liking for something
words.example_en: Charles has always had a predilection for red-haired women.
words.example_zh: 查尔斯对红头发女人一直有偏爱。

words.definition_en: adj. very careful about doing what you are supposed to do
words.example_en: She is very conscientious and never skimps her work.
words.example_zh: 她办事认真，从不敷衍了事。

words.definition_en: n. wrongdoing or misconduct especially by a public official
words.example_en: The mayor is condemned for the monstrous malfeasance of his administration.
words.example_zh: 市长被宣告有罪，由于其领导的政府渎职。

words.definition_en: v. to become liable or subject to: bring down upon oneself
words.example_en: She falls in love and incurs the wrath of her father.
words.example_zh: 她恋爱了，这引起了父亲的愤怒。

words.definition_en: adj. shocked and upset
words.example_en: Tania stared at him aghast, unable to speak.
words.example_zh: 塔妮娅吃惊地看着他，说不出话来。

words.definition_en: n. a place or locality especially when viewed in relation to a particular event or characteristic
words.example_en: An amusement park is the perfect locale for a bunch of irrepressible youngsters to have all sorts of adventures.
words.example_zh: 游乐场是一帮精力过剩的年轻人寻求各种惊险刺激的绝佳场所。

words.definition_en: adj. favoring, conducive to, or operating toward peace, moderation, or conciliation
words.example_en: Great players can hold an irenic attitude toward former antagonists.
words.example_zh: 伟大的运动员能够秉持对前对手的和平态度。

words.definition_en: n. a lack of belief or a strong disbelief in the existence of a god or any gods
words.example_en: David believes in atheism as though it were a new religion.
words.example_zh: 大卫信奉无神论就像信仰一种新的宗教。

words.definition_en: n. the act of causing two people or groups to become friendly again after an argument or disagreement
words.example_en: Their handshake appeared to be a gesture of reconciliation.
words.example_zh: 他们的握手似乎是和解的表示。

words.definition_en: adj. used, needed, or enjoyed constantly usually by many individuals
words.example_en: Rice is the staple food of more than half the world's population.
words.example_zh: 稻米是全世界一半以上人口的主食。

words.definition_en: v. to attract artfully or adroitly or by arousing hope or desire
words.example_en: The promise of higher pay enticed me into the new job.
words.example_zh: 高薪诱使我从事了这个新工作。

words.definition_en: n. preservation from destruction or failure
words.example_en: The church's message of salvation has changed the lives of many.
words.example_zh: 基督教中耶稣拯救灵魂的教义改变了很多人的生活。

words.definition_en: adj. very obvious and offensive
words.example_en: The delegation submitted a memorandum to the Commons on the blatant violations of basic human rights.
words.example_zh: 代表团向下院呈交了有关公然侵犯基本人权的备忘录。

words.definition_en: adj. very poor
words.example_en: The town government is responsible for assistance to indigent people.
words.example_zh: 镇政府负责给穷人提供帮助。

words.definition_en: adj. unshakable or insistent especially in maintaining a position or opinion
words.example_en: American negotiators are adamant that they will not budge on this point.
words.example_zh: 美国谈判者非常强硬，他们在这一点上是不会妥协的。

words.definition_en: n. the last small part that remains of something that existed before
words.example_en: These upright stones are the vestiges of some ancient religion.
words.example_zh: 这些竖立的石头是某种古代宗教的遗迹。

words.definition_en: n. a commonplace or hackneyed statement or notion
words.example_en: The meeting produced the usual bromides about macroeconomic policy, third-world debt and the environment.
words.example_zh: 会议上提出的还是那些惯常讨论的老套话题——宏观经济政策、第三世界债务和环境问题。

words.definition_en: adj. being in an early stage of development
words.example_en: It is still in an embryonic stage.
words.example_zh: 它还处于萌芽阶段。

words.definition_en: adj. easily duped or cheated
words.example_en: The swindlers had roped into a number of gullible persons.
words.example_zh: 骗子们已使一些轻信的人上了当。

words.definition_en: v. to reduce the speed of
words.example_en: But most analysts already expect Chinese growth to decelerate.
words.example_zh: 但大多数分析师已经预料到中国的增长会减速。

words.definition_en: adj. not energetic or excited
words.example_en: The critics's reaction to the film was rather tepid.
words.example_zh: 评论家对那部影片的反映不太热烈。

words.definition_en: n. a noisy disturbance or quarrel
words.example_en: After the row in a pub he drove off in a huff.
words.example_zh: 在酒吧里吵了一架后，他气鼓鼓地开车走了。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to take or use especially in a way that is illegal, unfair, etc. (2)adj. right or suited for some purpose or situation
words.example_en: So if you want people to get an education, maybe some sort of subsidy is appropriate.
words.example_zh: 所以如果你想要人们受教育，也许一定形式的补助是合适的。

words.definition_en: n. a terse formulation of a truth or sentiment
words.example_en: It is the aphorism of the Asian Games.
words.example_zh: 这是亚运会的格言。

words.definition_en: n. a strong inclination or interest
words.example_en: She has a natural bent for mathematics.
words.example_zh: 她在数学方面有天赋。

words.definition_en: v. to be in inward turmoil
words.example_en: The gardener exploded with the rage that had simmered all morning.
words.example_zh: 那名花匠憋了一个早上的愤怒终于爆发了。

words.definition_en: v. analyze critically
words.example_en: Then you could have your test automation read, parse, and use the data in this file.
words.example_zh: 然后您可以让您的测试自动化读取、解析和使用该文件中的数据。

words.definition_en: adj. currently or actually existing
words.example_en: Two fourteenth-century manuscripts of this text are still extant.
words.example_zh: 这个文本仍有两份14世纪手抄本存世。

words.definition_en: v. to throw away because it is useless or unwanted
words.example_en: On no account should we discard our fine traditions and styles of work.
words.example_zh: 好传统、好作风绝不能扔掉。

words.definition_en: adj. of, relating to, befitting, or suggestive of an empire or an emperor
words.example_en: They executed Russia's imperial family in 1918.
words.example_zh: 他们 1918 年处死了俄国沙皇皇族。

words.definition_en: n. a great disaster or complete failure
words.example_en: After the debacle of the war the world was never the same again.
words.example_zh: 经历了这场战争的惨败之后，世界再也不是原来的那个样子了。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to become worse in condition or quality (2)v. to say that you will not or cannot do something
words.example_en: The electoral fortunes of the Liberal Democratic party may decline.
words.example_zh: 自由民主党的选举运势可能会衰减。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. unqualified, utter (2)adj. marked by great and continuous steepness (3)v. to deviate from a course
words.definition_zh: 完全的，绝对的陡峭的偏航
words.example_en: His music is sheer delight
words.example_zh: 听他的音乐完全是一种享受。

words.definition_en: n. a feeble or ridiculous imitation
words.example_en: The parody was just a form of teasing.
words.example_zh: 这拙劣的模仿仅仅是戏弄的一个形式。

words.definition_en: adj. existing or continuing in the same way for a long time
words.example_en: It reflects a perennial worry in the Arab world about the state of the language.
words.example_zh: 它反映了长久以来对阿拉伯语世界内关于该语言现状的担忧。

words.definition_en: n. ancestor, forefather
words.example_en: I'll come back to the land of my forebears.
words.example_zh: 我将回到祖先的土地上。

words.definition_en: adj. having a pleasant or agreeable taste
words.example_en: This dish is quite palatable.
words.example_zh: 这个菜非常鲜美可口。

words.definition_en: v. to think of or describe something as larger or greater than it really is
words.example_en: Sheila admitted that she did sometimes exaggerate the demands of her job.
words.example_zh: 希拉承认自己有时候确实夸大了工作的难度。

words.definition_en: n. a situation in which you have to make a difficult choice
words.example_en: The issue raises a moral dilemma.
words.example_zh: 这个问题引发了一个道德上的两难抉择。

words.definition_en: n. a mixture of different elements
words.example_en: The film script is an amalgam of all three books.
words.example_zh: 这个电影脚本由三本书合成。

words.definition_en: adj. vast or ample in extent
words.example_en: The house has a spacious kitchen and dining area.
words.example_zh: 这座房子有一个宽敞的厨房和用餐区。

words.definition_en: v. to hide your true feelings, opinions, etc.
words.example_en: Henry was not slow to dissemble when it served his purposes.
words.example_zh: 在需要时，亨利会毫不迟疑地伪装自己。

words.definition_en: v. to stimulate or excite as if by an electric shock
words.example_en: The aid appeal has galvanized the German business community.
words.example_zh: 这份救助呼吁已经促使德国的工商业界行动了起来。

words.definition_en: adj. depending on each other
words.example_en: We live in an increasingly interdependent world.
words.example_zh: 我们生活在一个日益相互依赖的世界。

words.definition_en: v. to establish or bring about as if by force
words.example_en: Parents of either sex should beware of imposing their own tastes on their children.
words.example_zh: 父母双方都应切记不要把自己的喜好强加给孩子。

words.definition_en: n. truth or accuracy
words.example_en: They questioned the veracity of her story.
words.example_zh: 他们质疑她所述事情的真实性。

words.definition_en: adj. powerfully pleasing, appealing, or delightful
words.example_en: She has the most enchanting smile.
words.example_zh: 她有着最迷人的微笑。

words.definition_en: adj. not easily or naturally deduced or introduced
words.example_en: The storyline was too far-fetched and none of the actors was particularly good.
words.example_zh: 故事情节太离谱，演员表演也没有出彩的。

words.definition_en: adj. causing feelings of sadness and sympathy
words.example_en: The animal gave a pathetic little whimper.
words.example_zh: 这只动物低声哀叫，令人怜悯。

words.definition_en: adj. strikingly out of the ordinary
words.example_en: Grass's novels are peopled with outlandish characters.
words.example_zh: 格拉斯的小说里都是些稀奇古怪的人物。

words.definition_en: n. incapable of error
words.example_en: No country should claim infallibility and each country should be prepared to re-examine its own attitudes for the common good.
words.example_zh: 任何国家都不应自称一贯正确，各国都要准备为了共同的利益重新检查自己的态度。

words.definition_en: adj. standoffish
words.example_en: he was being offish with her.
words.example_zh: 他故意对她表现出冷漠。

words.definition_en: v. to let fall, exude, or precipitate in drops or in a wet mist
words.example_en: We were asked to distill the crucial points of the book.
words.example_zh: 我们被要求从这本书中提炼出关键点。

words.definition_en: (1)n. an official document conferring a right or privilege (2)adj. obvious or clear
words.example_en: P&G applied for a patent on its cookies.
words.example_zh: 宝洁公司为它的饼干申请专利权。

words.definition_en: v. to gather into a ball, mass, or cluster
words.example_en: Similarly charged colloidal particles cannot agglomerate into larger particles.
words.example_zh: 带相同电荷的胶粒不能聚集成较大的颗粒。

words.definition_en: n. a competition, dispute, or fight open to all comers and usually with no rules
words.definition_zh: 人人可参加的混战；无规则竞争
words.example_en: The lowering of trade barriers has led to a free-for-all among exporters.
words.example_zh: 降低贸易壁垒导致出口商各自为政。

words.definition_en: adj. not giving you enough useful information
words.example_en: The reports of the explosion were brief and uninformative.
words.example_zh: 有关爆炸事件的报道很简略，未及详情。

words.definition_en: n. the state of being old and in bad condition or poor health
words.example_en: The building had a general air of decrepitude and neglect.
words.example_zh: 这座建筑看上去破旧失修，无人照管。

words.definition_en: v. inflame, excite
words.example_en: Lady Lysa's face was flushed with fury.
words.example_zh: 莱莎夫人气得满脸通红。

words.definition_en: adj. performed or existing on a large scale especially without discrimination
words.example_en: Once wholesale prices are deregulated, consumer prices will also rise.
words.example_zh: 一旦批发价格放开，零售价格也会随之上涨。

words.definition_en: v. to treat as a very important and famous person
words.example_en: The press began to lionize him enthusiastically.
words.example_zh: 媒体开始狂热地吹捧他。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to say that you accept or do not deny the truth or existence of (2)v. to regard or describe (someone or something as having or deserving a particular status
words.example_en: Naylor acknowledged, in a letter to the judge, that he was a drug addict.
words.example_zh: 内勒在给法官的信中承认自己吸毒。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to pretend to have (2)v. to take as granted or true (3)v. to take to or upon oneself
words.example_en: He contented himself by assuming an air of superiority.
words.example_zh: 他表现出一副高高在上的样子以获得自我满足。

words.definition_en: v. to act as intermediary
words.example_en: The Vatican successfully mediated in a territorial dispute between Argentina and Chile in 1984.
words.example_zh: 梵蒂冈成功地调解了1984年阿根廷与智利之间的领土争端。

words.definition_en: adj. exciting distaste or aversion
words.definition_zh: 令人厌恶的，使人反感的
words.example_en: All food was repugnant to me during my illness.
words.example_zh: 生病时所有的食物都使我感到厌恶.

words.definition_en: adj. eerie, mysterious
words.example_en: Tony was unnerved by the uncanny familiarity of her face.
words.example_zh: 怪异的是，她看起来竟有些面熟，这让托尼心慌意乱。

words.definition_en: v. to make more prominent or noticeable
words.example_en: Happy couples accentuate the positive.
words.example_zh: 幸福伴侣总是强调积极的一面。

words.definition_en: v. to persuade with flattery or gentle urging especially in the face of reluctance
words.example_en: He cajoled me into agreeing to do the work.
words.example_zh: 他诱骗我同意接下那份工作。

words.definition_en: adj. not showing emotion
words.example_en: He was content to stand by as an impassive spectator.
words.example_zh: 他满足于站在旁边做一个冷漠的旁观者。

words.definition_en: adj. poorly done or made
words.example_en: We're not paying good money for shoddy goods.
words.example_zh: 我们不会花高价买劣质商品。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by arrogant assurance
words.example_en: From across the desk she gave him a witheringly imperious look.
words.example_zh: 她从桌子对面扫了他一眼，目光咄咄逼人，凌利而专横。

words.definition_en: v. to make agitated and angry
words.example_en: Cancellations and late departures rarely rile him.
words.example_zh: 他很少因为约定取消或出发太晚而恼火。

words.definition_en: adj. to be about to occur
words.example_en: On the morning of the expedition I awoke with a feeling of impending disaster.
words.example_zh: 出发远征的那个早上，我醒来就有一种灾难即将来临的感觉。

words.definition_en: n. the period of one's greatest popularity, vigor, or prosperity
words.example_en: In its heyday, the studio's boast was that it had more stars than there are in heaven.
words.example_zh: 在其全盛时期，该制片公司夸口说，他们旗下的明星比天上的星星还要多。

words.definition_en: v. to be present in large quantity
words.example_en: The advertisements teem with our life everywhere.
words.example_zh: 在我们的生活中到处充满了广告。

words.definition_en: n. high spirits: merriment
words.example_en: The colorful flags added to the gaiety of the occasion.
words.example_zh: 彩旗增添了盛会的欢乐气氛。

words.definition_en: v. to remove from power especially by force
words.example_en: That government was overthrown in a military coup three years ago.
words.example_zh: 那个政府在3年前的军事政变中被推翻。

words.definition_en: adj. difficult or complicated
words.example_en: The new management faces some knotty problems.
words.example_zh: 新的管理层面临着一些棘手的问题。

words.definition_en: v. to shrink away or crouch especially for shelter from something that menaces, domineers, or dismays
words.example_en: A gun went off and people cowered behind walls and under tables.
words.example_zh: 一声枪响，人们缩到墙后或桌子底下躲起来。

words.definition_en: adj. impossible to resist especially because of strength or attractiveness
words.example_en: The raging trend of reforms is irresistible.
words.example_zh: 改革浪潮是不可抗拒的。

words.definition_en: n. a mark of shame or discredit
words.example_en: There is no longer any stigma attached to being divorced.
words.example_zh: 离婚不再是什么羞耻的事。

words.definition_en: v. to revive from apparent death or from unconsciousness
words.example_en: He has submitted a bid to resuscitate the struggling magazine.
words.example_zh: 他已经投标来拯救这个岌岌可危的周刊。

words.definition_en: adj. very small in size or amount
words.example_en: There is scant evidence of strong economic growth to come.
words.example_zh: 没有足够的证据表明经济将会突飞猛进。

words.definition_en: v. to give support to
words.example_en: Prime minister unveiled new economic aid packages to bolster political and economic transitions in the region.
words.example_zh: 总理公布新的经济援助措施来支持该地区的政治和经济转变。

words.definition_en: adj. highly injurious or destructive
words.example_en: They don't really, in their ivory towers, understand how pernicious drug crime is.
words.example_zh: 他们身处象牙塔中，并不真正了解毒品犯罪的危害有多大。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to measure exactly (2)n. an instrument that is used for measuring something
words.example_en: Geologists gauged the wind at over thirty knots.
words.example_zh: 地质学家们测出风速在30节以上。

words.definition_en: v. to restrain, check, or control with or as if with a bridle
words.example_en: She tried with all her might to bridle her resentment.
words.example_zh: 她努力抑制住自己的怨恨。

words.definition_en: n. the state of being first
words.example_en: In this model, no one state is allowed primacy over the union's other members.
words.example_zh: 根据这一模式，没有哪个州可以超过联盟中的其他成员成为第一。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to entice by charm or attraction (2)n. power to attract
words.example_en: The window displays allure customers to buy goods.
words.example_zh: 橱窗陈列品引诱顾客购买商品。

words.definition_en: n. pleasing or sweet sound
words.example_en: Even the too-near table of boisterous boys contributes to its euphony.
words.example_zh: 连近旁谈笑的年轻人也贡献出了悦耳之声。

words.definition_en: v. to be or become inactive or dormant
words.example_en: Bears often hibernate in caves.
words.example_zh: 熊常在山洞里冬眠。

words.definition_en: v. to quickly move away from something that is shocking, frightening, or disgusting
words.example_en: He recoiled in horror at the sight of the corpse.
words.example_zh: 他一见到尸体就吓得往后缩了。

words.definition_en: adj. causing anger
words.example_en: People sported swastikas and walls were covered with inflammatory slogans.
words.example_zh: 人们佩戴着纳粹党的曲十字标志，墙上写满了煽动性口号。

words.definition_en: v. to deceive by false appearance
words.example_en: Many people are hoodwinked by the so-called beauty industry.
words.example_zh: 很多人都被所谓的美容业欺骗了。

words.definition_en: n. a person skilled in the use of tricks and illusion
words.example_en: Usually a trickster's life is pathetic.
words.example_zh: 通常一个骗子的一生都是很可怜的。

words.definition_en: n. the state of not being certain about what to do
words.example_en: As if he were in pain or in some torment of irresolution.
words.example_zh: 好像他很痛苦或者是处在某种犹豫不决的折磨中。

words.definition_en: n. a sudden stoppage of a forward course or progress
words.example_en: The setback put a temporary check on expansion.
words.example_zh: 这次挫折暂时遏制了扩张。

words.definition_en: v. to send away
words.example_en: John was banished from England.
words.example_zh: 约翰被逐出了英格兰。

words.definition_en: n. a gregarious and unreserved person
words.example_en: As an extrovert, he enjoys meeting new people.
words.example_zh: 作为一个外向的人，他喜欢结识新朋友。

words.definition_en: adj. following or occurring after death
words.example_en: His family and supporters have campaigned for many years for a posthumous pardon.
words.example_zh: 他的家人和支持者为争取其获得身后赦免而开展了多年的运动。

words.definition_en: v. to reason earnestly with a person for purposes of dissuasion or remonstrance
words.example_en: His family expostulated with him.
words.example_zh: 家人和他意见相左。

words.definition_en: adj. dominating or embracing all else
words.example_en: The overarching question seems to be what happens when the US pulls out?
words.example_zh: 首要的问题看来在于美国撤军后事态将会如何。

words.definition_en: v. to make public
words.example_en: Officials refuse to divulge details of the negotiations.
words.example_zh: 官员们拒绝透露谈判的细节。

words.definition_en: adj. able to be seen or noticed
words.example_en: The difference is scarcely perceptible to the average reader.
words.example_zh: 这种差别一般读者几乎是察觉不到的。

words.definition_en: v. to reach as an end
words.example_en: For us, it's something that we may aspire to but can never attain.
words.example_zh: 对我们来说，那是可望而不可及的。

words.definition_en: n. a situation resembling gridlock (as in congestion or lack of movement)
words.example_en: The risk then is that a continuation of political gridlock would stop further economic reforms in Germany.
words.example_zh: 到时候的风险是政治僵局的持续将会阻碍德国进一步的经济改革。

words.definition_en: n. oldster
words.example_en: Being a mature student does not mean being an old-timer.
words.example_zh: 做一个成熟的学生并不意味着成为一个老前辈。

words.definition_en: adj. deserving very strong criticism
words.example_en: Mr Cramer said the violence by anti-government protestors was reprehensible.
words.example_zh: 克拉默先生说反政府示威者的暴力活动应该受到谴责。

words.definition_en: v. to criticize or express disapproval of
words.example_en: Her colleagues reproached her for leaking the story to the press.
words.example_zh: 她的同事因她向媒体泄露此事而责备了她。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to lessen the intensity or extremeness of (2)adj. not violent, severe, or intense
words.definition_zh: 变缓和，变弱温和的，适度的
words.example_en: They are hoping that once in office he can be persuaded to moderate his views.
words.example_zh: 他们希望等他一上台，可以说服他观点别那么激进。

words.definition_en: n. the mass of the people as distinguished from those of a particular profession or those specially skilled
words.example_en: He shows painting skills which distinguish himself from the laity.
words.example_zh: 他展示出有别于门外汉的绘画技巧。

words.definition_en: adj. elastic, porous, and absorbent
words.example_en: We liked the bike's spongy handgrip for keeping our hands away from cold metal.
words.example_zh: 我们喜欢这辆自行车的软把手，有了它，我们的双手就不用再接触冰冷的金属了。

words.definition_en: v. to approach and speak to (someone) often in a challenging or aggressive way
words.example_en: A stranger accosted him on the bus.
words.example_zh: 一位陌生人在公共汽车上同他搭讪。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to say or state in a public, official, or definite way (2)v. to show clearly
words.example_en: His accent proclaimed him a Scot.
words.example_zh: 他的口音表明他是苏格兰人。

words.definition_en: adj. liable to spoil or decay
words.example_en: Raw eggs are highly perishable and must be chilled before and after cooking.
words.example_zh: 生鸡蛋非常容易变质，烹制前后必须冷藏。

words.definition_en: v. to give a lot of care and attention or too much care and attention
words.example_en: The hotel staff cosseted the guests with constant attention.
words.example_zh: 酒店员工无微不至地照顾客人。

words.definition_en: adj. happening, existing, or arising at precisely the same time
words.example_en: This synchronous behavior proved to be an immense advantage.
words.example_zh: 这种同步行为被证明有巨大的优势。

words.definition_en: adj. devoted to labor
words.example_en: My mother is a laborious woman.
words.example_zh: 我的母亲是一个勤劳的女人。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to spoil by a flaw (2)n. a mark that makes something imperfect or less beautiful
words.example_en: He wasn't about to blemish that pristine record.
words.example_zh: 他本不想去玷污那清白的过去。

words.definition_en: v. to collect into a group or crowd
words.example_en: The crowds congregated in the town square to hear the President.
words.example_zh: 人群聚集在城内广场上听总统讲话。

words.definition_en: n. dash or flamboyance in style and action
words.example_en: Her panache at dealing with the world's media is quite astonishing.
words.example_zh: 她应付世界媒体的派头非常令人吃惊。

words.definition_en: v. to make less strong or active
words.example_en: Brokers had feared they would damp foreign investment into local stocks.
words.example_zh: 经纪商曾担心，这会减少外资对印度股市的投资。

words.definition_en: adj. quick and careless
words.example_en: She has a very slapdash approach to keeping accounts.
words.example_zh: 她记账十分潦草马虎。

words.definition_en: v. to long persistently, wistfully, or sadly
words.example_en: The people yearned for peace.
words.example_zh: 人民渴望和平。

words.definition_en: n. government by the best individuals or by a small privileged class
words.example_en: This country has never had a genuine aristocracy or an aristocratic tradition.
words.example_zh: 这个国家从来没有出现真正的贵族和贵族传统。

words.definition_en: v. to be uncooperative, obstructive, or evasive
words.example_en: He did his best this week to stonewall questions and to block even the most modest proposals.
words.example_zh: 他这周竭力避免作出明确答复，甚至连一些最温和的建议都企图阻拦。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to the country or country life
words.example_en: Tomorrow I head off with my family to a bucolic place at the edge of the continent.
words.example_zh: 明天我将和家人去到这块大陆边缘的一个宁静乡村。

words.definition_en: v. to hit something or each other with strong force
words.example_en: The car and the van collided head-on in thick fog.
words.example_zh: 那辆小轿车和货车在浓雾中迎面相撞。

words.definition_en: v. to treat like a god or goddess
words.example_en: These young men deify financial success.
words.example_zh: 这些年轻人崇拜财务上的成功。

words.definition_en: adv. in this vicinity
words.example_en: It's a bit chilly and empty hereabouts.
words.example_zh: 这附近略显阴冷空旷。

words.definition_en: v. to settle judicially
words.example_en: A special subcommittee adjudicates on planning applications
words.example_zh: 一个特别小组委员会负责裁定规划申请。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. causing or characterized by emotional distress or tension (2)adj. well supplied or provided
words.example_en: The earliest operations employing this technique were fraught with dangers.
words.example_zh: 最早使用这种技术的手术充满了危险。

words.definition_en: n. an agent that provokes or speeds significant change or action
words.example_en: I very much hope that this case will prove to be a catalyst for change.
words.example_zh: 我非常希望这起事件能催生变革。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to bother or annoy with many comments or questions (2)v. to ask or tell someone again and again to do something
words.example_en: Richard's mother badgered him into taking a Spanish wife.
words.example_zh: 理查德的母亲磨破嘴皮子，终于说服他娶了个西班牙太太。

words.definition_en: n. a crucial dividing point, line, or factor
words.example_en: The meeting has the potential to be a watershed event.
words.example_zh: 这次会议有可能成为具有转折性的事件。

words.definition_en: n. a strong and continued inclination
words.example_en: She has a penchant for Indian food.
words.example_zh: 她爱吃印度食物。

words.definition_en: v. remodel, refashion
words.example_en: She recast her lecture as a radio talk.
words.example_zh: 她把讲稿修改成了一篇广播讲话。

words.definition_en: adj. exhibiting or marked by great intensity of feeling
words.example_en: The pros and cons of various solutions to society’s problems generated fervent debate in my family.
words.example_zh: 对解决社会问题各种方法的支持与反对经常引起家人热烈的争论。

words.definition_en: adj. not agreeing with established beliefs or standards
words.example_en: His opinions have always been distinctly heterodox.
words.example_zh: 他的观点时常带有明显的异端色彩。

words.definition_en: v. to recover from setback or frustration
words.example_en: Commodities have rebounded, but this is dwarfed by the rebound in commodity-based equities.
words.example_zh: 大宗商品价格反弹了，但其反弹幅度远远比不上大宗商品类股票。

words.definition_en: v. to delineate or impress clearly
words.example_en: The ugly scene in the study was still etched in her mind.
words.example_zh: 书房里丑陋的一幕在她脑海中依旧清晰。

words.definition_en: n. a character assumed by an author in a written work
words.example_en: These variables may be very similar to those you identified as part of your persona hypothesis.
words.example_zh: 这些变量可能会和你在人物角色假设时发现并确定下来的那些变量差不多。

words.definition_en: adj. very good
words.example_en: He has done sterling work on the finance committee.
words.example_zh: 他在财务委员会工作优异。

words.definition_en: n. an unfounded or false, deliberately misleading story
words.example_en: Since the age of internet was coming, canard of internet then challenged the authenticity of news.
words.example_zh: 自从人类进入互联网时代以来，网络谣言便对新闻真实提出了新的挑战。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by or suggestive of utter calm and unruffled repose or quietude
words.example_en: She looked as calm and serene as she always did.
words.example_zh: 她看上去和以往一样平静安详。

words.definition_en: adj. very exciting
words.example_en: He watched the breathtaking match to the end.
words.example_zh: 他把激动人心的比赛看到最后。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to give clear and effective utterance to (2)adj. clearly expressed and easily understood
words.example_en: The president has been accused of failing to articulate an overall vision in foreign affairs.
words.example_zh: 总统因未能清晰表述对外交事务的整体构想而受到指责。

words.definition_en: n. the science of human beings
words.example_en: Social anthropology is centrally concerned with the diversity of culture.
words.example_zh: 社会人类学主要关于文化多样性。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. full of or exuding emotion or spirit (2)adj. easily provoked
words.example_en: She has a fiery temper.
words.example_zh: 她脾气暴躁。

words.definition_en: adj. something choice or pleasing
words.example_en: The little girl wore a dainty dress.
words.example_zh: 小姑娘穿着一件漂亮的连衣裙。

words.definition_en: adj. tending to make people afraid or less confident
words.example_en: They were faced with the daunting task of restoring the house.
words.example_zh: 他们面临着修复房子的艰巨任务。

words.definition_en: adj. using few words in speech or writing
words.example_en: His laconic reply suggested a lack of interest in the topic.
words.example_zh: 他简明的回答显得他对该话题缺乏兴趣。

words.definition_en: v. to make shorter
words.example_en: The United States of America is commonly abbreviated to U.S.A.
words.example_zh: 美利坚合众国常被缩略为 U.S.A.。

words.definition_en: v. to turn aside especially from the main subject of attention or course of argument
words.example_en: I've digressed a little to explain the situation so far, so let me now recap.
words.example_zh: 刚才我稍稍离题说明了迄今的情况，现在让我来概述一下。

words.definition_en: adj. generous, charitable
words.example_en: He is a bighearted Irish.
words.example_zh: 他是个慷慨的爱尔兰人。

words.definition_en: v. to make a loud, high-pitched cry
words.example_en: She shrieked and leapt from the bed.
words.example_zh: 她尖叫一声从床上跳了起来

words.definition_en: v. waver, vacillate
words.example_en: The table wobbled when I leaned on it.
words.example_zh: 我往桌上一靠，桌子就摇晃了起来。

words.definition_en: v. to trick into believing or accepting as genuine something false and often preposterous
words.example_en: Don't hoax the new boy who's my nephew.
words.example_zh: 别戏弄新来的小伙子啊，他是我外甥。

words.definition_en: v. to hold spellbound
words.example_en: The boy was enthralled by the stories of adventure.
words.example_zh: 这孩子被冒险故事迷住了。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by a heavy dimness or obscurity caused by or like that caused by overhanging fog or smoke
words.example_en: He had a somewhat murky past.
words.example_zh: 他有一段不清白的过去。

words.definition_en: v. to fail to do what is required by
words.example_en: The moves contravene the peace plan agreed by both sides.
words.example_zh: 这些举措违反了双方同意的和平方案。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by lack of proper caution
words.example_en: The policeman chewed out the driver for reckless driving.
words.example_zh: 那位司机因为开车莽撞，被警察狠狠批评了一顿。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to make less or cause to appear less (2)v. to lessen the authority or reputation of
words.example_en: The evidence suggests that our sense of touch is programmed to diminish with age.
words.example_zh: 有证据表明，我们的触觉会随着年龄的增长而减损。

words.definition_en: adj. having or consisting of a physical body or form
words.example_en: We live in a culture dominated by pixels, increasingly unmoored from corporeal reality.
words.example_zh: 我们生活在一个被像素主宰的社会，日益脱离物质现实。

words.definition_en: n. the highest point or the highest level
words.example_en: At the apex of the party was its central committee.
words.example_zh: 该党的最高权力机构是中央委员会。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. extremely good and deserving to be admired and copied (2)adj. serving as an example of something
words.example_en: Her behavior was exemplary.
words.example_zh: 她的行为堪作楷模。

words.definition_en: adj. making less intense or harsh
words.example_en: Our president took an emollient approach to differences.
words.example_zh: 我的总统对分歧采取安抚策略。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking stability or steadiness
words.example_en: Isabelle was a frivolous little fool, vain and flighty.
words.example_zh: 伊莎贝尔是个轻佻、没头脑的小女人，非常虚荣又反复无常。

words.definition_en: n. one who lives permanently in a place
words.example_en: Fish are denizen of the sea.
words.example_zh: 鱼是生活在海里的动物。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to the sense or organs of hearing, to sound, or to the science of sounds
words.example_en: The microphone converts acoustic waves to electrical signals for transmission.
words.example_zh: 麦克风将声波转化成电信号进行传播。

words.definition_en: adj. tending to prevent or ward off
words.example_en: Vaccination and other prophylactic measures can be carried out.
words.example_zh: 可以采取疫苗接种和其他预防措施。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to use incorrectly (2)v. abuse, mistreat
words.example_en: The new law toughens up penalties for those that misuse guns.
words.example_zh: 新法对滥用枪支的处罚更为严厉。

words.definition_en: n. the removal or reducing of something that is painful or unpleasant
words.example_en: The news will come as a great relief to the French authorities.
words.example_zh: 这个消息会让法国当局大大松一口气。

words.definition_en: adj. very different and separate from each other
words.example_en: Theory must not be divorced from practice.
words.example_zh: 理论与实践不能脱节。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. keenly perceptive or discerning (2)adj. extremely sharp or severe
words.example_en: The war has aggravated an acute economic crisis.
words.example_zh: 战争加剧了原本已很严重的经济危机。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not too proud or confident about yourself or your abilities (2)adj. unpretentious (3)adj. limited in size, amount, or scope
words.definition_zh: 谦虚的朴实无华的适度的，有限的
words.example_en: Lord Carrington is modest about his achievements.
words.example_zh: 卡林顿勋爵对自己取得的成绩非常谦虚。

words.definition_en: v. to spend extravagantly or foolishly
words.example_en: Hobbs didn't squander his money on flashy cars or other vices.
words.example_zh: 霍布斯没有把钱挥霍在奢华汽车或其他恶习上。

words.definition_en: adj. not favoring one side or group over another
words.example_en: He is a broad-minded but evenhanded judge.
words.example_zh: 开明而公正的法官。

words.definition_en: v. to court favor by a cringing or flattering manner
words.example_en: People fawn over you when you're famous.
words.example_zh: 你出了名，大家就会来讨好你。

words.definition_en: n. something that is made by combining different things
words.example_en: This kind of lighting encourages vitamin D synthesis in the skin.
words.example_zh: 这种光照会促进皮肤中维生素 D 的合成。

words.definition_en: n. the end of something that is thought of as being like a death
words.example_en: Smoking, rather than genetics, was the cause of his early demise.
words.example_zh: 导致他英年早逝的原因是吸烟而非遗传。

words.definition_en: adj. very foolish
words.example_en: It is ludicrous to call a simple split-level house a mansion.
words.example_zh: 把一座简朴的错层式的房子叫做大厦真太可笑了

words.definition_en: adj. precisely adapted to a particular situation, need, or circumstance
words.example_en: It is only meet that she should be seated first.
words.example_zh: 她应该先坐，这才合适。

words.definition_en: n. when an activity, situation, or process begins again after a period when it had stopped
words.example_en: They will discuss the possible renewal of diplomatic relations.
words.example_zh: 他们将讨论恢复外交关系的可能性。

words.definition_en: adj. tending to hinder the attainment of a desired goal
words.example_en: We think an attempt to bully them into submission would be counterproductive.
words.example_zh: 我们认为试图迫使他们屈服会适得其反。

words.definition_en: v. to detract from the good quality of
words.example_en: The affair could tarnish the reputation of the prime minister.
words.example_zh: 这一事件可能有损首相的名誉。

words.definition_en: v. to make safe or secure
words.example_en: The problem was to safeguard sites from encroachment by property development.
words.example_zh: 问题是要保护遗址免受房地产开发商的侵占。

words.definition_en: n. one originating or coming from an ancestral stock or source
words.example_en: She is a direct descendant of Queen Victoria.
words.example_zh: 她是维多利亚女王的嫡系后代。

words.definition_en: v. to feel penitence, remorse, or regret for
words.example_en: Tavare was probably ruing his decision.
words.example_zh: 塔瓦雷可能正在为自己作出的决定感到后悔。

words.definition_en: adj. very important or noticeable
words.example_en: Chronic fatigue is also one of the salient features of depression.
words.example_zh: 慢性疲劳也是抑郁症的显著特点之一。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. involving, applicable to, or affecting the whole (2)adj. not specific
words.example_en: The general opinion is that the conference was a success.
words.example_zh: 人们普遍认为这次会议是成功的。

words.definition_en: adj. not willing to change a decision, opinion, method, etc.
words.example_en: Mrs Thatcher was a tough and uncompromising politician.
words.example_zh: 撒切尔夫人是一位态度强硬、不轻易妥协的政治家。

words.definition_en: n. ideas or guesses about something that is not known
words.example_en: The group's problems have led to speculation that heads will roll.
words.example_zh: 已经有人猜测，该集团的问题会导致一些人被解雇。

words.definition_en: v. to pass quickly or abruptly from one place or condition to another
words.example_en: Butterflies flitted from flower to flower.
words.example_zh: 蝴蝶在花丛中飞来飞去。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to visit often (2)v. to visit or inhabit as a ghost
words.example_en: People say ghosts haunt that old house.
words.example_zh: 据说那座老宅子有鬼魂出没。

words.definition_en: n. a person or thing of little consequence or significance
words.example_en: Amidst the current bunch of nonentities, he is a towering figure.
words.example_zh: 在目前这帮无足轻重的人里面，他算是鹤立鸡群了。

words.definition_en: adj. noisily turbulent
words.example_en: We can hear their boisterous laughter in the next room.
words.example_zh: 我们可以听到他们在隔壁房间的喧闹的笑声。

words.definition_en: v. to interpret the meaning of
words.example_en: The Coastguard needs to decipher garbled messages in a few minutes.
words.example_zh: 海岸警卫队需要在几分钟内解读这些含混不清的信息。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. able to be seen (2)v. to show clearly
words.example_en: The same alarm is manifest everywhere.
words.example_zh: 同样的恐慌在各地都已出现。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to be usual, common, or popular (2)v. to defeat an opponent especially in a long or difficult contest
words.example_en: Those beliefs still prevail among certain social groups.
words.example_zh: 这些信念在某些社会群体中仍很盛行。

words.definition_en: n. a selfish and excessive desire for more of something (such as money) than is needed
words.example_en: Greed was written on his face.
words.example_zh: 他脸上明显露出贪婪的表情。

words.definition_en: v. to deprive of courage, strength, or steadiness
words.definition_zh: 使失去勇气、信心或镇定
words.example_en: The experience completely unnerved me.
words.example_zh: 那段经历使我彻底丧失了信心。

words.definition_en: adj. making good health possible or likely
words.example_en: With its graceful movements and salubrious effects on health, it has a strong appeal to a vast multitude of people.
words.example_zh: 因其优美的运动姿态和对健康的有益影响，武术在广大群众中有很强的吸引力。

words.definition_en: v. to do something that is not allowed
words.example_en: No one is permitted to have privileges to transgress the law.
words.example_zh: 不允许任何人有超越法律的特权。

words.definition_en: adj. superior to all others
words.example_en: Nitrogen is of paramount importance to life on earth.
words.example_zh: 氮对地球上的生命至关重要。

words.definition_en: v. to release from something that engages or involves
words.example_en: He managed to disengage himself from the contract.
words.example_zh: 他成功地为自己解除了契约义务。

words.definition_en: adj. continuing for a long time
words.example_en: He had an abiding love of the English countryside.
words.example_zh: 他对英国的乡村有着永久的热爱之情。

words.definition_en: adj. not influenced by emotions or personal interest
words.example_en: It is written in a detached, precise style
words.example_zh: .行文冷静客观，一针见血。

words.definition_en: v. to instruct and improve especially in moral and religious knowledge
words.example_en: They tried to edify the child with music.
words.example_zh: 他们试图用音乐陶冶这孩子。

words.definition_en: adj. tedious from familiarity
words.example_en: The Government, he said, were sticking to stale ideas.
words.example_zh: 他说政府仍在抱残守缺。

words.definition_en: n. official strong criticism
words.example_en: I deserve neither such praise nor such censure.
words.example_zh: 这样的夸奖我不敢当，这样的责备我也不敢当。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by absence of speech
words.example_en: He is mute on the subject of social system.
words.example_zh: 他对社会制度的问题保持沉默。

words.definition_en: adj. not agreeing with what seems right or natural
words.example_en: This sounds counterintuitive but within the logic of accounting it makes some sense.
words.example_zh: 这听起来似乎违背常理，但从会计逻辑来说有一定道理。

words.definition_en: n. the act, manner, or amount of using something
words.example_en: Parts of the motor wore out because of constant usage.
words.example_zh: 发动机的一些部件由于不断使用而磨损了。

words.definition_en: n. a very dangerous or frightening situation
words.example_en: My boss felt he was on the edge of an abyss; one false move and he was done for.
words.example_zh: 我的上司觉得自己如临深渊，一步不慎便会永劫不复。

words.definition_en: adj. being over and above what is needed
words.example_en: They don't have a lot of spare cash.
words.example_zh: 他们没有多少闲钱。

words.definition_en: adj. feeling or showing readiness to fight
words.example_en: At the moment southerners are loyal mostly to belligerent tribal chiefs, not the nascent government that led the fight for independence.
words.example_zh: 目前，南部苏丹居民主要效忠于好战的部落首领，而不是带领他们为独立而战的新生的政府。

words.definition_en: adj. feeding on plants
words.example_en: Mammoths were herbivorous mammals.
words.example_zh: 猛犸是食草性哺乳动物。

words.definition_en: adj. causing a feeling of great surprise or wonder
words.example_en: The results are quite astounding.
words.example_zh: 结果令人无比震惊。

words.definition_en: adj. allowed according to rules or laws
words.example_en: The French government has condemned the coup in Haiti and has demanded the restoration of the legitimate government.
words.example_zh: 法国政府已经谴责了海地的政变，并要求恢复合法政府。

words.definition_en: v. to avoid or ignore especially to get something done quicker
words.example_en: A growing number of employers are trying to bypass the unions altogether.
words.example_zh: 越来越多的雇主试图完全绕过工会行事。

words.definition_en: n. a rude or discourteous act
words.example_en: It is gross incivility to refuse answer when spoken to.
words.example_zh: 当人家找你说话时拒绝答应是极其无礼的事.

words.definition_en: v. affirm, declare
words.example_en: We predicate goodness and mercy of god.
words.example_zh: 我们断定上帝之善与慈悲。

words.definition_en: v. to declare or make legally invalid or void
words.example_en: Their marriage was annulled after just six months.
words.example_zh: 他们的婚姻仅过半年就宣告取消。

words.definition_en: v. to give heart to: cheer
words.example_en: These initiatives appeared to hearten investors.
words.example_zh: 这些举措似乎鼓舞了投资者。

words.definition_en: n. a minor flaw or shortcoming in character or behavior
words.example_en: I shall never attempt to palliate my own foible by expose the error of another.
words.example_zh: 我从不想暴露别人的错误，以图减轻自己的缺点。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to induct into an office with suitable ceremonies (2)v. to bring about the beginning of
words.example_en: The new President will be inaugurated on January 20.
words.example_zh: 新总统将于1月20号举行就职典礼。

words.definition_en: v. to reduce in size
words.example_en: Sales have decreased so much that they need to downsize.
words.example_zh: 销售业绩减少很多，所以他们必须缩小规模。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by shrewdness in managing, contriving, or dealing
words.example_en: Many towns often found it politic to change their allegiance.
words.example_zh: 很多城镇经常发现改变拥戴对象才是上策。

words.definition_en: v. to confound with fear, amazement, or awe
words.example_en: The news petrified me.
words.example_zh: 这个新闻让我非常吃惊。

words.definition_en: adj. of, relating to, or marked by declamation or rhetorical display
words.example_en: Rebels like Katharine Hamnett have made a name for bold, declamatory statements.
words.example_zh: 诸如凯瑟琳·哈姆内特之类的叛逆者以口出大胆、夸张之词而闻名。

words.definition_en: adj. dynamic, energizing
words.example_en: The energy of motion is called kinetic energy.
words.example_zh: 运动的能量称为动能。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. believable or realistic (2)adj. superficially fair, reasonable, or valuable but often specious
words.definition_zh: 真实的看似真实（但实际虚假）
words.example_en: He was so plausible that he conned everybody.
words.example_zh: 他那么会花言巧语，以至于骗过了所有的人。

words.definition_en: adj. not capable of being defended against attack or criticism
words.example_en: This argument is untenable from an intellectual, moral and practical standpoint.
words.example_zh: 从智识、道德和现实的角度来看，这一论点是站不住脚的。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. hard to notice or see (2)adj. difficult to understand (3)adj. able to make fine distinctions
words.definition_zh: 不易觉察的难以理解的敏锐的
words.example_en: The job required a subtle mind.
words.example_zh: 那项工作需要一个头脑敏锐的人去做。

words.definition_en: v. to criticize very harshly
words.example_en: They lambasted the report as a gross distortion of the truth.
words.example_zh: 他们激烈批评该报告是严重歪曲事实。

words.definition_en: adj. arrested in development or adjustment
words.example_en: He is fixated on things that remind him of his childhood.
words.example_zh: 他异常依恋那些使他回忆起童年生活的东西。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. of little weight or importance (2)adj. lacking in seriousness
words.example_en: The group says it wants politicians to stop wasting public money on what it believes are frivolous projects.
words.example_zh: 这个团体表示希望从政者不要再把公共资金浪费在一些它认为无关痛痒的项目上。

words.definition_en: adj. full of danger
words.example_en: The journey through the jungle was perilous.
words.example_zh: 穿过丛林的旅行充满了危险。

words.definition_en: n. a main interest, quality, or goal
words.example_en: The course is essentially theoretical in orientation.
words.example_zh: 该课程的目标基本上是理论性的。

words.definition_en: n. the belief that a country should not be involved with other counties
words.example_en: Voices of economic isolationism do not represent the interests of the American people.
words.example_zh: 经济孤立主义的声音并不代表美国人民的利益。

words.definition_en: adj. fierce, cruel, terrible
words.example_en: The witch is gloating over her fell scheme.
words.example_zh: 女巫对自己想到的这个凶恶的诡计沾沾自喜。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to examine in order to suppress or delete anything considered objectionable (2)n. a person who supervises conduct and morals
words.example_en: Censor on film means you examine a film to see whether it can be released or not.
words.example_zh: 审查电影就是检查一部电影是否能够发行。

words.definition_en: n. a mania for great or grandiose performance
words.example_en: Early success may lead to megalomania.
words.example_zh: 少年得志可能导致妄自尊大。

words.definition_en: adj. proceeding without a specific goal, purpose, or direction
words.example_en: Don't waste time yours and your manager's with long preambles and rambling thoughts.
words.example_zh: 不要用长篇大论和杂乱无章的想法来浪费你和经理的时间。

words.definition_en: adj. of, relating to, or befitting a teacher or education
words.example_en: Three aspects are included: its pedagogical idea, the teaching reform and practice, and the teaching effects.
words.example_zh: 它从教育理念、教学改革与实践、教学效果三个方面展开。

words.definition_en: n. the state or custom of being married to only one person at a time
words.example_en: There are apparently some misconceptions about this whole monogamy thing.
words.example_zh: 很多人显然对一夫一妻制存在着误解。

words.definition_en: adj. leaning to one side
words.example_en: His suit had shoulders that made him look lopsided.
words.example_zh: 他那套西服的两肩不平，让他整个人看起来一边高一边低。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to begin to fail or weaken (2)v. to hesitate in purpose or action
words.example_en: The economy shows no signs of faltering.
words.example_zh: 经济没有衰退的迹象。

words.definition_en: adj. in a state of complete disorder and confusion
words.example_en: Mullins began to rummage among the chaotic mess of papers on his desk.
words.example_zh: 马林斯开始在桌子上一堆乱七八糟的文件里翻来翻去。

words.definition_en: n. tyro, beginner
words.example_en: He is a neophyte at politics.
words.example_zh: 他是个初涉政界的人。

words.definition_en: adj. having corresponding points whose connecting lines are bisected by a given point or perpendicularly bisected by a given line or plane
words.example_en: The two wings of the building are exactly symmetrical.
words.example_zh: 大楼的两翼完全对称。

words.definition_en: n. the beginning of an organization, activity, or process
words.example_en: Since its inception the company has produced 53 different aircraft designs.
words.example_zh: 该公司自成立以来已经完成了 53 种不同样式飞行器的设计。

words.definition_en: adj. not easily upset or excited
words.example_en: A placid parent makes a placid home.
words.example_zh: 温和的父母创造温馨的家。

words.definition_en: v. to descend or dip suddenly
words.example_en: The market plunged after the announcement.
words.example_zh: 消息公布后，市场暴跌。

words.definition_en: n. an indispensable and fundamental basis
words.example_en: The doctrine of surplus value is the cornerstone of Marx's economic theory.
words.example_zh: 剩余价值学说是马克思经济理论的基石。

words.definition_en: n. undivided rule or absolute sovereignty by a single person
words.example_en: The monarchy in England plays an important role in British culture.
words.example_zh: 英格兰的君主政体在英国文化中起重要作用。

words.definition_en: v. to supply what is required or desired
words.example_en: When companies try to cater for older customers, they do not always get it right.
words.example_zh: 公司试着迎合老年客户，但他们并不总是采取正确方式。

words.definition_en: adj. new and different from what has been known before
words.example_en: Protesters found a novel way of demonstrating against steeply rising oil prices.
words.example_zh: 抗议者们找到一种新奇的示威方式来反对石油价格的飙升。

words.definition_en: adj. treating all people and groups equally
words.example_en: Careers officers offer impartial advice to all pupils.
words.example_zh: 就业指导员向所有学生提供公正无私的建议。

words.definition_en: n. a deed notable especially for courage
words.example_en: The tunnel is a brilliant feat of engineering.
words.example_zh: 这条隧道是工程方面的辉煌成就。

words.definition_en: n. a person who hates or distrusts humankind
words.example_en: You must be some kind of cynic or misanthrope to object to such a laudable goal.
words.example_zh: 你一定是有点愤世嫉俗或反人类才会反对这个值得赞赏的目标。

words.definition_en: adj. happening by chance
words.example_en: Their success is the result of a fortuitous combination of circumstances.
words.example_zh: 他们的成功是各种环境因素偶然联系在一起的幸运产物。

words.definition_en: n. something that will make everything about a situation better
words.example_en: Membership of the ERM is not a panacea for Britain's economic problems.
words.example_zh: 加入欧洲汇率机制并不是解决英国经济问题的万灵丹。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing liveliness and enthusiasm
words.example_en: Besides ebullient congratulant word, organizer brought a lot of exquisite gifts.
words.example_zh: 除了热情洋溢的祝贺词外，主办方还带来了精美的小礼物。

words.definition_en: v. to talk or write foolishly
words.example_en: He waffled on about everything that didn't matter.
words.example_zh: 他继续对所有无关紧要的事胡扯了一通。

words.definition_en: adj. urgently important
words.example_en: It is one of the most pressing problems facing this country.
words.example_zh: 它是该国面临的最紧迫的问题之一。

words.definition_en: adj. causing wonder
words.example_en: We were driven across this wondrous vast land of lakes and forests.
words.example_zh: 我们乘车穿越这片有着湖泊及森林的广袤而神奇的土地。

words.definition_en: v. to convert into intelligible form
words.example_en: All he had to do was decode it and pass it over.
words.example_zh: 他需要做的就是将它破译然后转给他人。

words.definition_en: v. strengthen or toughen
words.example_en: Years of hardship annealed her resolve.
words.example_zh: 多年的困苦磨炼了她的意志。

words.definition_en: n. one who practices a profession
words.example_en: As a practitioner I discovered problem that I need to solve.
words.example_zh: 作为从业者，我会发现我需要解决的问题。

words.definition_en: v. to make greater, more numerous, larger, or more intense
words.example_en: While searching for a way to augment the family income, she began making dolls.
words.example_zh: 为了想法子增加家庭收入，她开始制作洋娃娃。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. covering or including everything (2)adj. including the stated limits and everything in between
words.definition_zh: 全包括的包括首尾两项的
words.example_en: An all-inclusive two-week holiday costs around £880 per person.
words.example_zh: 为期两周、包括所有费用的度假大约每人花费880英镑。

words.definition_en: n. a slight offense
words.example_en: People are not tolerant of extra-marital peccadilloes by public figures.
words.example_zh: 人们不能容忍公众人物婚外过失。

words.definition_en: v. to secretly plan with someone to do something that is harmful or illegal
words.example_en: She admitted conspiring with her lover to murder her husband.
words.example_zh: 她承认与情夫密谋杀害亲夫。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. having a thick edge or point (2)adj. being straight to the point
words.example_en: He was sawing the bread with a blunt knife.
words.example_zh: 他用一个很钝的小刀切面包。

words.definition_en: adj. possessing or acting with bravery or boldness
words.example_en: A valiant assault force does not fear a strong fortress.
words.example_zh: 攻坚不怕堡垒硬。

words.definition_en: adj. extremely old
words.example_en: But as the years have passed, what were once fresh ideas have hardened into hoary dogmas.
words.example_zh: 但是随着时间的流逝，一度新鲜的观念也变成了陈腐的教条。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to change into a vapor or to cause to change into a vapor (2)v. to cause to become dissipated
words.example_en: The benzene vaporized and formed a huge cloud of gas.
words.example_zh: 苯蒸发了，形成了一大团气体。

words.definition_en: n. a narrow exclusive circle or group of persons
words.example_en: At university, Taylor had fallen in with a small clique of literature students.
words.example_zh: 在大学里，泰勒曾经频繁参与一个文学小团体的活动。

words.definition_en: n. a natural or inherent aptitude, impulse, or capacity
words.example_en: I didn't have as strong a maternal instinct as some other mothers.
words.example_zh: 我不像其他一些母亲那样有那么强烈的母性本能。

words.definition_en: n. the technical terminology or characteristic idiom of a special activity or group
words.example_en: The manual is full of the jargon and slang of self-improvement courses.
words.example_zh: 手册里满是有关自修课程的行话术语。

words.definition_en: n. a doctrine or practice that emphasizes direct vigorous action
words.example_en: The hallmarks of our corporate culture include an emphasis on teamwork, meritocracry, client service, and activism in a wide variety of areas.
words.example_zh: 我们企业文化的特色包括：强调团队协作、精英领导、客户服务，以及在广泛领域的行动主义。

words.definition_en: adj. surpassing all others
words.example_en: Some superlative wines are made in this region.
words.example_zh: 一些上好的葡萄酒就是在这个地区酿造的。

words.definition_en: n. a person who has just started learning or doing something
words.example_en: I am a tyro in English.
words.example_zh: 我是学英语的新手。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to prevent from happening (2)v. to cause to decide not to do something
words.example_en: Arrests and jail sentences have done nothing to deter the protesters.
words.example_zh: 逮捕和监禁都没能吓倒抗议者。

words.definition_en: n. shelter or protection from danger or distress
words.example_en: He regarded the room as a refuge from the outside world.
words.example_zh: 他把这个屋子当作是逃避外界的避难所。

words.definition_en: adj. traveling from place to place
words.example_en: He is starting itinerant performance all over the world.
words.example_zh: 他正在世界各地巡回演出。

words.definition_en: v. to write or draw hastily and carelessly
words.example_en: She can't write yet, but she loves to scribble with a pencil.
words.example_zh: 她现在还不会写字, 但她喜欢用铅笔乱涂.

words.definition_en: adj. tending to talk a lot
words.example_en: My garrulous neighbor had given away the secret.
words.example_zh: 我那喋喋不休的邻居已把秘密泄露了。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. full of dust and unpleasant smells (2)adj. very old-fashioned
words.example_en: The fusty organization refused to recognize the demand for popular music.
words.example_zh: 这一古板的机构拒绝承认人们对流行音乐的需求。

words.definition_en: v. to become bigger quickly
words.example_en: In London, the use of the Tube has ballooned.
words.example_zh: 在伦敦，坐地铁的人激增。

words.definition_en: adj. profitable
words.example_en: Small local shops stop being economic when a supermarket opens up nearby.
words.example_zh: 附近的一家超市开业后，当地的小商店就没有利润可赚了。

words.definition_en: adj. hostile toward a rival or one believed to enjoy an advantage
words.example_en: She was jealous of his wealth.
words.example_zh: 她嫉妒他的富有。

words.definition_en: adj. having a very sour or sharp taste
words.example_en: Dissolved carbon dioxide makes the water more acidic.
words.example_zh: 二氧化碳溶解后能使水的酸性增加。

words.definition_en: n. a strong desire for money or possessions
words.example_en: There was a glimmer of cupidity in those small, cunning eyes of his.
words.example_zh: 他狡猾的小眼睛里露出了一丝贪婪的神情。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to faction
words.example_en: These factious groups are more violent than their predecessors and their fanaticism is even more irrational.
words.example_zh: 这些宗教派组织比他们的前辈更残暴，他们的狂热变得更加丧失理性了。

words.definition_en: n. a situation of perplexity or distress
words.example_en: She found herself in desperate financial straits.
words.example_zh: 她发觉自己经济状况极为窘迫。

words.definition_en: adj. volunteering one's services where they are neither asked nor needed
words.example_en: When people put on uniforms, their attitude becomes more confident and their manner more officious.
words.example_zh: 人们穿上制服后会信心倍增，也更爱管闲事了。

words.definition_en: adj. coolly and patronizingly haughty
words.example_en: Her supercilious attitude enraged me.
words.example_zh: 她的傲慢态度使我极为愤怒。

words.definition_en: v. to call something to mind
words.example_en: Ramona spoke with warmth when she recollected the doctor who used to be at the county hospital.
words.example_zh: 拉蒙纳回忆起曾在乡村医院工作的那位医生时，言语中透着温情。

words.definition_en: n. an unfortunate accident
words.example_en: This mishap reminded me of a traffic accident I once experienced.
words.example_zh: 这次小事故使我联想起我过去经历的一次交通事故。

words.definition_en: n. false information that is given to people in order to make them believe something or to hide the truth
words.example_en: They spread disinformation in order to discredit politicians.
words.example_zh: 他们散布假消息，企图败坏政治家的名声。

words.definition_en: adj. very noticeable
words.example_en: There has been a marked increase in crimes against property.
words.example_zh: 侵犯财产罪的数量有了明显的增加。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. commonly accepted or supposed (2)adj. assumed to exist or to have existed
words.example_en: Newt Gingrich, a putative Republican candidate next year, has thrown his weight behind the idea.
words.example_zh: 公认的明年共和党候选人Newt Gingrich，已经表明了对此意见的态度。

words.definition_en: v. reappear
words.example_en: The submarine resurfaced.
words.example_zh: 潜艇重新浮出水面。

words.definition_en: n. an irreligious or hedonistic person
words.example_en: The new religion was eager to convert the pagan world.
words.example_zh: 那种新宗教急于使异教徒皈依本教。

words.definition_en: v. to lessen the strength or effect of (something)
words.example_en: Nothing can extenuate his guilt.
words.example_zh: 他的罪无法减轻。

words.definition_en: n. a critical and usually censorious remark
words.example_en: This text is an animadversion to comment on an article.
words.example_zh: 本文是一篇批评性评论文章。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a tendency to believe that some people, ideas, etc., are better than others that usually results in treating some people unfairly (2)n. a strong interest in something or ability to do something
words.example_en: The report revealed a strong bias against younger applicants.
words.example_zh: 报告揭示了对年轻求职者的强烈偏见。

words.definition_en: v. to examine carefully especially in a critical way
words.example_en: The statement was carefully scrutinized before publication.
words.example_zh: 声明在发表前经过仔细审查。

words.definition_en: n. the farthest or highest point
words.example_en: The Alliance for Progress reached its apogee during the first half of the decade.
words.example_zh: 这十年中的前五年是进步联盟迈向顶峰的时期。

words.definition_en: adj. having little or no money
words.example_en: He is impecunious, does not know anyone who can lend money.
words.example_zh: 他身无分文，也不认识任何可以借钱的人。

words.definition_en: adj. causing wonder and admiration
words.example_en: Among the most spectacular sights are the great sea-bird colonies.
words.example_zh: 庞大的海鸟群是最壮观的景象之一。

words.definition_en: adj.
words.example_en: The Treasury has embraced a complicated and still only half-formulated scheme to guarantee conventional bank loans to such enterprises.
words.example_zh: 财政部采纳一项复杂的、仍未制定成型的方案去保障小企业取得常规银行贷款。

words.definition_en: adj. habitually lazy
words.example_en: Having nothing to do makes one indolent.
words.example_zh: 一个人老是无所事事就会懒散成性。

words.definition_en: n. something that exists only in the imagination and is not possible in reality
words.example_en: Religious unity remained as much a chimera as ever.
words.example_zh: 宗教统一仍然和从前一样，不过是个妄想。

words.definition_en: n. small bumps on your skin that are caused by cold, fear, or a sudden feeling of excitement
words.example_en: When I ride a bicycle on a windy night, I get goosebumps.
words.example_zh: 当我在起风的夜里骑脚踏车时，我会起鸡皮疙瘩。

words.definition_en: v. to determine beforehand
words.example_en: These factors predetermine to a large extent the outcome.
words.example_zh: 这些因素在很大程度上预先决定了其结果。

words.definition_en: adj. not loyal and cannot be trusted
words.example_en: Their feet will trample on the dead bodies of their perfidious aggressors.
words.example_zh: 他们将从背信弃义的侵略者的尸体上踏过。

words.definition_en: v. to give up, refuse, or resign usually by formal declaration
words.example_en: I call on everyone to renounce the use of violence and armed struggle.
words.example_zh: 我呼吁每个人都放弃使用暴力和武装斗争。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not letting light through (2)adj. difficult to understand or explain
words.example_en: You can always use opaque glass if you need to block a street view.
words.example_zh: 如果你需要挡住外面的街景，你随时可以使用不透明玻璃。

words.definition_en: n. the technical or special terms used in a business, art, science, or special subject
words.example_en: The disagreement arose over a different use of terminology.
words.example_zh: 分歧的缘起在于专门用语使用的不同。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to copy especially of humor (2)v. to naturally look like
words.example_en: Neural networks are computer systems which mimic the workings of the brain.
words.example_zh: 神经网络是模拟大脑工作方式的计算机系统。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. able to do many different things (2)adj. having many different uses
words.synonyms: protean, fickle, volatile, flighty, erratic, capricious
words.example_en: They bought a versatile Swiss Army knife for $ 20.
words.example_zh: 他们花二十美元买了一把多功能的瑞士军刀。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to feel weariness and restlessness through lack of interest
words.example_en: Dickie bored him all through the meal with stories of the Navy.
words.example_zh: 迪基吃饭时一直在讲海军的故事，让他不胜其烦。

words.definition_en: v. to increase in number or amount quickly
words.example_en: Computerized data bases are proliferating fast.
words.example_zh: 计算机数据库正在迅速激增。

words.definition_en: adj. excessive scanty
words.example_en: The rest of the old man's exiguous savings are donated to that boy.
words.example_zh: 那老人微薄积蓄中的剩余部分都捐赠给了那个男孩。

words.definition_en: adj. making you feel serious and thoughtful
words.example_en: It is sobering to realize that this is not a new problem.
words.example_zh: 意识到这并不是新问题，就会使人冷静严肃起来。

words.definition_en: n. the ability to think clearly and make good decisions
words.example_en: His sharp business acumen meant he quickly rose to the top.
words.example_zh: 精明的商业头脑令其青云直上。

words.definition_en: n. a model of excellence or perfection
words.example_en: He wasn't the paragon of virtue she had expected.
words.example_zh: 他不是她想象中的那种美德典范。

words.definition_en: v. to attack or criticize in a violent or angry way
words.example_en: The opposition's newspapers assail the government each day.
words.example_zh: 反对党的报纸每天都对政府进行猛烈抨击。

words.definition_en: adj. using conversational style
words.example_en: When reading, I'm always confused with idioms, slang and colloquial words.
words.example_zh: 在阅读中，我总是弄不清惯用语、俚语和口语词汇。

words.definition_en: v. to suspend indefinitely or until a later stated time
words.example_en: The trial has been adjourned until next week.
words.example_zh: 审判延期至下周。

words.definition_en: v. to revile or scold in harsh, insolent, or abusive language
words.example_en: Other Internet users rail at companies and government departments forcing staff to donate to national appeals.
words.example_zh: 其它互联网用户则抱怨企业与政府部门强迫员工响应国家号召，加入捐款活动。

words.definition_en: adj. crude or coarse in manner or style
words.example_en: He spent all day playing rackets on the beach, a plebeian sport if there ever was one.
words.example_zh: 他一整天都在海滩玩壁球，再没有比这更粗俗的运动了。

words.definition_en: n. advocating or involving a rigidly uncompromising course of action
words.example_en: This politician is a hardliner.
words.example_zh: 这个政治家是一个强硬分子

words.definition_en: adj. neither perpendicular nor parallel
words.example_en: Our laughter startled people at a nearby table.During the time left to us, out glances were furtive,oblique.
words.example_zh: 我们的笑声惊动了邻桌的人，接下来我们的目光躲躲闪闪，游离不定。

words.definition_en: v. to expose to view by or as if by digging away a covering
words.example_en: The site has been excavated by archaeologists.
words.example_zh: 这个遗址已被考古学家发掘出来。

words.definition_en: adj. stubbornly tenacious
words.example_en: A man is pertinacious when he defends his folly and trusts too greatly in his own wit.
words.example_zh: 一个人在辩解自己的荒唐事的时候如果过分相信自己的智慧那就是顽冥不化。

words.definition_en: v. to condemn or forbid as harmful or unlawful
words.example_en: In some cultures surgery is proscribed.
words.example_zh: 在一些文化里，外科手术是被禁止的。

words.definition_en: v. to drink especially liquor greedily, continually, or habitually
words.example_en: The kids seem to be guzzling soft drinks all day.
words.example_zh: 孩子们似乎整天都在猛喝汽水。

words.definition_en: v. to set about dealing with
words.example_en: I don't know how to tackle this problem.
words.example_zh: 我不知道该如何处理这个问题。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. showing innocent or childlike simplicity and candidness (2)adj. lacking craft or subtlety
words.example_en: Only the most ingenuous person would believe such a weak excuse!
words.example_zh: 只有最天真的人才会相信这么一个站不住脚的借口!

words.definition_en: (1)v. to bring together (2)v. to fit together the parts of
words.example_en: He has assembled a team of experts to handle queries.
words.example_zh: 他召集了一班专家来答疑解惑。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to take the job or position of (2)v. to force to leave the area where they live
words.example_en: These factories have displaced tourism as the country's largest source of foreign exchange.
words.example_zh: 这些工厂已取代旅游业成为该国最大的外汇来源。

words.definition_en: adj. capable of being used in place of each other
words.example_en: His greatest innovation was the use of interchangeable parts.
words.example_zh: 他最伟大的创新是使用了可互换部件。

words.definition_en: n. a great or foolish amount of pride or confidence
words.example_en: Hubris is the big worry.
words.example_zh: 傲慢是最大的隐患。

words.definition_en: v. to make confused or upset
words.example_en: He will be particularly discomfited by the minister's dismissal of his plan.
words.example_zh: 部长对他计划的不理会将使他特别尴尬。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by, held in, or conducted with secrecy
words.example_en: She is the director of clandestine operations of the CIA.
words.example_zh: 她是中央情报局秘密行动的负责人。

words.definition_en: n. a person who refuses to follow the customs or rules of a group
words.example_en: He was too much of a maverick ever to hold high office.
words.example_zh: 他太特立独行，永远不可能升职。

words.definition_en: adj. extremely important and necessary
words.example_en: Cars have become an indispensable part of our lives.
words.example_zh: 汽车已成了我们生活中必不可少的一部分。

words.definition_en: v. to have an inward conviction of (something, such as a coming ill or misfortune)
words.example_en: The poor girl saw a cloud rising in the horizon, which appeared to forebode a coming storm.
words.example_zh: 这位可怜的小女孩看见天上忽然上升起了一朵乌云，似乎预示一场暴风雨即将来临。

words.definition_en: adj. thinking carefully about something
words.example_en: Mary is a quiet, reflective man.
words.example_zh: 玛丽是一个文静、喜欢沉思的人。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to seize upon to the exclusion of others (2)v. to prevent from happening or taking place
words.example_en: They do not preempt the land surface that is useful for other purposes.
words.example_zh: 它们不需要预先占用对其它目的有用的地面。

words.definition_en: v. to present again in a slightly different form
words.example_en: He just rehashes songs from the 60s.
words.example_zh: 他只是把60年代的歌曲稍加改编而已。

words.definition_en: (1)n. sign, indication (2)v. to be a sign of (3)adj. very important or great
words.definition_zh: 信号，标志表明显著的，重要的
words.example_en: Kurdish leaders saw the visit as an important signal of support
words.example_zh: 库尔德领导人将这次访问视作一种表示支持的重要信号。

words.definition_en: v. to become thick and partly solid
words.example_en: A drug that stops blood from clotting during operations.
words.example_zh: 手术时防止血液凝结成块的药物。

words.definition_en: adj. impossible to see, hear, or know clearly
words.example_en: The transition was almost indiscernible.
words.example_zh: 那转变几乎不能察觉。

words.definition_en: v. to reduce or limit
words.example_en: NATO plans to curtail the number of troops being sent to the region.
words.example_zh: 北约计划缩减派往该地区的士兵数量。

words.definition_en: v. to stir up
words.example_en: Times of national turmoil generally roil a country's financial markets.
words.example_zh: 在国家动荡不安的时代，该国的金融市场一般都会出现混乱。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by propriety and good taste
words.example_en: They go for decorous walks every day in parks with their nanny.
words.example_zh: 他们每天都和保姆一起在公园里散步，表现得很有教养。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not likely to offend or upset anyone (2)adj. serving to alleviate pain
words.example_en: Through it all, there remained an anodyne constancy about the songs.
words.example_zh: 经历过风风雨雨，音乐始终是惠特妮·休斯顿永恒不变的止痛药。

words.definition_en: adj. fully or abundantly provided or filled
words.example_en: The Harbor was replete with boats.
words.example_zh: 港口里满是船只。

words.definition_en: n. a brief summary of a larger work or of a field of knowledge
words.example_en: The Roman Catholic Church has issued a compendium of its teachings.
words.example_zh: 罗马天主教会已经发行了一份其教义的概略。

words.definition_en: v. to describe in a false way especially in order to deceive someone
words.example_en: The press had misrepresented him as arrogant and bullying.
words.example_zh: 新闻界把他曲解成了一个傲慢霸道的人。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. resolute, determined (2)adj. unmistakable, unquestionable
words.example_en: This is a time of decisive action and quick thinking.
words.example_zh: 这个时候应该果断行动、快速反应。

words.definition_en: adj. specified, fixed
words.example_en: Over a given period, the value of shares will rise and fall.
words.example_zh: 股票的价值在某一特定的时期内会有涨跌。

words.definition_en: n. the main support or major sustaining factor
words.example_en: The youths are the backbones of the country.
words.example_zh: 年轻人是国家的栋梁。

words.definition_en: adj. suited to the occasion, purpose, or person
words.example_en: I felt it was not seemly to observe too closely.
words.example_zh: 我觉得凑得太近去观察不合适。

words.definition_en: adj. thinking carefully about possible risks before doing or saying something
words.example_en: The officials were very circumspect in their statements.
words.example_zh: 官员们说话非常谨慎。

words.definition_en: adj. offensive to the senses and especially to sight
words.example_en: She saw a hideous face at the window and screamed.
words.example_zh: 她在窗口看到了一张极丑陋的脸，吓得尖叫了起来。

words.definition_en: adj. done or happening at the right time
words.example_en: The timing of our statement is very opportune.
words.example_zh: 我们发表声明选择的时机很恰当。

words.definition_en: n. too much of something
words.example_en: The glut of coffee led to a sharp drop in prices.
words.example_zh: 咖啡供过于求道致价格急剧下跌。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to repeat or stress too much or too often (2)v. to attack or criticize
words.example_en: I don't want to belabor the point, but it is vital you understand how important this is.
words.example_zh: 我并不想啰嗦，但至关重要的是你得理解这件事的重要性

words.definition_en: v. to drop or slide down suddenly
words.example_en: Sales have slumped this year.
words.example_zh: 今年销售量锐减。

words.definition_en: adj. of, relating to, or being the basic or essential constituent of something
words.example_en: Learning to control one of the most elemental forces in nature is a pivotal mo ent in any child's personal history.
words.example_zh: 学会如何控制自然界中这一最基本的力量是每一个孩子个人成长历史中的关键时刻。

words.definition_en: adj. being the first or earliest known of its kind present in a region
words.example_en: The lndians are the aboriginal Americans.
words.example_zh: 印第安人是美国的土著人.

words.definition_en: v. to withdraw from a place in an organized way especially for protection
words.example_en: They were planning to evacuate the seventy American officials still in the country.
words.example_zh: 他们当时在计划将仍在那个国家的70位美国官员撤离。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to happen more quickly
words.example_en: The treatment she received may, in fact, have hastened her death.
words.example_zh: 实际上，她所接受的治疗可能加快了她的死亡。

words.definition_en: v. to hold or keep with limits
words.example_en: At the sight of this cruelty, he could hardly contain his anger.
words.example_zh: 见此残暴情形，他无法克制自己的愤怒。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. offering a pleasant or sustaining environment (2)adj. promising or suggesting generous and friendly welcome
words.example_en: The local people are very hospitable to strangers.
words.example_zh: 当地人对外来客人十分友好热情。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to bring into use in one's own country (2)v. to adapt (an animal or plant) over time from a wild or natural state especially by selective breeding to life in close association with and to the benefit of humans
words.example_en: We domesticated the dog to help us with hunting.
words.example_zh: 我们驯狗来帮我们打猎。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. dominant in position or influence (2)adj. moving upward
words.example_en: The reform movement is once again ascendant.
words.example_zh: 改革运动再次占据主导地位。

words.definition_en: (1)n. distinguished bravery (2)n. extraordinary ability
words.example_en: They tend to exaggerate their prowess and even the number of troops they have.
words.example_zh: 他们总是夸大自己的勇猛，甚至还续报己方的士兵数目。

words.definition_en: v. to ooze out as if under pressure
words.example_en: The volcano spewed out more scorching volcanic ashes, gases and rocks.
words.example_zh: 火山喷出更多灼热的火山灰、气体和岩块。

words.definition_en: adj. having the same connotations, implications, or reference
