words.example_en: Paris has always been synonymous with elegance, luxury and style.
words.example_zh: 巴黎一直是优雅、奢华和时尚的代名词。

words.definition_en: adj. too eager to help or obey someone important
words.example_en: He looked at the two ladies with an obsequious air.
words.example_zh: 他看着两位太太，满脸谄媚的神情.

words.definition_en: v. to repair, adjust, or experiment with
words.example_en: It is not enough to tinker at the edges; our objective must be to reconstruct the entire system.
words.example_zh: 只在外缘做一些小修小补是不够的，我们的目标必须是重建整个系统。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by often imperious or arrogant self-assurance
words.example_en: With a brief, almost peremptory gesture he pointed to a chair.
words.example_zh: 他做了一个简单的手势，近乎霸道地指着椅子。

words.definition_en: n. a rude or insensitive person
words.example_en: I'm a bit of a boor, so I hope you won't mind if I speak bluntly.
words.example_zh: 我是一个粗人，说话直来直去，你可别见怪。

words.definition_en: v. trouble, harass
words.example_en: The country is beset by severe economic problems.
words.example_zh: 这个国家被严重的经济问题所困扰。

words.definition_en: n. recompense, pay
words.example_en: He received a generous remuneration for his services.
words.example_zh: 他收到一笔丰厚的劳务酬金。

words.definition_en: n. the place or general locality where a person or thing is
words.example_en: The police are anxious to hear from anyone who may know the whereabouts of the firearms.
words.example_zh: 警察急于从任何可能知道那些枪支下落的人那里得到消息。

words.definition_en: adj. pretentious, presumptuous
words.example_en: An assuming manner irritates most people.
words.example_zh: 傲慢的态度使多数人感到不快。

words.definition_en: adj. capable of being held, maintained, or defended
words.example_en: The old idea that this work was not suitable for women was no longer tenable.
words.example_zh: 认为这种工作不适合妇女的旧想法再也站不住脚了。

words.definition_en: n. a specific causative agent (such as a bacterium or virus) of disease
words.example_en: The reservoir of this pathogen appears to be mainly cattle and other ruminants such as camels.
words.example_zh: 这一病菌的贮主看来主要是家畜和其它反刍动物，例如骆驼。

words.definition_en: v. to deprive of courage or confidence
words.example_en: Clearly, one of the objectives is to demoralize the enemy troops in any way they can.
words.example_zh: 很明显，目标之一就是要用一切可能的手段打击敌军的士气。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a historical account of events arranged in order of time usually without analysis or interpretation (2)v. to present a record of
words.example_en: Her latest novel is a chronicle of life in a Devon village.
words.example_zh: 她的最近一部小说是德文郡一个小村庄的生活记事。

words.definition_en: v. to introduce (something, such as an idea) gradually or in a subtle, indirect, or covert way
words.example_en: The libel claim followed an article which insinuated that the President was lying.
words.example_zh: 一篇文章因含沙射影声称总统撒谎而遭到诽谤索赔。

words.definition_en: adj. having a surface luster or brightness
words.example_en: The leaves were dark and glossy.
words.example_zh: 树叶颜色深暗，颇有光泽。

words.definition_en: v. to incite to action or accelerated growth or development
words.example_en: It's the money that spurs these fishermen to risk a long ocean journey in their flimsy boats
words.example_zh: 是金钱驱使这些渔民驾着破旧小船冒险出海远航。

words.definition_en: v. to dishonestly take money from a business, account etc to use it for a purpose for which it was not intended
words.example_en: She has been accused of siphoning off thousands of pounds from the company into her own bank account.
words.example_zh: 她被指控把公司的几千英镑转移到了自己的账户里。

words.definition_en: adj. always wanting more
words.example_en: The public has an insatiable appetite for stories about the famous
words.example_zh: 公众对名人有着难以满足的好奇心。

words.definition_en: v. to think about and revolve in the mind beforehand
words.example_en: The happiness is a kind of very spoiled thing, it can't premeditate , can't store, can't be make by will.
words.example_zh: 快乐是一种很娇贵的东西，它不能预谋，不能储备，不能靠意志来制造。

words.definition_en: adj. essentially similar, related, or compatible
words.example_en: Listening to his life story is akin to reading a good adventure novel.
words.example_zh: 听他的人生故事犹如阅读一本精彩的冒险小说。

words.definition_en: v. to unite or make firm by or as if by cement
words.example_en: Nothing cements a friendship between countries so much as trade.
words.example_zh: 贸易最能增强国家间的友谊。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. restrained in words and actions (2)adj. kept or set apart or aside for future or special use
words.example_en: He was unemotional, quite quiet, and reserved.
words.example_zh: 他感情淡漠，沉默寡言，性格内敛。

words.definition_en: v. to have a very strong desire for
words.example_en: We crave to have more of those delicious moments we may have had with a romantic partner.
words.example_zh: 我们渴望能与爱情伴侣一起拥有更多这样美好的时刻。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by friendly goodwill
words.example_en: The two countries made settlement by amicable arrangement.
words.example_zh: 两国以友好调解解决争端。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to utter taunting words (2)v. to be in accord
words.example_en: His account does not jibe with the evidence.
words.example_zh: 他的说法与证据不一致。

words.definition_en: v. to list or rate (projects, goals, etc.) in order of priority
words.example_en: The government is prioritising the service sector, rather than investing in industry and production.
words.example_zh: 政府将优先投资服务行业，而非工业和生产行业。

words.definition_en: n. one who insists on exactness or completeness in the observance of something
words.example_en: Lucy was a stickler for perfection, and everything had to be exactly right, rehearsed down to a T.
words.example_zh: 露西什么都追求十全十美，样样都不能出一点问题，要准备得滴水不漏。

words.definition_en: adj. producing or characterized by gratification of the senses
words.example_en: I'm drawn to the poetic, sensuous qualities of her paintings.
words.example_zh: 我喜欢她的画中那种充满诗意、赏心悦目的特性。

words.definition_en: v. to give up
words.example_en: He was forced to relinquish control of the company.
words.example_zh: 他被迫放弃对公司的控制权。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing knowledge that is learned by studying
words.example_en: Even the most erudite native speaker does not know the meaning of every word.
words.example_zh: 就算最博学的母语使用者也不可能知道每个字的意思。

words.definition_en: adj. overstepping due bounds
words.example_en: It would be presumptuous to judge what the outcome will be.
words.example_zh: 现在就判断结果将会怎样未免有些冒昧。

words.definition_en: n. a division among the members of a group that occurs because they disagree on something
words.example_en: The church seems to be on the brink of schism.
words.example_zh: 教会似乎处于分裂的边缘。

words.definition_en: v. to get back again
words.example_en: The police have managed to retrieve some of the stolen money.
words.example_zh: 警方已经追回了部分被盗钱款。

words.definition_en: v. to soothe or content (someone) by indulgence
words.example_en: He told me to change my office layout, and I did it, mostly to humor my wife.
words.example_zh: 他要我改变办公室的格局，我照做了，多半是为了迎合我太太

words.definition_en: adj. very busy or full of activity
words.example_en: My life was very hectic but empty before I met him.
words.example_zh: 在遇到他之前，我的生活非常繁忙却很空虚。

words.definition_en: n. something that naturally follows or results from another thing
words.example_en: The corollary of this simplistic analysis is that if reforms stop now, so will growth.
words.example_zh: 根据这种过分单纯的分析得出的推论是，如果现在停止改革，那么经济增长也将停顿。

words.definition_en: v. to make something less boring, serious, or sad
words.example_en: Her speech was leavened with a touch of humour.
words.example_zh: 几分幽默使她的讲话更为有趣。

words.definition_en: n. harsh or discordant sound
words.example_en: We can always hear a cacophony of horns during the traffic jam.
words.example_zh: 交通阻塞时，我们总能听到一片不和谐的喇叭声。

words.definition_en: adj. different from each other
words.example_en: The nine republics are immensely disparate in size, culture and wealth.
words.example_zh: 这9个共和国在面积、文化和财富上迥然不同。

words.definition_en: adj. deserving attention, admiration, or respect
words.definition_zh: 令人敬畏的，值得尊敬的
words.example_en: While some of his peers might quarrel with the title, his credentials remain impressive.
words.example_zh: 尽管他的一些同龄人也许并不认同这个称号，他的资历仍然令人钦佩不已。

words.definition_en: adj. not harsh, severe, or strict
words.example_en: He believes the government already is lenient with drug traffickers.
words.example_zh: 他认为政府对毒贩已经很宽容了。

words.definition_en: adj. difficult or impossible to reach, approach, or understand
words.definition_zh: 难以到达的，难以理解的
words.example_en: The route took us through scenery quite inaccessible to the motorist.
words.example_zh: 这条路线带我们穿越了驾车者难以抵达的风景区。

words.definition_en: v. to take the place of
words.example_en: By the 1930s the wristwatch had almost completely supplanted the pocket watch.
words.example_zh: 到了20世纪30年代，手表几乎完全取代了怀表。

words.definition_en: n. the outline or outer edge of something
words.example_en: She traced the contours of his face with her finger.
words.example_zh: 她用手指描绘他的脸部轮廓。

words.definition_en: adj. not able to be stopped or changed
words.example_en: His own career continues its inexorable ascent
words.example_zh: 他事业的上升势头锐不可当。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to the worldly or temporal
words.example_en: Cardinal Daly has said that churches should not be used for profane or secular purposes.
words.example_zh: 戴利主教已经说过，教堂不应该用于非宗教或世俗的目的。

words.definition_en: adj. being tough, stubborn, or uncompromising
words.example_en: My uncle was a hard - nosed army officer who had seen service in two wars.
words.example_zh: 我叔叔是位性格坚韧的军医,他曾身经两次大战.

words.definition_en: v. to keep within limits
words.example_en: He did not confine himself to the one language.
words.example_zh: 他没把自己局限于这一门语言。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to cause confusion in (2)n. a state of confusion or disorder
words.example_en: Later they may muddle up your names with those of your cousins.
words.example_zh: 以后他们可能会把你的名字跟你堂兄弟们的名字相混淆。

words.definition_en: n. a concealed or unexpected difficulty or obstacle
words.example_en: The attack hit a snag and made no headway.
words.example_zh: 进攻受阻，攻不进去。

words.definition_en: adj. imaginable or possible
words.example_en: Through the centuries, flowers have been used for cooking in every conceivable way.
words.example_zh: 几个世纪以来，人们以各种想象得到的方式将鲜花入菜。

words.definition_en: v. to confront with assured power of resistance
words.example_en: The towering pine and cypress trees defy frost and snow.
words.example_zh: 松柏参天傲霜雪。

words.definition_en: n. a person having a superficial interest in an art or a branch of knowledge
words.example_en: Dilettante watch the scene of bustle, adept guard the entrance.
words.example_zh: 外行看热闹, 内行看门道。

words.definition_en: adj. acting or intended to enhance one's power, wealth, position, or reputation
words.definition_zh: 自我扩张的（仅用于抽象事物）
words.example_en: Desperate Local families firmly believe Lakwena's self-aggrandizing claims to supernatural power, they begged her for help.
words.example_zh: 当地绝望的家庭，迷信于拉奎那自我吹嘘的超能力，向她祈求帮助。

words.definition_en: n. skillful handling of a situation : adroit maneuvering
words.example_en: The chairman managed that situation with great finesse.
words.example_zh: 主席应付那件事情很有手段。

words.definition_en: adj. capable of being used or dealt with successfully
words.example_en: She questioned whether it was feasible to stimulate investment in these regions.
words.example_zh: 她对在这些地区刺激投资是否可行表示怀疑。

words.definition_en: n. something that causes great destruction, violence, etc.
words.example_en: The Great Depression was an economic cataclysm on a scale hard to imagine.
words.example_zh: 大萧条是一场规模难以想像的经济灾难。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. capable of being discovered or perceived (2)adj. conspicuous
words.example_en: Her hand was just visible by the light from the sitting room.
words.example_zh: 借着起居室的灯光，勉强能看见她的手。

words.definition_en: adj. beginning to come into being or to become apparent
words.example_en: She felt for him an incipient tenderness, but scarcely any passion.
words.example_zh: 她对他的好感只是一种刚刚萌芽的柔情, 还根本谈不上什么爱情。

words.definition_en: n. an impression or view of something brief or transitory
words.example_en: Let me take a snapshot of you guys, so friends back home can see you.
words.example_zh: 让我给你们大家拍张快照，这样家里的朋友们可以看到你们。

words.definition_en: adj. expending or bestowing profusely
words.example_en: He drew criticism for his lavish spending.
words.example_zh: 他因挥霍无度而招致批评。

words.definition_en: adj. exceeding the customary or appropriate limits in intensity, quality, amount, or size
words.example_en: That seaside hotel charges exorbitant prices during the summer holidays.
words.example_zh: 那家海滨旅馆在夏天休假期间漫天要价。

words.definition_en: adj. very great in size or amount
words.example_en: He saved up an enormous amount of money for his daughter.
words.example_zh: 他为女儿存了一大笔钱。

words.definition_en: v. to examine one's own thoughts and feelings
words.example_en: Take as much time as you need to introspect upon the areas above.
words.example_zh: 对以上方面请花尽可能多的时间来思考和反省。

words.definition_en: adj. being out of proportion
words.example_en: The punishment was grossly disproportionate to the crime.
words.example_zh: 这种惩罚与罪行极不相称。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. fashioned from whatever is immediately available (2)adj. formed or used for a special purpose
words.example_en: For many companies, overhead reduction is the first line of attack when trying to reduce costs, yet many approach it in an ad hoc way.
words.example_zh: 许多公司谈及精简成本，首先想到的是从裁员着手，这只能称得上是临时措施。

words.definition_en: adj. pedestrian, itinerant
words.example_en: Her father was in the army and the family led a peripatetic existence.
words.example_zh: 她父亲是军人，所以全家人随军过着一种流动的生活。

words.definition_en: n. language that describes something as better or worse than it really is
words.example_en: American humor is founded largely on hyperbole.
words.example_zh: 美式幽默主要以夸张为基础.

words.definition_en: adj. suggesting that something bad is going to happen in the future
words.example_en: There was an ominous silence at the other end of the phone
words.example_zh: 电话那头出现了不祥的沉默。

words.definition_en: v. to make cheerful and excited
words.example_en: We were exhilarated by the cool, pine-scented air.
words.example_zh: 凉爽的、带有松香味的空气振作起我们的精神。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to make more important (2)n. an important position
words.definition_zh: 使重要，使显眼重要位置
words.example_en: The play foregrounds the relationship between father and daughter.
words.example_zh: 这部戏剧凸显了父女之间的关系。

words.definition_en: v. to entreat or appeal earnestly
words.example_en: She pleaded with him not to go.
words.example_zh: 她恳求他不要离开。

words.definition_en: v. to deprive of vital content or force
words.example_en: The censors eviscerated the book to make it inoffensive to the President.
words.example_zh: 审查员删去了该书的精华以取悦于总统。

words.definition_en: adj. separate and different from each other
words.example_en: Social structures are not discrete objects; they overlap and interweave.
words.example_zh: 社会结构之间不是离散的，他们相互重叠交织。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. tending to catch the interest or attention (2)adj. tricky
words.definition_zh: 引人注意的使人上当的
words.example_en: If you can think of a catchy domain name, get it registered for less than $10.
words.example_zh: 如果你能想到一个引人注目的域名，注册费用不到 10 美元。

words.definition_en: v. to diminish or destroy by degrees
words.example_en: The cliffs on this coast have been eroded by the sea.
words.example_zh: 该海岸上的峭壁受到了海水的侵蚀。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to fluctuate or veer between one point, position, or opinion and another (2)v. to exert a building or controlling influence on
words.example_en: The branches were swaying in the wind.
words.example_zh: 树枝在风中摇曳。

words.definition_en: n. a cause or occasion of enticement
words.example_en: I couldn't resist the temptation to open the letter.
words.example_zh: 我控制不住诱惑把信打开了。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by incisive sarcasm
words.example_en: He delights in making caustic remarks.
words.example_zh: 他说话刻薄，还以此为乐。

words.definition_en: v. to give or be a sign of
words.example_en: The incident is believed to presage war.
words.example_zh: 这件事件据信是战争的前兆。

words.definition_en: adj. matching or in agreement with something
words.example_en: Our bilateral economic relationship is anchored in the realization that our long-term interests are essentially congruent and mutually reinforcing.
words.example_zh: 我们的双边经济关系植根于两国的长期利益基本上是一致且相辅相成的。

words.definition_en: adj. violently hostile or aggressive in temperament
words.example_en: They look like the teeth of some fierce animal.
words.example_zh: 它们看上去像某种猛兽的牙齿。

words.definition_en: (1)n. an insulting or disparaging remark or innuendo (2)v. to make indistinct
words.example_en: She had dared to cast a slur on his character.
words.example_zh: 她竟敢对他的人品加以诋毁。

words.definition_en: v. to make fun of
words.example_en: He teased me mercilessly about going Hollywood.
words.example_zh: 他无情地取笑我想去好莱坞的想法。

words.definition_en: adj. feeling or showing a lack of interest or enthusiasm
words.example_en: Joanna had made one or two half-hearted attempts to befriend Graham's young wife.
words.example_zh: 乔安娜曾有一两次敷衍着去亲近格雷厄姆年轻的妻子。

words.definition_en: adj. made up of parts that are different
words.example_en: America has a very heterogeneous population.
words.example_zh: 美国人口是由不同种族组成的。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. very cruel or unfair (2)adj. overwhelming or depressing to the spirit or senses
words.example_en: This country still had a deeply oppressive, unequal and divisive political system.
words.example_zh: 这个国家的政治体制依然残暴异常，缺少平等，四分五裂。

words.definition_en: v. to think deeply or carefully about
words.example_en: The young surgeon contemplated the difficult operation of kidney transplant.
words.example_zh: 年轻的外科医生为肾移植这一棘手的手术苦思冥想。

words.definition_en: adj. willing to allow excessive leniency, generosity, or consideration
words.example_en: His indulgent mother was willing to let him do anything he wanted.
words.example_zh: 他母亲很纵容他，任由他想干什么就干什么。

words.definition_en: n. something that is from a past time, place, culture, etc.
words.example_en: The building stands as the last remaining relic of the town's cotton industry.
words.example_zh: 这座建筑物是小镇棉纺业仅存的遗迹。

words.definition_en: v. to show in an improper or selfish way that you are happy with you own success or another person’s failure
words.example_en: Do not gloat when your enemy falls.
words.example_zh: 不要因为敌人的失败而幸灾乐祸。

words.definition_en: adj. possible to verify
words.example_en: This is not a romantic notion but verifiable fact.
words.example_zh: 这不是一个不切实际的念头，而是一个可以证明的事实。

words.definition_en: n. a state of intellectual or emotional balance
words.example_en: For the economy to be in equilibrium, income must equal expenditure.
words.example_zh: 经济要达到平衡，收支必须均衡。

words.definition_en: (1)n. something that you say, give, or do to show respect or affection for someone (2)n. money or goods that a ruler or country gives to another ruler or country especially for protection
words.example_en: At her funeral her oldest friend paid tribute to her life and work.
words.example_zh: 在葬礼上，她最早的朋友对她的一生和工作给予了高度的赞扬。

words.definition_en: adj. aiming for equal wealth, status, etc.
words.example_en: I still believe in the notion of an egalitarian society.
words.example_zh: 我仍然相信构建人人平等的社会的理念。

words.definition_en: adj. having an odd and amusing quality
words.example_en: The band have a droll sense of humor.
words.example_zh: 这个乐队有一种滑稽古怪的幽默感。

words.definition_en: n. a large amount of something
words.example_en: Olive groves, grapes, and citrus fruits grow in profusion.
words.example_zh: 橄榄、葡萄和柑橘类水果产量丰富。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. dull and ordinary (2)adj. relating to ordinary life on earth rather than to spiritual things
words.example_en: I found the job very mundane.
words.example_zh: 我觉得这工作非常单调。

words.definition_en: adj. impossible to understand
words.example_en: His feeling was confused and drifting, but deep unfathomable.
words.example_zh: 这思绪是散乱而飘浮的, 又是幽深而莫测的.

words.definition_en: v. to complain about peevishly
words.example_en: I would crab about sand in my food on picnics.
words.example_zh: 野餐时，我会为食物中有沙子而抱怨。

words.definition_en: adj. not straight, short, and direct
words.example_en: His explanation was circuitous and puzzling.
words.example_zh: 他的解释在兜圈子，让人费解。

words.definition_en: v. to bring into danger or peril
words.example_en: It is illegal and could endanger other people's lives.
words.example_zh: 这是非法的，可能会危及他人的生命。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. wary, careful (2)adj. crafty, shrewd
words.definition_zh: 小心的，谨慎的狡猾的，机敏的
words.example_en: The cagey fox could not be easily trapped by the farmer.
words.example_zh: 那只狡猾的狐狸不会轻易落入农夫的陷阱。

words.definition_en: v. conspire, plot
words.example_en: Several local officials are in jail on charges of colluding with the Mafia.
words.example_zh: 几位当地官员因被控勾结黑手党而入狱。

words.definition_en: (1)n. salient or essential element or meaning (2)v. to cause to enter or pierce something by or as if by pushing
words.example_en: It provides the thrust that makes the craft move forward.
words.example_zh: 它提供了飞机前进所需的推力。

words.definition_en: n. an indication of approval carrying great prestige
words.example_en: No other shipping company had quite the cachet of Cunard.
words.example_zh: 没哪家船运公司有肯纳德轮船公司这样的声望。

words.definition_en: v. to make someone too afraid to do something
words.example_en: I'm sure it was deliberately done to cow us.
words.example_zh: 我确信，这一事件经过精心策划，目的是恐吓我们。

words.definition_en: adj. dealing with or knowing a subject thoroughly or completely
words.example_en: She has an encyclopedic knowledge of natural history.
words.example_zh: 她具有广博的自然史知识。

words.definition_en: v. to give official authority or legal power to
words.example_en: The new law empowered the police to search private house.
words.example_zh: 新法律给予警察搜查私人住宅的权利。

words.definition_en: adj. simple or plain
words.example_en: He was a man of austere habits, in marked contrast to his more flamboyant wife.
words.example_zh: 他朴素的作风与其张扬的妻子形成了鲜明的对比。

words.definition_en: adj. not truly honest or sincere
words.example_en: It would be disingenuous of me to claim I had never seen it.
words.example_zh: 要说我从未看到过，那就是言不由衷了。

words.definition_en: v. to be thought about or worried about by (someone) very often or constantly
words.example_en: An intelligent man considers the prospect of failure, but he doesn't preoccupy himself with pointless worry.
words.example_zh: 一个聪明的人会可能失败的情况，但他绝不让自己专注在无谓的担心之中。

words.definition_en: adj. not clear
words.example_en: The notions we children were able to form of the great world beyond were exceedingly nebulous.
words.example_zh: 我们这些孩子对外部世界所能形成的认识是非常模糊的。

words.definition_en: n. the development of friendlier relations between countries or groups of people who have been enemies
words.example_en: We will not make UN entry a precondition for cross-strait rapprochement.
words.example_zh: 我们不会将进入联合国作为两岸和解的先决条件。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. extremely or unusually large (2)adj. extraordinarily ugly or vicious
words.example_en: The killer committed monstrous acts.
words.example_zh: 那个嗜杀成性的凶手犯下了骇人听闻的罪行。

words.definition_en: adj. being in a position to incur
words.example_en: Children are liable to catch cold.
words.example_zh: 小孩易患感冒。

words.definition_en: adj. extending in scope or effect to a prior time or to conditions that existed or originated in the past
words.example_en: There are few precedents for this sort of retroactive legislation.
words.example_zh: 这种有追溯力的法规鲜有先例。

words.definition_en: v. to take the goods of by force (as in war)
words.example_en: They plundered and burned the market town of Leominster.
words.example_zh: 他们洗劫并焚烧了集镇莱姆斯特。

words.definition_en: v. undermine
words.example_en: Analysts say the recession in Japan has sapped investor confidence.
words.example_zh: 分析家称日本的经济衰退已经打击了投资者的信心。

words.definition_en: (1)n. the use of something (2)n. the utilization of economic goods in the satisfaction of wants or in the process of production resulting chiefly in their destruction, deterioration, or transformation
words.example_en: Energy consumption rises as countries industrialize.
words.example_zh: 随着各国实现工业化，能源消费增加了。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to a whole group or class
words.example_en: Parmesan is a generic term used to describe a family of hard Italian cheeses.
words.example_zh: 帕尔玛干酪是意大利硬奶酪的通称。

words.definition_en: n. harshness of behavior or speech that expresses bitterness or anger
words.example_en: He spoke to the boy with asperity.
words.example_zh: 他严厉地对那男孩讲话。

words.definition_en: adj. given or provided abundantly
words.example_en: Mr Yang has a bountiful experience working with education.
words.example_zh: 杨先生有着丰富的教育工作经验。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. full of anger and disagreement (2)adj. hard to manage or control
words.example_en: Children often get fractious and tearful when tired.
words.example_zh: 孩子们疲倦时易烦躁好哭。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing no understanding of what is proper or acceptable
words.example_en: The government has behaved with crass insensitivity.
words.example_zh: 该政府行事愚蠢而且麻木不仁。

words.definition_en: n. a state in which all activity or motion is stopped
words.example_en: Argentina came to a virtual standstill while the game was being played.
words.example_zh: 在比赛进行的时候，阿根廷全国几乎陷入了停顿状态。

words.definition_en: v. to produce especially as an effect or outgrowth
words.example_en: War beget misery and ruin.
words.example_zh: 战争导致苦难与毁灭。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by very widespread growth or extent
words.example_en: Influenza reached epidemic proportions in Moscow.
words.example_zh: 流感在莫斯科已达到大规模流行的程度。

words.definition_en: adj. able to be understood
words.example_en: The language of Darwin was intelligible to experts and non-experts alike.
words.example_zh: 达尔文的语言对专家和平常人来说都容易理解。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to moderate the intensity of (2)v. the cover by excuses and apologies
words.example_en: I shall never attempt to palliate my own foibles by exposing the error of another.
words.example_zh: 我从不想暴露别人的错误,以图掩饰自己的缺点。

words.definition_en: n. alternating change
words.example_en: The vicissitudes of life may suddenly make a rich man very poor.
words.example_zh: 在人世的盛衰变迁中, 一个有钱人突然也会变成穷光蛋。

words.definition_en: n. exemption or freedom from punishment, harm, or loss
words.example_en: These gangs operate with apparent impunity.
words.example_zh: 这些团伙明目张胆地活动,却不受任何惩罚。

words.definition_en: adj. blatantly and disdainfully proud
words.example_en: He spoke in a haughty tone.
words.example_zh: 他说起话来口气傲慢。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to sprout or develop
words.example_en: An idea for a novel began to germinate in her mind.
words.example_zh: 一部小说的构思已经在她的头脑中萌发。

words.definition_en: adj. poised and serene especially in the face of setbacks or confusion
words.example_en: Mr Obama seems unruffled.
words.example_zh: 欧巴马还是显得不慌不忙。

words.definition_en: adj. politely pleasant and friendly
words.example_en: The conversation was carried on in a cordial and friendly atmosphere.
words.example_zh: 会谈是在亲切友好的气氛中进行的。

words.definition_en: (1)n. an indication of future success or improvement (2)n. a reason to expect that something will happen in the future
words.example_en: The boy first showed promise as an athlete in grade school.
words.example_zh: 这个男孩最初是在小学显示出成为一名成功的运动员的迹象。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to become smaller (2)n. a legal agreement between people, companies, etc. (3)v. to become affected with
words.example_en: Blood is only expelled from the heart when it contracts.
words.example_zh: 血液在心脏收缩的时候从心脏流出。

words.definition_en: adj. deliberately affected: overly dramatic or emotional
words.example_en: Fortune or misfortune, I feel this world is full of histrionic.
words.example_zh: 无论幸运或不幸时，我都觉得这世界充满了戏剧性。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by lack of interest, energy, or spirit
words.example_en: The illness left her feeling listless and depressed.
words.example_zh: 那场病使她感到虚弱无力，提不起精神。

words.definition_en: adj. happy and satisfied
words.example_en: Whenever he returns to this place he is happy and contented.
words.example_zh: 每次回到这个地方，他都十分快乐，心满意足。

words.definition_en: adj. easy to see or understand
words.example_en: we at least have a well-defined system of contributions and benefits.
words.example_zh: 我们至少有一个定义明确的缴付款项和享受福利的系统。

words.definition_en: n. an account of the descent of a person, family, or group from an ancestor or from older forms
words.example_en: Darwin argued that this kind of similarity — known as homology — was just a matter of genealogy.
words.example_zh: 达尔文认为这种相似性是物种之间的宗谱关系的结果。

words.definition_en: n. a large number or amount
words.example_en: Refugees crossed the border in full spate.
words.example_zh: 大量的难民越过了边境。

words.definition_en: v. to renounce allegiance or subjection
words.example_en: Finally the people revolted against the military dictatorship.
words.example_zh: 人民最终起来反抗军事独裁。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a record of how well you have paid your bills in the past (2)v. to give honor or recognition to for doing something or for making something happen
words.example_en: The credit card business is down, and more borrowers are defaulting onloans
words.example_zh: .信用卡业务出现了下滑，而且越来越多的借款者都不按期还款。

words.definition_en: v. to introduce something into the mind of
words.example_en: Today many parents inoculate their children with the idea only the students graduate from the famous universities will have good future.
words.example_zh: 当今很多家长给孩子灌输这样的思想：只有在名牌大学毕业的学生将来才有好前途。

words.definition_en: v. to release or deliver from some liability or requirement to which others are subject
words.example_en: The law exempts soldiers from paying local taxes.
words.example_zh: 该法律免除士兵缴纳地方税的义务。

words.definition_en: adj. desirous to the point of greed
words.example_en: She was avid for praise from her coach.
words.example_zh: 那女孩渴望得到教练的称赞.

words.definition_en: adj. crafty
words.example_en: His appointment as prime minister owed much to the wily manoeuvring of the President.
words.example_zh: 他之所以被任命为总理，很大程度上是因为总统在阴谋操纵。

words.definition_en: adj. very bad and easily noticed
words.example_en: In a few cases, such egregious online behavior has led to their dismissal from medical school.
words.example_zh: 在少数情况下，这种恶劣的上网行为会导致他们被医学院开除。

words.definition_en: adj. very large or great
words.example_en: He brought a tremendous infusion of hope to the people.
words.example_zh: 他给人们带来巨大希望。

words.definition_en: adj. arousing or appealing to sexual desire or imagination
words.example_en: The newspapers once again filled their columns with salacious details.
words.example_zh: 赤裸裸的猥亵细节再度充斥了报纸的版面。

words.definition_en: adj. wonderful, marvelous
words.example_en: The scenery and weather were fabulous.
words.example_zh: 景色和天气都好极了。

words.definition_en: adj. differing from each other or from a standard
words.example_en: China's advertising and McDonald's are widely divergent.
words.example_zh: 中国的广告和麦当劳大相径庭。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing careful good judgment
words.example_en: It might be more prudent to get a second opinion before going ahead.
words.example_zh: 行动之前再征求一下意见也许更为慎重。

words.definition_en: n. the leading actor or principal character in a television show, movie, book, etc.
words.example_en: She has created a dashing young man as the protagonist of her new novel.
words.example_zh: 她在她的新小说中创造了一个精力充沛的年轻人作主角。

words.definition_en: adj. extremely violent
words.example_en: The party has distanced itself from the more rabid nationalist groups in the country.
words.example_zh: 此党派与国内较偏激的民族主义团体保持了距离。

words.definition_en: v. to arrange or direct the movements, progress, or detail of
words.example_en: There was some carefully choreographed flag-waving as the President drove by.
words.example_zh: 总统的车经过时，人们按精心编排的动作挥舞着旗帜。

words.definition_en: (1)n. punishment for breaking a rule or law (2)n. a disadvantage or difficulty you experience
words.example_en: The maximum penalty is up to 7 years imprisonment or an unlimited fine.
words.example_zh: 最高处罚是最多7年监禁或者无上限罚款。

words.definition_en: adj. having or consisting of one color or hue
words.example_en: The resolution of white light into its separate monochromatic components is called dispersion.
words.example_zh: 这种白光分解为各单色成分的现象叫做色散。

words.definition_en: v. to mutter in discontent
words.example_en: There is nothing like a rise in daily expenses to make people grumble, but this is a particular problem in China.
words.example_zh: 没有什么比日常开销增大更能引起人们的抱怨，不过在中国，这个问题有所不同。

words.definition_en: v. to make someone do something by using force or threats
words.example_en: Potter had argued that the government coerced him into pleading guilty.
words.example_zh: 波特辩称是政府强迫他认罪的。

words.definition_en: v. to damage seriously
words.example_en: Our camping trip was spoilt by bad weather.
words.example_zh: 天气不好，破坏了我们的露营旅行。

words.definition_en: v. to draw in by breathing
words.example_en: Local residents needed hospital treatment after inhaling fumes from the fire.
words.example_zh: 当地居民吸入了大火的浓烟，需要入院治疗。

words.definition_en: v. to free from restraint, control, or the power of another
words.example_en: This new machine will emancipate us from the hard work.
words.example_zh: 这部新机器将把我们从繁重劳动中解放出来。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by often extreme fervor
words.example_en: He is a fervid orator.
words.example_zh: 他是个慷慨激昂的演说者。

words.definition_en: v. to make less severe or extreme
words.example_en: If you temper criticism with reason everyone can obey you.
words.example_zh: 如果以说理来缓和批评，谁都会服从你。

words.definition_en: adj. not causing happiness or warm feelings
words.example_en: It is cold and cheerless in the yard with no one there.
words.example_zh: 院子里冷冷清清，一个人也没有。

words.definition_en: n. the act of giving money, food, or other kinks of help to people who are poor, sick, etc.
words.example_en: Most of the runners in the London Marathon are raising money for charity.
words.example_zh: 大多数人参加伦敦马拉松赛跑是为慈善事业募集资金。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. capable of causing infection (2)adj. spreading or capable of spreading rapidly to others
words.example_en: These viruses affect children and are highly infectious.
words.example_zh: 这些病毒会侵袭儿童，而且具有很强的传染性。

words.definition_en: adj. very great in size or amount
words.example_en: We are not in this to reap immense financial rewards.
words.example_zh: 我们从事这一行不是为了获得巨大的经济回报。

words.definition_en: adj. impressively high or great
words.example_en: This town is beset with towering mountains.
words.example_zh: 这座城市为高山所环抱。

words.definition_en: (1)n. very bad events or situations that cannot be avoided (2)v. to make certain to fail, suffer, die, etc.
words.example_en: The chairman insisted that all was not doom and gloom.
words.example_zh: 主席坚持说不是所有的事情都是消极和注定失败的。

words.definition_en: v. to stray especially from a standard, principle, or topic
words.example_en: On this occasion the plane deviated from its usual flight path.
words.example_zh: 这一次那架飞机偏离了正常的航线。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by affectation of grandeur or splendor or by absurd exaggeration
words.example_en: Not one of Tom's grandiose plans has even begun.
words.example_zh: 汤姆那些华而不实的计划甚至一个都还没有开始实施。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by or given to vehement insistent outcry
words.example_en: The crowds at stations were growing enormous, their greetings more vociferous.
words.example_zh: 车站上的人群越聚越多，他们欢迎和问候的话音更加喧嚣。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a strongly condemnatory utterance (2)n. the condition of one that is discredited
words.example_en: I have had enough obloquy for one lifetime.
words.example_zh: 我一辈子受够了诽谤。

words.definition_en: adj. not genuine or real
words.example_en: He accepted the invitation with feigned enthusiasm.
words.example_zh: 他假装热情地接受了邀请。

words.definition_en: adj. relating to the human race's actual life on this earth
words.example_en: He preached the renunciation of all earthly pleasures.
words.example_zh: 他鼓吹对所有的世俗享乐的摒弃。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a figure of speech in which a word or phrase literally denoting one kind of object or idea is used in place of another to suggest a likeness or analogy between them (2)n. an object, activity, or idea that is used as a symbol of something else
words.example_en: In poetry the rose is often a metaphor for love.
words.example_zh: 诗歌中常用玫瑰花来隐喻爱情。

words.definition_en: n. respect for someone or something
words.example_en: He made a deep obeisance to the queen.
words.example_zh: 他向女王深深鞠了一躬。

words.definition_en: n. a period of new growth or activity
words.example_en: Popular art is experiencing a renaissance.
words.example_zh: 通俗艺术正在复兴。

words.definition_en: n. an opponent who is easy to defeat or a victim who is capable of no effective resistance
words.example_en: He is a tough negotiator. We did not expect to find him a pushover and he has not been one.
words.example_zh: 他是个难对付的谈判者。我们没指望说服他，而且也没说服他。

words.definition_en: v. to renounce a throne, high office, dignity, or function
words.example_en: He abdicated all responsibility for the care of the child.
words.example_zh: 他放弃了抚养孩子的全部责任。

words.definition_en: adj. having or seeming to have no end
words.example_en: The play dragged on, and seemed interminable.
words.example_zh: 演出拖拖拉拉地进行着，仿佛没完没了。

words.definition_en: n. a peculiar or personal point of view, attitude, or opinion
words.example_en: She put a new slant on the play.
words.example_zh: 她对那出戏提出了一种新的见解。

words.definition_en: v. to force or impose without warrant or request
words.example_en: You have better not obtrude your opinion upon others.
words.example_zh: 你最好不要强使人接受你的意见。

words.definition_en: adj. not being harmed or spoiled
words.example_en: Be sure to select firm, unblemished fruit.
words.example_zh: 一定要选坚实、无疤痕的果子

words.definition_en: v. to make compensation to for incurred hurt, loss, or damage
words.example_en: They agreed to indemnify the taxpayers against any loss
words.example_zh: 他们同意补偿纳税人的任何损失。

words.definition_en: adj. of much importance or consequence
words.example_en: He wants to shrink the weighty state subsidy that Whitehall gives to Northern Ireland.
words.example_zh: 他希望能够将政府给予北爱的重大国家补贴予以压缩。

words.definition_en: adj. used to describe something that is done without energy or enthusiasm because of habit or because it is expected
words.example_en: The lazy little boy gave his face a perfunctory washing.
words.example_zh: 那懒惰的小男孩草草地把脸洗了一下。

words.definition_en: (1)v. affording, receiving, or sharing nurture or parental care though not related by blood or legal ties (2)v. to help grow or develop
words.example_en: The club's aim is to foster better relations within the community.
words.example_zh: 俱乐部的宗旨是促进团体内部的关系。

words.definition_en: v. to become larger and rounder because of pressure from inside
words.example_en: Air is introduced into the stomach to distend it.
words.example_zh: 将空气导入胃部使之膨胀。

words.definition_en: n. an act of self-abasement, mortification, or devotion performed to show sorrow or repentance for sin
words.example_en: The Koran recommends fasting as a penance before pilgrimages.
words.example_zh: 《古兰经》劝告人们在朝圣前斋戒，以作为一种补赎。

words.definition_en: adj. producing a large amount of something
words.example_en: Farmers worked the fertile valleys.
words.example_zh: 农民们在肥沃的山谷里耕种。

words.definition_en: n. a memorable event or date
words.example_en: The birth of Christ was the beginning of a major epoch of world history.
words.example_zh: 基督诞生是世界历史上一个重要纪元的开端。

words.definition_en: adj. changing often and quickly
words.example_en: The Union accused Walesa of being capricious and undemocratic.
words.example_zh: 工会指责威尔沙反复无常，不讲民主。

words.definition_en: v. to be drawn or attracted especially by natural inclination
words.example_en: Most visitors to New York gravitate to Times Square.
words.example_zh: 大多数来纽约的游客都被吸引到时代广场。

words.definition_en: v. to make less severe, harmful, or painful
words.example_en: Governments should endeavor to mitigate distress.
words.example_zh: 政府应努力缓解贫困问题。

words.definition_en: v. to show that something is not true
words.example_en: His theories have been debunked by recent research.
words.example_zh: 最近的研究揭穿了他的理论的真相。

words.definition_en: v. to annoy persistently
words.example_en: She claims she has been sexually harassed at work.
words.example_zh: 她声称在工作中受到性骚扰。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to cause to correspond (2)v. to set in competition or opposition (3)n. an exact counterpart
words.definition_zh: 和...一致与...较量相配之物
words.example_en: The pillow cover can match up with the sheets.
words.example_zh: 这个枕巾可以和这些被单相配。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a connoisseur of food and drink (2)adj. of, relating to, or being high quality, expensive, or specialty food typically requiring elaborate and expert preparation
words.example_en: The seafood here is a gourmet's delight.
words.example_zh: 这儿的海鲜是美食家的一大享受。

words.definition_en: v. to enclose in or as if in a capsule
words.example_en: The electronic mail allows you to encapsulate your greetings with video images and sound as well as data.
words.example_zh: 可以用电子邮件把问候语与电视图像、声音、数据等压缩在一起。

words.definition_en: adj. wildly extravagant
words.example_en: Their profligate lifestyle resulted in bankruptcy.
words.example_zh: 他们挥霍的生活方式导致的破产.

words.definition_en: adj. marked by or given to offhand and often disdainful dismissal of important matters
words.example_en: The chief editor takes a cavalier attitude to the concept of fact checking.
words.example_zh: 总编辑对于核对事实持轻蔑的态度。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. obstinately and often perversely self-willed (2)adj. done deliberately
words.example_en: She was a willful child.
words.example_zh: 她是个任性的孩子。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by or full of clever humor or wit
words.example_en: He is a very witty speaker.
words.example_zh: 他是位置说话风趣的讲者。

words.definition_en: v. to praise something or someone in order to persuade people that they are important or worth a lot
words.example_en: She's being touted as the next leader of the party.
words.example_zh: 她被吹捧为该党的下一任领导人。

words.definition_en: n. someone who very strongly supports and is guided by the ideology of a particular group
words.definition_zh: 狂热支持某种理念的人
words.example_en: Nor is the author a left-wing ideologue.
words.example_zh: 作者也不是什么左翼理论支持者。

words.definition_en: n. the amount that remains when use or need is satisfied
words.example_en: Wheat was in surplus that year.
words.example_zh: 那一年小麦过剩。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to feel or show great love for (2)v. to remember or hold in a deeply felt way
words.example_en: I cherish for you the liveliest feeling of affection and gratitude.
words.example_zh: 我对你怀有最强烈的爱和感激之情。

words.definition_en: n. a feeling of being offended by what someone has said or done
words.example_en: He takes umbrage against anyone who criticizes him.
words.example_zh: 不管谁批评他，他都心生愤懑。

words.definition_en: adj. seeming to be seen everywhere
words.example_en: In the US, the camcorder has become ubiquitous
words.example_zh: 便携式摄录机在美国随处可见。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. able to move quickly, easily, and lightly (2)adj. able to learn and understand things quickly and easily
words.example_en: A nimble mind backed by a degree in economics gave him a firm grasp of financial matters.
words.example_zh: 机敏的头脑再加上经济学学位作后盾使他对金融问题把握非常准。

words.definition_en: adj. turned away from what is right or good
words.example_en: I do not like you, because I think that you are perverse!
words.example_zh: 我不喜欢你，因为我认为你不正经！

words.definition_en: adj. not genuinely felt
words.example_en: The persons and events portrayed in this production are fictitious.
words.example_zh: 这部作品中描述的人物和事件均属虚构。

words.definition_en: n. inability to take a stand
words.example_en: Vacillation is the cause of his failure.
words.example_zh: 优柔寡断是他失败的原因。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to make larger or greater than normal (2)v. to speak or write about in a more complete way
words.example_en: Please amplify your remarks by giving us some examples.
words.example_zh: 请举例详述你的话。

words.definition_en: v. to free from illusion
words.example_en: If something could disenchant voters, it would be the image of a political leader who is relying on an omnipotent image to woo votes.
words.example_zh: 如果有什么会让选民失望，那就是政治领导人靠塑造无所不能的形象来拉票。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. in a fair and reasonable way (2)adj. calm and thinks clearly, even in a difficult situation
words.example_en: I have to prove myself as a respectable, balanced, person.
words.example_zh: 我必须证明自己是个正派、稳重的人。

words.definition_en: adj. firm in belief, determination, or adherence
words.example_en: He remained steadfast in his belief that he had done the right thing.
words.example_zh: 他一直坚信自己做得对。

words.definition_en: v. to come together and unite in a common interest or focus
words.example_en: Some of the brightest inventors converge at these automobile conventions.
words.example_zh: 这些汽车会议云集了一些最优秀的发明家。

words.definition_en: n. agreement, harmony
words.example_en: Old enemies can become new friends even if all their national interests are not in complete accord.
words.example_zh: 即使国家利益不尽相同，他们也可以化敌为友

words.definition_en: v. to play a role with subdued force
words.example_en: We often underplay the skills we have
words.example_zh: 我们常常贬低自身的技能。

words.definition_en: n. an act or instance of revolting against civil authority or an established government
words.example_en: A group of farmers were plotting to stage an armed insurrection.
words.example_zh: 一群农民正密谋发动武装起义。

words.definition_en: v. to search into and explore very thoroughly
words.example_en: The more they probed into his background, the more inflamed their suspicions would become.
words.example_zh: 他们越调查他的背景，疑团就越多。

words.definition_en: adj. representing something that cannot be seen by itself
words.example_en: In some works, flowers take on a powerful emblematic quality.
words.example_zh: 在一些作品中，花具有强烈的象征意义。

words.definition_en: adj. more important, powerful, successful, or noticeable than other people or things
words.example_en: There are several factors, but the predominant one is climate change.
words.example_zh: 其中因素有几个，但最主要的就是气候变化。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. expressive of a mood (2)adj. subject to depression
words.example_en: David's mother was unstable and moody.
words.example_zh: 戴维的妈妈情绪不稳定，心情也不好。

words.definition_en: n. cheep falseness
words.example_en: Are those real diamonds or only shams?
words.example_zh: 那些是真钻石还是赝品?

words.definition_en: v. to cause to divide into two branches or parts
words.example_en: The stream bifurcates into two narrow winding channels.
words.example_zh: 那条小溪分成两股窄而弯曲的支流。

words.definition_en: v. form the foundation of
words.example_en: These ideas underlie much of his work.
words.example_zh: 他的作品大部分都是以这些主题思想为基础。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to do something
words.example_en: Doctors said surgery could induce a heart attack.
words.example_zh: 医生们说手术有可能引起心脏病发作。

words.definition_en: v. to go to the aid of
words.example_en: The government succored the refugees with food and shelter.
words.example_zh: 政府为难民提供食物和住所以救助他们。

words.definition_en: n. a mutually advantageous conjunction or compatibility of distinct business participants or elements
words.example_en: Of course, there's quite obviously a lot of synergy between the two companies.
words.example_zh: 当然，这两家公司间非常明显地产生了巨大的协同效应。

words.definition_en: adj. expressing harsh criticism especially in a way that is funny
words.example_en: His mordant wit appealed to students.
words.example_zh: 他那尖刻的妙语受到学生的欢迎。

words.definition_en: n. glowing and warmly enthusiastic praise
words.example_en: He pronounced a splendid encomium upon Alice in the forum.
words.example_zh: 他在广场上为爱丽丝作了华丽的赞颂。

words.definition_en: v. to give shape or form to
words.example_en: She fashioned a pot from the clay.
words.example_zh: 她用黏土制成一个罐。

words.definition_en: v. to present again
words.example_en: The atmosphere of the novel is successfully reproduced in the movie.
words.example_zh: 小说的氛围在电影中得到了成功的再现。

words.definition_en: v. to revolve rapidly
words.example_en: The plane was spinning out of control.
words.example_zh: 飞机失去控制，不停地旋转。

words.definition_en: n. a distinguishing characteristic, trait, or feature
words.example_en: Police said the explosion bore all the hallmarks of a terrorist attack.
words.example_zh: 警方称这次爆炸具有恐怖分子袭击的所有特征。

words.definition_en: adj. occurring occasionally, singly, or in irregular or random instances
words.example_en: The sound of sporadic shooting could still be heard.
words.example_zh: 仍能听见零星的枪声。

words.definition_en: n. deception or trickery
words.example_en: Charges of political chicanery were brought against him.
words.example_zh: 他被指控行驶政治欺骗手段。

words.definition_en: adj. tastelessly showy
words.example_en: They climbed the garish purple-carpeted stairs.
words.example_zh: 他们登上铺着俗艳的紫色地毯的楼梯。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to become smaller or less (2)n. a time of phase of gradual decrease
words.example_en: Her enthusiasm for the whole idea was waning rapidly.
words.example_zh: 她对整个想法的热情迅速冷淡了下来。

words.definition_en: n. the dispensing or receiving of reward or punishment
words.example_en: People are seeking retribution for the latest terrorist outrages.
words.example_zh: 人们在设法对恐怖分子最近的暴行进行严惩。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. careful about doing something in an accurate and exact way (2)adj. complete or absolute
words.example_en: The police carried out a thorough investigation.
words.example_zh: 警方展开了全面的调查。

words.definition_en: v. to be present in large numbers or in great quantity
words.example_en: Natural resources abound in our country.
words.example_zh: 我国自然资源丰富。

words.definition_en: n. helpful information or criticism that is given to someone to say what can be done to improve a performance, product, etc.
words.example_en: We need both positive and negative feedback from our customers.
words.example_zh: 我们需要顾客正反两方面的反馈意见。

words.definition_en: adj. used for decoration
words.example_en: The stream was dammed up to form ornamental lakes.
words.example_zh: 溪流用水坝拦挡起来,形成了装饰性的湖泊。

words.definition_en: v. to make up for some defect or weakness
words.example_en: To ease financial difficulties, farmers could be compensated for their loss of subsidies.
words.example_zh: 为了缓解经济困难，农场主们损失的补贴会得到补偿。

words.definition_en: v. to evaluate the worth, significance or status of
words.example_en: In China, she said, workers are often asked to appraise their peers, but this turns out to be less feedback than snitching.
words.example_zh: 她说，在中国，老板经常要求员工对同事进行评价，但结果往往是打小报告的成分多于反馈。

words.definition_en: n. a league or compact for mutual support or common action
words.example_en: They've entered this new confederacy because the central government's been unable to control the collapsing economy.
words.example_zh: 由于中央政府无力控制逐渐崩溃的经济，所以他们加入了这个新联盟。

words.definition_en: adj. agreeing with what seems naturally right
words.example_en: Her perception of the right seemed almost intuitive.
words.example_zh: 她对正确事物的理解几乎是凭借直觉的。

words.definition_en: v. to transmit to another
words.example_en: They rendered assistance to the disaster victims.
words.example_zh: 他们给灾民提供了援助。

words.definition_en: adj. growing quickly and in a way that is difficult to control
words.example_en: Malaria is still rampant in some swampy regions.
words.example_zh: 疟疾在一些沼泽地区仍很猖獗。

words.definition_en: v. to get or win back
words.example_en: He had realized the mistake he had made and wanted to redeem himself.
words.example_zh: 他已经认识到了自己的错误，想要进行补救。

words.definition_en: n. a minute or minor detail
words.example_en: They are take great pains for the every minutia.
words.example_zh: 他们为每个细节煞费苦心。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking discretion, wisdom, or good judgment
words.example_en: The Government of Jamaica consider it imprudent to abolish the death penalty.
words.example_zh: 牙买加政府认为废除死刑是轻率之举。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. extremely poisonous or venomous (2)adj. full of malice
words.example_en: The media has waged a virulent hate campaign against her.
words.example_zh: 新闻界向她发起了恶毒攻击。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to stop from happening or to cause to happen at a later time (2)v. to act before in order to prevent something
words.example_en: Try to anticipate what your child will do and forestall problems.
words.example_zh: 尽量预见你的孩子会干什么，并预先阻止问题发生。

words.definition_en: v. to cut off or cut back parts of for better shape or more fruitful growth
words.example_en: He pruned the longer branches off the tree.
words.example_zh: 他把较长的树枝剪掉了。

words.definition_en: n. a ranting piece of writing
words.example_en: The screed tired the audience.
words.example_zh: 那篇冗长的演说使听众厌烦了。

words.definition_en: v. to combine into a composite whole
words.example_en: The two meanings conflated.
words.example_zh: 这两个意思混在一起了。

words.definition_en: v. to exceed in importance
words.example_en: Hester is overshadowed by her younger and more attractive sister.
words.example_zh: 赫斯特被她那更年轻而且更有魅力的妹妹超越了。

words.definition_en: v. to divide and share out according to a plan
words.example_en: They dispensed new clothes to the children in the orphanage.
words.example_zh: 他们把新衣服发给孤儿院的小孩们。

words.definition_en: n. idealizing or idolizing biography
words.example_en: In this hagiography, his success is exaggerated.
words.example_zh: 在这本理想化的传记当中，他的成功被夸大了。

words.definition_en: adj. valuable beyond estimation
words.example_en: I was able to gain invaluable experience over that year.
words.example_zh: 在那一年里我获得了十分宝贵的经验。

words.definition_en: n. magnificence
words.example_en: Venezuela is the ideal starting point to explore the grandeur and natural beauty of South America.
words.example_zh: 委内瑞拉是探索南美的宏伟壮观与自然美景的理想出发点。

words.definition_en: adj. aware of something
words.example_en: We are cognizant of the problem.
words.example_zh: 我们意识到了这个问题。

words.definition_en: adj. not real or genuine
words.example_en: The scholar produced some bogus documents to support her claim.
words.example_zh: 该学者提出了一些伪造的文献来支持她的观点。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by complexity, fullness of detail, or ornateness
words.example_en: This is an elaborate management training scheme for graduates.
words.example_zh: 这是一份针对毕业生的详尽的管理培训计划。

words.definition_en: n. a line at the beginning of a news story, magazine article, or book giving the writer's name
words.example_en: Finally, we wish to thank the author of this article which carries no byline.
words.example_zh: 最后，我们要感谢这篇文章的那位没有署名的作者。

words.definition_en: n. a prolonged lamentation or complaint
words.example_en: Siegel's book is a jeremiad against the ills the Internet has visited upon our lives.
words.example_zh: 西格尔的书是一篇针对互联网给我们生活带来种种弊病的长篇悲叹。

words.definition_en: adj. belonging to the essential nature of a thing
words.example_en: Diamonds have little intrinsic value and their price depends almost entirely on their scarcity.
words.example_zh: 钻石没有多少本质价值，它们的价格几乎完全取决于其稀有程度。

words.definition_en: n. subsequent development
words.example_en: The police said the clash was a sequel to yesterday's nationwide strike.
words.example_zh: 警方说这场冲突是昨天全国大罢工的余波。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. yielding abundantly (2)adj. abundantly and often extravagantly rich and varied
words.example_en: There were two very large oak trees in front of our house with wide spreading branches and luxuriant foliage.
words.example_zh: 那时我们家门口有两棵枝广叶茂的大橡树。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. of or relating to art or beauty (2)adj. pleasing in appearance
words.example_en: Their furniture was more aesthetic than practical.
words.example_zh: 他们的家具华而不实。

words.definition_en: adj. exuding fragrance
words.example_en: Their own yard is redolent with lilacs.
words.example_zh: 他们家自己的院子里散发着丁香花的芬芳。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to speak ill of (2)v. to take away
words.example_en: That does not detract from his merit.
words.example_zh: 那没有贬低他的功绩。

words.definition_en: adj. written in or consisting of elegiac couplets
words.example_en: The mourning hall is full of wreaths, and on all the walls elegiac scrolls are hung.
words.example_zh: 灵堂里摆满了花圈，墙上也挂满了挽幛。

words.definition_en: n. someone or something that has the same job or purpose as another
words.example_en: It is not a unique instance, but has its counterpart.
words.example_zh: 无独有偶。

words.definition_en: n. a feeling or belief that good things will happen in the future
words.example_en: Optimism was gradually taking the place of pessimism.
words.example_zh: 乐观情绪逐渐取代了悲观主义。

words.definition_en: n. a measure around a body
words.example_en: A girl he knew had upset him by commenting on his increasing girth.
words.example_zh: 他认识的一位女孩对他越来越粗的腰身说三道四，这让他心里很不痛快。

words.definition_en: adj. exaggerated or disposed to exaggerate one’s own worth or importance often by an overbearing manner
words.example_en: Trump said that the press had misrepresented him as arrogant and bullying.
words.example_zh: 他说新闻媒体把他歪曲成了一个傲慢而又霸道的人。

words.definition_en: v. to give joy to
words.example_en: When the war ended, people finally had cause to rejoice.
words.example_zh: 战争结束，人们终于可以欢欣鼓舞了。

words.definition_en: v. to come together to form a group
words.example_en: The passengers clustered together in small groups.
words.example_zh: 乘客们三五成群聚在一起。

words.definition_en: v. to hide or disguise
words.example_en: She cloaked her embarrassment by rushing into speech.
words.example_zh: 她急忙开口说话，以掩饰自己的尴尬。

words.definition_en: adj. prevalent especially to an increasing degree
words.example_en: Bribery and corruption were rife in the industry.
words.example_zh: 在这个行业中，贿赂和腐败现象猖獗。

words.definition_en: adj. devoted to or biased in support of a party, group, or cause
words.example_en: The audience was very partisan, and refused to listen to the points she was making in her speech.
words.example_zh: 观众们很有偏见, 拒绝聆听她发言中的要点。

words.definition_en: adj. of, relating to, involving, or forming a periphery or surface part
words.example_en: Among high-income economies, those with most slack are those in the peripheral eurozone.
words.example_zh: 在高收入经济体中，拥有最多闲置资源的是那些处于欧元区外围的经济体。

words.definition_en: adj. beginning to exist
words.example_en: Our manager discovered a nascent talent.
words.example_zh: 我们经理发现了一个新人才。

words.definition_en: adj. open to view
words.example_en: There was little overt support for the project.
words.example_zh: 对这个项目公开表示支持的很少。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to be at a loss as to what to say, think, or do
words.example_en: The unexpected question nonplussed the professor.
words.example_zh: 这个意外的问题使教授不知所措。

words.definition_en: adj. not able to be proved wrong
words.example_en: The pictures provide irrefutable evidence of the incident.
words.example_zh: 这些图片为该事件提供了无法反驳的证据。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to talk loudly and in a way that shows anger (2)v. to scold vehemently
words.example_en: As the boss began to rant, I stood up and went out.
words.example_zh: 老板开始咆哮的时候，我起身走了出去。

words.definition_en: adj. hortative, exhortatory
words.example_en: In his speech, he quoted the hortatory moralism of many contemporary churchmen.
words.example_zh: 在他的演讲中，他援引了来自许多当代牧师的劝诫格言。

words.definition_en: adj. expressing a strong opinion
words.example_en: Such tendentious statements are likely to provoke strong opposition.
words.example_zh: 这种有倾向性的说法可能招致强烈的反对。

words.definition_en: adj. feeling or showing anger because of something unjust or unworthy
words.example_en: They were indignant that they hadn't been invited.
words.example_zh: 他们因没有受到邀请而愤愤不平。

words.definition_en: n. something that came before something else and may have influenced or caused it
words.example_en: This law has several historical antecedents.
words.example_zh: 这项法律有几个历史先例。

words.definition_en: v. support, stimulate
words.example_en: The result will inevitably fuel speculation about the Prime Minister's future.
words.example_zh: 这一结果将不可避免地加剧人们对首相未来的揣测。

words.definition_en: v. to thrust oneself in without invitation, permission, or welcome
words.example_en: The press has been blamed for intruding into people's personal lives in an unacceptable way.
words.example_zh: 新闻媒体因以一种令人无法接受的方式侵入人们的私生活而受到谴责。

words.definition_en: n. a person who says something will not work or is not possible
words.example_en: One naysayer pointed out that these athletes were simply reverting to the mean.
words.example_zh: 一位反对者指出，这些运动员只不过是从巅峰状态回复到了平均水平。

words.definition_en: adj. dealing with the problems that exist in a specific situation in a reasonable and logical way instead of depending on ideas and theories
words.example_en: Robin took a pragmatic look at her situation.
words.example_zh: 罗宾从务实的角度看待她的状况。

words.definition_en: adj. fanciful or playfully unusual; also subject to unpredictable change
words.definition_zh: 异想天开的；反复无常的
words.example_en: Some people may think that this project whimsical, but with a few years ago, has substantially increased its feasibility.
words.example_zh: 或许有人会认为这一项目异想天开，不过与几年前相比，其可行性已大幅提高。

words.definition_en: v. to speak slightingly of
words.example_en: It makes no sense to belittle the enormity of the disaster.
words.example_zh: 没有理由轻视这场灾难的严重性。

words.definition_en: adj. fit to be eaten
words.example_en: The meat of the herbivore is, in comparison, far more edible and smell wise.
words.example_zh: 相比之下，食草动物的肉更易食用，闻起来更美味。

words.definition_en: n. a charitable act or gift
words.example_en: The enterpriser's beneficence is well-known.
words.example_zh: 这位企业家的善行是众所皆知的。

words.definition_en: adj. very unusual in a way that does not seem natural
words.example_en: Their parents had an almost preternatural ability to understand what was going on in their children's minds.
words.example_zh: 他们的父母有着一种近乎神奇的能力，能够洞悉自己的孩子脑子里正在想什么。

words.definition_en: adj. slightly salty; distasteful or unpalatable
words.example_en: She refused to have a thin, brackish gruel.
words.example_zh: 她拒绝喝稀而难以下咽的粥。

words.definition_en: adj. complete in every detail
words.example_en: It was one of the most consummate triumphs of David Beckham's career.
words.example_zh: 这是贝克汉姆职业生涯中最完美的凯旋之一。

words.definition_en: n. something offered for consideration or acceptance
words.example_en: The proposition that democracies do not fight each other is based on a tiny historical sample.
words.example_zh: 认为民主国家互不争斗的观点是以一个极小的历史样本为根据的。

words.definition_en: v. to perplex or confuse especially by a complexity, variety, or multitude of objects or considerations
words.example_en: The big city bewildered the old woman from the countryside.
words.example_zh: 大城市把乡下来的老奶奶弄糊涂了。

words.definition_en: adj. showing or suggesting that future success is likely
words.example_en: With favorable weather conditions it was an auspicious moment to set sail.
words.example_zh: 风和日丽，正是扬帆出海的黄道吉日。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by or manifesting unrest especially of mind
words.example_en: The audience grew restless and inattentive.
words.example_zh: 观众变得焦躁不安，注意力不集中。

words.definition_en: adj. very small in amount or number
words.example_en: Food was scarce and expensive.
words.example_zh: 食物供不应求，而且价格昂贵。

words.definition_en: n. egoism, egocentrism
words.example_en: Those who suffer from narcissism become self-absorbed or chronic show-offs.
words.example_zh: 被自恋症折磨的人会变得只专注于自己的事情，或者不断地自我炫耀。

words.definition_en: v. to praise
words.example_en: We celebrate somebody like a Steve Jobs, who has created different revolutionary products.
words.example_zh: 我们赞美像史蒂夫•乔布斯那样的人，他创造出了差异化的革命性产品。

words.definition_en: adj. meticulously or excessively critical
words.example_en: He is so hypercritical that he corrects every mistake his students made.
words.example_zh: 他很苛刻，对于学生的每个错误都要纠正。

words.definition_en: v. to publicly state that someone or something is bad or wrong
words.example_en: The letter called for trade union freedom and civil rights, but did not openly denounce the regime.
words.example_zh: 这封信呼吁保障工会自由和公民权利，但没有公开谴责该政权。

words.definition_en: n. the origin and the history of something
words.example_en: She had an impeccable aristocratic pedigree.
words.example_zh: 她有纯正的贵族血统。

words.definition_en: v. to make nervous, worried, or upset
words.example_en: The presence of the two policemen unsettled her.
words.example_zh: 两名警察的出现让她感到不安。

words.definition_en: v. to concentrate in a small or narrow space
words.example_en: Trucks congested the tunnel.
words.example_zh: 货车堵塞了通道。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to treat in a harsh or harmful way (2)v. to use wrongly (3)n. a corrupt practice or custom
words.example_en: Cases of child abuse often go unreported or ignored by uninformed citizens.
words.example_zh: 虐待儿童案件常常没人举报，或者为缺乏相关知识的公民所忽略。

words.definition_en: n. an act or instance of burning
words.example_en: Soot is usually the product of the imperfect combustion of fuel.
words.example_zh: 煤烟通常是燃料不完全燃烧的产物。

words.definition_en: adj. not capable of being solved
words.example_en: Life is a mystery, an inextricable mystery for human society.
words.example_zh: 生命是一个谜，是红尘中的人永远也解不开的谜。

words.definition_en: v. to lead astray: misdirect
words.example_en: Low-quality auditing will misguide the voucher users to make decision and let them lose money.
words.example_zh: 低质量的审计工作则会使报表使用者决策失误，从而使其利益遭受损失。

words.definition_en: adj. given to or abounding in excessive moralizing
words.example_en: His article is sententious and pompous.
words.example_zh: 他写的文章是说教式的，华而不实。

words.definition_en: v. to bring into a condition of decay or partial ruin
words.example_en: The building had been dilapidated by neglect.
words.example_zh: 这座大楼因失修而圮毁。

words.definition_en: v. to follow as a pattern, model, or example
words.example_en: Clarence screws up his face and imitates the Colonel again.
words.example_zh: 克拉伦斯又板起面孔，模仿起上校来。

words.definition_en: adj. causing a very large amount of damage
words.example_en: Severe damage witnessed the destructive force of the storm.
words.example_zh: 严重的损失表明了这场暴风雨巨大的破坏力。

words.definition_en: adj. having a very noticeable quality that attracts a lot of attention
words.example_en: Freddie Mercury was a flamboyant star of the British hard rock scene.
words.example_zh: 弗雷迪·摩克瑞是英国硬摇滚乐界一颗耀眼的明星。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. having a large estate or property (2)adj. amply or plentifully provided or fashioned often to the point of ostentation
words.example_en: Most of the cash went on supporting his opulent lifestyle.
words.example_zh: 大部分现金都花在了维系他挥金如土的生活方式上。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. having a rough quality (2)adj. rude or offensive
words.example_en: The young mother set her son on the bed and rubbed him down with a coarse towel.
words.example_zh: 那位年轻的母亲让她的儿子躺到床上，用一条粗糙的毛巾给他擦干了全身。

words.definition_en: adj. remaining after a process, event etc is finished
words.example_en: There are still a few residual problems with the computer program.
words.example_zh: 电脑程序还有一些残留问题。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to continue for a long time without activity or progress in an unpleasant or unwanted situation (2)v. to suffer neglect
words.example_en: The flowers languished from lack of water.
words.example_zh: 花因缺水而凋萎。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to cause to become dishonest, immoral, etc. (2)adj. doing things that are dishonest or illegal in order to make money or to gain or keep power
words.example_en: He had accused three opposition members of corrupt practices.
words.example_zh: 他指控3名反对派成员营私舞弊。

words.definition_en: v. to treat with insolence, indignity, or contempt
words.example_en: Forgive me, I don't mean to insult you.
words.example_zh: 请原谅，我并非有意侮辱你。

words.definition_en: v. to adjust precisely for a particular function
words.example_en: He is reading instructions on how to calibrate a thermometer.
words.example_zh: 他在看校准温度计的说明。

words.definition_en: adj. radiant splendor
words.example_en: Effulgent compose acts the role of ablaze series, extremely attractive inwrought craft series, be today the mainstream that Xia Liang pulls.
words.example_zh: 光辉灿烂的缀饰闪亮系列、极具魅力的刺绣工艺系列，是今夏凉拖的主流。

words.definition_en: v. to suffer agony, torture or anguish
words.example_en: If you agonize over getting the perfect answer, you won't get very far.
words.example_zh: 如果你为求得完美的答案而苦恼不堪，那你不会有多大的成就。

words.definition_en: adj. shared in common
words.example_en: The East and the West can work together for their mutual benefit and progress.
words.example_zh: 东西方可以为互惠和进步而合作。

words.definition_en: adj. having strong moral principles
words.example_en: Such behavior is inconsistent with her high - minded principles.
words.example_zh: 这样的行为与她情操高尚的原则是格格不入的。

words.definition_en: v. to make more attractive by adding something beautiful
words.example_en: His watercolor designs adorn a wide range of books
words.example_zh: 他的水彩设计使许多图书大为生色。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. happening or beginning now or in recent times (2)adj. from the same time period
words.example_en: Shakespeare was not contemporary with Dickens.
words.example_zh: 莎士比亚与狄更斯不是同时代的人。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. being less dense (2)adj. of, relating to, or interesting to a select group
words.definition_zh: 稀薄的仅限于小圈子的，曲高和寡的
words.example_en: Both animals and people were gasping for breath in the rarefied air.
words.example_zh: 动物和人在稀薄的空气中都大口喘着气。

words.definition_en: v. to promote for or as if for sale
words.example_en: The company merchandises its products through social media.
words.example_zh: 该公司通过社交媒体推销产品。

words.definition_en: v. to conceal or disguise
words.example_en: The soldiers camouflaged themselves with leaves.
words.example_zh: 士兵用树叶来伪装自己。

words.definition_en: adj. filled with or expressing great joy or triumph
words.example_en: The exultant crowds were dancing in the streets.
words.example_zh: 欢欣的人群在大街上跳起了舞。

words.definition_en: n. a eulogistic oration or writing
words.example_en: He made a speech of panegyric.
words.example_zh: 他作了一个颂扬性的演讲。

words.definition_en: n. a deceptive device or stratagem
words.example_en: The party has predictably rejected the proposals as a subterfuge.
words.example_zh: 不出所料，该党以这些提议是在耍花招为由而加以拒绝。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. offering or expressing a choice (2)n. something that can be chosen instead of something else
words.example_en: We are currently investigating a number of alternative options.
words.example_zh: 我们现在正在研究一系列可供替代的选择方案。

words.definition_en: n. a group of people who develop new and often very surprising ideas in art, literature, etc.
words.example_en: It was one of the first avant-garde works to appeal to a wide audience.
words.example_zh: 这是受到听众广泛欢迎的第一批先锋派作品之一。

words.definition_en: adj. if someone is obsessed with a person or thing, they keep thinking about them and find it difficult to think about anything else
words.example_en: She wasn't in love with Steve, she was obsessed by him physically.
words.example_zh: 她并没有爱上史蒂夫，只是被他的外表迷住了。

words.definition_en: adj. great or imposing in size or extent
words.example_en: A mighty empire once ruled the world.
words.example_zh: 一个强大的帝国曾经统治了世界。

words.definition_en: adj. meriting condemnation or blame especially as wrong or harmful
words.example_en: They held him culpable for the offence.
words.example_zh: 他们认为他有罪应受惩罚。

words.definition_en: v. to restrain from motion, action, or progress
words.example_en: We revere tradition but will not be fettered by it.
words.example_zh: 我们尊重传统，但不会受其束缚。

words.definition_en: adj. showing or characterized by a patronizing or superior attitude toward others
words.example_en: I'm fed up with your money and your whole condescending attitude.
words.example_zh: 我讨厌你的钱，也受够了你居高临下的态度。

words.definition_en: adj. lasting for a limited time
words.example_en: He afforded a temporary shelter for the needy.
words.example_zh: 他为穷人提供暂时的安身之处。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to bring together at a single point or place (2)v. to bring under the control of one authority
words.example_en: In the mass production era multinational firms tended to centralize their operations.
words.example_zh: 在大规模生产的时代，跨国公司往往实行集权化经营。

words.definition_en: n. calm, peaceful
words.example_en: I am owed and savor the halcyon times.
words.example_zh: 这样的太平时光是我应得的，我要尽情享受。

words.definition_en: adj. low in spirits
words.example_en: Everyone has days when they feel dejected or down.
words.example_zh: 每个人都有沮丧失意或情绪低落的时候。

words.definition_en: n. behavior exhibiting overwhelming or unmanageable fear or emotional excess
words.definition_zh: 不正常的兴奋，歇斯底里
words.example_en: All the lights went off, and mass hysteria broke out.
words.example_zh: 所有的灯都熄灭了，大家都变得歇斯底里起来。

words.definition_en: adj. using words that mean the opposite of what you really think especially in order to be funny
words.example_en: The film is more of an ironic fantasy than a horror story.
words.example_zh: 这部电影比较像是讽刺的幻想故事，而不是恐怖片。

words.definition_en: adj. existing before a war
words.example_en: There are many antebellum houses in the area.
words.example_zh: 这个地区有许多南北战争以前的房屋.

words.definition_en: adj. filled, covered, or completely overrun as if by a flood
words.example_en: This, after all, is a company which is awash with cash.
words.example_zh: 这毕竟是一家现金充裕的公司。

words.definition_en: v. to extend or expand beyond a safe or reasonable point
words.example_en: They should not overextend themselves on the mortgage.
words.example_zh: 他们不应该用抵押借款过多。

words.definition_en: n. a person who rules with total power
words.example_en: An autocrat prefers his subjects to be automatons , rather than intelligent human beings.
words.example_zh: 独裁者希望他的国民都是自动调节器，像智能机器人。

words.definition_en: n. an impractical scheme for social improvement
words.example_en: As Russia was opening up to the world, it was bidding farewell to America as a dream and a Utopia.
words.example_zh: 随着俄罗斯向世界开放，它向美国这个曾经的梦与乌托邦说再见。

words.definition_en: adj. impossible to understand
words.example_en: His books are notoriously impenetrable.
words.example_zh: 他的书出了名的晦涩难懂。

words.definition_en: n. distress or suffering resulting from oppression or persecution
words.example_en: Even in our awful tribulation we were quite optimistic.
words.example_zh: 即使在极端痛苦时，我们仍十分乐观。

words.definition_en: adj. marked or governed by feeling, sensibility, or emotional idealism
words.example_en: I'm trying not to be sentimental about the past
words.example_zh: 我努力不让自己表现得感怀过去。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to drink alcohol (2)v. to receive into the mind and retain
words.example_en: These plants imbibe moisture through their roots.
words.example_zh: 这些植物通过根部吸收水分。

words.definition_en: v. to publicly or officially say that you support or approve of
words.example_en: I can endorse their opinion wholeheartedly.
words.example_zh: 我可以全力支持他们的意见。

words.definition_en: adj. based on or reflecting a belief that human conduct is motivated primarily by self-interest
words.example_en: With that cynical outlook, he doesn't trust anyone.
words.example_zh: 抱着那种愤世嫉俗的观点，他不相信任何人。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. having a very close relationship (2)v. to say or suggest in an indirect way
words.example_en: Professor Li has already intimated that he intends to retire.
words.example_zh: 李教授已经暗示他打算退休。

words.definition_en: v. to dispose in advance
words.example_en: Franklin was predisposed to believe him.
words.example_zh: 富兰克林倾向于相信他。

words.definition_en: adj. having very little food or money
words.example_en: There will be efforts to get larger amounts of food to the needy.
words.example_zh: 将努力把更多的食品送给穷人。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. valiant (2)adj. showing respect and politeness especially toward women
words.definition_zh: 勇敢的绅士风度的，礼貌的
words.example_en: He was handsome, upright and chivalrous.
words.example_zh: 他长相英俊，为人正直，风度翩翩。

words.definition_en: v. to lose or lose the right to especially by some error, offense, or crime
words.example_en: If you cancel your flight, you will forfeit your deposit.
words.example_zh: 乘客取消航班订位，定金概不退还。

words.definition_en: v. to make weaker or worse
words.example_en: Consumption of alcohol impairs your ability to drive a car or operate machinery.
words.example_zh: 饮酒会削弱你驾驶汽车或操控机器的能力。

words.definition_en: n. violent and uncontrolled anger
words.example_en: The gardener exploded with the rage that had simmered all mornin.
words.example_zh: 花匠憋了一个早上的愤怒终于爆发了。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by ease and fluency in speaking or writing often to the point of being insincere or deceitful
words.example_en: Some people have a glib tongue but an empty head.
words.example_zh: 有些人喜欢夸夸其谈，其实肚子里却是空空如也。

words.definition_en: n. mockery usually by caricature
words.example_en: The antics of the defense attorneys turned the trial into a burlesque of justice.
words.example_zh: 辩护律师们滑稽的动作将审判变成了对正义的滑稽讽刺

words.definition_en: adj. has not done anything wrong
words.example_en: The US itself, of course, is not entirely blameless in trading matters.
words.example_zh: 当然，美国本身在贸易问题上也不是完全没有任何过错。

words.definition_en: v. to exert (oneself, one's senses, etc.) to the utmost
words.example_en: I strained my ears to catch what they were saying.
words.example_zh: 我竖起耳朵尽量去听到他们在说些什么。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking in sheen, brilliance, or vitality: dull, mediocre
words.example_en: He has already been blamed for his party's lackluster performance during the election campaign.
words.example_zh: 他的政党在竞选活动中表现平平，他因此已受到指责。

words.definition_en: adj. divided into different classes or social layers
words.example_en: Feudal society was rigidly stratified.
words.example_zh: 封建社会等级森严。

words.definition_en: n. a list or supply of dramas, operas, pieces, or parts that a company or person is prepared to perform
words.example_en: His repertoire encompassed everything from Bach to Schoenberg.
words.example_zh: 他能演奏从巴赫到勋伯格的所有作品。

words.definition_en: adj. awkwardly blundering or faltering
words.example_en: He has often been portrayed as Holmes’ rather bumbling assistant.
words.example_zh: 作为福尔摩斯的助手，他通常被刻画成一个笨手笨脚的人。

words.definition_en: v. to shake or move with a slight trembling motion
words.example_en: Her lip quivered and then she started to cry.
words.example_zh: 她嘴唇微微一颤就哭了起来。

words.definition_en: adj. attracting special attention
words.example_en: He answered the question with an emphatic “No".
words.example_zh: 他用一个加强语气的'不'字回答了这个问题。

words.definition_en: v. to tell beforehand
words.example_en: The outcome of the war is hard to foretell.
words.example_zh: 战争胜负难以预卜。

words.definition_en: adj. very important
words.example_en: The elections may prove to be pivotal in Colombia's political history.
words.example_zh: 这些选举也许会证明其在哥伦比亚政治史上是至关重要的。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to use the power of the law to take and keep (2)v. to stop the progress or movement of (3)v. to attract and hold the attention of
words.example_en: The deepening economic gloom may well mean that it will eventually prove necessary to cut interest rates to arrest a sharper downturn.
words.example_zh: 逐渐加强的经济衰退迹象也许终将证明降低利率以抑制经济衰退是必要的手段。

words.definition_en: adj. planned or contrived to accomplish a purpose
words.example_en: Student's metacognition capability will be improved much quickly by the calculated guidance from teachers.
words.example_zh: 教师有计划地知道可以让学生的元认知能力的提高得更快。

words.definition_en: v. to rise above or go beyond the normal limits of
words.example_en: These issues transcend the limits of language study.
words.example_zh: 这些问题超出了语言研究的范围。

words.definition_en: n. a friendly, harmonious relationship
words.example_en: She understood the importance of establishing a close rapport with clients.
words.example_zh: 她懂得与客户建立密切和睦关系的重要性。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to obey or agree with something (2)v. to be in agreement or harmony
words.example_en: He did not feel obliged to conform to the rules that applied to ordinary men.
words.example_zh: 他觉得自己不必遵守那些适用于普通人的规定。

words.definition_en: (1)n. the act of founding (2)n. a basis upon which something stands or is supported
words.example_en: Respect and friendship provide a solid foundation for marriage.
words.example_zh: 尊重和友爱是婚姻的牢固基础。

words.definition_en: v. to make a fetish of
words.example_en: Politicians fetishize ratings, too.
words.example_zh: 政治家们也盲目崇拜评级。

words.definition_en: n. empty boasting
words.example_en: He was disliked because his manner was always full of braggadocio.
words.example_zh: 人们讨厌他，因为他老是吹牛。

words.definition_en: (1)n. an initial and often tentative attempt to do something in a new or different field or area of activity (2)v. to make a raid or brief invasion
words.example_en: China's biggest property developer has made its first foray into the US with a deal for a high-end residential complex in San Francisco.
words.example_zh: 中国最大的房地产开发商首次进军美国市场，签约参与旧金山一个高档住宅项目。

words.definition_en: v. to steal and pass off (the ideas or words of another) as one's own
