words.example_en: The poem employs as its first lines a verse plagiarized from a billboard.
words.example_zh: 这首诗开头的几行抄袭了一个广告牌上的一节诗。

words.definition_en: v. become active or flourishing again
words.example_en: The economy is beginning to revive.
words.example_zh: 经济开始复苏。

words.definition_en: n. a mistake made in a social situation
words.example_en: My manager transposed a gaffe by the mayor into a public-relations advantage.
words.example_zh: 我的经理把市长的失态转变成了公关优势。

words.definition_en: adj. feeling or showing concern about someone who is in a bad situation
words.example_en: She was very sympathetic to the problems of adult students.
words.example_zh: 她对成年学生的问题深表同情。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to clothes
words.example_en: She was famous for his sartorial elegance.
words.example_zh: 她以服装优雅著称。

words.definition_en: adj. lucky
words.example_en: This was providential rest and shelter until the moon rose.
words.example_zh: 在月亮上来之前，这是一个幸运的休憩与隐蔽之所。

words.definition_en: n. the distinguishing character, sentiment, moral nature, or guiding beliefs of a person, group, or institution
words.example_en: As a notion of value, national ethos possesses the value of cohesion, that of spiritual sustenance and that of spiritual motivation.
words.example_zh: 民族精神作为一种价值观念，具有凝聚力价值、精神支撑价值和精神动力价值。

words.definition_en: n. gladness or gaiety as shown by or accompanied with laughter
words.example_en: The performance produced much mirth among the audience.
words.example_zh: 这场演出使观众笑声不断。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to adhere firmly and closely or loyally and unwaveringly (2)v. to split with sharp instrument
words.example_en: They just cleave the stone along the cracks.
words.example_zh: 他们就是顺着裂缝把石头劈开。

words.definition_en: n. something that is unusual or unexpected
words.example_en: The British public's wariness of opera is an anomaly in Europe.
words.example_zh: 英国公众对歌剧不大轻易接受的态度在欧洲来说很反常。

words.definition_en: v. to walk or travel over
words.example_en: Some peasants left their home village and peregrinated the country for seasonal jobs.
words.example_zh: 一些农民离开老家，走遍全国寻找季节工作。

words.definition_en: v. to direct or impose by authoritative order or with urgent admonition
words.example_en: She enjoined me strictly not to tell anyone else.
words.example_zh: 她严令我不准告诉其他任何人。

words.definition_en: v. to divide into separate sections
words.example_en: Traditionally men have compartmentalized their lives, never letting their personal lives encroach upon their professional lives.
words.example_zh: 传统上男人把自己的生活划分清楚，他们不会让私生活影响其工作。

words.definition_en: adj. carried through the air
words.example_en: Many people are allergic to airborne pollutants such as pollen.
words.example_zh: 许多人对空气传播的污染物过敏，比如花粉。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing too much pride in your abilities or achievements
words.example_en: He was rather vainglorious about his own war record.
words.example_zh: 他非常喜欢吹嘘自己的战争经历。

words.definition_en: n. a person who is in charge of the things in a museum, zoo, etc.
words.example_en: As the curator of the island, this is the largest compromise I can make.
words.example_zh: 这就是，作为岛的管理者，我能够做到的最大的让步了。

words.definition_en: v. to grow or develop quickly
words.example_en: Manufacturers are keen to cash in on the burgeoning demand.
words.example_zh: 需求急速增长，制造商急着从中捞一把。

words.definition_en: n. the art or practice that seeks to foresee or foretell future events or discover hidden knowledge usually by the interpretation of omens or by the aid of supernatural powers
words.example_en: They're part of an ancient Greek divination ritual.
words.example_zh: 他们是古希腊占卜仪式的一部分。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. naming a real thing or class of things (2)adj. specific or particular
words.example_en: There were no concrete proposals on the table.
words.example_zh: 没有具体的提议可供讨论。

words.definition_en: n. intense, disordered, and often destructive rage
words.example_en: He stood up in a fury and flounced out.
words.example_zh: 他气得一跃而起，冲了出去。

words.definition_en: v. to express support for
words.example_en: She ran away with him to Mexico and espoused the revolutionary cause.
words.example_zh: 她跟着他逃到墨西哥，并支持革命事业。

words.definition_en: n. disinclination to action or labor
words.example_en: We should overcome our natural sloth and complacency.
words.example_zh: 我们应当克服自己天性中的懒惰和自满。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. bad or unfavorable (2)adj. acting against or in a contrary direction
words.example_en: Universities in the west and China follow two adverse trends: "top-down" and “bottom-up".
words.example_zh: 中西方大学的形成中有着自上而下及自下而上两个相逆的趋势。

words.definition_en: n. an ignorant, misinformed, or dishonest practitioner of medicine
words.example_en: It has two government schools, but no health-care facilities, aside from a few quack doctors in the bazaar.
words.example_zh: 这有两个公立学校，没有医疗设施，只有集市上的几个民间庸医。

words.definition_en: n. the close or termination of something
words.example_en: The festival ended with a grand finale in Hyde Park.
words.example_zh: 节日的压轴活动是在海德公园举行的盛大演出。

words.definition_en: adj. odiously or disgustingly objectionable
words.example_en: One of the parents was a most obnoxious character. No-one liked him.
words.example_zh: 其中一位家长极为惹人厌，没人喜欢他。

words.definition_en: (1)n. the quality of having or showing discernment or good judgment (2)n. power of free decision or latitude of choice within certain legal bounds
words.example_en: Discretion in speech is more than eloquence.
words.example_zh: 出言慎重胜过口若悬河。

words.definition_en: adj. odd or unpredictable in their appearance, character, or behavior.
words.example_en: We've developed a reputation for being quite quirky and original.
words.example_zh: 我们因为风格奇特又独具创意而名声在外。

words.definition_en: adj. expressing or containing praise
words.example_en: Beth spoke of Dr. Hammer in laudatory terms.
words.example_zh: 贝丝谈及哈默医生时赞不绝口。

words.definition_en: v. to have the reverse of the desired or expected effect
words.example_en: The President's tactics could backfire.
words.example_zh: 总统的策略可能会适得其反。

words.definition_en: v. decorate, embellish
words.example_en: A chef garnished the plate with whipped cream rosettes and fresh fruits.
words.example_zh: 主厨用玫瑰花形的掼奶油和新鲜的水果作配菜点缀盘子。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by or proceeding from an intense and serious state of mind
words.example_en: His expression is as earnest when he smiles as when he is arguing.
words.example_zh: 他不论笑的时候还是争论的时候表情都同样真诚。

words.definition_en: adj. of, relating to, or conforming to idiom
words.example_en: In his lecture,he bore down on the importance of idiomatic usage in a language.
words.example_zh: 他在演讲中着重强调了语言中习惯用法的重要性。

words.definition_en: v. to use abusive language
words.example_en: The tramp reviled the man who drove him off.
words.example_zh: 流浪汉辱骂那位赶他走开的人。

words.definition_en: (1)n. the act or process by which someone rises to a powerful and important position (2)n. adherence
words.example_en: Trump’s accession to the important post was a big event.
words.example_zh: 特朗普就任这一重要职务是一个重大事件.

words.definition_en: (1)adj. being without content or occupant (2)adj. devoid of thought, reflection, or expression
words.example_en: The position of chairman has been vacant for some time.
words.example_zh: 主席的职位已经空缺一段时间了。

words.definition_en: adj. nobody controls it or prevents it from growing or developing
words.example_en: If left unchecked, weeds will flourish.
words.example_zh: 如果不加以遏制，杂草就会疯长。

words.definition_en: adj. hatefully or shockingly evil
words.example_en: Her life has been permanently blighted by his heinous crime...
words.example_zh: 他犯下的滔天罪行给她的生活蒙上了永久的阴影。

words.definition_en: adj. having a heavy offensive smell
words.example_en: The air of the room was fetid with stale tobacco smoke.
words.example_zh: 房间里的空气有陈烟的臭味。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to a citizen, a city, citizenship, or community affairs
words.example_en: The study showed that most teens felt little sense of civic pride or responsibility.
words.example_zh: 研究有明，大多数青少年很少有作为公民的自豪感和责任感。

words.definition_en: n. something that impedes progress or achievement
words.example_en: A lack of qualifications can be a major obstacle to finding a job.
words.example_zh: 学历不足可能成为谋职的主要障碍。

words.definition_en: adj. temperamentally disinclined to talk
words.example_en: A taciturn man, he replied to my questions in monosyllables.
words.example_zh: 他寡言少语，对我的问题一两个字就打发掉了。

words.definition_en: v. to make a careful or detailed search for information
words.example_en: If you're interested in a subject, use the Internet to delve deeper.
words.example_zh: 如果对某一主题感兴趣，就上网进一步深入研究。

words.definition_en: v. appropriated by the government
words.example_en: The teacher threatened to confiscate their phones if they kept using them in class.
words.example_zh: 老师警告说，如果他们上课时继续使用手机就予以没收。

words.definition_en: v. to flourish in youthful beauty, freshness, or excellence
words.example_en: Not many economies bloomed in 1990, least of all gold exporters like Australia.
words.example_zh: 1990年经济繁荣的国家并不多，尤其是像澳大利亚这样的黄金出口国。

words.definition_en: n. denial especially: self-denial
words.example_en: Life is the abnegation of self to gain the bliss of Nirvana.
words.example_zh: 生命是为了达到极乐的涅磐而摒弃自身。

words.definition_en: v. to prevent or impede the free play of
words.example_en: No longer trammel by his responsibility as chairman, he can say what he wish.
words.example_zh: 他不当董事长就不受职务约束了, 想说什么就说什么。

words.definition_en: n. the feeling of not having much emotion or interest
words.example_en: Apathy is the long-standing curse of British local democracy.
words.example_zh: 冷漠是英国地方民主制度长期以来难以推广的祸根。

words.definition_en: adj. relating to or causing much discussion, disagreement, or argument
words.example_en: Immigration is a controversial issue in many countries.
words.example_zh: 在很多国家，移民都是一个颇有争议的问题。

words.definition_en: v. to make rich or richer especially by the addition or increase of some desirable quality, attribute, or ingredient
words.example_en: Some cream will enrich the sauce.
words.example_zh: 在调味汁里加一些奶油会使其味道更加丰富。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to create or imagine (2)v. to make you think of
words.example_en: That smell always conjures up memories of holidays in France.
words.example_zh: 那种气味总是令人勾起在法国度假那段日子的回忆。

words.definition_en: adj. willing to agree or to accept something that is wanted or asked for
words.example_en: The Jordanian leader seemed amenable to attending a conference.
words.example_zh: 约旦领导人似乎愿意参加会议。

words.definition_en: n. a single thin piece of thread, wire, hair etc
words.example_en: She tried to blow a gray strand of hair from her eyes.
words.example_zh: 她竭力想把眼前的一缕白发吹开。

words.definition_en: v. to improve or perfect by pruning or polishing
words.example_en: Surgical techniques are constantly being refined.
words.example_zh: 外科手术的技术不断得到完善。

words.definition_en: n. a special magnetic charm or appeal
words.example_en: He was elected to power on the strength of his charisma
words.example_zh: 他凭借其个人魅力而当选掌权。

words.definition_en: adj. needed for a particular purpose
words.example_en: The state provided the requisite capital for this farm.
words.example_zh: 国家为这个农场提供了必要的资金。

words.definition_en: (1)n. the act or process of freeing someone or something from another’s control (2)n. the removal of traditional social or sexual rules, attitudes, etc.
words.example_en: All his life was submerged in the liberation of the people.
words.example_zh: 他的一生全部献身于人民的解放事业。

words.definition_en: adj. very strong or tough
words.example_en: Heavy cast-iron cylinder used to flatten lawns.
words.example_zh: 用来使草坪变平的圆筒沉重而又坚固。

words.definition_en: n. a person undertaking a mission and especially a religious mission
words.example_en: An American missionary was released today after more than two months of captivity.
words.example_zh: 一位美国传教士在被关押两个多月后于今天获释。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. sparing of words (2)adj. marked by rude or peremptory shortness
words.example_en: Her tone of voice was curt.
words.example_zh: 她说话的语气唐突无礼。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a good or desirable quality or feature (2)n. benefit or gain
words.example_en: The Chinese team enjoyed the height advantage.
words.example_zh: 中国队占有身高优势。

words.definition_en: adj. serving as a crucial means, agent, or tool
words.example_en: In his first years as chairman he was instrumental in raising the company's wider profile.
words.example_zh: 在刚担任董事长的头几年，他在提升公司形象方面发挥了作用。

words.definition_en: adj. fully and unconditionally guaranteed as a legal right, benefit, or privilege
words.example_en: We have no vested interest in this respect.
words.example_zh: 我们在这方面没有既得利益。

words.definition_en: v. to accustom to accept something undesirable
words.example_en: We should inure ourselves to hard life.
words.example_zh: 我们应该使自己习惯艰苦生活。

words.definition_en: v. to have recourse
words.example_en: His punishing work schedule had made him resort to drugs.
words.example_zh: 异常紧张的工作安排使他开始吸毒。

words.definition_en: v. to make seem smaller or less important
words.example_en: The government is trying to downplay the violence.
words.example_zh: 政府试图对暴力行为低调处理。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by decay or decline
words.example_en: Don’t let decadent ideas eat into yourselves.
words.example_zh: 别让腐朽的思想侵蚀你。

words.definition_en: adj. consisting of one or more codons that are genetic nonsense
words.example_en: It seemed to me that Robert's arguments were nonsensical.
words.example_zh: 在我看来，罗伯特的论点很荒谬。

words.definition_en: adj. providing information
words.example_en: Burton's text is concise and informative.
words.example_zh: 伯顿的文章语言简洁，信息丰富。

words.definition_en: v. to make very happy and excited
words.example_en: The news of victory elate the whole nation.
words.example_zh: 胜利的消息使得举国欢悦。

words.definition_en: n. excessive or unseemly frivolity
words.example_en: At the time, Arnold had disapproved of such levity.
words.example_zh: 那时候的阿诺德对这种轻浮行为很看不惯。

words.definition_en: n. a rapid or sudden rise
words.example_en: A new upsurge in construction is in the making.
words.example_zh: 一个建设的新高潮正在兴起。

words.definition_en: adj. tending to cause discontent, animosity, or envy
words.example_en: It would be invidious to single out any one person to thank.
words.example_zh: 单独感谢任何一个人都易引起反感。

words.definition_en: adj. acting or done quickly and without thought
words.example_en: He tended to react in a heated and impetuous way.
words.example_zh: 他往往反应过火又显鲁莽。

words.definition_en: n. a name that is wrong or not proper or appropriate
words.example_en: Herbal "tea" is something of a misnomer because these drinks contain no tea at all.
words.example_zh: 花草“茶”是个误称，因为这类饮料里根本不含茶。

words.definition_en: adj. getting what is wanted in a clever and often deceptive way
words.example_en: In the story, the cunning fox outsmarts the hunters.
words.example_zh: 在这个故事中，狡猾的狐狸智取了猎人们。

words.definition_en: n. simultaneous and contradictory attitudes for feelings toward an object, person, or action
words.example_en: Torn between loving her parents one minute and hating them the next, she was confused by the ambivalence of her feelings.
words.example_zh: 她深陷于对她父母时爱时恨的矛盾心态中而感到困惑不堪。

words.definition_en: adj. acting as a mediator
words.example_en: Until the students to intern, schools play an intermediary role, students and the schools there is still an intermediary between the nature of legal relationship.
words.example_zh: 直至学生进入实习单位学校始终扮演着中间人的角色，学生与学校之间还存在着中介性质的法律关系。

words.definition_en: v. to make weaker or less effective usually in a secret or gradual way
words.example_en: She undermined him and destroyed his confidence in his own talent
words.example_zh: 她暗中打击他，让他对自己的才华信心全无。

words.definition_en: v. to behave so as to invite or incur
words.example_en: If he thinks he can remain in power by force he is courting disaster.
words.example_zh: 如果他认为可以通过武力继续掌权，那他就是在自寻死路。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to send quickly to a particular place for a particular purpose (2)n. promptness and efficiency in performance or transmission
words.example_en: The Italian government was preparing to dispatch 4,000 soldiers to search the island.
words.example_zh: 意大利政府正准备派遣4,000名士兵搜索该岛。

words.definition_en: v. to praise excessively especially from motives of self-interest
words.example_en: Jamie was adroit at flattering others.
words.example_zh: 杰米很会拍马屁。

words.definition_en: v. to involve in the nature or operation of something
words.example_en: He tried to avoid saying anything that would implicate him further.
words.example_zh: 他尽力避免说出任何会进一步牵连他的事情。

words.definition_en: adj. allowing liquids or gases to pass through
words.example_en: A number of products have been developed which are permeable to air and water.
words.example_zh: 已开发出许多可渗水透气的产品。

words.definition_en: adj. used to say that two or more things are very similar and can be compared to each other
words.example_en: A comparable house in the south of the city would cost twice as much.
words.example_zh: 一栋类似的房子位于城南部就要贵一倍的价钱。

words.definition_en: adj. being without a usual, typical, or expected attribute or accompaniment
words.example_en: The article is devoid of substantial matter.
words.example_zh: 这篇文章缺乏实质性的内容。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to happen faster
words.example_en: The government has been extremely reluctant to expedite investigations that might result in his trial.
words.example_zh: 当局极不情愿推进调查，因为其结果可能会让他接受审判。

words.definition_en: adj. able to know or predict what was going to happen in the future
words.example_en: Charles Adams was prescient in his quick understanding of this issue.
words.example_zh: 查尔斯亚当斯很快就明白了这个问题，真是有先见之明。

words.definition_en: adj. indirect, devious
words.example_en: In a backhanded way, I think a lot of my energy and strength comes from my campaigning.
words.example_zh: 我觉得我的活力和力量很多都间接来自于我的竞选运动。

words.definition_en: v. to feel great hatred for
words.example_en: All the teachers abominate cheating in examinations.
words.example_zh: 所有教师都憎恶考试作弊。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not uniform (2)adj. not level or smooth
words.example_en: He staggered on the uneven surface of the car park.
words.example_zh: 他摇摇晃晃地行走在停车场凹凸不平的地面上。

words.definition_en: v. to repeat without variation
words.example_en: Children from certain backgrounds tend to be stereotyped by their teachers.
words.example_zh: 教师往往刻板地根据学生的某些背景把他们归为某一类学生。

words.definition_en: adj. worldwide in scope; promoting unity among Christian churches
words.example_en: Habitat for Humanity International, founded in 1976, is a nonprofit, ecumenical housing ministry.
words.example_zh: 国际仁人家园成立于 1976 年，是一个非营利性的跨教派住房事工组织。

words.definition_en: adj. capable of being impeached or accused
words.example_en: Thus, Congress cannot remove an executive official except for impeachable offenses.
words.example_zh: 因此，除非有可弹劾的行为，否则国会不能罢免行政官员。

words.definition_en: adj. malicious
words.example_en: It was very spiteful of him to do that.
words.example_zh: 他干那件事是怀有恶意的。

words.definition_en: adj. being about to appear or to be produced or made available
words.example_en: The party is all geared up for the forthcoming election campaign.
words.example_zh: 该党为即将来临的竞选活动做好了准备.

words.definition_en: adj. not interesting or exciting
words.example_en: It tasted bland and insipid, like warmed cardboard.
words.example_zh: 这吃起来淡而无味，像加热过的纸板。

words.definition_en: v. to prove or show to be just, right, or reasonable
words.example_en: He tried to justify his absence with lame excuses.
words.example_zh: 他想用站不住脚的借口为自己的缺席辩解。

words.definition_en: v. to make an exact copy of in order to trick people
words.example_en: The gang counterfeited banknotes and used them in local shops.
words.example_zh: 该团伙伪造钞票，并在当地商店使用。

words.definition_en: n. the origin or source of something
words.example_en: They try to understand the whole universe, its provenance and fate.
words.example_zh: 他们试图认识整个宇宙，其起源及命运。

words.definition_en: n. a disadvantage that makes achievement unusually difficult
words.example_en: Not speaking the language proved to be a bigger handicap than I'd imagined.
words.example_zh: 事实证明不会讲这种语言造成的障碍比我所想象的大。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. having a coarse uneven surface that is rough or unpleasant to the touch (2)adj. unduly exacting (3)adj. severe or cruel
words.example_en: The harsh truth is that luck plays a big part in who will live or die.
words.example_zh: 残酷的事实是，谁死谁活在很大程度上要靠运气。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to limit or restrict (2)v. to use pressure to force to do something
words.example_en: Criticism tends to undermine and constrain the artist's creativity.
words.example_zh: 批评家总是削弱和限制艺术家的创造力。

words.definition_en: adj. kind and friendly
words.example_en: Orlando is a genial man.
words.example_zh: 奥兰多是一位和蔼可亲的人。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to suppress or extinguish summarily and completely (2)v. to nullify especially by judicial action
words.example_en: The rumours were quickly quashed.
words.example_zh: 流言很快被制止了。

words.definition_en: adj. obstinately defiant of authority or restraint
words.example_en: The university suspended the most recalcitrant demonstrators.
words.example_zh: 该大学对最为桀骜不驯的学生做了暂令停学的处理。

words.definition_en: n. something by which one is entangled, involved in difficulties, or impeded
words.example_en: Given data which are free from bias there are further snares to avoid in statistical work.
words.example_zh: 即使数据本身不存在偏差在统计工作中仍需要避免其他陷阱。

words.definition_en: n. the practice of not doing or having something that is wanted or enjoyable
words.example_en: The meaning of life was freedom and the abstinence from evil.
words.example_zh: 生命的真谛是自由和避免作恶。

words.definition_en: adj. cheerful, optimistic
words.example_en: The Defense Secretary gave an upbeat assessment of the war so far.
words.example_zh: 国防部长对迄今为止的战事进行了乐观的评价。

words.definition_en: adv. by surprise
words.example_en: Cross was a little taken aback by her abrupt manner.
words.example_zh: 她鲁莽的态度让克罗斯感到有点吃惊。

words.definition_en: v. to empty of a principal substance
words.example_en: They fired in long bursts, which depleted their ammunition.
words.example_zh: 他们长时间开火，耗尽了弹药。

words.definition_en: n. an exclusive or special right, power, or privilege
words.example_en: Constitutional changes are exclusively the prerogative of the parliament.
words.example_zh: 修改宪法是议会独有的权力。

words.definition_en: adj. not mixed with something else
words.example_en: After the steepest decline since the Great Depression, unalloyed optimism among veteran stock market hands is hard to find.
words.example_zh: 股市发生了大萧条以来最大跌幅之后，资深股市老手中是很难找到纯粹的乐观情绪的。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a feeling of like or dislike for someone or something especially when it is not reasonable or logical (2)n. preconceived judgment or opinion
words.example_en: There was a deep-rooted racial prejudice long before the two countries went to war.
words.example_zh: 早在两国交战之前就有了根深蒂固的种族偏见。

words.definition_en: n. a family tree
words.example_en: Stemma is a scroll recording the genealogy of a family and the stories of key members.
words.example_zh: 家谱即记载本族世系、重要人物事迹的书。

words.definition_en: v. to soil, stain, corrupt, or infect by contact or association
words.example_en: Aflatoxins are carcinogenic fungal toxins that can contaminate corn, peanuts and other food crops such as tree nuts under certain conditions.
words.example_zh: 黄曲霉毒素是致癌的真菌毒素，可在某些条件下污染玉米、花生及其他粮食作物，如树坚果。

words.definition_en: n. a lack of knowledge, understanding, or education
words.example_en: I was alarmed by his naivety and ignorance of international affairs.
words.example_zh: 他对国际事务缺乏经验且无知，这很让我吃惊。

words.definition_en: n. the state of being very annoyed or upset
words.example_en: She rolled her eyes in sheer exasperation.
words.example_zh: 她气急败坏地转动着眼珠。

words.definition_en: n. one that occupies a particular position or place
words.example_en: The challenger defeated the incumbent by a large margin.
words.example_zh: 挑战者以大幅优势击败了现任者。

words.definition_en: adj. having wide or considerable extent
words.example_en: Extensive research has been done into this disease.
words.example_zh: 对这种疾病已进行了广泛研究。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. musingly or dreamily thoughtful (2)adj. suggestive of sad thoughtfulness
words.example_en: She grew pensive as she watched the rain.
words.example_zh: 她望着雨，渐渐陷入沉思。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to cause or force to leave a position of power, a competition, etc. (2)v. to take the place of
words.example_en: In this African country, the rebels finally managed to oust the government from power.
words.example_zh: 在这个非洲国家，反叛者最后总算推翻了政府。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing qualities that are considered good in business
words.example_en: This activity was carried on in a businesslike manner.
words.example_zh: 这个活动开展的效率很高。

words.definition_en: adj. feeling or showing regret for bad behavior
words.example_en: She assumed a contrite expression.
words.example_zh: 她装出一副后悔的表情。

words.definition_en: v. to become dry and sapless
words.example_en: As the leaves of autumn wither and fall, so has my own life become barren.
words.example_zh: 我的生命就如秋叶般枯萎凋零。

words.definition_en: adj. emphasizing that it is better or greater than anything else of its kind
words.example_en: In another literary form, poetry, the Bible is unsurpassed.
words.example_zh: 在另一种文学形式即诗歌方面来看, 《圣经》是无与伦比的。

words.definition_en: adj. unknown
words.example_en: It marks a largely uncharted area of medical science.
words.example_zh: 这标志着医学中一个几乎全新的领域。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to think of (something that will or might happen in the future) (2)v. to do something before someone else
words.example_en: At the time we couldn't have anticipated the result of our campaigning.
words.example_zh: 那时我们不可能预料到我们这项运动的结果。

words.definition_en: n. great technical skill (as in the practice of a fine art)
words.example_en: At that time, his virtuosity on the trumpet had no parallel in jazz.
words.example_zh: 那时，他高超的小号吹奏技巧在爵士乐界无人能比。

words.definition_en: v. to forgive or approve
words.example_en: Terrorism can never be condoned.
words.example_zh: 决不能容忍恐怖主义。

words.definition_en: v. to take away the peace or tranquility of
words.example_en: The bad news disquiets me a great deal.
words.example_zh: 这个坏消息使我非常不安。

words.definition_en: adj. of doubtful authenticity
words.example_en: This may well be an apocryphal story.
words.example_zh: 这很可能是个杜撰的故事。

words.definition_en: v. to control the course of
words.example_en: He managed to steer the conversation away from his divorce.
words.example_zh: 他设法把话题从他离婚一事上引开。

words.definition_en: v. to set in motion
words.example_en: There was one teacher who really ignited my interest in words
words.example_zh: 曾经有一位老师真正激起了我对文字的兴趣。

words.definition_en: v. to dig up out of or as if out of the earth
words.example_en: Other financial scandals are out there waiting to be unearthed.
words.example_zh: 其他金融丑闻正有待于被揭发。

words.definition_en: adj. causing unhappiness or sad feelings
words.example_en: It is a dreary little town where few would choose to linger.
words.example_zh: 这是个沉闷的小镇，没几个人会愿意在此流连。

words.definition_en: adj. very attractive or pleasing in a way that holds your attention
words.example_en: Many people were attracted by her engaging smile.
words.example_zh: 许多人被她迷人的微笑所吸引。

words.definition_en: n. the spirit and enthusiasm animating artistic composition or performance
words.example_en: She reveled in big MGM musicals with their colour and verve.
words.example_zh: 她酷爱米高梅电影公司制作的色彩绚丽、激情四溢的大型歌舞片。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by assurance and self-control
words.example_en: Donald Duck-the irascible but unflappable waterfowl-prepared to be feted in grand fashion,as he turned 75 years old Tuesday.
words.example_zh: 本周是唐老鸭的75岁生日，这位脾气暴躁却镇定自若的鸭先生将迎来盛大的生日庆典。

words.definition_en: n. an amount that is too much or more than you need
words.example_en: Rationing had put an end to a surfeit of biscuits long ago.
words.example_zh: 定量供应很久以前就结束了饼干过剩的状况。

words.definition_en: adj. not original
words.example_en: One heart expert thinks this study provides further evidence of the dangers of secondhand smoke.
words.example_zh: 一位心脏专家认为这一研究提供了间接吸烟危险性的进一步证据。

words.definition_en: v. to charge with a fault or offense
words.example_en: The senator was indicted for murder.
words.example_zh: 那位参议员被控犯谋杀罪。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by firm determination
words.example_en: He described the situation as very dangerous and called for resolute action.
words.example_zh: 他将形势描述得非常危险，并号召采取果断行动。

words.definition_en: v. to throw in between or among other things
words.example_en: He listened thoughtfully, interjecting only the odd word.
words.example_zh: 他细心地聆听，只插了几句话。

words.definition_en: (1)n. an artist, performer, or player of consummate skill (2)n. one having control (3)v. to become skilled or proficient in the use of
words.definition_zh: 能手有控制权的人精通
words.example_en: She was a master of the English language.
words.example_zh: 她是英文高手。

words.definition_en: adj. clearly expressed or understood
words.example_en: The close-up photography and commentary are clear and unambiguous.
words.example_zh: 那组特写照片和附带的评论一目了然，毫无含糊之处。

words.definition_en: (1) adj. complete and total (2) v. to say
words.example_en: To my utter amazement she agreed.
words.example_zh: 令我大感意外的是，她同意了。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by transparency
words.example_en: The limpid lake is like a mirror.
words.example_zh: 湖面同明镜一样清澈。

words.definition_en: adj. done with or showing artistic skill
words.example_en: There is also an artful contrast of shapes.
words.example_zh: 形状的对比也很巧妙。

words.definition_en: v. to ruin or diminish the perfection or wholeness of
words.example_en: A number of problems marred the smooth running of this event.
words.example_zh: 许多问题破坏了该事件的顺利进行。

words.definition_en: adj. easily led or influenced
words.example_en: Steel in this class is tough, ductile and easily machined.
words.example_zh: 这种钢强韧, 延展性好,而且容易加工。

words.definition_en: v. to suffer violent internal excitement
words.example_en: She grinned derisively while I seethed with rage
words.example_zh: 我怒火中烧，她却嘲弄地咧着嘴笑。

words.definition_en: v. to understand
words.example_en: Whenever she failed to comprehend she invariably laughed.
words.example_zh: 每当她听不懂的时候，她总是会笑。

words.definition_en: adj. extremely silly, foolish, or unreasonable
words.example_en: It's patently absurd not to wear a coat in such cold weather.
words.example_zh: 这么冷的天气不穿外衣，显然很荒唐。

words.definition_en: adj. very small
words.example_en: There has been a minute improvement in the working conditions of the factory.
words.example_zh: 工厂的工作情形已有微小的改善。

words.definition_en: adj. unusual or surprising: likely to be noticed
words.example_en: There was a remarkable resemblance between him and Pete.
words.example_zh: 他和皮特之间有着惊人的相似之处。

words.definition_en: adj. open to attack or damage
words.example_en: Lacking self-confidence and maturity, many teenagers are left feeling very vulnerable.
words.example_zh: 许多十几岁的孩子既缺乏自信又不够懂事，所以感情上十分脆弱。

words.definition_en: n. a viewpoint that traditional values and beliefs are unfounded and that existence is senseless and useless
words.definition_zh: 虚无主义（认为一切没有价值或意义）
words.example_en: The novel explores the despair of modern nihilism.
words.example_zh: 这部小说探讨了现代虚无主义所带来的绝望。

words.definition_en: n. a mixture of different things
words.example_en: Peter Baker's bathroom is a brilliant pastiche of expensive interior design.
words.example_zh: 彼得·贝克的浴室是高档室内设计的集大成之作。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a sudden invasion or attack (2)n. an entering in or into (something, such as an activity or undertaking)
words.example_en: The English incursion was doomed from the outset.
words.example_zh: 从一开始，英格兰人的入侵就注定了失败。

words.definition_en: v. to cease marching or journeying
words.example_en: They halted at a short distance from the house.
words.example_zh: 他们在离房子不远处停了下来。

words.definition_en: adj. taking up the attention completely
words.definition_zh: 引人入胜的，非常有趣的
words.example_en: He told us an engrossing story.
words.example_zh: 他给我们讲了一个引人入胜的故事。

words.definition_en: v. to change direction or course
words.example_en: The plane veered off the runway and crashed through the perimeter fence.
words.example_zh: 这架飞机突然偏离了跑道并且冲断了周边的护栏。

words.definition_en: adj. enormously or inconceivably large or great
words.example_en: Astronomical sums of money will be needed for this river tunnel plan.
words.example_zh: 这条过江隧道的计划需要极大的一笔钱。

words.definition_en: adj. inquisitive in an annoying, officious, or meddlesome way
words.example_en: Imelda might think she was prying.
words.example_zh: 艾美达可能认为她在刺探别人的隐私。

words.definition_en: adj. feeling or showing deep hatred or disapproval
words.example_en: She cast a contemptuous glance at him.
words.example_zh: 她用轻蔑的目光扫了他一眼。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing a generous and kind nature
words.example_en: I was prepared to be magnanimous, prepared to feel compassion for him.
words.example_zh: 我准备表现得宽宏大量，同情他的境遇。

words.definition_en: adj. very harsh or severe
words.example_en: He is given to making scathing remarks.
words.example_zh: 他这人说起话来老这么尖酸刻薄。

words.definition_en: adj. including or involving many things
words.example_en: The new government has promised sweeping reforms.
words.example_zh: 新政府承诺进行彻底的改革。

words.definition_en: (1)n. lack of vitality (2)n. a condition in which the blood is deficient in red blood cells, in hemoglobin, or in total volume
words.example_en: Victims of this disease suffer from a severe and usually fatal anemia.
words.example_zh: 这种疾病的患者常患严重的,往往是致命性的贫血症。

words.definition_en: adj. having a bad smell
words.example_en: Presently, most of the ground water in China gradually tend to be malodorous black river due to the deteriorating pollution.
words.example_zh: 目前我国大部分城市地表水由于污染日趋严重已经演变成黑臭水体。

words.definition_en: v. to ask in a serious and emotional way
words.example_en: Trevor Steven entreated them to delay their departure.
words.example_zh: 弗·史蒂文恳求他们晚点走。

words.definition_en: adj. very flowery in style
words.example_en: Her usually florid complexion seemed drained of colour.
words.example_zh: 她通常红润的脸色看上去没有了血色

words.definition_en: v. to give new life or vigor to
words.example_en: This hair conditioner is excellent for revitalizing dry, lifeless hair.
words.example_zh: 这种护发素能够非常有效地修复干枯的头发。

words.definition_en: adj. having little or no importance or significance
words.example_en: You know, this may sound petty to you.
words.example_zh: 你知道，这听起来对你是不重要的。

words.definition_en: n. a product of disintegration, destruction, or wearing away
words.example_en: A channel chokes in its own detritus.
words.example_zh: 河道被它自己的碎屑物质堵塞。

words.definition_en: n. a skill or instinctive ability to appreciate or make good use of something
words.example_en: Helen Keller has a flair for languages.
words.example_zh: 海伦·凯勒有语言的天赋。

words.definition_en: v. to put or keep apart
words.example_en: The two countries have severed all diplomatic links.
words.example_zh: 两国断绝了一切外交关系。

words.definition_en: adj. difficult to understand
words.example_en: He has issued a short, cryptic statement denying the spying charges.
words.example_zh: 他发表了一个简短隐晦的声明，否认对其间谍行为的指控。

words.definition_en: adj. very clear and easy to understand
words.example_en: His explanation was lucid and to the point.
words.example_zh: 他的解释扼要易懂。

words.definition_en: adj. showing great care, attention and effort
words.example_en: My grandpa has been assiduous in learning foreign language.
words.example_zh: 爷爷一直很努力地学习外语。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to avoid or escape by being quick, skillful, or clever (2)v. to fail to be understood or remembered by
words.example_en: Professor Ma proposed a metaphor that eluded them.
words.example_zh: 马教授提出了一个他们理解不了的隐喻

words.definition_en: v. to give spiritual insight to
words.example_en: I hope the results of my research will enlighten my colleagues.
words.example_zh: 我希望我的研究成果会对我的同事们有所启发。

words.definition_en: adj. causing or tending to cause dizziness
words.example_en: From the path there was a vertiginous drop to the valley below.
words.example_zh: 从小路向谷底望去令人眩晕。

words.definition_en: adj. unduly adhering to one’s own opinion or to preconceived notions
words.example_en: He is too opinionated to listen to anyone else.
words.example_zh: 他太固执己见，听不进别人的意见。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by great liberality or generosity
words.example_en: He is one of the country's most munificent artistic benefactors.
words.example_zh: 他是这个国家最慷慨的艺术资助人之一。

words.definition_en: v. to abolish by authoritative action
words.example_en: The next prime minister could abrogate the treaty.
words.example_zh: 下一任首相可能会废除这个条约。

words.definition_en: n. something (such as a state of affairs) that alternates between opposites
words.example_en: The pendulum has swung back and the American car companies have made dramatic advances in safety.
words.example_zh: 三十年河东，三十年河西，美国汽车公司在安全方面已经取得了巨大进步。

words.definition_en: adj. not ostentatious
words.example_en: It's classic Amazon: unostentatious, and no company signs on any of the buildings.
words.example_zh: 这就是经典的亚马逊：朴素而不声张，在楼上没有任何公司的标识。

words.definition_en: adj. being not original and having been used many times before in similar situations
words.example_en: His paintings are contrived and formulaic.
words.example_zh: 他的绘画作品做作而又俗套。

words.definition_en: n. a sensation of motion in which the individual or the individual's surroundings seem to whirl dizzily
words.example_en: If you have vertigo it seems as if the whole room is spinning round you.
words.example_zh: 如果你头晕，就会觉得整个房间都旋转起来。

words.definition_en: adj. remaining the same, and not likely to change
words.example_en: He decided to lead a more settled life with his partner.
words.example_zh: 他决定和爱人一起过一种更加安定的生活。

words.definition_en: n. the respect and admiration that someone or something gets for being successful or important
words.example_en: It was his responsibility for foreign affairs that gained him international prestige.
words.example_zh: 是他在外交事务中的尽职尽责为他赢得了国际声望。

words.definition_en: v. to adapt to a new purpose or need
words.example_en: Much of this business involves retrofitting existing planes.
words.example_zh: 这项业务主要是改造更新现有的飞机。

words.definition_en: adj. pointless or useless
words.example_en: It is always futile to try to hold back the progress of history.
words.example_zh: 要想阻止历史潮流是徒劳的。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to lose its value or to have no effect
words.example_en: A missing or illegible name will nullify your vote.
words.example_zh: 遗漏或字迹难以辨认的名字会使你的投票无效。

words.definition_en: adj. to have a pleasant tune
words.example_en: She spoke in a quietly melodious voice.
words.example_zh: 她说话轻声细语，嗓音甜美。

words.definition_en: adj. expressing a lot of emotion
words.definition_zh: 溢于言表的，感情丰富的
words.example_en: He was effusive in his praise for the general.
words.example_zh: 他对那位将军极尽溢美之词。

words.definition_en: adj. elegant or graceful in manner, appearance, or shape
words.example_en: There are not as many genteel young men in Devonshire as Sussex.
words.example_zh: 与苏塞克斯相比，德文郡有教养的年轻人不多。

words.definition_en: adj. of, relating to, or resulting from the influence of human beings on nature
words.example_en: Since the influences of industrial and anthropogenic activities, the content of atmospheric aerosols increased obviously.
words.example_zh: 由于工业和人类活动的影响，大气中气溶胶含量明显增加。

words.definition_en: n. a coming or flowing together, meeting, or gathering at one point
words.example_en: The 160-metre falls mark the dramatic confluence of the rivers Nera and Velino.
words.example_zh: 内拉河和韦利诺河的汇合处是落差达160米的瀑布，景象十分壮观。

words.definition_en: adj. noisily insistent
words.example_en: They are clamorous for better pay.
words.example_zh: 他们吵吵嚷嚷要求增加工资。

words.definition_en: v. to continue to do something or to try to do something even though it is difficult or other people want you to stop
words.example_en: When I set my mind to something, I persist.
words.example_zh: 一旦我对某事拿定主意，我就会坚持到底。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by or suggestive of a state of sleep or lethargy
words.example_en: Lord Henry turned and looked at the duchess with his slumberous eyes.
words.example_zh: 亨利勋爵转过头来，用昏昏欲睡的眼睛望着公爵夫人。

words.definition_en: adj. readily understood
words.example_en: The company has to make its accounts and operations as transparent as possible.
words.example_zh: 公司必须使其账目与运作尽可能地清晰透明。

words.definition_en: v. banish, exile
words.example_en: She was expatriated for some political reason.
words.example_zh: 她因某种政治原因而被流放国外。

words.definition_en: n. the payment of damages
words.example_en: There is a clear demand amongst victims for some sort of reparation from offenders.
words.example_zh: 受害者明确要求责任人给予一定的赔偿。

words.definition_en: v. to deprive of something
words.example_en: The recent outbreak of hepatitis bereaved the happy family of three members.
words.example_zh: 最近暴发的肝炎使这个幸福的家庭丧失了三条性命。

words.definition_en: v. to crumble into particles
words.example_en: Her grandmother's shawl had moldered away in the trunk.
words.example_zh: 她奶奶的围巾已经在箱子里腐烂了。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing a lack of hope for the future
words.example_en: Not everyone is so pessimistic about the future.
words.example_zh: 不是每个人都对未来如此悲观。

words.definition_en: v. to officially force to leave a place or organization
words.example_en: An American academic was expelled from the country yesterday.
words.example_zh: 昨天一位美国学者被逐出该国。

words.definition_en: v. to cleanse of something morally harmful, offensive, or erroneous
words.example_en: My favorite writer heavily expurgated the work in the second edition.
words.example_zh: 在第二版作品中，我最喜爱的那位作家进行了大幅删减。

words.definition_en: adj. clearly true and requiring no proof or explanation
words.example_en: This point is self-evident and needs no elaboration.
words.example_zh: 含义自明，无须多说。

words.definition_en: v. to examine or consider with attention and in detail
words.example_en: If you peruse the software industry, you'll find hundreds of commercial plug-ins and products for Eclipse.
words.example_zh: 如果你仔细的研究软件业，你会发现成百上千的Eclipse的商用插件和产品。

words.definition_en: v. bypass, evade
words.example_en: He was trying to sidestep responsibility.
words.example_zh: 他正试图回避责任。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a person who belongs to the same age group or social group as someone else (2)v. to look searchingly at something difficult to discern
words.definition_zh: 同龄人，同地位的人凝视
words.example_en: His engaging personality made him popular with his peers.
words.example_zh: 他迷人的个性使他深受同龄人的喜爱。

words.definition_en: v. to say that you accept or do not deny the truth or existence of
words.example_en: The organizers of the demonstration concede that they hadn't sought permission for it.
words.example_zh: 游行示威的组织者承认他们并未申请批准这次活动。

words.definition_en: n. a person who has very strong feelings about something and who wants other people to have those feelings
words.example_en: I am an index believer, but not a zealot.
words.example_zh: 我本人虽然也相信指数投资，但还没那么狂热。

words.definition_en: conj. although
words.example_en: China slowdown would bring some benefits for Asia, albeit small ones.
words.example_zh: 中国经济增长放缓会为亚洲带来了一些好处，即使是些小的好处。

words.definition_en: v. to show or promise something that someone really wants, but then not allow them to have it
words.example_en: The tantalizing aroma of fresh coffee wafted towards them.
words.example_zh: 新鲜咖啡那诱人的香味向他们飘来。

words.definition_en: adj. cheap and gaudy in appearance or quality
words.example_en: These two colours look tawdry when put together.
words.example_zh: 这两种颜色配起来显得很俗丽。

words.definition_en: n. laborious or intensive study
words.example_en: In order to meet the need of our country railroad's development, lucubration of tunnel aerodynamic effect's spread abroad rules and corresponding alleviation measure is very necessary.
words.example_zh: 为了适应我国铁路发展的需要，有必要对隧道空气动力学效应的规律及其减缓措施进行深入研究。

words.definition_en: adj. doing something involves the use of logical reasoning
words.definition_zh: 善于分析的，冷静客观的
words.example_en: Programming is for analytical mind.
words.example_zh: 编程适合善于分析的人。

words.definition_en: adj. limited in character or scope
words.example_en: The police said the murder was sectarian.
words.example_zh: 警方说这是一起派系谋杀。

words.definition_en: (1)n. trap, snare (2)n. a hidden or not easily recognized danger or difficulty
words.example_en: The pitfalls of working abroad are numerous.
words.example_zh: 到国外工作存在许多陷阱。

words.definition_en: v. to excite or arouse especially by a provocation, challenge, or rebuff
words.example_en: This phenomenon piqued Dr Morris' interest.
words.example_zh: 这一现象激起了莫里斯医生的兴趣。

words.definition_en: n. someone or something that is very exaggerated in a funny or foolish way
words.example_en: He had unfairly presented a caricature of my views.
words.example_zh: 他歪曲了我的观点。

words.definition_en: adj. staying strong and in good condition over a long period of time
words.example_en: He bought himself a pair of durable trousers.
words.example_zh: 他给自己买了一条耐穿的裤子。

words.definition_en: adj. highly objectionable
words.example_en: Governments around the world will strongly criticize the villainous crimes of the terrorists.
words.example_zh: 世界各国政府都会强烈指责恐怖分子的罪恶行为。

words.definition_en: adj. pulsating with life, vigor, or activity
words.example_en: The vibrant blue contrasts so well with the yellows and the reds, making the whole scene alive with vitality.
words.example_zh: 充满生气的蓝色与黄色和红色形成了很好的反差，使整个场景栩栩如生。

words.definition_en: v. to think of as coming from a specified cause, source or author
words.example_en: The actor ascribed his success to the support of his family.
words.example_zh: 这位演员将他的成功归功于家人的支持。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to feel happy or confident
words.example_en: The party was buoyed by an election victory.
words.example_zh: 竞选获胜，该党情绪一片高涨。

words.definition_en: adj. not understood or known by many people
words.example_en: Her poems are modishly experimental in style and recondite in subject matter.
words.example_zh: 她的诗在风格上时尚朦胧，在主题上深奥难懂。

words.definition_en: n. regard for or consideration of oneself or one's own interests
words.example_en: With such self - regard as you display, what room is left for the public's love?
words.example_zh: 像你这样表现自私的人心里哪里还放得下公众的爱戴?

words.definition_en: (1)v. to lose vigor, health, or flesh (2)v. to yearn intensely and persistently especially for something unattainable
words.example_en: Men were also more likely to say I love you first and to pine after their first love.
words.example_zh: 男性更可能首先说我爱你，并在初恋结束后更易憔悴痛苦。

words.definition_en: v. to destroy the self-possession or self-confidence
words.example_en: The girl was abashed by the laughter of her classmates.
words.example_zh: 那小姑娘因同学的哄笑而局促不安。

words.definition_en: n. mummery, mumbo jumbo
words.example_en: Samantha hated the flummery of public relations.
words.example_zh: 萨曼莎讨厌公关工作中的虚夸恭维。

words.definition_en: adj. including a multitude of individuals
words.example_en: There is a multitudinous array of chemical substances that exist in the natural world.
words.example_zh: 自然界中存在着各种各样的化学物质。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. having no definite or clear shape or form (2)adj. of no particular type
words.definition_zh: 无固定形状的难归类的
words.example_en: The cloud image indicated something amorphous, intangible, but still necessary to include in the diagram.
words.example_zh: 云镜像常被用来表示无固定形状的、不清晰但又必须包含在图表中的一些内容。

words.definition_en: v. to make weaker or less in amount, effect or force
words.example_en: You could never eliminate risk, but preparation and training could attenuate it
words.example_zh: 风险不可能完全消除，但可以通过防范和培训来降低。

words.definition_en: v. to convey in addition to exact explicit meaning
words.example_en: The critique of religion as a social phenomenon did not connote a dismissal of the issues behind it.
words.example_zh: 去批判作为一种社会现象的宗教并不意味着对它背后那些话题的否弃。

words.definition_en: v. to prove that someone is not guilty of doing something wrong
words.example_en: He was exculpated by the testimony of several witnesses.
words.example_zh: 几名目击者的证词证明他无罪。

words.definition_en: adj. completely
words.example_en: The response of the audience varied from outright rejection to warm hospitality.
words.example_zh: 观众反应不一，有人完全不能接受，也有人击掌叫好。

words.definition_en: v. to set aside: annul
words.example_en: They need 12 votes to override his veto.
words.example_zh: 他们需要12票来推翻他的否决。

words.definition_en: (1)n. one who is excessively fond of eating and drinking (2)n. one who is heartily interested in good food and drink
words.example_en: As a gourmand known by everyone, Andy knows all the restaurants in the district.
words.example_zh: 作为人人皆知的吃货，安迪知道这个地区所有的饭店。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by grasping meanness and penuriousness
words.example_en: He is miserly with both his time and his money.
words.example_zh: 他对时间和金钱都非常吝啬。

words.definition_en: n. extreme cruelty
words.example_en: The pirates treated their captives with barbarity.
words.example_zh: 盗们残暴地对待他们的俘虏。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by grotesqueness, extravagance, complexity or flamboyance
words.example_en: Some of baroque architecture over the pursuit of luxury boldness, even to the point of tedious piling up.
words.example_zh: 有些复杂的建筑过分追求华贵气魄，甚至到了繁琐堆砌的地步。

words.definition_en: n. one having spiritual or ecclesiastical supervision
words.example_en: He resigned from his post as bishop when the scandal broke.
words.example_zh: 丑闻传开后他辞去了主教职务。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to get caught in or twisted with something else
words.example_en: He became entangled in a series of conflicts with the management.
words.example_zh: 他卷入了与管理层的一系列冲突之中。

words.definition_en: (1)n. very strong disapproval or criticism of a person or thing especially by a large number of people (2)n. something that brings disgrace
words.example_en: His political opinions have attracted the opprobrium of the Left.
words.example_zh: 他的政治观点遭到左派的抨击。

words.definition_en: n. an enthusiastic advocate
words.example_en: He is an evangelist of junk bonds.
words.example_zh: 他是低档风险债券的鼓吹者。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to give the necessary skill or knowledge to do a particular job or activity (2)v. to limit or modify the meaning of
words.example_en: The test qualifies you to drive heavy vehicles.
words.example_zh: 通过这一考试就有资格驾驶重型车辆。

words.definition_en: v. to make longer or to grow longer
words.example_en: Corn is treated when the stalk starts to elongate.
words.example_zh: 在玉米秆开始拔节时，给玉米打药。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. incapable of being realized or achieved (2)adj. having or marked by foresight and imagination
words.definition_zh: 幻想的，不切实际的有眼光的
words.example_en: Ruth is a visionary girl, she spends her time daydreaming.
words.example_zh: 露丝是个好幻想的女孩，她常把时间消磨于做白日梦。

words.definition_en: v. to give a brief summary of something
words.example_en: Let's just recapitulate the essential points.
words.example_zh: 让我们只概述一下基本要点吧。

words.definition_en: n. a route more direct than the one ordinarily taken
words.example_en: He was always looking for a shortcut to fame and fortune.
words.example_zh: 他总是在找成名发财的捷径.

words.definition_en: v. to lay down a rule
words.example_en: Many countries have yet to prescribe such limits.
words.example_zh: 很多国家也对此类限制进行了规定。

words.definition_en: adj. excessively grasping or covetous
words.example_en: Rapacious developers soon bought up the land.
words.example_zh: 不久贪婪的开发商就把这块地全部买下了。

words.definition_en: adj. sharing a border, wall, or point
words.example_en: The house adjacent to ours has been sold, so we have a new neighbour.
words.example_zh: 与我们毗邻的房子卖出去了，因此我们有了一个新邻居。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. consisting of or constituting a single unit (2)adj. constituting a massive undifferentiated and often rigid whole
words.example_en: Mathematics is not a single monolithic structure of absolute truth!
words.example_zh: 数学并不是绝对真理的单一整体结构。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to enter
words.example_en: The secretary ushered me into his office.
words.example_zh: 秘书把我领进他的办公室。

words.definition_en: v. to make nervous
words.example_en: Officials are not normally rattled by any reporter's question.
words.example_zh: 官员们一般不会因为任何记者的提问而紧张不安。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to quickly move away from something shocking, frightening, or disgusting (2)v. to become smaller in amount, size, or value
words.example_en: One child shrinks away from me when I try to talk to him.
words.example_zh: 当我试图和一个孩子说话时，他避开了我。

words.definition_en: adj. no longer useful or acceptable
words.example_en: The tourists snigger at the locals' outdated ways and dress.
words.example_zh: 游客们偷偷地取笑当地人过时的做法和衣着。

words.definition_en: n. the unfair practice by a powerful person of giving jobs and other favors to friends
words.example_en: In America, many small town mayor and city official practice cronyism.
words.example_zh: 在美国，许多小镇的市长和大城市的官员都有任人唯亲的行为。

words.definition_en: n. an ancestor in the direct line
words.example_en: He was the progenitor of a family of distinguished actors.
words.example_zh: 他是一个著名演艺世家的先辈。

words.definition_en: n. great brightness or luster
words.example_en: Never in his life had he gazed on such splendor.
words.example_zh: 他生平从没有见过如此辉煌壮丽的场面。

words.definition_en: adj. careless, slovenly
words.example_en: Slipshod work will never pass muster.
words.example_zh: 草率的工作绝对经不起检查。

words.definition_en: n. a written or typewritten composition or document as distinguished from a printed copy
words.example_en: The lost manuscript was found in a repository in France.
words.example_zh: 丢失的手稿在法国的一个存放处被找到了。

words.definition_en: n. dishonest behavior that is meant to trick someone
words.example_en: Samantha believed he was guilty of duplicity in his private dealings.
words.example_zh: 萨曼莎认为他在为其私底下两面派的行为而心生愧疚。

words.definition_en: v. to utter or pronounce with affectation
words.example_en: The young lady didn't mince their words, and predicted the worst...
words.example_zh: 这位女士直言相告，并做了最坏的预测。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. causing no injury (2)adj. not likely to bother or offend anyone
words.example_en: Even seemingly innocuous words are offensive in certain contexts.
words.example_zh: 甚至看似毫无恶意的言辞在某些语境下都会引起冒犯。

words.definition_en: n. the condition of being secluded
words.example_en: In spite of the extremity of her seclusion she was sane
words.example_zh: 尽管完全与世隔绝，她依然心智健全。

words.definition_en: adj. Marked by unduly fearful of what is foreign and especially of people of foreign origin
words.example_en: The French are proud and highly xenophobic when it comes to cooking.
words.example_zh: 谈到烹饪，法国人会颇为得意并且极端排斥外国菜。

words.definition_en: adj. having particles that easily move and change their relative position without a separation of the mass and that easily yield to pressure
words.example_en: The forehand stroke should be fluid and well balanced.
words.example_zh: 正手击球应该保证动作流畅和平稳。

words.definition_en: n. noisy confusion
words.example_en: She was distracted by a commotion across the street.
words.example_zh: 她因街对面的骚动而心烦意乱。

words.definition_en: adj. comically quaint
words.example_en: Slowly each expression became more quizzical than the last as everyone tried to recollect my name from the New York Times Best Seller List.
words.example_zh: 慢慢地，每个人的表情都变得古怪起来，他们努力回忆在《纽约时报》的畅销书作家中有没有我的名字。

words.definition_en: adj. commonly spoken of
words.example_en: The proverbial calm before the storm.
words.example_zh: 俗话说这是暴风雨前的平静。

words.definition_en: adj. given to grumbling
words.example_en: Your grandmother has nothing to stop her from being bored, grouchy and lonely.
words.example_zh: 你奶奶既无聊又孤独，总是没完没了地发牢骚。

words.definition_en: adj. formal, stiff
words.example_en: His delivery was stilted and occasionally stumbling.
words.example_zh: 他的发言很生硬，有时还打结巴。

words.definition_en: adj. very confident and daring
words.example_en: Your plan of going abroad is an audacious decision.
words.example_zh: 你计划出国是一项大胆的决定。

words.definition_en: n. a feeling of guilt or regret
words.example_en: He has no compunction about relating how he killed his father.
words.example_zh: 他在讲述自己杀害父亲的过程时，没有感到丝毫的内疚。

words.definition_en: adj. not compatible
words.example_en: New computer software is often incompatible with older computers.
words.example_zh: 新的计算机软件往往和旧式计算机不兼容。

words.definition_en: v. to complain about
words.example_en: The poor shouldn't underestimate their own capabilities and constantly bemoan their fate.
words.example_zh: 穷人不应低估自身能力，也不要自怨自艾，哀叹命运。

words.definition_en: v. to adjust to or keep in proper measure or proportion
words.example_en: These chemicals modulate the effect of potassium.
words.example_zh: 这些化学物质可以调节钾的功效。

words.definition_en: adj. of a kink that is appropriate for the situation or purpose
words.example_en: The research indicates that the adding quantity of each additive has a fitting range.
words.example_zh: 研究表明各种添加剂的加入量都有一个适宜的范围。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a symbol written above and to the right of a mathematical expression to indicate the operation of raising to a power (2)n. one that champions, practices, or exemplifies
words.example_en: She was a leading exponent of free trade during her political career.
words.example_zh: 她从政期间是自由贸易的主要倡导者。

words.definition_en: v. to place (different things) side by side
words.example_en: The technique Mr. Wilson uses most often is to juxtapose things for dramatic effect.
words.example_zh: 威尔逊先生最常用的技巧是把事物并列起来以获得戏剧性效果。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to the group of books, plays, poems, etc., that are traditionally considered to be very important
words.example_en: This award strengthened Ballard's status as a canonical writer.
words.example_zh: 这项奖巩固了巴拉德作为一名经典作家地地位。

words.definition_en: adj. forestelling events
words.example_en: This ominous warning soon proved prophetic.
words.example_zh: 这个不祥的警告很快证明是有预见性的。

words.definition_en: v. to spread abroad as though sowing seed
words.example_en: It took years to disseminate information about Aids in Africa.
words.example_zh: 在非洲传播关于艾滋病方面的知识耗时数年。

words.definition_en: n. a mixture especially of incongruous elements
words.example_en: The collection was a motley of styles and eras.
words.example_zh: 这批藏品是各种风格和时代的混合体。

words.definition_en: adj. difficult to understand
words.example_en: His impenetrable eyes and inscrutable countenance give little away.
words.example_zh: 他让人捉摸不透的眼神和深不可测的表情几乎什么也没流露。

words.definition_en: adj. impalpable
words.example_en: There are intangible benefits beyond a rise in the share price.
words.example_zh: 除股价上升之外还有无形利益。

words.definition_en: v. to make a very loud and unpleasant sound
words.example_en: The cats caterwauled outside all night.
words.example_zh: 猫整夜在外面尖声嚎叫。

words.definition_en: v. to muster for a common purpose
words.example_en: The leader rallied the crowd around a common cause.
words.example_zh: 那位领导者把群众团结到共同事业周围。

words.definition_en: n. a usually localized act of popular violence in defiance usually of an established government
words.example_en: Isolated attacks in the north-east of the country have now turned into a full-scale uprising.
words.example_zh: 该国东北部的零星袭击现在已经演变成为全面暴动。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to taxation, public revenues, or public debt
words.example_en: The party was torn apart by wrangles over fiscal policy.
words.example_zh: 该党内部因为在财政政策上争执不休而出现了分裂。

words.definition_en: n. something given up or lost
words.example_en: He was willing to make any sacrifice for peace.
words.example_zh: 他愿意为和平作出任何牺牲。

words.definition_en: v. to throw into confusion
words.example_en: The ambassador was clearly disconcerted by the British reaction.
words.example_zh: 显然，英国的反应让这位大使很不安。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not occupied or employed (2)adj. shiftless, lazy
words.definition_zh: 不工作的，闲置的懒散的
words.example_en: Employees have been idle almost a month because of shortages.
words.example_zh: 由于原料短缺，员工们已将近一个月无事可做。

words.definition_en: v. to break or ignore without hiding what you are doing or showing fear of shame
words.example_en: No one can flout the law with impunity.
words.example_zh: 任何人都不能不受惩罚地藐视法律。

words.definition_en: adj. departing markedly from the natural, the expected, or the typical
words.example_en: The Spanish Steps were crowded with the sellers of grotesque caricatures.
words.example_zh: 西班牙阶梯上挤满了出售荒诞漫画的人。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to exercise continuous sovereign authority over (2)v. to control or guide the actions of
words.example_en: He accused the opposition party of being unfit to govern.
words.example_zh: 他指责反对党不适合治理国事。

words.definition_en: n. a strong feeling of dislike
words.example_en: I never overcame a visceral antipathy for the monarchy.
words.example_zh: 我从未能够消除内心对君主政体的反感。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing an ability to understand things and to make good judgments
words.example_en: He's a shrewd businessman and hard as nails.
words.example_zh: 他是一个既精明又铁石心肠的生意人。

words.definition_en: n. careful use of money so that it is not wasted
words.example_en: They were rightly praised for their thrift and enterprise.
words.example_zh: 他们勤俭节约，积极进取，得到了当之无愧的赞扬。

words.definition_en: adj. overly persistent in request or demand
words.example_en: His victory is an evidence of the power of importunate prayer.
words.example_zh: 他的胜利证明了恳切不懈的祈祷所具有的力量。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to follow a winding or intricate course (2)v. to wander aimlessly or casually without urgent destination
words.example_en: The stream meanders slowly down to the sea.
words.example_zh: 这条小河弯弯曲曲缓慢地流向大海。

words.definition_en: v. show, indicate
words.example_en: He betrayed nervousness by stammering.
words.example_zh: 他说话结结巴巴表明他胆子小。

words.definition_en: v. to require as an antecedent in logic or fact
words.example_en: His argument presupposes that it does not matter who is in power.
words.example_zh: 他的论点前提是谁掌权都无关紧要。

words.definition_en: adj. failing to give proper care and attention
words.example_en: Rumor had it that although Louise lived in a derelict house, he was really very wealthy.
words.example_zh: 传言说虽然路易斯住在破败的房子里，但他其实很有钱。

words.definition_en: adj. extremely interesting
words.example_en: This intriguing book is both thoughtful and informative.
words.example_zh: 这本引人入胜的书既有思想性又富知识性。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to get value or use form (2)v. to use in a way that helps you unfairly
words.example_en: The government and its opponents compete to exploit the troubles to their advantage.
words.example_zh: 政府及其反对派竞相利用这些问题为自己谋利。

words.definition_en: v. to disagree politely with another person’s statement or suggestion
words.example_en: If even a few nations demur or cheat, the whole system can break down.
words.example_zh: 若再有一些国家出现反对或欺骗的情况，那么整个系统都将崩溃。

words.definition_en: adj. likely to have or produce good results
words.example_en: It was not a propitious time to start a new business.
words.example_zh: 那不是营业开张的吉时佳日。

words.definition_en: v. to make rigidly conventional and opposed to change
words.example_en: It reckons that rationing would ossify the farm industry
words.example_zh: 人们认为实行配给制会使农业僵化。

words.definition_en: v. to criticize harshly
words.example_en: He castigated himself for being so stupid.
words.example_zh: 他责怪自己太笨。

words.definition_en: adj. behaving in a way that shows you think you are better than other people because you are from a higher social class or know more than they do
words.example_en: They had a snobbish dislike for their intellectual and social inferiors.
words.example_zh: 他们非常势利，不喜欢智力和社会地位不如自己的人。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. gloomy and somber (2)adj. cold and cutting (3)adj. unsheltered and barren
words.example_en: The weather can be quite bleak on the coast.
words.example_zh: 沿海地区的天气会非常阴冷。

words.definition_en: v. to ask questions or express doubt about
words.example_en: We queried the bill as it seemed far too high.
words.example_zh: 账单上的费用似乎太高了，我们对此表示怀疑。

words.definition_en: adj. involving or expressing moral approval or disapproval
words.example_en: The prime minister has a blind spot on ethical issues.
words.example_zh: 这位首相无视道德问题。

words.definition_en: v. to conform, submit, or adapt (as to a regulation or to another's wishes) as required or requested
words.example_en: The commander said that the army would comply with the ceasefire.
words.example_zh: 指挥官说军队会遵守停火协议。

words.definition_en: adj. full of mystery and difficult to understand
words.example_en: His reply was highly enigmatic.
words.example_zh: 他的回答高深莫测。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to cause to sleep or rest (2)v. to cause to feel safe and relaxed instead of careful and alert (3)n. a brief time when an action or activity stops
words.example_en: The rhythm of the boat lulled her to sleep.
words.example_zh: 小船有节奏的摇动将她送入了梦乡。

words.definition_en: adj. showing dislike or opposition
words.example_en: Cats and mouses are often antagonistic.
words.example_zh: 猫和老鼠是敌对的。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. done or experienced by everyone (2)adj. existing or true at all times or in all places
words.definition_zh: 广泛的普遍适用的，无处不在的
words.example_en: No economic principle is universal.
words.example_zh: 没有任何经济学原理是普遍适用的。

words.definition_en: adj. relating to having a strict and simple way of living that avoids physical pleasure
words.example_en: He lives an ascetic life.
words.example_zh: 他过着苦行的生活。

words.definition_en: v. to assert without proof or before proving
words.example_en: The people arguing for an attack on Iran allege that containment is failing.
words.example_zh: 主张打击伊朗的人断言，遏制政策正走向失败。

words.definition_en: n. a method of employing forces in combat
words.example_en: This was just the latest in a series of delaying tactics .
words.example_zh: 这只是一系列拖延战术中的一个新花招。

words.definition_en: adj. small or narrow in circumference or width in proportion to length or height
words.example_en: Everybody of us admired her slender figure.
words.example_zh: 我们人人都羡慕她的苗条身材。

words.definition_en: v. to enter or escape through an opening usually by a fault or mistake
words.example_en: Helium leaking slowly from the balloon.
words.example_zh: 氦气慢慢地从气球中漏走

words.definition_en: adj. hard to understand
words.example_en: Einstein's theory of relativity is very abstruse.
words.example_zh: 爱因斯坦的相对论非常难懂。

words.definition_en: v. to state or do over again or repeatedly sometimes with wearying effect
words.example_en: We reiterate that such cooperation is inclusive and non-confrontational.
words.example_zh: 我们重申这种合作是包容的、非对抗性的。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. required by a law or rule (2)adj. always or often included as a familiar and expected part of something
words.example_en: It is obligatory for all employees to wear protective clothing.
words.example_zh: 所有员工必须穿防护服装。

words.definition_en: v. to reflect in or as if in a mirror
words.example_en: The book inevitably mirrors my own interests and experiences.
words.example_zh: 这本书不可避免地反映出我个人的喜好与经历。

words.definition_en: n. the act or an instance of conjoining
words.example_en: Now this conjunction of an immense military establishment and a large arms industry is new in the American experience.
words.example_zh: 在美国历史上，这种庞大的军事组织和庞大的军事工业的结合时前所未有的。

words.definition_en: v. to hold onto tightly with your hand
words.example_en: I staggered and had to clutch at a chair for support.
words.example_zh: 我踉踉跄跄地，不得不抓住一把椅子稳住自己。

words.definition_en: n. an element in experience or in artistic representation evoking pity or compassion
words.definition_zh: 引起怜悯或同情的元素
words.example_en: The music is full of pathos.
words.example_zh: 这首曲子充满了哀伤的情调。

words.definition_en: n. an interval of rest or relief
words.example_en: It was some weeks now since they had had any respite from shellfire.
words.example_zh: 他们已经好几周没有在炮火中获得任何喘息的机会了。

words.definition_en: n. a religious discourse delivered in public usually by a member of the clergy as a part of a worship service
words.example_en: At High Mass the priest preached a sermon on the devil.
words.example_zh: 牧师在大弥撒上布道驱邪。

words.definition_en: v. to take vengeance for or on behalf of
words.example_en: He promised to avenge his father's murder.
words.example_zh: 他发誓要报杀父之仇。

words.definition_en: v. to greatly increase the size or amount of
words.example_en: The president also called on nations to redouble their efforts to negotiate an international trade agreement.
words.example_zh: 总统还呼吁各国就签订国际贸易协定加倍努力进行磋商。

words.definition_en: adj. feeling or expressing humble or regretful pain or sorrow for sins or offenses
words.example_en: Those who were penitent obtained absolution.
words.example_zh: 悔过者获得了赦免。

words.definition_en: n. the recorded events of one year
words.example_en: The deed will live forever in the annals of infamy.
words.example_zh: 这事将永远存在丑行史记载中。

words.definition_en: v. to determine or influence the quality or nature of
words.example_en: Too often we try to mold our children into something they do not wish to be.
words.example_zh: 我们常常试图把孩子塑造成他们自己不想成为的样子。

words.definition_en: adj. feeling a lack of energy or a lack of interest in doing things
words.example_en: Anything longer will make you lethargic, but a short nap will refresh you.
words.example_zh: 再睡久一点就会让你感到昏昏沉沉了，但是小睡片刻会让你神清气爽。

words.definition_en: adj. lasting a very short time
words.example_en: These paintings are in some ways a reminder that earthly pleasures are ephemeral.
words.example_zh: 这些画在某种意义上提醒人们世俗的享乐是短暂的。

words.definition_en: n. a setting forth of the meaning or purpose (as of a writing)
words.example_en: His speech was an exposition of the genesis of the conflict.
words.example_zh: 他的演讲阐述了冲突的起源。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to spend or use up wastefully or foolishly (2)v. to cause to spread thin or scatter and gradually vanish
words.example_en: The body must dissipate heat as fast as it produces it.
words.example_zh: 人体散发热量必须和产生热量一样快。

words.definition_en: adj. having great volume in proportion to wight
words.example_en: He struggled out of his bulky jacket and threw it on to the back seat
words.example_zh: 他费劲脱下他那件笨重的夹克，随手把它扔在后座上。

words.definition_en: n. a great disaster
words.example_en: Driving into Tripoli is to drive into a ghost city, you will feel like wondering in a set for a Hollywood apocalypse film.
words.example_zh: 车进入的黎波里就好像进入鬼城，仿佛置身好莱坞灾难影片中的场景。

words.definition_en: adj. consisting of documents: written down
words.example_en: Last Sunday I watched a documentary program about Manchester United.
words.example_zh: 上周日我看了有关曼彻斯特联队的一档纪实节目。

words.definition_en: v. to make slow or difficult
words.example_en: Heavy snow hindered construction work.
words.example_zh: 大雪阻碍了建筑工程的进展。

words.definition_en: v. to provide with a motive
words.example_en: Our challenge is to motivate those voters and inspire them to join our cause.
words.example_zh: 我们面临的挑战是如何调动那些选民的积极性并鼓励他们加入我们的事业。

words.definition_en: v. mishandle, botch
words.example_en: I was afraid you would bungle this assignment but I had no one else to send.
words.example_zh: 我担心这份工作给你搞砸了，可是我再也派不出别人了

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not letting up or weakening in vigor or pace (2)adj. not softening or yielding in determination
words.example_en: An unrelenting downpour of rain disturbed her.
words.example_zh: 一场下不停的倾盆大雨让她心烦意乱。

words.definition_en: n. a feeling of not wanting to do something
words.example_en: They are showing a marked disinclination to pursue these opportunities.
words.example_zh: 他们对争取这些机会明显表现出不乐意。

words.definition_en: adj. causing laughter especially because of a startlingly or unexpectedly humorous impact
words.example_en: Her expression is almost comical.
words.example_zh: 她的表情近乎滑稽。

words.definition_en: adj. full of or covered with grime: dirty
words.example_en: They crept toward the grimy window and looked down.
words.example_zh: 仍然披着隐形衣，悄悄走到满是污垢的窗前向下张望。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not liking or wanting to give or spend money (2)adj. small in size or amount
words.example_en: Winston was not a stingy man.
words.example_zh: 温斯顿不是个小气之人

words.definition_en: adj. going beyond what is usual, regular, or customary
words.example_en: She was a truly extraordinary woman.
words.example_zh: 她是位非常杰出的女性。

words.definition_en: adj. providing amusement or entertainment
words.example_en: It was a witty and diverting program.
words.example_zh: 这是个妙趣横生的娱乐节目。

words.definition_en: adj. not fully worked out or developed
words.example_en: It's just a tentative schedule.
words.example_zh: 仅仅是个试验性的计划。

words.definition_en: adj. noticeable in an unpleasant or annoying way
words.example_en: The sofa would be less obtrusive in a paler color.
words.example_zh: 沙发的颜色再浅一点就不那么扎眼了。

words.definition_en: adj. talking a lot in an energetic and rapid way
words.example_en: The new comer in our company is voluble.
words.example_zh: 我们公司新来的同事很健谈。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. angry or unhappy because of unfair treatment (2)adj. being the opposite of sweet
words.example_en: Mary is very bitter about losing her job.
words.example_zh: 玛丽丢掉了工作，心里很不服气。

words.definition_en: adj. cannot be achieved or is not available
words.definition_zh: 无法获得的，无法达到的
words.example_en: Man approaches the unattainable truth through a succession of errors.
words.example_zh: 人们通过不断犯错误接近不可企及的真理。

words.definition_en: adv. up to this or that time
words.example_en: The polytechnics have hitherto been at an unfair disadvantage in competing for pupils and money.
words.example_zh: 到目前为止，理工专科学校在争取生源和资金方面一直处于劣势。

words.definition_en: adj. cruel, severe
words.example_en: There has been an overall growth in population, despite some draconian efforts to contain it.
words.example_zh: 尽管有严厉的遏制措施,人口还是在全面增长。

words.definition_en: v. to mislead the mind or judgment of
words.example_en: He had been unwittingly deluded by their mystical nonsense.
words.example_zh: 他不知不觉被他们故弄玄虚的胡说八道给骗了。

words.definition_en: adj. heartbreaking
words.example_en: Time dulls the most exquisite emotions and softens the most heartrending grief.
words.example_zh: 时间会缓和最激烈的情感，减轻最伤心的悲痛.

words.definition_en: v. to give or assume the appearance or effect of often with the intent to deceive
words.example_en: I tried to simulate surprise at the news.
words.example_zh: 听到这个消息后，我竭力装出一副吃惊的样子。

words.definition_en: n. basis
words.example_en: Honesty is the bedrock of any healthy relationship.
words.example_zh: 诚实是维持一切良好关系的基础。

words.definition_en: n. good-natured and usually witty and animated joking
words.example_en: Their friendly banter eased the tension.
words.example_zh: 他们友好的玩笑缓和了紧张气氛。

words.definition_en: v. to drink a large amount of quickly
words.example_en: He's quaffed many a glass of champagne in his time.
words.example_zh: 他年轻时曾经开怀畅饮过不少香槟美酒。

words.definition_en: n. descendants, children
words.example_en: He was surrounded by his numerous progeny.
words.example_zh: 众多的子孙簇拥着他。

words.definition_en: n. someone or something that provides help or an advantage
words.example_en: An anonymous benefactor stepped in to provide the prize money.
words.example_zh: 一位匿名捐助者施以援手提供了奖金。

words.definition_en: (1)n. someone or something that makes action or progress slower or more difficult (2)v. to pull (someone or something that is heavy or difficult to move)
words.example_en: Larry was turning out to be a drag on her career.
words.example_zh: 拉里最后成了她事业上的累赘。

words.definition_en: v. to impute guilt to
words.example_en: Someone placed the pistol in your room in order to inculpate you.
words.example_zh: 有人把手枪放在你房间里是为了指控你。

words.definition_en: v. to persist in a state, enterprise, or undertaking in spite of counterinfluences, opposition, or discouragement
words.example_en: But we need to continue to strive, to learn from other countries and persevere.
words.example_zh: 然而，我们也必须力争上游，借鉴其他国家的经验，学习他们不屈不挠的精神。

words.definition_en: v. to hold supported or suspended without motion in a steady position
words.example_en: She tried her best to poise herself.
words.example_zh: 她尽全力使身体保持平衡。

words.definition_en: (1)n. an action that is taken or an order that is given to force a country to obey international laws by limiting or stopping trade with that country, by not allowing economic aid for that country, etc. （ (2)v. to officially accept or allow
words.example_en: He expressed his opposition to the lifting of sanctions.
words.example_zh: 他对解除制裁表示反对。

words.definition_en: adj. producing little or no vegetation
words.example_en: He also wants to use the water to irrigate barren desert land.
words.example_zh: 他也想用这些水灌溉荒漠。

words.definition_en: adj. not able to be explained or understand
words.example_en: His behavior was extraordinary and inexplicable...
words.example_zh: 他的行为不同寻常,让人费解。

words.definition_en: n. slant, bias
words.example_en: The table is at a slight tilt.
words.example_zh: 这桌子有点儿歪。

words.definition_en: n. one who is legally appointed to transact business on another's behalf
words.example_en: He needed to consult with an attorney
words.example_zh: 他需要找个律师咨询一下。

words.definition_en: n. a manner or the content of thinking characteristic of an individual, group, or culture
words.example_en: Such an approach accords with the principles of socialist ideology.
words.example_zh: 这种方法与社会主义意识形态的原则相符。

words.definition_en: adj. impossible to understand
words.example_en: He found his son's actions totally incomprehensible.
words.example_zh: 他发现自己对儿子的行为完全无法理解。

words.definition_en: n. a harsh rebuke
words.example_en: I cant't accept such an unwarranted objurgation.
words.example_zh: 我不会接受这样一种莫须有的责备。

words.definition_en: adj. obvious and noticeable
words.example_en: The tension in the room was almost palpable.
words.example_zh: 屋子里的紧张气氛几乎能感觉到。

words.definition_en: adj. sunk to or existing in a low state or condition
words.example_en: Both of them died in abject poverty.
words.example_zh: 他们两人都于穷困潦倒中死去。

words.definition_en: adj. feeling or showing a lack of courage or confidence
words.example_en: A timid child, Isabella had learned obedience at an early age.
words.example_zh: 伊莎贝拉是一个羞怯的孩子，很小就学会了顺从。

words.definition_en: v. repeat, imitate
words.example_en: Their views often echo each other.
words.example_zh: 他们常常你唱我和。

words.definition_en: n. weariness or exhaustion from labor, exertion, or stress
words.example_en: Severe fatigue forced him to stop working.
words.example_zh: 严重的疲劳迫使他停止工作。

words.definition_en: n. something that indicates or fixes a limit or extent
words.example_en: The boundaries between history and storytelling are always being blurred and muddled.
words.example_zh: 历史和轶闻的分界向来是模糊而混乱的。

words.definition_en: v. engage in sycophancy
words.example_en: They came backstage afterward, cooing and toadying to him.
words.example_zh: 她们随后来到后台，对他娇语谄媚。

words.definition_en: v. to make usually small adjustments in or to
words.example_en: He expects the system to get even better as the engineers tweak its performance.
words.example_zh: 他预计在工程师对其性能稍加改进后，该系统会运行得更加良好。

words.definition_en: n. the state or quality of being inexperienced or unsophisticated
words.example_en: How foolish I was in treating such a profoundly important matter with such simple-minded naiveté.
words.example_zh: 当时的我是多麽地愚蠢，竟然把这麽深远重大的事情，想得那麽天真。

words.definition_en: n. someone who knows a lot about many different things
words.example_en: Mr English, an associate editor of the Guardian, is a polymath who wears his learning lightly.
words.example_zh: 英格力士先生是《卫报》的副编辑，是一位略显才华的博学者。

words.definition_en: adj. happening at the correct or most useful time
words.example_en: This has been a timely reminder to us all.
words.example_zh: 对我们大家来说这个提醒非常及时。

words.definition_en: adj. grandiloquent
words.example_en: Before that oxymoron was a technical term describing a rhetorical device.
words.example_zh: 在此之前，oxymoron是一个技术术语，用来描述一种夸张的装置。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by compact precise expression without wasted words
words.example_en: The book gives an admirably succinct account of the technology and its history.
words.example_zh: 这本书对该项技术及其历史作了极其简明的叙述。

words.definition_en: n. a statement in which you express too much pride in yourself or in something you have, have done, or are connected to in some way
words.example_en: His boast proved to be empty.
words.example_zh: 他的夸口被证明是空话。

words.definition_en: adj. covering completely or broadly
words.example_en: The Rough Guide to Nepal is a comprehensive guide to the region.
words.example_zh: 《尼泊尔概况》是一本全面介绍该地区的旅游指南。

words.definition_en: n. a tactic intended to embarrass or frustrate an opponent
words.example_en: Christmas should be a time of excitement and wonder, not a cynical marketing ploy.
words.example_zh: 圣诞节应该是兴奋和美妙的时刻，而不该是一种肆无忌惮的营销策略。

words.definition_en: adj. satisfied with how things are and not wanting to change them
