words.example_en: We must not become complacent about progress.
words.example_zh: 我们决不能因进步变得自满。

words.definition_en: adj. significantly serious
words.example_en: Anxiously, she examined his unusually grave face.
words.example_zh: 她忐忑不安地审视着他异常严肃的脸色。

words.definition_en: v. put up with
words.example_en: I could never stomach the cruelty involved in the wounding of animals.
words.example_zh: 我永远都无法忍受伤害动物时的那种残忍。

words.definition_en: adj. not based on facts or proof
words.example_en: The allegations were totally unfounded.
words.example_zh: 这些指控完全没有事实根据。

words.definition_en: v. to give or leave by will
words.example_en: He bequeathed all his money to his children after death.
words.example_zh: 他死后把所有的钱都留给了孩子。

words.definition_en: v. to permit as a right, privilege, or favor
words.example_en: France has agreed to grant him political asylum.
words.example_zh: 法国已经同意给予他政治避难。

words.definition_en: (1)v. by please by or as if by attention or concern (2)v. relieve, alleviate
words.example_en: It did not take long for the central bank to soothe investors' fears.
words.example_zh: 中央银行很快便平息了投资者的担忧。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a large number of harmful or annoying things (2)v. to cause constant or repeated trouble, illness, etc.
words.definition_zh: 让人讨厌的东西困扰，纠缠
words.example_en: She was plagued by weakness, fatigue, and dizziness.
words.example_zh: 她被虚弱、疲劳和眩晕折磨。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a strong written or spoken attack against someone else’s opinions, beliefs, practices, etc. (2)n. the art or practice of using language to defend or harshly criticize something or someone
words.definition_zh: 猛烈抨击态度鲜明（支持或者批评）
words.example_en: The book is both a history and a passionate polemic for tolerance.
words.example_zh: 这本书既是一段历史，也是一篇主张宽容的激情论辩。

words.definition_en: adj. tawdrily and falsely attractive
words.example_en: Advertisements convey an impression, however meretricious, of the importance of the goods being sold.
words.example_zh: 商业广告尽管华而不实，却能使人对正在销售的商品的重要性留有印象。

words.definition_en: adj. producing money or wealth
words.example_en: She has a lucrative business selling leather goods.
words.example_zh: 她做皮货生意, 利润丰厚。

words.definition_en: (1) adj. complete and total (2) adj. not limited in any way
words.example_en: Mankind are the only beings who may be said to have gained an absolute control over the production of food.
words.example_zh: 一切生物之中，只有人类达到了完全控制食物生产的地步。

words.definition_en: (1)n. something that prevents people from acting freely (2)v. to put shackles on
words.example_en: The trade unions are shackled by the law.
words.example_zh: 工会受法律的制约。

words.definition_en: v. to deviate from the truth
words.example_en: British ministers continued to prevaricate.
words.example_zh: 英国的大臣们仍然闪烁其词。

words.definition_en: v. confuse, perplex
words.example_en: Don't befuddle me with all those masses of detail.
words.example_zh: 不要拿一大堆琐事把我搞迷糊。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to take a child of other parents legally as your own child (2)v. to begin to use or have
words.example_en: The council is expected to adopt the new policy at its next meeting.
words.example_zh: 委员会有望在下次会议上正式通过这项新政策。

words.definition_en: adj. delayed beyond the usual time
words.example_en: I'm sorry for this belated translation.
words.example_zh: 对于这篇迟来的翻译，我很抱歉。

words.definition_en: adj. not important
words.example_en: I know it sounds trivial, but I'm worried about it.
words.example_zh: 我知道这事听起来微不足道，但我还是放心不下

words.definition_en: n. a person who spends improvidently or wastefully
words.example_en: My mother warned me about marrying a spendthrift.
words.example_zh: 我妈妈告诫我，不要与挥霍无度的人结婚。

words.definition_en: adj. inflated
words.example_en: The reporting of the hostage story was fair, if sometimes overblown.
words.example_zh: 尽管这篇关于人质的报道有些夸大其词，但还算公正。

words.definition_en: v. to make furious
words.example_en: Their insults infuriated him.
words.example_zh: 他们的侮辱激怒了他。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing sound judgment
words.example_en: Far from being a dreamer, she's a level-headed pragmatist.
words.example_zh: 她是个头脑冷静的务实派，绝不是一个不切实际的人。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a statement saying that you are sorry about something (2)n. something that is said or written to defend something that other people criticize
words.example_en: Union officials made no apologies for the threatened chaos.
words.example_zh: 工会干部们对于迫在眉睫的混乱局面不予道歉。

words.definition_en: v. to reduce or cause to contract
words.example_en: The chancellor was forced to deflate the economy still further.
words.example_zh: 财政大臣被迫更进一步紧缩经济。

words.definition_en: adj. convinced of one's own righteousness especially in contrast with the actions and beliefs of others
words.example_en: He is critical of the monks, whom he considers narrow-minded and self-righteous.
words.example_zh: 他对僧侣们颇有微词，认为他们思想偏狭，自视清高。

words.definition_en: adj. happening very soon
words.example_en: The black clouds show that a storm is imminent.
words.example_zh: 乌云预示暴风雨即将来临。

words.definition_en: n. an alteration designed to correct or improve
words.example_en: Chief editor finally accepted the textual emendations made by associate chief editor.
words.example_zh: 主编最终接受了副编辑的修订文稿。

words.definition_en: adj. based on testing or experience
words.example_en: More than ever, economics today is an empirical discipline.
words.example_zh: 如今，经济学比以往任何时候都是一门实证学科。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to try to keep from happening (2)v. to kill by depriving of air
words.example_en: She summoned up all her pity for him, to smother her self-pity.
words.example_zh: 她抑制着心中的自怜，将满腔的怜悯之情都倾注在了他的身上。

words.definition_en: adj. existing from the time someone is born
words.example_en: It is clear that the ability to smile is inborn.
words.example_zh: 显然，笑的能力是与生俱来的。

words.definition_en: adj. strikingly, excitingly, or mysteriously different or unusual
words.definition_zh: 外来的，异国风情的（含褒义）
words.example_en: The chorus is given oriental coloring by the use of exotic instruments.
words.example_zh: 使用外来乐器给合唱增添了东方色彩。

words.definition_en: adj. of, relating to, or characteristic of a person inflexibly attached toa practice or theory
words.example_en: He is not a doctrinaire socialist.
words.example_zh: 他不是一个教条的社会主义者。

words.definition_en: v. to cause great damage or harm to
words.example_en: A few days before, a fire had devastated large parts of Windsor Castle.
words.example_zh: 几天前，温莎城堡的大部分被一场大火烧毁。

words.definition_en: n. a small amount of something
words.example_en: Even the film's impressive finale can't hide the first hour's paucity of imagination.
words.example_zh: 这部电影的结局可圈可点，即使这样也无法掩盖前一个小时想象力贫乏的缺憾。

words.definition_en: adj. exciting horror
words.example_en: A government split would have dire consequences for domestic peace.
words.example_zh: 政府的分裂将会给国内和平带来严重后果。

words.definition_en: adj. done or used when necessary
words.example_en: Magistrates were given wider discretionary powers.
words.example_zh: 地方法官被赋予了更广泛的自由决定权。

words.definition_en: v. to discover or notice the presence of
words.example_en: Arnold could detect a certain sadness in the old man's face.
words.example_zh: 阿诺德能觉察到老人脸上的一丝悲伤。

words.definition_en: adj. separated from other people or cultures
words.example_en: Having lived in one place all his life, his views are insular.
words.example_zh: 他一辈子住在一个地方，所以思想狭隘。

words.definition_en: adj. rude and showing a lack of respect
words.example_en: I don't like strangers who ask impertinent questions.
words.example_zh: 我不喜欢冒昧提问的陌生人。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to make as small as possible (2)v. to treat or describe as smaller or less important than it is
words.example_en: Concerned people want to minimize the risk of developing cancer.
words.example_zh: 相关人员希望尽可能降低罹癌风险。

words.definition_en: v. to keep separate from something unpleasant, dangerous, etc.
words.example_en: Is there any way we can insulate our home from the noise?
words.example_zh: 有什么办法可以使我们的住宅隔音吗？

words.definition_en: n. the reason or explanation for something
words.example_en: However, the rationale for such initiatives is not, of course, solely economic.
words.example_zh: 然而，这种提议的根据当然并不仅仅是经济方面的。

words.definition_en: v. to begin
words.example_en: The academic year commences at the beginning of October.
words.example_zh: 学年于10月初开始。

words.definition_en: n. a predicament affording no obvious escape
words.example_en: The proposal offered both sides a way out of the diplomatic impasse.
words.example_zh: 这一提议使双方都能摆脫外交僵局。

words.definition_en: adj. feeling or showing doubt about doing something: not willing or eager to do something
words.example_en: Scientific institutions have been reluctant to take corrective action.
words.example_zh: 科学机构一直不愿作出更正。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to leave and never return to (2)v. to give over unrestrainedly
words.example_en: The scheme's investors, fearful of bankruptcy, decided to abandon the project.
words.example_zh: 因为担心破产，该计划的投资者决定放弃这个项目。

words.definition_en: n. a change or a set of changes that corrects or improves something
words.example_en: The phase of writing that is actually most important is revision.
words.example_zh: 实际上写作过程中最重要的阶段是修改。

words.definition_en: v. to get ahead of
words.example_en: He can outstrip his friend both in sports and in studies.
words.example_zh: 他能在体育和学习方面胜过他的朋友。

words.definition_en: adj. working very hard
words.example_en: The Chinese are industrious.
words.example_zh: 中国人是勤劳的。

words.definition_en: n. an emotion variously combining dread, veneration, and wonder that is inspired by authority or by the sacred or sublime
words.example_en: While Diana was in awe of her grandfather, she adored her grandmother.
words.example_zh: 黛安娜敬畏她的祖父，但敬佩她的祖母。

words.definition_en: adj. strongly critical or condemnatory
words.example_en: This was a dreadful crime and a severe sentence is necessary.
words.example_zh: 这一罪行十分恶劣，必须要重判。

words.definition_en: n. a disease or illness
words.example_en: He tried the baths there for an internal malady.
words.example_zh: 他到那儿洗温泉对他的内科病进行浴疗。

words.definition_en: v. to place in a very strong position that cannot easily be changed
words.example_en: These dictators have entrenched themselves politically and are difficult to move.
words.example_zh: 这些独裁者在政治上已经站稳了脚跟，很难推翻他们。

words.definition_en: v. to make worse
words.example_en: It may even compound the problem that it purports to solve and encourage parents to reveal still less.
words.example_zh: 该提议的执行甚至有可能加重其原本要解决的问题，促使父母们更加隐瞒孩子的身世。

words.definition_en: n. a formal inquiry into or discussion of a subject
words.example_en: Built on the significant findings of the disquisition, this thesis raises some correlative and effective suggestions.
words.example_zh: 据研究得出的相关结论，本文提出了有效的相关政策建议。

words.definition_en: adj. acting in a silly or funny way
words.example_en: The antics of wildebeests are often clownish and entertaining.
words.example_zh: 姿态古怪的牛羚常常叫人忍俊不禁。

words.definition_en: v. to stop fighting an enemy or opponent
words.example_en: In less than two hours Cohen capitulated to virtually every demand.
words.example_zh: 还不到两个小时，科恩就投降了，接受了几乎一切要求。

words.definition_en: v. to shape a soft substance by pressing or rolling it or by putting it into a mould
words.example_en: Using 2 spoons, mould the cheese mixture into small balls or ovals.
words.example_zh: 用两把小匙将奶酪混合物塑成圆球或椭圆球形状。

words.definition_en: adj. fashionable, stylish
words.example_en: She is always crazy at modish things.
words.example_zh: 她疯狂热爱流行物品。

words.definition_en: n. symbol, emblem
words.example_en: Black is a token of mourning.
words.example_zh: 黑衣是居丧的表征。

words.definition_en: n. the quality or state of being equal or equivalent
words.example_en: Prison officers are demanding pay parity with the police force.
words.example_zh: 狱警正要求与警察同工同酬。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to talk or act with noisy swaggering threats (2)n. loud, arrogant speech, often full of empty threats
words.example_en: The lady simply ignored his bluster as empty rhetoric.
words.example_zh: 那位女士把他的咆哮只当作虚张声势的言词而不予理睬。

words.definition_en: n. someone who does not specialize in a particular area, activity, field of research, etc.
words.example_en: The nonspecialist will be able to obtain at least a notion of the sorts of data and argument that support evolutionary theory.
words.example_zh: 非专业人士至少可以了解支持进化论的各种数据和观点。

words.definition_en: v. to officially require (something)
words.example_en: He'd been mandated by the West African Economic Community to go in and to enforce a ceasefire
words.example_zh: 他受西非经济共同体的命令介入并执行停火协定。

words.definition_en: n. an undoubted or self-evident truth
words.example_en: Whilst this might sound like a truism, it is nevertheless a crucial problem to address.
words.example_zh: 尽管这可能听起来像是老生常谈，但却是待解决的重要问题。

words.definition_en: n. something that clothes or adorns
words.example_en: This shop sells sports apparel.
words.example_zh: 这家商店卖运动服。

words.definition_en: n. something used to slow down or stop movement or activity
words.example_en: The driver applied the brake suddenly.
words.example_zh: 司机突然踩下刹车。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to state or declare positively and often forcefully or aggressively (2)v. the demonstrate the existence of
words.example_en: The defendants, who continue to assert their innocence, are expected to appeal.
words.example_zh: 各被告仍然宣称自己无辜，并会提起上诉。

words.definition_en: n. a rapid expansion or increase
words.example_en: Living standards improved rapidly during the post-war boom.
words.example_zh: 在战后那段繁荣昌盛的时期里，生活水平得到了迅速提高。

words.definition_en: v. to file or collect in or as if in an archive
words.example_en: The library archived the documents for future research.
words.example_zh: 图书馆将这些文件存档，以供日后研究。

words.definition_en: n. a person who argues for or supports something
words.example_en: Halsey was identified as a leading proponent of the values of progressive education.
words.example_zh: 哈尔西被认为是进步教育价值观的主要拥护者。

words.definition_en: n. the quality or state of someone or something that will never die or be forgotten
words.example_en: The flame is a quasi-religious emblem of immortality.
words.example_zh: 火焰可以算作不朽的宗教象征。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. lamentable (2)adj. deserving censure or contempt
words.example_en: It is deplorable that anyone should come between a child and its parents.
words.example_zh: 如果有人想离间孩子和父母，那将是可悲的。

words.definition_en: v. to assail vigorously or persistently
words.example_en: The town has been heavily bombarded by the army backed by the airforce.
words.example_zh: 该镇遭到了有空军掩护的陆军部队的猛烈攻击。

words.definition_en: v. to gain favor or favorable acceptance for by deliberate effort
words.example_en: He did his best to ingratiate himself with the royal family.
words.example_zh: 他竭力向王室献媚。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to claim to be or do a particular thing when this claim may not be true (2)n. the main or general meaning of something
words.example_en: His plans are not what they purport to be.
words.example_zh: 他的计划并不如他们所声称的那样。

words.definition_en: adj. in favor of using peaceful and diplomatic methods to achieve something, rather than using force and violence
words.example_en: The defense minister is a little bit more dovish than other people in the ruling elite.
words.example_zh: 国防大臣比上层统治集团中其他成员都要温和。

words.definition_en: n. a ruler who exercises absolute power oppressively or brutally
words.example_en: Since 1804 the country has mostly been ruled by tyrants.
words.example_zh: 自 1804 年以来，该国大部分时间都处于暴君的统治之下。

words.definition_en: n. a terrible disaster
words.example_en: From all points of view, war would be a catastrophe.
words.example_zh: 不管从哪方面说，战争都将是场灾难。

words.definition_en: adj. consisting of many different small parts
words.example_en: Any action on the basis of such fragmentary evidence would be foolish.
words.example_zh: 基于如此不完整的证据采取的任何行动都是愚蠢的。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to feel angry or irritated especially for a long time
words.example_en: Their refusal to sell the country arms in 1937 still rankled with him.
words.example_zh: 他们在1937年拒绝向该国出售武器一事仍然令他愤愤不平。

words.definition_en: n. an irrational abject attitude of mind toward the supernatural, nature, or God resulting from superstition
words.example_en: According to superstition, breaking a mirror brings bad luck.
words.example_zh: 按照迷信的说法，摔碎镜子会带来噩运。

words.definition_en: v. to destroy completely
words.example_en: An atomic bomb can annihilate a city.
words.example_zh: 一枚原子弹能毁灭一个城市。

words.definition_en: n. a feeling of weariness and dissatisfaction
words.example_en: He decided to take up a hobby to relieve the ennui of retirement.
words.example_zh: 他决定养成一种嗜好以消除退休后的无聊。

words.definition_en: v. to refrain deliberately and often with an effort of self-denial from an action or practice
words.example_en: His doctor ordered him to abstain from beer and wine.
words.example_zh: 他的医生嘱咐他戒酒。

words.definition_en: adj. of, relating to, or occurring in the night
words.example_en: When there is a full moon, this nocturnal rodent is careful to stay in its burrow.
words.example_zh: 月圆之夜，这种夜间活动的啮齿类动物会小心地呆在地洞里不出来。

words.definition_en: v. to take something away from
words.example_en: This law will deprive us of our most basic rights.
words.example_zh: 这条法律将剥夺我们最基本的权利。

words.definition_en: v. to feel astonishment or perplexity at or about
words.example_en: We all marveled at his success.
words.example_zh: 我们对他的成功感到惊奇。

words.definition_en: adj. boring and too slow or long
words.example_en: The journey soon became tedious.
words.example_zh: 那次旅行不久就变得乏味起来。

words.definition_en: n. the people who have the most wealth and status in a society
words.example_en: A tiny, educated elite profited from the misery of their two million fellow countrymen.
words.example_zh: 一小撮受过良好教育的精英分子从他们200万同胞的贫苦生活中获取利益。

words.definition_en: n. competition
words.example_en: In this way, it effectively shut out all price rivalry.
words.example_zh: 用这种方法有效地排斥了一切价格竞争。

words.definition_en: adj. telling private information about a person’s life
words.example_en: Their first album was painfully frank to the point of being confessional.
words.example_zh: 他们的第一张专辑是那么的坦率，近乎自白。

words.definition_en: v. to discourage
words.example_en: But the reality of these formidable challenges should not dishearten us.
words.example_zh: 但是，这些巨大挑战的现实不应使我们心灰意冷。

words.definition_en: adj. using too many words
words.example_en: Her writing style is tediously prolix.
words.example_zh: 她写的文章冗长而乏味。

words.definition_en: adj. consisting of two parts
words.example_en: The system uses a binary code.
words.example_zh: 该系统使用二进制代码。

words.definition_en: adj. if you describe something as unwarranted, you are critical of it because there is no need or reason for it
words.example_en: He accused the police of using unwarranted brutality.
words.example_zh: 他指控警方无端施暴。

words.definition_en: n. a state in which you feel excited or very alert
words.example_en: This type of rumination and agitation ignites stress hormones that keep us in a state of perpetual arousal.
words.example_zh: 这类反复思虑和焦躁会激活应激激素，使我们长期处于亢奋和警觉状态。

words.definition_en: v. to make young or youthful again: give new vigor to
words.example_en: Shelley was advised that the Italian climate would rejuvenate him...
words.example_zh: 有人建议雪莱去意大利，那里的天气会使他恢复活力。

words.definition_en: adj. eminent beyond comparison
words.example_en: All this fully demonstrates the incomparable superiority of the socialist system.
words.example_zh: 这一切充分显示了社会主义制度的无比优越。

words.definition_en: n. a method of writing rapidly by substituting characters, abbreviations, or symbols for letters, sounds, words, or phrases
words.example_en: Ben took notes in shorthand.
words.example_zh: 本用速记法记笔记。

words.definition_en: adj. causing or related to a great complete change
words.example_en: Invented in 1951, the rotary engine is a revolutionary concept in internal combustion.
words.example_zh: 1951年发明的旋转引擎是内燃领域的一个革命性观念。

words.definition_en: v. to go through very carefully in order to find something useful or valuable
words.example_en: Sift out the wheat from the chaff.
words.example_zh: 把小麦的壳筛出来。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to make dark or to become dark or darker in color (2)v. to make less happy or to become less happy
words.example_en: The sky darkened abruptly.
words.example_zh: 天突然暗了下来。

words.definition_en: adj. not bound by formal rules, procedures, or guidelines
words.definition_zh: 随心所欲的，自由放纵的
words.example_en: He has given up his freewheeling lifestyle to settle down with his baby daughter.
words.example_zh: 他放弃了自由放纵的生活方式，和幼小的女儿安顿下来。

words.definition_en: adj. acting to defeat its own purpose
words.example_en: Paying children too much attention when they misbehave can be self-defeating.
words.example_zh: 孩子有不良行为时，小题大做可能适得其反。

words.definition_en: (1)n. someone or something that comes before another (2)n. a sign of something that is going to happen
words.example_en: Dr. Sun Yat-sen was the forerunner of Chinese revolution.
words.example_zh: 孙中山先生是中国革命的先行者。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. impermeable to air or nearly so (2)adj. having no noticeable weakness, flaw or loophole
words.example_en: Store the peppercorns in an airtight container and grind the pepper as you need it.
words.example_zh: 将胡椒粒储存在密封容器中，在需要的时候磨成粉。

words.definition_en: n. the act of making atonement
words.example_en: Jesus made expiation for our sins on the cross.
words.example_zh: 耶稣在十字架上为我们赎了罪。

words.definition_en: v. to keep secret
words.example_en: Robert could not conceal his relief.
words.example_zh: 罗伯特无法掩饰自己如释重负的心情。

words.definition_en: v. to make more violent, bitter, or severe
words.example_en: Utter waste, together with inefficient use of water reserves exacerbate the crisis.
words.example_zh: 浪费加水资源的非有效利用更加加剧了水危机。

words.definition_en: (1)n. the study of ideas about knowledge, truth, the nature and meaning of life, etc. (2)n. a set of ideas about how to do something or how to live
words.example_en: Annie's work reflects her philosophy that life is full of mysteries.
words.example_zh: 安妮的作品反映了她的人生哲学，即生活充满神秘色彩。

words.definition_en: adj. having a mysterious, holy, or spiritual quality adj. supernatural, mysterious
words.definition_zh: 神圣的超自然的，神秘的
words.example_en: They led me to a numinous place on that day.
words.example_zh: 那天他们领着我去了一个神秘的地方。

words.definition_en: n. a convinced adherent of a school or individual
words.example_en: He was an ardent disciple of Gandhi.
words.example_zh: 他是甘地的忠实信徒。

words.definition_en: adj. having or marked by great volume or bulk
words.example_en: The FBI kept a voluminous file on Pablo Picasso.
words.example_zh: 联邦调查局保存了有关巴勃罗·毕加索的大量档案。

words.definition_en: v. to play or act so as to impress onlookers
words.example_en: The politician grandstanded for the cameras instead of answering the question.
words.example_zh: 这名政客没有回答问题，反而在镜头前哗众取宠。

words.definition_en: v. to appease by indulging to the full
words.example_en: Nothing could sate the careerist's greed for power.
words.example_zh: 什么也满足不了这个野心家的权力欲。

words.definition_en: v. to supply or fill with people
words.example_en: Grass's novels are peopled with outlandish characters.
words.example_zh: 格拉斯的小说里都是些稀奇古怪的人物。

words.definition_en: adj. easily frightened or excited
words.example_en: Investors could become even more skittish.
words.example_zh: 投资者有可能会变得胆小。

words.definition_en: n. a person's relatives
words.example_en: I sent my other son to the country to stay with kinfolk.
words.example_zh: 我把另一个儿子送到乡下和亲戚住在一起。

words.definition_en: v. to break apart into many small parts or pieces
words.example_en: During October 1918 the Austro-Hungarian Empire began to disintegrate.
words.example_zh: 在1918年10月间，奥匈帝国开始瓦解。

words.definition_en: adj. not new or original
words.example_en: A lot of what you see in stand-up comedy today is very derivative.
words.example_zh: 如今人们看到的单人喜剧表演好多内容都是沿袭前人。

words.definition_en: adj. very large or great
words.example_en: There has been a colossal waste of public money.
words.example_zh: 一直存在巨大的公款浪费。

words.definition_en: n. a prying observer who is usually seeking the sordid or the scandalous
words.example_en: A voyeur was seen lurking around the girl's dormitory.
words.example_zh: 有人看到一位偷窥狂躲藏在女生宿舍附近.

words.definition_en: v. to exhibit especially in an attractive or favorable aspect
words.example_en: Restored films are being showcased this month at a festival in Paris.
words.example_zh: 本月的巴黎电影节将展映一些修复版的电影。

words.definition_en: adj. very small or unimportant
words.example_en: His suggestion is always considered as negligible to her.
words.example_zh: 对她而言，他的建议总是微不足道的。

words.definition_en: adj. very surprising, shocking, or frightening
words.example_en: Sometimes the results may be rather startling.
words.example_zh: 有时结果可能会令人吃惊。

words.definition_en: n. a sudden change or disturbance that affects a country, organization, etc.
words.example_en: The country was in ruin and convulsion.
words.example_zh: 这个国家没落于动乱之中。

words.definition_en: n. a state of agreement
words.example_en: They expressed the hope that he would pursue a neutral and balanced policy for the sake of national concord.
words.example_zh: 他们希望，为了国家的和谐，他会奉行中立、不偏不倚的政策。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to money
words.example_en: She denies obtaining a pecuniary advantage by deception.
words.example_zh: 她否认通过欺骗手段获得经济利益。

words.definition_en: n. the ability to see what will or might happen in the future
words.example_en: They had the foresight to invest in new technology.
words.example_zh: 他们有投资新技术的远见。

words.definition_en: v. to take the place of by or as if by force
words.example_en: The Congress wants to reverse the reforms and usurp the power of the presidency.
words.example_zh: 议会企图颠覆改革，夺取总统权力。

words.definition_en: n. the quality of seeming real
words.example_en: To add verisimilitude, the stage is covered with sand for the desert scenes.
words.example_zh: 为了更加逼真，舞台上铺满了沙子作为沙漠的场景。

words.definition_en: adj. having a loud resonant metallic sound
words.example_en: Based on reasons mentioned above, the artist endows the work with such the clangorous and glorious title.
words.example_zh: 这幅作品基于上述理念而拥有了这个响亮而充满光辉的名字。

words.definition_en: (1)n. an amount of money that is used for a special purpose (2)v. to provide money for
words.example_en: The Bush Foundation has funded a variety of faculty development programs.
words.example_zh: 布什基金会已经资助了许多教员发展项目。

words.definition_en: adj. expressing harsh or sharp criticism in a clever way
words.example_en: The playwright allows an acerbic tone to pierce through arid or flowery prose.
words.example_zh: 剧作家允许在枯燥或华丽的散文中插入刻薄的语调。

words.definition_en: v. to describe as unimportant, weak, bad, etc.
words.example_en: Do not disparage anyone's contribution.
words.example_zh: 不要贬低任何人的贡献。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. possessing or characterized by life (2)v. to give spirit and support
words.example_en: We are animate beings, living creatures.
words.example_zh: 我们是有生命的存在，有生命的动物。

words.definition_en: adj. not eating and drinking too much
words.example_en: Already someone trying to live well would seem eccentrically abstemious in most of the US.
words.example_zh: 在美国大部分地区，想过健康生活的人会显得节制得有些古怪。

words.definition_en: adj. easily moved to often unreasonable or excessive anger
words.example_en: He was affable at one moment, choleric the next.
words.example_zh: 他时而友善和蔼，时而暴躁易怒。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to produce an effect upon (2)v. to make a display of liking or using (3)v. to put on a pretense of
words.example_en: Poor housing and family stress can affect both physical and mental health.
words.example_zh: 住房条件差、家庭压力大会影响身心两方面的健康。

words.definition_en: adj. no longer used by anyone
words.example_en: With technological changes many traditional skills have become obsolete.
words.example_zh: 随着技术的革新，许多传统技艺已被淘汰。

words.definition_en: adj. composed of elements drawn from various sources
words.example_en: There are more and more anecdotal examples of an eclectic art taste developing in the Asian region.
words.example_zh: 来越多的逸闻例证表明，兼收并蓄的艺术品味已在亚洲地区逐渐显现出来。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to associate as a member (2)n. an organization that is a member of a larger organization
words.example_en: Our New York company has an affiliate in Los Angeles.
words.example_zh: 我们的纽约公司在洛杉矶有一个下属企业。

words.definition_en: adj. continuing or following without interruption
words.example_en: The noise of bombs and guns was incessant.
words.example_zh: 炸弹和枪炮接连不断。

words.definition_en: adj. of the nature of a paradox
words.example_en: Some sedatives produce the paradoxical effect of making the person more anxious.
words.example_zh: 一些镇静剂产生出使人更紧张这样自相矛盾的结果。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. having a huge appetite (2)adj. excessively eager
words.example_en: Joseph Smith was a voracious book collector.
words.example_zh: 约瑟夫·史密斯是个如饥似渴的藏书家。

words.definition_en: v. to assume or affirm the existence of
words.example_en: Several writers have posited the idea of a universal consciousness.
words.example_zh: 有几个作者都假设存在普遍意识。

words.definition_en: adj. not likely to upset or embarrass other people
words.example_en: She gets them to accept these changes by tactful management.
words.example_zh: 她用圆滑的手腕说服他们接受这些变动。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a captious or fallacious reasoner (2)n. philosopher
words.example_en: Taylor, the founder of the Milidu School，is a famous sophist.
words.example_zh: 米利都学派的创始人泰勒斯是著名的哲学家。

words.definition_en: (1)n. an inadvertent omission or error (2)n. watchful and responsible care
words.example_en: I didn't mean to leave her name off the list, it was an oversight.
words.example_zh: 我不是有意在名单上漏掉她的名字的，这是个疏忽。

words.definition_en: v. to make thinner or more liquid by admixture
words.example_en: If you give your baby juice, dilute it well with cooled, boiled water.
words.example_zh: 如果给婴儿喝果汁，要用凉开水充分稀释。

words.definition_en: n. a code prescribing strict adherence to correct etiquette and precedence
words.example_en: According to protocol, he first reported to the Director of the hospital.
words.example_zh: 根据礼节，他先向医院院长报到。

words.definition_en: v. to make angry
words.example_en: She was nettled by his rude manner.
words.example_zh: 他粗鲁的举止惹恼了她。

words.definition_en: (1)n. the attitude a person or group has toward a subject (2)v. to assume an artificial or pretended attitude
words.example_en: The military machine is ready to change its defensive posture to one prepared for action.
words.example_zh: 军事当局已准备从防御态势变为备战态势。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to understand or become aware of (2)v. to achieve
words.example_en: People don't realize how serious this recession has actually been.
words.example_zh: 人们没有意识到这次经济衰退事实上有多严重。

words.definition_en: adj. carrying great weight and producing a marked effect
words.example_en: China's dramatic rise to near superpower status and its telling effects politically, economically and culturally are driving the interest to learn the Chinese language.
words.example_zh: 中国惊人地崛起为超级大国，在政治、经济、文化方面具有显著的影响力，这些都引起了人们学习汉语的兴趣。

words.definition_en: adj. showing kindness and gentleness
words.example_en: He did not believe in the terrible aspect of Kali, much less in her benign aspect.
words.example_zh: 他不相信外貌骇人的卡利女神，更加不相信她和善的一面。

words.definition_en: n. positive, active, and typically mutual hatred or ill will
words.example_en: The enemy mask their enmity under an appearance of friendliness.
words.example_zh: 敌人用友好的表象来掩饰内心的敌意。

words.definition_en: adj. very sad and disappointed
words.example_en: When he heard that he had failed the exam, he hung his head looking crestfallen.
words.example_zh: 听说自己没考及格，他耷拉下脑袋，看上去垂头丧气的。

words.definition_en: adj. able to see beyond the range of ordinary perception
words.example_en: It is said the love is blind but friendship is clairvoyant.
words.example_zh: 据说爱情令人盲目，而友谊却能洞察一切。

words.definition_en: n. difference of opinion
words.example_en: He is the toughest military ruler yet and has responded harshly to any dissent.
words.example_zh: 他是迄今最强硬的军事统治者，对任何异议都一律进行打压。

words.definition_en: adj. extending beyond the usual or ordinary especially in size or scope
words.example_en: Their epic fight is the subject of a new film.
words.example_zh: 他们史诗般的战斗是一部新电影的主题。

words.definition_en: n. something that corrects or improves the bad effects of something
words.example_en: Knowledge is the antidote of fear.
words.example_zh: 知识是恐惧的解毒剂。

words.definition_en: v. extol, laud
words.example_en: The middle-class woman of the late eighteenth century had good reason to accept and magnify these values of sensibility and passionate love.
words.example_zh: 十八世纪后期的中产阶级妇女完全有理由接受并赞美这些感情和炽热爱情价值。

words.definition_en: adj. of, relating to, or characteristic of the social middle class
words.example_en: This is a typical bourgeois neighborhood.
words.example_zh: 这是一个典型的中产阶级地区。

words.definition_en: adj. showing that you know all about something, even if it has not been discussed directly
words.example_en: Forget the self-help books，read this wonderful story and become, by association, a ore self-knowing, more sensuous, spiritual and loving person.
words.example_zh: 忘记那些寻求自我帮助的书来读读这本出色的故事吧，并通过思考成为更加自知，更加敏感，更加崇高和有爱的人。

words.definition_en: v. to make strong
words.example_en: The news merely fortified their determination.
words.example_zh: 这消息只是增强了他们的决心。

words.definition_en: v. to lower in rank, office, prestige, or esteem
words.example_en: The president is not willing to abase himself before the nation.。
words.example_zh: 总统不愿在全国人民面前降低自己的威信.

words.definition_en: n. the act or process of ministering
words.example_en: He had a kitchen made spotless by the ministrations of a cleaning lady.
words.example_zh: 他的厨房被清洁女工照料得一尘不染。

words.definition_en: adj. very difficult
words.example_en: The task is arduous; besides, there's not much time.
words.example_zh: 任务艰巨，同时时间又很紧迫。

words.definition_en: prep. in or into the middle of
words.example_en: They could provide a ray of hope amid the general business and economic gloom.
words.example_zh: 在普遍的商业和经济低潮中，他们能带来一线希望。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to bear up against and come safely through (2)v. to expose to the open air
words.example_en: Unpainted wooden furniture weathers to a grey colour.
words.example_zh: 没有上漆的木质家具会被风化成灰色。

words.definition_en: adj. not usually good, interesting, etc.
words.example_en: Since then, Michael has lived an unexceptional life.
words.example_zh: 自那以后，迈克过着平淡的生活。

words.definition_en: v. to provide justification or defense for
words.example_en: Ministers and officials are confident their decision will be vindicated.
words.example_zh: 部长们和官员们相信他们的决定会被证明是正确的。

words.definition_en: (1)n. pleasure and sadness that is caused by remembering something from the past and wishing that you could experience it again (2)n. the state of being homesick
words.example_en: There's been a big run on nostalgia toys this year.
words.example_zh: 今年对怀旧玩具的需求猛增。

words.definition_en: v. to make more difficult to understand
words.example_en: The writer often obfuscates the real issues with petty details.
words.example_zh: 那作家常以细枝末节来混淆实质问题。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to start or happen
words.example_en: The current recession was triggered by a slump in consumer spending.
words.example_zh: 目前的经济衰退是由消费支出骤跌引起的。

words.definition_en: v. to deny responsibility for
words.example_en: Dr. Samuels immediately disavowed the newspaper story.
words.example_zh: 赛缪尔斯博士当即对新闻报道予以了否认。

words.definition_en: adj. leaving no doubt
words.example_en: The report's message was unequivocal
words.example_zh: 报告中传达的信息是明确的。

words.definition_en: v. to change or stop the effect of
words.example_en: A heavy-handed approach from the police could undo that good impression.
words.example_zh: 警方采取高压手段可能会毁掉之前留下的好印象。

words.definition_en: v. to regard with loving admiration and devotion
words.example_en: I adore him very much.
words.example_zh: 我很崇拜他。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to cut in pieces in order to display or examine the structure and use of the parts (2)v. to analyze
words.example_en: This paper tries to anatomize China's regional disparity issue from the dimension of modern state establishment.
words.example_zh: 论文试图从现代国家构建的维度来剖析中国的区域差距问题。

words.definition_en: adj. not having knowledge about something
words.example_en: We have found some drivers clueless as to the law.
words.example_zh: 我们发现有些司机对这一法规一无所知。

words.definition_en: v. to place on a shelf
words.example_en: The government has shelved the idea until at least next year.
words.example_zh: 政府决定把这个想法先放一放，至少推迟到明年再说。

words.definition_en: n. the anger and resentment aroused by injury or insult
words.example_en: The decision provoked outrage from women and human rights groups.
words.example_zh: 这一决定激起了妇女和人权组织的强烈愤慨。

words.definition_en: adj. sluggish in character or disposition
words.example_en: To his delight a familiar, tall, languid figure lowered itself down the steps of a club.
words.example_zh: 看到一个熟悉的高大身影沿着俱乐部的台阶无精打采地走下来，他高兴极了。

words.definition_en: v. to slow the movement, progress, or action of
words.example_en: Fallen rock is impeding the progress of rescue workers.
words.example_zh: 坠落的石头阻碍了救援人员的救援进程。

words.definition_en: v. to impose, involve, or imply as a necessary accompaniment or result
words.example_en: The larger budget deficits entailed by tax reform would underpin yields on long-term Treasury bonds.
words.example_zh: 因税收改革而造成的更大的预算赤字会支撑起长期国债的收益。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to become viscous or thickened into a coherent mass
words.example_en: Blood began to coagulate around the edges of the wound.
words.example_zh: 血液开始在伤口的边缘凝固。

words.definition_en: adj. not commensurable broadly
words.example_en: Many photographers liked to take photos for her in order to get the incommensurable beauty.
words.example_zh: 很多摄影师喜欢为她拍照以捕捉那无法比拟的美。

words.definition_en: n. an idea that is not correct
words.example_en: It is a misconception that Peggy was fabulously wealthy.
words.example_zh: 人们误以为佩吉相当有钱。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to agree (2)v. to happen at the same time
words.example_en: It's extremely unlikely that two such improbable events would concur.
words.example_zh: 两个如此不相干的事件会同时发生是非常不可能的。

words.definition_en: n. proof or evidence that something exists or is true
words.example_en: The testimony given by him is dubious.
words.example_zh: 他所作的证据是可疑的。

words.definition_en: v. to say that something is true in a confident way
words.example_en: I can affirm that no one will lose their job.
words.example_zh: 我可以肯定，谁都不会丢掉工作。

words.definition_en: adj. tending to spread especially in a quick or aggressive manner
words.example_en: Not all invasive species are bad, of course.
words.example_zh: 当然，并非所有的入侵物种都不好。

words.definition_en: v. to administer a vaccine to usually by injection
words.example_en: Dogs must be vaccinated against distemper
words.example_zh: 狗必须接种犬瘟热疫苗。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to decide not to think about or consider (2)v. to permit or cause to leave (3)v. to remove from position or service (4)v. to reject serious consideration of
words.example_en: Mr Wakeham dismissed the reports as speculation.
words.example_zh: 韦克厄姆先生把这些报道当作臆测而不予考虑。

words.definition_en: adj. markedly lacking in strength
words.example_en: The newborn baby gave a feeble cry.
words.example_zh: 那个新生儿哭声很弱。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a pretense of bravery (2)n. the quality or state of being foolhardy
words.example_en: He behaved aggressively out of bravado.
words.example_zh: 他摆出一副挑衅的架势，但只是虚张声势。

words.definition_en: v. to engage in a concerted refusal to have dealings with (a person, a store, an organization, etc.) usually to express disapproval or to force acceptance of certain conditions
words.example_en: The main opposition parties are boycotting the elections.
words.example_zh: 主要反对党都抵制此次选举。

words.definition_en: adj. markedly good-humored especially as evidenced by cheerfulness and conviviality
words.example_en: Amidst the drumbeats and jovial chants, time passed.
words.example_zh: 时间在鼓声和愉快的圣歌声中流逝。

words.definition_en: adj. feeling or showing concern for someone who is sick, hurt, poor, etc.
words.example_en: These charities depend on the compassionate feelings and generosity of the general public.
words.example_zh: 这些慈善团体依赖于公众的怜悯之心和慷慨解囊。

words.definition_en: adj. not safe, strong, or steady
words.example_en: The museum is in a financially precarious position.
words.example_zh: 这家博物馆的财政状况不稳定。

words.definition_en: adj. large in extent or degree
words.example_en: The project wasted a considerable amount of time and money.
words.example_zh: 那项工程耗费了相当多的时间和资金。

words.definition_en: adj. more important, skillful, or successful than others
words.example_en: She is the preeminent scholar in her field.
words.example_zh: 她是该领域最杰出的学者。

words.definition_en: adj. tersely cogent
words.example_en: Many of them made a point of praising the film's pithy dialogue.
words.example_zh: 他们中很多人特别赞扬了影片精炼的对白。

words.definition_en: n. ideas, facts, or allegations spread deliberately to further one's cause or to damage an opposing cause
words.example_en: They even set up their own news agency to peddle anti-isolationist propaganda.
words.example_zh: 他们甚至设立了自己的通讯社来宣扬反孤立主义。

words.definition_en: adj. exhibiting boldness or effrontery
words.example_en: An insolent boy stuck his tongue out at us.
words.example_zh: 一个无礼的男孩对我们伸出了舌头。

words.definition_en: adj. done suddenly and without planning
words.example_en: Letting her friend borrow her car was an impulsive act that she immediately regretted.
words.example_zh: 让朋友借走她的车是她立刻就感到后悔的冲动行为。

words.definition_en: n. an usually great interest in something or a need to do or have something
words.example_en: I discovered an addiction to housework which I had never felt before.
words.example_zh: 我发现自己迷上了做家务，之前我从没有这种感觉。

words.definition_en: adj. not showing enough thought or effort
words.example_en: The explanation is too facile for such a complex phenomenon.
words.example_zh: 这种解释对于这样复杂的情况来说过于肤浅了。

words.definition_en: n. a great personage
words.example_en: The football captain was the mogul of the school.
words.example_zh: 那足球队长是该校的大人物.

words.definition_en: (1)adj. very dirty (2)adj. very bad or dishonest
words.definition_zh: 肮脏的卑鄙的，不诚实的
words.example_en: I didn't want to hear the sordid details of their relationship.
words.example_zh: 我不想听他们之间的那些龌龊细节。

words.definition_en: v. to offer as example, reason, or proof in discussion or analysis
words.example_en: There are other arguments I might adduce to vindicate shakespeare's authorship.
words.example_zh: 我还可以引用其他论据证明莎士比亚为原作者。

words.definition_en: v. to support with evidence or authority
words.example_en: Nato published satellite images that appeared to corroborate this.
words.example_zh: 北约公布了似乎证实该说法的卫星图像。

words.definition_en: adj. feeling or showing strong and energetic support for a person, cause, etc.
words.example_en: She was a zealous worker for charitable bodies.
words.example_zh: 她对慈善事业充满热忱。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to add to something earlier, larger or more important (2)v. to incorporate (a country or other territory) within the domain of a state
words.example_en: Hitler was determined to annex Austria to Germany.
words.example_zh: 希特勒决定将奥地利并入德国。

words.definition_en: n. excess, superfluity
words.example_en: A plethora of new operators will be allowed to enter the market.
words.example_zh: 大批新的运营商将获准进入该市场。

words.definition_en: v. to prove wrong by argument or evidence
words.example_en: She refutes any suggestion that she behaved unprofessionally.
words.example_zh: 谁要是表示她不在行，她都予以反驳。

words.definition_en: adj. providing or offering a comprehensive range of goods or services at one location
words.example_en: A marvellous discovery for every bride-to-be, The Wedding Centre is the ultimate one-stop shop.
words.example_zh: 每位准新娘都会惊喜地发现，婚庆中心提供全方位的一站式服务。

words.definition_en: n. the linking together of principles from different disciplines especially when forming a comprehensive theory
words.example_en: The consilience of the three main themes (enlightenment, salvation and revolution) between the Taiwan Straits proved the influence relation in the in-depth level.
words.example_zh: 海峡两岸新文学创作在“救亡”、“启蒙”、“革命”等三大主题上的契合，在深层次上证明了这种影响关系。

words.definition_en: adj. able or likely to change often
words.example_en: She was of the sex born mutable and explosive.
words.example_zh: 她是一个天生变化无常和容易发火的女人。

words.definition_en: adj. very important and needing immediate attention
words.example_en: There is an urgent need for food and water.
words.example_zh: 现在亟需食物和水。

words.definition_en: adj. not proper or appropriate for the situation
words.example_en: Everyone was shocked by his unseemly conduct.
words.example_zh: 大家都被他不得体的举动震惊了。

words.definition_en: adj. capable of relieving pain
words.example_en: The analgesic effects of acupuncture are well-documented.
words.example_zh: 针灸止痛的效果是举世公认的。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. of, belonging to, or seeming to come from an early time in the very ancient past (2)adj. very simple and basic
words.example_en: It is a primitive instinct to flee a place of danger.
words.example_zh: 逃离危险的地方是一种原始本能。

words.definition_en: adj. free from ambiguity or uncertainty
words.example_en: A clear-cut financial plan is a good idea at any age.
words.example_zh: 不管什么年纪，制定一个清晰的财务规划都是好主意。

words.definition_en: n. a discussion or conference, especially with an enemy
words.example_en: The general held a parley with the enemy's leader about exchanging prisoners.
words.example_zh: 将军和敌军的领袖商议交换俘虏之事。

words.definition_en: v. to read, study, or examine superficially and rapidly
words.example_en: I always skim the financial section of the newspaper.
words.example_zh: 我总要浏览一下报纸上的财经版。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to accept something as inevitable (2)v. to give up one's office or position
words.example_en: He was under pressure to resign and was about to be sidelined.
words.example_zh: 他承受着被迫辞职的压力，很快就要被排挤出去了。

words.definition_en: adj. capable of existing together in harmony
words.example_en: This printer is fully compatible with all leading software.
words.example_zh: 这个打印机和所有的主要软件完全兼容。

words.definition_en: adj. easily annoyed
words.example_en: Joe's getting a little testy in his old age.
words.example_zh: 上了年纪的乔伊变得有点爱生气了。

words.definition_en: v. to trick or deceive
words.example_en: He used his newspapers to beguile the readers into buying shares in his company.
words.example_zh: 他利用他的报纸诱骗读者买他公司的股票。

words.definition_en: v. to predict or indicate a future outcome
words.example_en: The renewed violence this week hardly augurs well for smooth or peaceful change.
words.example_zh: 本周新一轮的暴力事件预示着顺利平和的转变几乎不可能实现。

words.definition_en: adj. full of intense interest or excitement
words.example_en: The children were all agog to hear the story.
words.example_zh: 孩子们都渴望着要听这个故事。

words.definition_en: v. to think of as having little importance or value
words.example_en: In his plans he discounted the expense.
words.example_zh: 他在计划中没有考虑费用。

words.definition_en: n. a mystifying, misleading, or puzzling question posed as a problem to be solved or guessed
words.example_en: Scientists claimed yesterday to have solved the riddle of the birth of the universe.
words.example_zh: 科学家们昨天宣布已经解开了宇宙诞生之谜。

words.definition_en: v. conform, adhere
words.example_en: Every student must hew to the rules and regulations of the college.
words.example_zh: 每个学生都要遵守学校的规章制度。

words.definition_en: v. to excite and often trouble the mind or feelings of
words.example_en: They sent agents to agitate the local people.
words.example_zh: 他们派遣情报人员煽动当地的民众

words.definition_en: (1)adj. having or showing great knowledge or understanding (2)adj. difficult to understand (3)adj. coming from, reaching to, or situated at a depth
words.example_en: Liu Jun was a scholar with profound knowledge and a noted writer of parallel prose in the Liang dynasty.
words.example_zh: 刘峻是梁代学识渊博的学者，也是著名的骈文作家。

words.definition_en: adj. presaging ill fortune or trouble
words.example_en: The air of desertion lenDS the place a sinister aspect.
words.example_zh: 荒芜的景象给这个地方蒙上了一种不祥的色彩。

words.definition_en: v. to penetrate and come to understand
words.example_en: It is hard to fathom the pain felt at the death of a child.
words.example_zh: 丧子之痛是难以体会的。

words.definition_en: adj. having the power of forcing someone to do something
words.example_en: Public secondary and university education is also free but is not compulsory.
words.example_zh: 公立高中和大学教育也实行免费教育，但不是强制性的.

words.definition_en: n. the state of being old or the process of becoming old
words.example_en: It affects tuberization, leaf senescence, and dormancy.
words.example_zh: 它影响块茎形成、叶子衰老和休眠。

words.definition_en: v. to separate or set apart from others or from the general mass
words.example_en: Police segregated the two rival camps of protesters.
words.example_zh: 警察把两个敌对阵营的抗议人群隔离开来。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to cause to stop burning (2)v. to cause the end or death of
words.example_en: The message extinguished her hopes of Richard's return.
words.example_zh: 那条消息使她对理查德归来的希望彻底破灭。

words.definition_en: v. to cut with something sharp
words.example_en: Workers shear the sheep every spring.
words.example_zh: 工人们每年春天给羊剪毛。

words.definition_en: adj. showing people and things in a way that resembles how they really look
words.example_en: The word here is used in its figurative sense.
words.example_zh: 此词在这儿取的是它的比喻意义。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. very lively, happy, or energetic (2)adj. existing in large amounts
words.example_en: She gave an exuberant performance.
words.example_zh: 她的表演热情洋溢。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by an overbearingly dignified or assured manner or aspect
words.example_en: The Cambridge World History of Human Disease is a magisterial work.
words.example_zh: 《剑桥世界人类疾病史》是一部权威著作。

words.definition_en: adj. appealing to the emotions: sensational
words.example_en: She wanted to laugh at the melodramatic way he was acting.
words.example_zh: 他戏剧化的夸张架势使她忍不住想笑。

words.definition_en: adj. not feeling or showing emotions such as affection or kindness
words.example_en: She's hard-boiled, tough and funny.
words.example_zh: 她冷峻、坚韧而风趣。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to ask for something in a serious way (2)n. a quality that causes people to like someone or something
words.example_en: The United Nations has appealed for help from the international community.
words.example_zh: 联合国已经呼吁国际社会提供援助。

words.definition_en: adj. having great magnitude, force, or power
words.example_en: The earth buckled under the titanic stress.
words.example_zh: 在巨大的压力下，地面下陷了。

words.definition_en: n. a small amount
words.example_en: They should win, given a modicum of luck.
words.example_zh: 只要有一点点运气，他们就会赢。

words.definition_en: adj. lasting for only a short time
words.example_en: Each moment in history is a fleeting time, precious and unique.
words.example_zh: 历史上的每一刻都是飞逝的时光，珍贵而独特。

words.definition_en: n. strength or force gained by motion or by a series of events
words.example_en: They began to lose momentum in the second half of the game.
words.example_zh: 在比赛的下半场，他们的势头就逐渐减弱。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to weaken or destroy gradually (2)v. to slowly break apart and destroy through a chemical process
words.example_en: Engineers found the structure had been corroded by moisture.
words.example_zh: 工程师们发现该建筑已经受潮腐蚀。

words.definition_en: v. to reverse in position, order, or relationship
words.example_en: The cook inverted the cake onto a serving plate.
words.example_zh: 厨师把蛋糕倒扣过来放到一个盘子上。

words.definition_en: adj. serious, boring, or old-fashioned
words.example_en: Her thoughts were staid and solemnly adapted to a condition.
words.example_zh: 她的思想已经定形,安于一板一眼地顺应现实.

words.definition_en: n. amazement or dismay that hinders or throws into confusion
words.example_en: As the ball hurtles through the air, the varying air flow around it can send it on unexpected paths, to the consternation of many soccer players.
words.example_zh: 当球在空中疾驰时，球表面变化多样的气流可以将它送往意料之外的路径，让许多足球运动员惊愕不已。

words.definition_en: adj. coming before and usually forming a necessary prelude to something else
words.example_en: A preliminary analysis of regional components was conducted for the Bombus fauna of this Province.
words.example_zh: 另外，作者对该地区的熊蜂区系成分进行了初步的分析。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. having worldwide rather than limited or provincial scope or bearing (2)adj. composed of persons, constituents or elements from all or many parts of the world
words.example_en: The family are rich, and extremely sophisticated and cosmopolitan.
words.example_zh: 这家人很富有，老于世故，而且见多识广。

words.definition_en: adj. fit for or used for the growing of crops
words.example_en: The soil proved too infertile to sustain real pasture or arable crops.
words.example_zh: 这土壤被证明太贫瘠，不能真正用作放牧或耕种作物。

words.definition_en: n. an overwrought expression of concern or guilt
words.example_en: There's also been much hand-wringing over ballooning mortgage debt.
words.example_zh: 不断膨胀的抵押贷款债务也令人坐立不安.

words.definition_en: n. a feeling of doubt or suspicion especially concerning a future event
words.example_en: She had some misgivings about what she was about to do.
words.example_zh: 她对自己要做的事有些疑虑。

words.definition_en: adj. looking rather plain or ordinary
words.example_en: We eventually located the cottage-tucked away behind an unprepossessing row of shops.
words.example_zh: 我们终于找到了那座小屋，它隐藏在一排不引人注目的店铺后面。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to cause to happen quickly or suddenly (2)n. a solid substance that is separated from al liquid especially by a chemical process (3)adj. happening very quickly or too quickly without enough thought or planning
words.definition_zh: 引发，加速沉淀冲动的，鲁莽的
words.example_en: A slight mistake could precipitate a disaster.
words.example_zh: 小错误可能会招致大灾难。

words.definition_en: v. to give support or encouragement to
words.example_en: Bryan Sutton seconded the motion against fox hunting.
words.example_zh: 布赖恩·萨顿赞同反对猎狐动议。

words.definition_en: n. a hackneyed theme, characterization or situation
words.example_en: Stay away from stock and cliche answers if you want to leave a lasting impression on your interviewer.
words.example_zh: 如果你想给面试官留下深刻的印象，就要避免那些陈腐，陈词滥调的回答。

words.definition_en: adj. not important
words.example_en: Seemingly inconsequential details can sometimes contain significant clues.
words.example_zh: 貌似无足轻重的细节有时可能隐含着重大的线索。

words.definition_en: n. an outward sign
words.example_en: The redness around his eyes was an unmistakable telltale of fatigue.
words.example_zh: 他眼圈发红，显然是疲劳的迹象。

words.definition_en: n. a source of danger
words.example_en: A new report suggests that chewing-gum may be a health hazard.
words.example_zh: 一份新报告指出，口香糖可能对健康有害。

words.definition_en: adj. extremely good
words.example_en: What a terrific idea!
words.example_zh: 多么好的主意！

words.definition_en: (1)n. something that is very special, important, or valuable (2)v. to value very much
words.example_en: She treasures her memories of those joyous days.
words.example_zh: 她珍视那段快乐时光的记忆。

words.definition_en: v. to close up or block off
words.example_en: The tourniquet should occlude venous and lymphatic return.
words.example_zh: 止血带应阻断静脉及淋巴回流.

words.definition_en: v. to establish by proof or competent evidence
words.example_en: There is little scientific evidence to substantiate the claims.
words.example_zh: 这些主张几乎找不到科学依据来证实。

words.definition_en: adj. not decreasing or becoming weaker
words.example_en: His unflagging enthusiasm was an inspiration for all of us.
words.example_zh: 他经久不衰的热情鼓舞了我们所有人。

words.definition_en: adj. made to be useful rather than to be decorative or comfortable
words.example_en: This basic utilitarian model gives a relatively unsophisticated account of human behavior.
words.example_zh: 这个实用的基础模型较为简略地解释了人类的行为。

words.definition_en: n. the quality or state of being faithful
words.example_en: He believed in marital fidelity.
words.example_zh: 他认为婚内应该忠诚。

words.definition_en: adj. willing to do dishonest things in return for money
words.example_en: A venal moving-van company had revealed her address.
words.example_zh: 一家唯利是图的搬运汽车公司还是把她的住址泄露了。

words.definition_en: adj. of the same mind or habit of thought
words.example_en: Some like-minded people got together and set up a group.
words.example_zh: 一些志趣相投的人聚在一起，成立了一个小组。

words.definition_en: n. a feigning to be what one is not or to believe what one does not
words.example_en: He called it 'rank hypocrisy' that the government was now promoting equal rights.
words.example_zh: 他把目前政府提倡平等权利的举措称为“彻头彻尾的伪善”。

words.definition_en: v. to overwhelm with shock, surprise, or wonder
words.example_en: He was flabbergasted when he heard that his friend had been accused of murder.
words.example_zh: 听说朋友被控谋杀，他大吃一惊。

words.definition_en: n. a general agreement about something
words.example_en: There seems to be a consensus that the plan should be rejected.
words.example_zh: 看来人们一致同意放弃这一计划。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. in good health (2)adj. free from mistakes (3)adj. solid and strong
words.example_en: I am not sure that this is sound democratic practice.
words.example_zh: 我不能肯定这是正确的民主行为。

words.definition_en: n. the quality or state of being accurate and correct
words.example_en: They cannot determine their optimum price and output with neat exactitude.
words.example_zh: 他们不能精确无误地知道最优价格和产量。

words.definition_en: n. one inclined to raise or excite alarms especially needlessly
words.example_en: The senator is just a scaremonger, his political act won't realistically result in changed behavior.
words.example_zh: 这名议员仅仅是个危言耸听者，他的政治举措并不能解决实际问题。

words.definition_en: v. to reach its highest altitude
words.example_en: In just a few days, a century-long struggle will culminate in a historic vote.
words.example_zh: 几天之后，在历史性的投票中，长达一个世纪的努力即将达到高潮

words.definition_en: (1)adj. basic or simple (2)adj. not very developed or advanced
words.example_en: They are deprived of the ability to exercise the most rudimentary workers' rights.
words.example_zh: 他们被剥夺了行使最基本的工人权利的资格。

words.definition_en: adj. arranged or organized in a logical or regular way
words.example_en: The officers and crew prepared to abandon ship in an orderly fashion.
words.example_zh: 全体船员秩序井然地准备弃船。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to weaken or destroy the effect of (2)v. to show or prove to be false or incorrect
words.example_en: An official decree invalidated the vote in the capital.
words.example_zh: 一项官方法令使首都的投票失去了效力。

words.definition_en: v. to draw back or in
words.example_en: They tried to persuade me to retract my words.
words.example_zh: 他们试图说服我收回我的话。

words.definition_en: adj. affording a general view of a whole
words.example_en: There are many vortex patterns on synoptic maps.
words.example_zh: 摘要天气图上有许多种涡旋型式。

words.definition_en: adj. willing to believe that other people are good and honest
words.example_en: Perhaps I was too trusting.
words.example_zh: 也许我太轻信了。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to reach above or beyond (2)v. to try to do more than you have the power, ability, or money to do
words.definition_zh: 过度伸张试图做力不所及的事
words.example_en: In making these promises, the company had clearly overreached itself.
words.example_zh: 这家公司作出这些承诺，显然是不自量力。

words.definition_en: v. to frustrate the progress or efforts of
words.example_en: John is stumped by an unexpected question.
words.example_zh: 一个意想不到的问题把约翰难住了。

words.definition_en: n. loyalty to a person, group, etc.
words.example_en: They owed fealty to the Earl rather than the King.
words.example_zh: 与其说他们应效忠国王，还不如说他们该效忠伯爵。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to check or go counter to (2)v. to occur or develop as a consequence
words.example_en: Austria has sent three army battalions to its border with Hungary to stem the flow of illegal immigrants.
words.example_zh: 奥地利已派出 3 个营的兵力去本国和匈牙利交界的边境地区阻止非法移民的流入。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to impede or stop the development of (2)v. to stop the respiration of
words.example_en: The governor's proposals would actually cost millions of jobs and suffocate the economy.
words.example_zh: 总督的计划可能会造成上百万人失业，并抑制经济发展。

words.definition_en: n. something that is joined or added to another thing but is not an essential part of it
words.example_en: The Party was certainly not an official adjunct of the police department.
words.example_zh: 该党派肯定不是公安部门的官方附属机构。

words.definition_en: adj. having the characteristics of a platitude
words.example_en: The whole speech was platitudinous nonsense.
words.example_zh: 整篇讲话都是陈谷子烂芝麻。

words.definition_en: adj. cruel, savage
words.example_en: The regime used brute violence to suppress dissent.
words.example_zh: 该政权用残暴的暴力手段镇压异议。

words.definition_en: adj. big and very impressive
words.example_en: This grand building lies in the center of town.
words.example_zh: 这幢宏伟建筑位于市中心。

words.definition_en: adj. not having or providing enough of what is needed
words.example_en: He decided there was insufficient evidence to justify criminal proceedings...
words.example_zh: 他认定证据不充分无法提起刑事诉讼。

words.definition_en: adj. not confined, limited, or impeded
words.example_en: When his schoolmate made the last goal, the boy gave out with an untrammeled yell.
words.example_zh: 那个男孩在他的同学踢进最后一球时不禁纵声欢呼。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a loss of power, success, popularity, etc. (2)v. to make less important or popular
words.example_en: The gramophone had been eclipsed by new technology such as the compact disc.
words.example_zh: 激光唱片等新技术使留声机黯然失色。

words.definition_en: v. to disturb the quiet of
words.example_en: Remove the bowl from the ice and stir in the cream.
words.example_zh: 从冰里把碗取出，搅入奶油。

words.definition_en: adj. not wanting or willing to do something
words.example_en: He was loath to admit his mistake.
words.example_zh: 他不愿承认自己的错误。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking proper respect or seriousness
words.example_en: He now dismisses that as a flippant comment.
words.example_zh: 他现在把那当作轻率的随口一说不再理会了。

words.definition_en: n. freedom of action or choice
words.example_en: Generally, courts give local administrators great latitude.
words.example_zh: 总体上，法庭还是给了当地管理者以较大的自由。

words.definition_en: adj. similar in some way
words.example_en: The relationship of a teacher and his students is analogous to that of a director and the actors.
words.example_zh: 老师和学生之间的关系就像导演和演员之间的关系。

words.definition_en: adj. impossible to see or notice
words.example_en: The slight change in the taste was imperceptible to most people.
words.example_zh: 这种味道的细微变化对大多数人而言是无法觉察的。

words.definition_en: v. to breathe out
words.example_en: She exhaled the smoke through her nose.
words.example_zh: 她从鼻子里喷出烟雾。

words.definition_en: (1) n. a comment or remark (2) n. something you notice by watching and listening (3) n. an act or instance of observing a custom, rule, or law
words.example_en: In hospital she'll be under observation all the time.
words.example_zh: 医院会对她24小时密切观察。

words.definition_en: adj. of, relating to, or resembling a labyrinth
words.example_en: The streets of the Old City are narrow and labyrinthine.
words.example_zh: 老城区的街道狭促曲折，好似迷宫一般。

words.definition_en: v. to make a sudden, unsteady movement forward or sideways
words.example_en: As the car sped over a pothole she lurched forward.
words.example_zh: 车子飞驶过一个坑洼时，她身子猛地向前一倾。

words.definition_en: v. inveigle
words.example_en: She inveigles Paco into a plot to swindle Tania out of her savings.
words.example_zh: 她引诱帕科一起合谋来骗取塔妮娅的积蓄。

words.definition_en: adj. having a good or helpful result especially after something unpleasant has happened
words.example_en: The team's defeat is a salutary warning before the World Cup.
words.example_zh: 这次失利是在世界杯比赛之前对整个队的一个有益警示.

words.definition_en: adj. involving two people or two sides who oppose each other
words.example_en: There is almost an adversarial feeling between businesses and consumers.
words.example_zh: 在商家和消费者之间几乎产生了一种敌对的情绪。

words.definition_en: n. the process or result of being slowly destroyed by natural precesses
words.example_en: Decay had weakened the wooden beams.
words.example_zh: 腐朽已经削弱了木梁。

words.definition_en: adj. harshly and haughtily arrogant
words.example_en: He's so overbearing that no one wants to work for him.
words.example_zh: 他那么专横，没人愿为他工作。

words.definition_en: adj. easily broken or destroyed
words.example_en: The fragile economies of several southern African nations could be irreparably damaged.
words.example_zh: 一些南非国家的脆弱经济可能会被彻底破坏，无法挽回。

words.definition_en: v. to rescind or annul by authoritative act
words.example_en: Parliament repealed the property law.
words.example_zh: 议会废除了那项财产法。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking conviction: half-hearted
words.example_en: The study received a lukewarm response from the Home Secretary.
words.example_zh: 内政大臣对这项研究反应不是很热烈。

words.definition_en: adj. causing great fear
words.example_en: I still find it terrifying to find myself surrounded by large numbers of horses
words.example_zh: 我仍旧觉得被一大群马包围很可怕。

words.definition_en: v. to feel or show deep respect for
words.example_en: Most Italians venerate the name of Dante.
words.example_zh: 大多数意大利人崇拜但丁的名字。

words.definition_en: (1)n. morally good behavior or character (2)n. a commendable quality or courage
words.example_en: His virtue is patience.
words.example_zh: 耐心是他的美德。

words.definition_en: adj. fawning, obsequious
words.example_en: We heard the sound of sycophantic laughter.
words.example_zh: 我们听到了谄媚的笑声。

words.definition_en: v. to steal things that are not very valuable or to steal a small amount of something
words.example_en: When food stores close, they go to work, pilfering food for resale on the black market.
words.example_zh: 食品店关门后，他们就行动起来，偷食品拿到黑市上出售。

words.definition_en: adj. doing something that has not been done before
words.example_en: The school has won awards for its pioneering work with the community.
words.example_zh: 该校因与社区开创性合作而获奖。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to the countryside or to the lives of people who live in the country
words.example_en: We watched from the train window as the pastoral landscapes of Burgundy unfolded.
words.example_zh: 透过火车车窗，勃艮第的乡村风光展示在我们眼前。

words.definition_en: adj. involving two or more academic, scientific, or artistic disciplines
words.example_en: We will have an interdisciplinary educational infrastructure in order to create and disseminate knowledge.
words.example_zh: 我们将有一个跨学科的教育基础设施，以创造和传播知识。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking sharpness or quickness of sensibility or intellect
words.example_en: I've really been very obtuse and stupid.
words.example_zh: 我实在笨得不可理喻。

words.definition_en: adj. not proud or haughty
words.example_en: He gave a great performance, but he was very humble.
words.example_zh: 他的表演非常出色，但他却很谦虚。

words.definition_en: n. insulting or contemptuous action or speech
words.example_en: This action makes a mockery of the Government's continuing protestations of concern.
words.example_zh: 这一行动是对一再声明表示关注的政府的嘲弄。

words.definition_en: adj. not fresh or original
words.example_en: The movie is teeming with obvious and trite ideas.
words.example_zh: 这部电影充满着明显而陈腐的观点。

words.definition_en: v. to no longer have any hope or belief that a situation will improve or change
words.example_en: He does despair at much of the press criticism.
words.example_zh: 面对媒体的诸多批评他的确感到绝望。

words.definition_en: v. to make glorious by bestowing honor, praise, or admiration
words.example_en: Singing hymns is one of the ways to glorify God.
words.example_zh: 唱圣歌是赞美上帝的一种方式。

words.definition_en: n. movement away from a place or situation especially because it is dangerous, unpleasant, etc.
words.example_en: The army's retreat began at dawn.
words.example_zh: 军队于黎明时开始撤退。

words.definition_en: v. imprison
words.example_en: He was immured in a dark room.
words.example_zh: 他被禁闭在一间暗室中。

words.definition_en: adj. carefully noticing problems or signs of danger
words.example_en: The dog kept a vigilant guard over the house.
words.example_zh: 这只狗警醒地守护着这所房屋。

words.definition_en: v. to throw out especially by physical force, authority, or influence
words.example_en: Officials used guard dogs to eject the protesters.
words.example_zh: 官员们用警犬驱散抗议人群。

words.definition_en: adj. not forming a necessary part of something
words.example_en: We ought not to bring in extraneous matters in trying to find a basis for a settlement.
words.example_zh: 我们努力寻求解决问题的基础时，不应该牵扯进无关的事情。

words.definition_en: adj. having a gradual and cumulative effect
words.example_en: They focus on overt discrimination rather than insidious aspects of racism.
words.example_zh: 他们关注的是公开的歧视，而不是种族主义的一些潜在问题。

words.definition_en: v. to make lucid especially by explanation or analysis
words.example_en: Haig went on to elucidate his personal principle of war.
words.example_zh: 海格继续解释他个人的战争原则。

words.definition_en: v. to restore to a former state
words.example_en: Considerable efforts have been made to rehabilitate patients who have suffered in this way.
words.example_zh: 为使受这种苦的病人康复已经付出了相当大的努力。

words.definition_en: adj. friendly and easy to talk to
words.example_en: My boss is affable enough when she is not preoccupied with business problems.
words.example_zh: 我的老板不忙于公事的时候还是和蔼可亲的。

words.definition_en: adj. occurring, appearing, or changing at usually irregular intervals
words.example_en: My memories of childhood are hazy and episodic.
words.example_zh: 我儿时的回忆是一些朦朦胧胧的松散片段。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing a slow and stolid temperament
words.example_en: Her phlegmatic response itself is now a driver in this financial crisis, and people will rightly blame her for any serious accident.
words.example_zh: 默克尔冷漠反应的本身，如今就是此次金融危机的舵手，如果出现重大事故，人们责备她也理所应当。

words.definition_en: n. a group of retainers or attendants
words.example_en: Mind trainers are now part of a tennis star's retinue.
words.example_zh: 现在，网球明星的随行人员中有些是心理培训师。

words.definition_en: adj. very complicated and difficult to understand
words.example_en: Despite its length and convoluted plot, Asta's Book is a rich and rewarding read.
words.example_zh: 虽然篇幅很长，而且情节复杂，但是《阿斯塔的书》仍不失为一部内容丰富、值得一读的作品。

words.definition_en: adj. done in haste without regard for consequences
words.example_en: On stage she seems hard, brash and uncompromising.
words.example_zh: 在舞台上,她显得冷酷、自以为是而且不妥协。

words.definition_en: v. to change to an opposite state or condition
words.example_en: Because the normal word order is reversed in passive sentences, they are sometimes hard to follow.
words.example_zh: 由于正常语序在被动句中被颠倒过来了，所以有时很难理解。

words.definition_en: adj. involving or having information that disagrees with other information
words.example_en: The contradictory statements are symptoms of disarray in the administration.
words.example_zh: 这些互相矛盾的说法反映了管理的混乱。

words.definition_en: v. to make unclear or difficult to see or remember
words.example_en: The writing blurred and danced before his eyes.
words.example_zh: 字迹变得一片模糊，在他眼前晃动。

words.definition_en: n. a scheming or crafty action or artful design intended to accomplish some usually evil end
words.example_en: They are attempting to counter o enemy's machination.
words.example_zh: 他们在试图反击敌人的阴谋。

words.definition_en: v. to praise highly
words.example_en: They kept extolling my managerial skills.
words.example_zh: 他们不停地赞美我的管理技能。

words.definition_en: adv. absolutely
words.example_en: Staff are often discourteous and sometimes downright rude.
words.example_zh: 职员常常态度很不礼貌，有时候极其蛮横。

words.definition_en: n. the place or point of entering or beginning
words.example_en: He stopped at the threshold of the bedroom.
words.example_zh: 他在卧室门口停住了。

words.definition_en: v. to abuse or censure severely or abusively
words.example_en: Human is not God. God is reasoning, human isn't, human would rather vituperate than reasoning.
words.example_zh: 人不是上帝, 上帝是讲道理的, 人更易通过谩骂的方式而不是讲道理。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing a desire to cause harm to someone
words.example_en: These are not necessarily hateful, malicious people.
words.example_zh: 这些人不一定心怀仇恨，恶意伤人。

words.definition_en: adj. in the past
words.example_en: Erstwhile workers may have become managers.
words.example_zh: 过去的工人可能已经成了经理。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to relate to someone or something (2)v. to belong as a part, member, accessory or product
words.example_en: His remark did not pertain to the question.
words.example_zh: 他的话同这个问题不相干。

words.definition_en: adj. appealing to or expressing emotion
words.example_en: These comparisons are emotive rather than analytic.
words.example_zh: 这些比较带有个人感情色彩，而不是客观分析。

words.definition_en: v. to set apart
words.example_en: This jury is expected to be sequestered for at least two months.
words.example_zh: 预计这个陪审团将至少被隔离两个月。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to an enemy
words.example_en: She fixed her questioner with a hostile glare.
words.example_zh: 她敌意地瞪着向她提问的人。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by firmness or obduracy
words.example_en: The authorities proved unyielding on one crucial opposition demand.
words.example_zh: 面对反对党这一重大诉求，当局立场坚定。

words.definition_en: n.
words.example_en: Mr. Grandet is a profitmonger, all he cares is money, not his family.
words.example_zh: 葛朗台先生是个唯利是图的人，他只关心钱，而不是他的家人。

words.definition_en: n. the belief that your country, race, etc., is better than any other
words.example_en: It may also appeal to the latent chauvinism of many ordinary people.
words.example_zh: 它可能也迎合了许多普通人心中的沙文主义情结。

words.definition_en: v. to use or change in a skillful way or for a particular purpose
words.example_en: The technology uses a pen to manipulate a computer.
words.example_zh: 这项技术使用笔来操控计算机。

words.definition_en: v. to check by or as if by pressure
words.example_en: People who repress their emotions risk having nightmares...
words.example_zh: 压抑情感的人容易做噩梦。

words.definition_en: adj. having superior weight, force, or influence
words.example_en: This is still a possible outcome but no longer the preponderant probability.
words.example_zh: 这仍是一个可能的结果，但不再具有占优势的可能性。

words.definition_en: adj. likely to betray trust
words.example_en: He publicly left the party and denounced its treacherous leaders...
words.example_zh: 他公开脱离该党，并谴责该党领袖背信弃义。

words.definition_en: n. the right of voting
words.example_en: He was an advocate of universal suffrage as a basis for social equality.
words.example_zh: 他提倡普选权，认为这是社会公平的基础。

words.definition_en: n. a crown of laurel awarded as an honor
words.example_en: she has rightly won laurels for this brilliantly perceptive novel.
words.example_zh: 她因为这部极具洞悉力的小说无愧地赢得了荣誉。

words.definition_en: adj. having or resulting from a weak character or nature
words.example_en: He regarded the young man as feckless and irresponsible.
words.example_zh: 他认为这个年轻人不光无能，还不负责任。

words.definition_en: adj. having many different parts
words.example_en: Critical reflection is a multifaceted construct requiring a multifaceted approach.
words.example_zh: 批判性思考是一个多面向的建构,需要多面向的方法。

words.definition_en: v. to make timid or fearful
words.example_en: Jones had set out to intimidate and dominate Paul.
words.example_zh: 琼斯开始恐吓并控制保罗。

words.definition_en: adj. limited to only the things that affect your local area
words.example_en: He is rather too parochial in his outlook.
words.example_zh: 他的眼界未免过於狭隘了。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. used, made, or sold only by the particular person or company that has the legal right to do so (2)adj. kept private by an owner
words.example_en: We had to take action to protect the proprietary technology.
words.example_zh: 我们必须采取措施保护专利技术。

words.definition_en: v. to infer or estimate something from known facts or existing data
words.example_en: We have extrapolated these results from research done in other countries.
words.example_zh: 我们从其他国家所作的研究中推断出这些结果。

words.definition_en: v. to give life and energy to
words.example_en: Take a deep breath in to invigorate you.
words.example_zh: 深吸一口气来提提神。

words.definition_en: adj. energetic, quick
words.example_en: Taking a brisk walk can often induce a feeling of well-being.
words.example_zh: 轻快的散步经常能使人心旷神怡。

words.definition_en: adj. free of encumbrance
words.example_en: I hoped hang gliding would deliver the unencumbered essence of flight.
words.example_zh: 我希望悬挂式滑翔能给我带来无拘无束的纯粹飞行体验。

words.definition_en: v. to feed on growing herbage, attached algae, or phytoplankton
words.example_en: There were cows grazing beside the river.
words.example_zh: 有些牛在河边吃草。

words.definition_en: n. characteristic or habitual inclination or mode of emotional response
words.example_en: His impulsive temperament regularly got him into difficulties...
words.example_zh: 他容易冲动，经常因此惹麻烦。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. relating to or involving general ideas or qualities rather than specific people, objects, or actions (2)adj. difficult to understand (3)n. a brief written statement of the main points or facts in a longer report, speech, etc. (4)v. to obtain or remove from a source (5)v. to draw away the attention of
words.definition_zh: 笼统的抽象的，深奥的摘要提取分散注意力
words.example_en: His painting went through both representational and abstract periods.
words.example_zh: 他的绘画经历了具象风格和抽象风格两个阶段。

words.definition_en: n. passive assent or agreement without protest
words.example_en: Mike claimed that he did it under the acquiescence of his boss.
words.example_zh: 迈克声称他是在上司的默许下做这件事的。

words.definition_en: adj. insulting to someone or something
words.example_en: I agree I am ambitious, and I don't see that as a pejorative term。
words.example_zh: 我承认我有野心，但我不认为这是个贬义词。

words.definition_en: v. engross, absorb
words.example_en: Don't immerse yourself in the infliction too long.
words.example_zh: 不要长时间沉浸在痛苦经历中。

words.definition_en: adj. existing or accepted for the present time but likely to be changed
words.example_en: If you have never held a driving licence before, you should apply for a provisional licence.
words.example_zh: 如果你从未有过驾照，那就应该申请一个临时的驾照。

words.definition_en: n. exclusive ownership through legal privilege, command of supply, or concerted action
words.example_en: Planters enjoyed a natural monopoly because they had no competitors.
words.example_zh: 种植园主享受着自然的垄断，因为他们没有竞争者。

words.definition_en: adj. not capable of bing mistaken or misunderstood
words.example_en: Black clouds are an unmistakable sign of coming rain.
words.example_zh: 乌云是下雨的明显先兆。

words.definition_en: adj. free from fault or error
words.example_en: She had impeccable taste in clothes.
words.example_zh: 她的穿衣品位无可挑剔。

words.definition_en: v. subdue completely
words.example_en: He tried to vanquish his fears.
words.example_zh: 他努力克服恐惧心理.

words.definition_en: v. to avoid being stopped by
words.example_en: They found a way of circumventing the law.
words.example_zh: 他们找到了规避法律的途径。

words.definition_en: v. to make stronger or more secure
words.example_en: Chile is ready to work with China to further consolidate and develop bilateral comprehensive cooperative partnership.
words.example_zh: 智利愿同中国一道，进一步巩固和发展两国全面合作伙伴关系。

words.definition_en: adj. extremely light, delicate, or tenuous
words.example_en: When you think about silk, you probably think of gossamer fibers woven into lustrous garments.
words.example_zh: 当你想到丝绸的时候，你可能想到是那些织成华丽服饰的薄纱。

words.definition_en: adj. recurrence in an organism of a trait or character typical of an ancestral form and usually due to genetic recombination
words.example_en: We all have an atavistic fear of snakes.
words.example_zh: 我们都有对蛇的原始恐惧。

words.definition_en: v. to understand in a particular way
words.example_en: What may seem helpful behavior to you can be construed as interference by others.
words.example_zh: 在你看来似乎是施助的行为在别人眼中可能就会被解读成一种干涉。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. refusing to change your ideas or to stop doing something (2)adj. difficult to deal with, remove, etc.
words.example_en: He is a stubborn character used to getting his own way.
words.example_zh: 他性格固执，惯于一意孤行。

words.definition_en: adj. willing to end a disagreement with someone
words.example_en: They approached the talks in a conciliatory spirit.
words.example_zh: 他们抱一种和解的态度去参加谈判。

words.definition_en: n. a state of wild disorder
words.example_en: It's difficult to winnow out the truth amidst this welter of confusing signals.
words.example_zh: 在杂乱无章的信号之中，要辨别真相很困难。

words.definition_en: adj. forming or relating to the most important part of something
words.example_en: But on this question, the two leaders have very fundamental differences.
words.example_zh: 但在这个问题上，两位领导人有着根本性的分歧。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to learn so that it is fully understood and can be used (2)v. to make similar
words.example_en: French Jews generally had been assimilated into the nation's culture.
words.example_zh: 法裔犹太人已经大体上被这个国家的文化同化了。
