words.definition_en: v. to bring to view, attention, or use again
words.example_en: It is said that this medicine can resurrect the dead.
words.example_zh: 据说这种药能起死回生。

words.definition_en: v. to shorten in duration or extent
words.example_en: Book publisher may alter or abridge a work with the permission of the copyright owner.
words.example_zh: 图书出版者经作者许可,可以对作品修改、删减。

words.definition_en: adj. hard to understand, explain, or deal with
words.example_en: The problem is not as complicated as he imagined.
words.example_zh: 问题没他想像的那么复杂。

words.definition_en: n. an expression or mode of expression that occurs in ordinary speech rather than formal writing
words.example_en: Most of these sermons were written in Sanskrit rather than the vernacular.
words.example_zh: 这些布道稿大多用梵语而不是当地通俗语言写成。

words.definition_en: adj. approaching death
words.example_en: They said it was outdated, moribund, and in decline.
words.example_zh: 投资者说这家公司已经过时了，奄奄一息而且在下滑。

words.definition_en: n. the performance of a lawful action in an illegal or improper manner
words.example_en: The wisdom of the common law's stress on the crucial distinction between misfeasance and nonfeasance.
words.example_zh: 普通法的智慧在于，它重点强调了违法行为与消极不行为之间的区别。

words.definition_en: v. to call by a distinctive title, epithet, or nickname
words.example_en: He should be dubbed Sir Hubert.
words.example_zh: 他应被授予休伯特爵士称号。

words.definition_en: n. a difference especially between things that should be the same
words.example_en: Several explanations spring to mind for the discrepancy between perceived opportunities and actual behavior.
words.example_zh: 有些理由可以解释理想的机会和现实的行为之间存在的矛盾。

words.definition_en: v. to avoid especially because you do not think it is right, proper, etc.
words.example_en: A wise person eschews bad company.
words.example_zh: 聪明人远避恶友。

words.definition_en: adj. widely and unfavorably known
words.example_en: The lawyer has defended some of the most notorious criminals.
words.example_zh: 这名法官已为一些最臭名昭著的罪犯当过辩护律师。

words.definition_en: v. to have an intense desire
words.example_en: She yens for a chance to travel around the world.
words.example_zh: 她渴望有机会周游世界。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to a judgment, the function of judging, the administration of justice, or the judiciary
words.example_en: Hearing the case in open court is only one part of the judicial process.
words.example_zh: 公开审理这一案件只是司法程序的一部分。

words.definition_en: adj. happy and lively in a way that is attractive
words.example_en: The vivacious girl made a good cheer-leader.
words.example_zh: 这个活泼的女孩做了啦啦队队长。

words.definition_en: adj. making it easy, possible, or likely for something to happen or exist
words.example_en: Fresh air is conducive to health.
words.example_zh: 新鲜空气有利于健康。

words.definition_en: adj. impervious to external influence
words.example_en: The hermetic seal on the packet means that the food lasts longer.
words.example_zh: 封袋上的密封印表明食物能保存更长的时间。

words.definition_en: adj. existing or valid in equity as distinguished from law
words.example_en: He has urged them to come to an equitable compromise that gives Hughes his proper due.
words.example_zh: 他已敦促他们达成公平合理的妥协，给休斯应得的权益。

words.definition_en: v. to represent falsely
words.example_en: The charges against him include fraud, bribery, and falsifying business records.
words.example_zh: 对他的指控包括诈骗、行贿和伪造商业记录。

words.definition_en: v. to come together to form one group or mass
words.example_en: Cities, if unrestricted, tend to coalesce into bigger and bigger conurbations.
words.example_zh: 如果不加限制，城市往往会联合发展成越来越大的集合城市。

words.definition_en: adj. capable of continuing or renewing oneself indefinitely
words.example_en: Revenge leads to a self-perpetuating cycle of violence.
words.example_zh: 怨怨相报会导致永不休止的暴力。

words.definition_en: v. to oppose directly and stubbornly
words.example_en: He wants to be the tough rebel who bucks the system.
words.example_zh: 他想成为抵制体制的强硬叛逆者。

words.definition_en: adj. fast and typically superficial
words.example_en: The Government should not be pressured into making hasty decisions.
words.example_zh: 政府不应该迫于压力仓促作出决定。

words.definition_en: n. a test or criterion for determining the quality or genuineness of a thing
words.example_en: Job security has become the touchstone of a good job for many employees.
words.example_zh: 对许多雇员来说，工作的稳定性已经成了一份好工作的标准。

words.definition_en: v. to allow to exist or happen
words.example_en: Jenny would brook no criticism of Matthew.
words.example_zh: 珍妮不会容忍任何对马修的批评。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. opposite in nature or effect (2)n. something that is the opposite of something else
words.definition_zh: 相反的相反事物，相反数（数学）
words.example_en: The tension grew in inverse proportion to the distance from their final destination.
words.example_zh: 拉力的增加与到达它们最终点的距离成相反的比例。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to feel less angry about something
words.example_en: I tried to placate her by offering to pay for the repairs.
words.example_zh: 我提出支付修理费来抚慰他。

words.definition_en: adj. resisting control or authority
words.example_en: He has a refractory pony.
words.example_zh: 他有一匹匹桀骜不驯的马驹

words.definition_en: v. to get possession of (something)
words.example_en: She managed to procure a ticket for the concert.
words.example_zh: 她好不容易弄到一张音乐会入场券。

words.definition_en: adj. accepted, done, or happening often or over a large area at a particular time
words.example_en: This custom is particularly prevalent among people living in the North.
words.example_zh: 这种习俗在北方人中尤为常见。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by or concerned about precise accordance with the details of codes or conventions
words.example_en: The mistake was spotted immediately by their punctilious lawyer.
words.example_zh: 这个错误马上被他们一丝不苟的律师发现了。

words.definition_en: adj. firmly restraining response to pain or distress
words.example_en: To a stoic believer, the human soul was a divine spark.
words.example_zh: 对于禁欲主义者来说，人的灵魂是神圣的火花。

words.definition_en: n. the quality or state of having many different forms, types, ideas, etc.
words.example_en: Few countries have as rich a diversity of habitat as South Africa.
words.example_zh: 几乎没有哪个国家像南非那样拥有如此多样化的动植物栖生地。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to contaminate morally (2)v. to affect with putrefaction (3)n. something that causes a person or thing to be thought of as bad, dishonest, etc.
words.example_en: The air was tainted by fumes from the cars.
words.example_zh: 空气被汽车排出的废气所污染。

words.definition_en: adj. lasting a very short time
words.example_en: I had some momentary misgivings that were quickly resolved.
words.example_zh: 我曾有过短暂的疑虑，但很快都烟消云散了。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to move faster (2)v. to cause to happen sooner or more quickly
words.example_en: The government is to accelerate its privatization programs.
words.example_zh: 政府将加快其私有化计划的进程。

words.definition_en: v. to cause someone to be no longer friendly or close to another person or group
words.example_en: He became estranged from his family after the argument.
words.example_zh: 那场争吵后他便与家人疏远了。

words.definition_en: adj. very clear
words.example_en: She has a pair of pellucid blue eyes.
words.example_zh: 她有一双清澈的蓝眼睛。

words.definition_en: adj. deadly or pernicious in influence
words.example_en: The polls corroborate the baleful economic portents.
words.example_zh: 民意调查加重了这种经济的不祥之兆。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to cause to do something (2)adj. performed readily or immediately
words.example_en: Japan's recession has prompted consumers to cut back on buying cars.
words.example_zh: 日本的经济衰退已促使消费者们削减购车花销。

words.definition_en: adj. capable of being understood or appreciated
words.example_en: The director believed that program can make science more accessible to young people.
words.example_zh: 该负责人认为这项计划能让年轻人更容易理解科学。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. coming from the imagination (2)adj. existing in fancy only
words.example_en: The Moon Maiden is one of a number of fanciful lunar inhabitants.
words.example_zh: 嫦娥是人们臆造出来的诸多月球居住者之一。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking in physical strength or substance
words.example_en: The storm flattened the flimsy wooden huts that the villagers lived in.
words.example_zh: 风暴刮平了村民们居住的脆弱的木屋。

words.definition_en: adj. fruitful in offspring or vegetation
words.example_en: The pampas are still among the most fecund lands in the world.
words.example_zh: 南美大草原仍然是世界上最肥沃的土地之一。

words.definition_en: v. to make indirect reference
words.example_en: Many passages in Scripture allude to this concept.
words.example_zh: 圣经中有许多经文间接地提到这样的概念。

words.definition_en: adj. skillful and clever
words.example_en: The pianist has deft fingers.
words.example_zh: 钢琴家有灵巧的双手。

words.definition_en: v. to make less painful, difficult, or severe
words.example_en: Tranquilizers help alleviate the distressing symptoms of anxiety.
words.example_zh: 镇静剂有助于减轻焦虑所带来的不安症状。

words.definition_en: n. lacking of honesty
words.example_en: Politicians are often accused of mendacity.
words.example_zh: 政客们常被斥为扯谎成性。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to subdue or deaden (the body, bodily appetites, etc.) (2)v. to cause someone to feel extremely embarrassed or ashamed
words.example_en: Jane mortified her family by leaving her husband.
words.example_zh: 简离开丈夫的做法使她的家庭蒙羞。

words.definition_en: adj. aggressively malicious
words.example_en: He was died of a malignant tumor.
words.example_zh: 他死于恶性肿瘤。

words.definition_en: n. bitter deep-seated ill will
words.example_en: I have no rancor against him.
words.example_zh: 我对他毫无怨恨。

words.definition_en: adj. of a happy lighthearted character or disposition
words.example_en: Face everyday in your life with a blithe spirit , try to experience , to taste , to appreciate.
words.example_zh: 以愉快的心态去面对你生命中的每一天，尝试着去体会，去品味，去感激。

words.definition_en: n. a drawing position in chess in which a player is not in checkmate but has no legal move to play
words.example_en: The talks ended in (a) stalemate.
words.example_zh: 谈判陷入僵局，无果而终。

words.definition_en: n. edge
words.example_en: The country is teetering on the brink of civil war.
words.example_zh: 这个国家正处在内战的边缘。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to form an opinion about something without definitely knowing the truth (2)n. a thought or idea based on scanty evidence
words.example_en: From the looks on their faces, I surmised that they had had an argument.
words.example_zh: 看他们的脸色，我猜想他们之间发生了争执。

words.definition_en: adj. causing envy: very desirable
words.example_en: She learned to speak foreign languages with enviable fluency.
words.example_zh: 她学会了说几种外语，其流利程度令人羡慕。

words.definition_en: v. to regard with extreme repugnance
words.example_en: They abhor all forms of racial discrimination.
words.example_zh: 他们憎恶任何形式的种族歧视。

words.definition_en: adj. having the same or coincident boundaries
words.definition_zh: 边界重合的；范围相同的
words.example_en: The two jurisdictions are coterminous.
words.example_zh: 这两个司法辖区的边界重合。

words.definition_en: adj. very cruel
words.example_en: I couldn't believe they were so heartless.
words.example_zh: 我无法相信他们竟如此狠心。

words.definition_en: n. a desire or liking for something
words.example_en: She gave him just enough information to whet his appetite.
words.example_zh: 她向他透露的信息刚好能吊起他的胃口。

words.definition_en: adj. cloudy or muddy
words.example_en: Alum will settle turbid water.
words.example_zh: 明矾可以澄清浊水。

words.definition_en: v. besiege
words.example_en: Civilians trapped in the beleaguered city were airlifted to safety.
words.example_zh: 被困在这座遭围困城市里的平民被空运到安全地带。

words.definition_en: adj. well known because it has existed for a long time
words.example_en: This unit is now an established part of the course.
words.example_zh: 这个单元现在为本课程既定的一部分。

words.definition_en: (1)v. break, disregard (2)v. interrupt, disturb
words.example_en: They violated the ceasefire agreement.
words.example_zh: 他们违反了停火协议。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. being such to a degree that is extreme, excessive, or markedly abnormal (2)adj. altered or caused by disease
words.definition_zh: 很不正常的疾病引起的
words.example_en: He experiences chronic, almost pathological jealousy
words.example_zh: 他长期心怀近乎病态的嫉妒。

words.definition_en: v. to happen, go, or come before
words.example_en: His resignation was preceded by weeks of speculation.
words.example_zh: 在他辞职之前，有关的猜测已持续了几个星期。

words.definition_en: adj. easily disrupted, overthrown, or damaged
words.example_en: Pine is brittle and breaks.
words.example_zh: 松木硬脆易折。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by warmth of feeling typically expressed in eager zealous support or activity
words.example_en: He's been one of the most ardent supporters of the administration's policy.
words.example_zh: 他一向是政府政策最热情的支持者之一。

words.definition_en: n. the action of rejecting
words.example_en: The rejection of this measure aroused the people to indignation.
words.example_zh: 对该议案的否决激起人民的愤慨。

words.definition_en: v. to grow or develop successfully
words.example_en: Today his company continues to thrive.
words.example_zh: 今天，他的公司继续蓬勃发展。

words.definition_en: n. a usually pharmacologically inert preparation prescribed more for the mental relief of the patient than for its actual effect on a disorder
words.example_en: The placebo effect can be understood only if we acknowledge the unity of mind and body.
words.example_zh: 只有我们承认身心一体，安慰剂效果才能被理解。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. having extreme political or social views that are not shared by most people (2)adj. very basic and important
words.example_en: The Socialists introduced fairly radical reforms.
words.example_zh: 社会主义者推行了相当激进的改革。

words.definition_en: n. a person of prominence or brilliant achievement
words.example_en: Corporate luminaries cackle on about the importance of quality.
words.example_zh: 企业界名人喋喋不休地谈论质量的重要性。

words.definition_en: v. to take apart so that it is in separate pieces
words.example_en: I had to dismantle the engine in order to repair it.
words.example_zh: 我得把发动机拆开来修理。

words.definition_en: adj. not likely to be seen or noticed by many people
words.example_en: I should make a few discreet enquiries about the firm before signing anything.
words.example_zh: 我应先审慎打探一下这家公司的底细,然后再签字。

words.definition_en: n. a quick and cheerful readiness to do something
words.example_en: Such books we read with resignation rather than with alacrity.
words.example_zh: 这样的书我们是无可奈何地而不是轻松愉快地去读的.

words.definition_en: v. to break into small pieces
words.example_en: Rice flour makes the cake less likely to crumble.
words.example_zh: 这种糕饼用米粉做不那么容易碎。

words.definition_en: v. to treat with too much care or kindness
words.example_en: She coddled her youngest son madly.
words.example_zh: 她对小儿子过于溺爱。

words.definition_en: v. to encroach on the rights (as in trade) of others
words.example_en: He was accused of interloping in a field outside his expertise.
words.example_zh: 他被指责擅自插手自己专业之外的领域。

words.definition_en: adj. used for ideas, requirements, or interests that cannot all be right or satisfied at the same time
words.example_en: They talked about the competing theories of the origin of life.
words.example_zh: 他们讨论了几种相互矛盾的生命起源理论。

words.definition_en: v. to check or hinder the growth, development or activity of
words.example_en: High prices have choked off demand.
words.example_zh: 高昂的价格抑制了需求。

words.definition_en: v. to call upon in supplication
words.example_en: Mercede's come to implore the protection of Mr. de Villefort.
words.example_zh: 美茜蒂丝来请求维尔福先生庇护。

words.definition_en: n. careful use of money, resources, etc.
words.example_en: It seems a false economy to me to cut down on libraries.
words.example_zh: 我看来，减少图书馆的数量并不是真正的节约。

words.definition_en: v. to make up or form something
words.example_en: The long-term unemployed now constitute a sort of underclass.
words.example_zh: 长期失业人员现在构成了一个底层阶级。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to refuse to accept or support (2)v. to refuse to having anything to do with
words.example_en: He used his position to repudiate the charge.
words.example_zh: 他利用自己的职位来否认指控.

words.definition_en: adj. marked by excessive enthusiasm and often intense uncritical devotion
words.example_en: His fanatic devotion to the cause alarmed his friends.
words.example_zh: 他对这项事业的狂热投入令朋友们担忧。

words.definition_en: adj. feeding on both animal and vegetable substances
words.example_en: Brown bears are omnivorous, eating anything that they can get their paws on.
words.example_zh: 棕熊是杂食性动物，抓到什么吃什么。

words.definition_en: v. to criticize harshly for doing something wrong
words.example_en: Thomas Rane chastised Peters for his cruelty.
words.example_zh: 托马斯·拉纳严厉训斥彼得斯的残酷行为。

words.definition_en: v. to examine thoroughly
words.example_en: They had ensured the plumbing was overhauled a year ago.
words.example_zh: 他们曾保证水管一年前彻底检修过。

words.definition_en: v. to make perpetual or cause to last indefinitely
words.example_en: We must not perpetuate the religious divisions of the past.
words.example_zh: 我们绝对不能让过去的宗教分裂继续下去。

words.definition_en: v. to do away with: destroy
words.example_en: A storm moved directly over the island, demolishing buildings and flooding streets.
words.example_zh: 暴风雨径直席卷该岛，摧毁了建筑，淹没了街道。

words.definition_en: n. a ruler with absolute power and authority
words.example_en: He was a cruel despot.
words.example_zh: 他是一个残酷无情的暴君。

words.definition_en: adj. changing often
words.example_en: Emily's mercurial temperament made her difficult to live with.
words.example_zh: 埃米莉脾气反复无常，很难与她相处。

words.definition_en: n. the act of obeying
words.example_en: Obedience by women towards men is a remnant of religious teaching.
words.example_zh: 女人对男人的顺从是宗教教义的残迹。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by shameless or disrespectful boldness
words.example_en: The brazen woman laughed loudly at the judge who sentenced her.
words.example_zh: 那无耻的女子冲着给她判刑的法官高声大笑。

words.definition_en: adj. being in an early stage of development
words.example_en: The forum is now open to all budding entrepreneurs.
words.example_zh: 这个论坛目前向所有企业家新秀敞开大门。

words.definition_en: n. a reason that you give to hide your real reason for doing something
words.example_en: They wanted a pretext for subduing the region by force.
words.example_zh: 他们需要一个用武力征服那个地区的托词。

words.definition_en: adj. causing destructive or serious damage
words.example_en: Undoubtedly, racism has baneful impact not only on globalization but on multi-cultures as well.
words.example_zh: 毫无疑问的是，种族主义对于全球化和多元化文化交流有不利影响。

words.definition_en: n. something that is unusual or unexpected
words.example_en: But others believe the U.S. slowdown was an aberration.
words.example_zh: 但也有人认为美国增长的放缓是反常的。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by steady, earnest, and energetic effort
words.example_en: Meyers is a diligent and prolific writer.
words.example_zh: 迈耶斯是个勤奋多产的工人。

words.definition_en: adj. being in agreement or harmony
words.example_en: Other governments are far from consonant on a range of issues, such as how to structure a regime for verifying countries' adherence to promised carbon reductions.
words.example_zh: 其它政府也远远没能够协调一系列问题，比如如何构建一个体制去保证国家所承诺的减排目标实施。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to surprise and confuse (2)v. to prove wrong
words.example_en: Don't confound the means with the ends.
words.example_zh: 不要混淆手段与目的。

words.definition_en: v. to refuse to accept
words.example_en: Eve spurned Mark's invitation.
words.example_zh: 伊夫一口回绝了马克的邀请。

words.definition_en: adj. conspicuously offensive
words.example_en: Obviously, cyclists’ flagrant disrespect for the law is a threat to public safety.
words.example_zh: 显然，骑车族公然藐视法律，对公众安全是一种威胁。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to stop trying to resist something (2)v. to die
words.example_en: The Minister said his country would never succumb to pressure.
words.example_zh: 首相说他的国家永远都不会屈服于压迫。

words.definition_en: n. an imperfection that impairs worth or utility
words.example_en: A report has pointed out the defects of the present system.
words.example_zh: 一份报告指出了当前体制存在的缺陷。

words.definition_en: adj. having a clear decisive relevance to the matter in hand
words.example_en: Mrs Southern listened keenly, occasionally breaking in with pertinent questions.
words.example_zh: 萨瑟恩夫人兴致勃勃地听着，间或插话问一些相关的问题。

words.definition_en: v. to make an exact copy of
words.example_en: His task will be to duplicate his success overseas here at home.
words.example_zh: 他在海外取得了成功，现在的任务就是要在国内再创辉煌。

words.definition_en: v. to assume the burden or responsibility of
words.example_en: He has had to shoulder the responsibility of his father's mistakes...
words.example_zh: 他得为他父亲的过错承担责任。

words.definition_en: n. a large number
words.example_en: He asked me a slew of questions.
words.example_zh: 他问了我许多问题。

words.definition_en: adj. behaving in an unreasonably impatient and angry way, like a child
words.example_en: Bryant is often portrayed as petulant, selfish, and at times overbearing in newspaper coverage and magazine profiles.
words.example_zh: 科比常被媒体描述为脾气坏的、自私的球员，有时还在报纸封面和杂志外形中显得傲慢。

words.definition_en: v. to move rapidly or forcefully
words.example_en: A pretty young girl came hurtling down the stairs.
words.example_zh: 一个漂亮的小女孩从楼梯上冲下来。

words.definition_en: adj. using too many words in speaking or writing
words.example_en: So much needless repetition makes the article long-winded.
words.example_zh: 这样叠床架屋，文章就太罗嗦了。

words.definition_en: v. to subdue or reduce in intensity or severity
words.example_en: They are trying to allay public fears about the spread of the disease.
words.example_zh: 他们正竭力减轻公众对这种疾病传播的恐惧。

words.definition_en: v. to flow or pass slowly through fine pores or small openings
words.example_en: Water seeped from a crack in the pipe.
words.example_zh: 水从管道的一个裂缝中渗出。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to cause to exist or continue without changing (2)v. to affirm in or as if in argument
words.example_en: The two countries have always maintained close relations.
words.example_zh: 这两个国家一直保持着密切关系。

words.definition_en: adj. annoying or irritating
words.example_en: She desired a fixed occupation-no matter how onerous, how irksome.
words.example_zh: 她希望有个固定的职业，不管这个职业多么繁重，多么令人厌倦。

words.definition_en: v. to give and take mutually
words.example_en: He needs these people to fulfill his ambitions and reciprocates by bringing out the best in each of them.
words.example_zh: 他需要这些人去实现他的雄心壮志，作为回报，他也会让他们充分施展各自的才能。

words.definition_en: v. to destroy or remove completely
words.example_en: The country must extirpate the evils of drug abuse.
words.example_zh: 这个国家必须根除毒品泛滥的罪恶。

words.definition_en: v. to destroy completely so that nothing is left
words.example_en: There was time enough to obliterate memories of how things once were for him.
words.example_zh: 时间足以抹去他对过去经历的记忆。

words.definition_en: adj. producing young or fruit especially freely
words.example_en: She is a prolific writer of novels and short stories.
words.example_zh: 她是一位多产的作家，写了很多小说和短篇故事。

words.definition_en: v. to gather into storage
words.example_en: His priceless collection of Chinese art and artefacts was garnered over three decades.
words.example_zh: 他那批价值连城的中国艺术品和手工艺品是花了30多年才收集起来的。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. erect in carriage or posture (2)adj. marked by strong moral rectitude
words.example_en: Helen sat in an upright position.
words.example_zh: 海伦以笔直的姿势坐在椅子上。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a way of reaching agreement in which each person or group gives up something that was wanted in order to end an argument or dispute (2)v. to damage or weaken
words.example_en: His radicalism and refusal to compromise isolated him.
words.example_zh: 他的激进主义与拒绝妥协使他受到孤立。

words.definition_en: n. a legal prohibition on commerce
words.example_en: The United Nations imposed an arms embargo against the country.
words.example_zh: 联合国对那个国家实施了武器禁运。

words.definition_en: adj. very easy to see or notice
words.example_en: The advertisements were all posted in a conspicuous place.
words.example_zh: 广告都贴在了显眼的地方。

words.definition_en: n. a usually crude and temporary expedient
words.example_en: And it is a rational choice to modern society management pattern, not a makeshift.
words.example_zh: 它也不是一种权宜之计，而是对现代社会治理模式的理性选择。

words.definition_en: adj. not subject to control of the will
words.example_en: Another surge of pain in my ankle caused me to give an involuntary shudder.
words.example_zh: 踝关节的又一阵疼痛使我不由自主地颤抖了一下。

words.definition_en: adj. tending to remain calm
words.example_en: He was a man of the most equable temper.
words.example_zh: 他是个脾气非常好的人。

words.definition_en: adj. lasting or enduring through all time
words.example_en: I have loved you with an everlasting love!
words.example_zh: 我对你的爱从未改变！

words.definition_en: (1)n. a solemn oath (2)n. an earnest urging or advising
words.example_en: Her last adjuration to daughter was to escape from dinginess if she could.
words.example_zh: 她对女儿最后的叮嘱是要竭尽全力摆脱这种困难。

words.definition_en: (1)n. the act of providing money to create or support a school, organization, etc. (2)n. a person’s natural ability or talent
words.example_en: The hospital was founded on an endowment fund.
words.example_zh: 这所医院是用捐赠基金创办的。

words.definition_en: adj. brief and direct in a way that may see rude or unfriendly
words.example_en: The President issued a terse statement denying the charges.
words.example_zh: 总统发表了一份简短的声明，否认那些指控。

words.definition_en: adj. accepted as true or correct by most people
words.example_en: They are orthodox jews.
words.example_zh: 他们是正统的犹太教徒。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. unsure or uncertain (2)adj. causing doubt, uncertainty, or suspicion
words.example_en: This claim seems to us to be rather dubious.
words.example_zh: 在我们看来这种说法非常可疑。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. of a serious mien (2)adj. of a dull or heavy cast or shade
words.example_en: That was a somber January day.
words.example_zh: 那是一个阴沉的正月天.

words.definition_en: adj. lavish or superfluous ornament especially in architecture
words.definition_zh: 过分装饰的，华而不实的
words.example_en: There is a high-gabled gingerbread house.
words.example_zh: 一幢有高高的山墙、外部装饰过分雕琢的房屋。

words.definition_en: adj. generally incompetent
words.example_en: He was inept and lacked the intelligence to govern.
words.example_zh: 他没有什么能力，缺乏管理才干。

words.definition_en: adj. repeating something else and therefore unnecessary
words.example_en: Changes in technology may mean that once-valued skills are now redundant.
words.example_zh: 技术上的革新可能意味着曾经被重视的技术现在已变得多余。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to try to be like (2)v. to strive to equal or excel
words.example_en: You must work hard to emulate your sister.
words.example_zh: 你必须努力工作, 赶上你姐姐。

words.definition_en: adj. expressing or characteristic of weariness
words.example_en: She was weary of being alone.
words.example_zh: 她厌倦了独处。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to regard or think of as (2)v. estimate, compute
words.example_en: The sale has been held up because the price is reckoned to be too high.
words.example_zh: 销售陷入停滞，因为大家认为价格太高了。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. likely to cause damage or have a bad effect (2)adj. not friendly
words.example_en: His methods are inimical to radical change.
words.example_zh: 他的方法不利于彻底的变革。

words.definition_en: n. a condition of weariness or debility
words.example_en: Symptoms of anaemia include general fatigue and lassitude.
words.example_zh: 贫血症状包括全身疲劳和困乏。

words.definition_en: v. to affirm or declare positively or earnestly
words.example_en: I will asseverate my conviction that she is guilty.
words.example_zh: 我将郑重声明：我确信她有罪。

words.definition_en: adj. moving slowly or lazily
words.example_en: She suggests ways to jump-start the sluggish educational system.
words.example_zh: 她提出了几种方法来推动发展迟缓的教育体制。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by unusual delicacy or refinement
words.example_en: She's the prettiest, most ethereal romantic heroine in the movies.
words.example_zh: 她是所有电影中最美丽、最超凡的爱情女主角。

words.definition_en: n. one who is an acknowledged leader or chief proponent
words.example_en: He was enthroned as the guru of the avant-garde.
words.example_zh: 他被推崇为先锋派的权威。

words.definition_en: n. needless repetition of an idea, statement, or word
words.example_en: Modern logicians regard it as little more than tautology.
words.example_zh: 现代的逻辑学家仅仅把它看作同义反复.

words.definition_en: adj. attracting or seeking to attract attention, admiration, or envy often by gaudiness or obviousness
words.example_en: She's got a lovely way with language without ever sounding ostentatious.
words.example_zh: 她的语言优美，丝毫无装腔作势之感。

words.definition_en: v. help bring about
words.example_en: Friendly contacts between different peoples facilitate the cultural and economic interchange.
words.example_zh: 各国人民的友好接触促进文化和经济交流。

words.definition_en: v. to become impeded or stuck
words.example_en: Your car will bog down in the mud.
words.example_zh: 你的车会陷进泥沼的。

words.definition_en: adj. accustomed to dealing with fatigue or hardships
words.example_en: It should not surprise us that such an environment has produced a hardy and independent people.
words.example_zh: 这样的环境造就了一个吃苦耐劳、独立自主的民族，对此我们不应该感到意外。

words.definition_en: adj. agreeably stimulating because of freshness or newness
words.definition_zh: 提神的，使人重新振作的
words.example_en: People drink tea because of its variety and refreshing effect.
words.example_zh: 人们喝茶是因为茶的不同品种和提神的效果。

words.definition_en: v. to make very weak or tired
words.example_en: Idleness enervates the will to succeed.
words.example_zh: 懒惰削弱了成功的决心。

words.definition_en: adj. having a frightening or threatening appearance
words.example_en: There was something forbidding on her face and I didn't dare to look at her.
words.example_zh: 她的脸上露出令人生畏的表情，令我不敢正眼看她。

words.definition_en: v. compare
words.example_en: We often liken the heart to a pump.
words.example_zh: 我们常把心脏比作泵。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. very thin (2)adj. having little substance or strength
words.example_en: The cultural and historical links between the many provinces were seen to be very tenuous
words.example_zh: 许多省份之间的文化和历史纽带被认为是很脆弱的。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. punctiliously exact (2)adj. having moral integrity
words.example_en: Both readers commend Knutson for his scrupulous attention to detail.
words.example_zh: 两位读者都称赞克努森对细节一丝不苟。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. dirty and in bad condition form being neglected (2)adj. dishonest or immoral
words.definition_zh: 肮脏的不诚实的，邪恶的
words.example_en: They took me to a sleazy back-street hotel.
words.example_zh: 他们把我带到一个肮脏的后街旅馆。

words.definition_en: v. to avoid
words.example_en: From that time forward everybody shunned him.
words.example_zh: 从那时候起，每个人都有意回避他。

words.definition_en: n. an idea or opinion that someone has before learning about or experiencing something directly
words.example_en: He did not allow his preconceptions to compromise his scientific work.
words.example_zh: 他不允许自己的先入之见破坏自己的科学工作。

words.definition_en: adj. having an erratic or intermittent character
words.example_en: The government is making slow and fitful progress in these negotiations.
words.example_zh: 政府从这些谈判中正取得缓慢的、间断性的进展。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. appear disturbed and upset or they seem to be slightly crazy. (2)adj. unfair or inaccurate because it emphasizes some things and ignores others
words.example_en: I knew how unbalanced Paula had been since my uncle Peter died.
words.example_zh: 我知道葆拉自从我叔叔彼得去世后情绪一直很不稳定。

words.definition_en: adj. very serious about doing things in a direct and efficient way without any foolishness or nonsense
words.example_en: She saw herself as a direct, no-nonsense, modern woman.
words.example_zh: 她视自己为一个率直、高效干练的现代女性。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to be upside down
words.example_en: Their results upend some of our basic assumptions.
words.example_zh: 研究结果颠覆了我们的一些基本假设。

words.definition_en: v. to avoid giving a promise or direct answer
words.example_en: They hedged in answering various questions about the operation.
words.example_zh: 他们闪烁其词，不愿回答有关此次行动的各种问题。

words.definition_en: v. to increase in amount
words.example_en: For several hours, tension mounted.
words.example_zh: 有几个小时，紧张局势有所加剧。

words.definition_en: adj. having no meaning, purpose, or effect
words.example_en: Without an audience the performance is pointless.
words.example_zh: 没有观众，表演也就没有意义了。

words.definition_en: adj. perfectly typical or representative of a particular kind of person or thing
words.example_en: Chopstick is the quintessential cutlery choice throughout most of Asia.
words.example_zh: 筷子是亚洲大部分国家最典型的餐具选择。

words.definition_en: v. to torment by or as if by constant attack
words.example_en: She has been harried by the press all week.
words.example_zh: 整个星期她都受到新闻界的不断烦扰。

words.definition_en: v. to put things in an orderly form
words.example_en: The latest draft of the agreement codifies the panel's decision.
words.example_zh: 最新的协议草案把专家小组的决定加以整理，形成了条文。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not capable of being affected or disturbed (2)adj. not allowing entrance or passage
words.definition_zh: 不受影响的不能渗透的
words.example_en: She seems almost impervious to the criticism from all sides.
words.example_zh: 对于来自各方的指责，她似乎无动于衷。

words.definition_en: v. to lose turgor from lack of water
words.example_en: The roses wilted the day after she bought them.
words.example_zh: 她买的玫瑰第二天就凋谢了。

words.definition_en: v. to make it work again after a period in which it has not been working
words.example_en: American government has launched a series of economic reforms to reactivate the economy.
words.example_zh: 美国政府推行了一系列旨在复苏经济的经济改革。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a feeling of uneasiness about a point especially of conscience or propriety (2)n. a sudden feeling of usually disturbing emotion (such as doubt or fear)
words.example_en: I have no qualms about recommending this approach.
words.example_zh: 我会毫不犹豫地推荐这一方法。

words.definition_en: adj. expressing opinions and feelings in an honest and sincere way
words.example_en: I haven't been completely candid with him.
words.example_zh: 我对他并非完全坦诚

words.definition_en: adj. owing gratitude or recognition to another
words.example_en: I am deeply indebted to my family for all their help.
words.example_zh: 我深深感激我的家人给我的所有帮助。

words.definition_en: v. to make powerless or ineffective
words.example_en: In combat they paralyze the enemy's will, and easily gain advantage over less experienced foes.
words.example_zh: 在战斗中他们能瘫痪敌人的意志，在对付经验相对较少的敌人时轻易地取得战斗优势。

words.definition_en: adj. self-evident
words.example_en: It is axiomatic that as people grow older they generally become less agile.
words.example_zh: 人年纪越大通常灵活性越差，这是不言而喻的。

words.definition_en: n. something that comes before something else and that often leads to or influences its development
words.example_en: Westwood is always the precursor of the punk revolution after all, whose goal is to challenge the traditional raiment of the fashion world.
words.example_zh: 韦斯特伍德始终是朋克革命的先驱者，她的目标就是向时装界的传统服饰挑战。

words.definition_en: v. to make or adapt to suit a special need or purpose
words.example_en: Most travel agents are prepared to tailor travel arrangements to meet individual requirements.
words.example_zh: 为了满足个别需要，大多数旅行社都愿意做出专门的旅游安排。

words.definition_en: n. a condition characterized by the excessive accumulation and storage of fat in the body
words.example_en: The study also demonstrated a direct link between obesity and mortality.
words.example_zh: 该研究还表明了肥胖症和死亡率之间存在直接的联系。

words.definition_en: n. an animal or plant much below normal size
words.example_en: In most stories, dwarfs often have magical powers.
words.example_zh: 在大部分的故事中，小矮人有神奇魔法。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by fast and nervous, disordered, or anxiety-driven activity
words.example_en: A bird had been locked in and was by now quite frantic.
words.example_zh: 一只鸟被关进了笼子，到现在它都十分狂躁。

words.definition_en: v. to make known to many people
words.example_en: The shipping industry promulgated a voluntary code.
words.example_zh: 航运业对自律守则进行了宣传。

words.definition_en: adj. completely unwilling to change
words.example_en: They put pressure on the Government to change its intransigent stance.
words.example_zh: 他们向政府施压以迫使其改变不妥协的立场。

words.definition_en: adj. dull or ordinary
words.example_en: His instructor offered a more prosaic explanation for the surge in interest.
words.example_zh: 他的老师对利率的急剧上升给出了一个更枯燥乏味的解释。

words.definition_en: n. a confusing or difficult problem
words.example_en: Faith-based institutions appear to offer a solution to the conundrum.
words.example_zh: 宗教机构看起来为难题提供了解决方法。

words.definition_en: adj. secret or mysterious
words.example_en: Communities built around common goals and beliefs, rather than arcane licensing policies trying to protect a company's mobile market.
words.example_zh: 在群体里大家有着共同的目标和信仰，不会使用那些晦涩难懂的许可条款来保护某些公司的移动市场。

words.definition_en: n. a comprehensive presentation of a subject
words.example_en: The tower offers a breathtaking panorama of Prague.
words.example_zh: 从塔上可看到壮丽的布拉格全景。

words.definition_en: n. a place, employment, status, or activity for which a person or thing is best fitted
words.definition_zh: 合适的职业，合适的工作
words.example_en: He eventually found his niche in sports journalism.
words.example_zh: 最后他在体育新闻界找到了理想的工作。

words.definition_en: v. to tell everyone about something that you are proud of, especially in an annoying way
words.example_en: Mark Morris, who is trumpeted as the dance talent of his generation…
words.example_zh: 马克·莫里斯，被吹捧为他那一代的舞蹈奇才

words.definition_en: (1)adj. having a sickness that can be passed to someone else by touching (2)adj. capable of being easily spread to others
words.definition_zh: 接触传染的有感染力的
words.example_en: People with contagious diseases should be isolated.
words.example_zh: 传染病患者应予以隔离。

words.definition_en: n. seller
words.example_en: Kamal was a vendor in Egypt's open-air bazaar.
words.example_zh: 卡迈勒是埃及露天集市里的一个小贩。

words.definition_en: n. a large amount of something
words.example_en: Irrigation is easy in the South because of the abundance of water.
words.example_zh: 南方多水,利于灌溉。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking remembrance, memory, or mindful attention
words.example_en: He was oblivious of what was happening.
words.example_zh: 他没有察觉正在发生什么。

words.definition_en: n. a right or immunity granted as a peculiar benefit, advantage, or favor
words.example_en: The Russian Federation has issued a decree abolishing special privileges for government officials.
words.example_zh: 俄罗斯联邦已经颁布法令废除政府官员的特权。

words.definition_en: n. the highest point of something
words.example_en: His work is considered the acme of cinematic art.
words.example_zh: 他的作品被认为是电影艺术的巅峰之作。

words.definition_en: v. to pay attention to
words.example_en: If Democrats want to get anything done this year, they must heed such fears.
words.example_zh: 如果民主党打算今年做出点实事，他们就应当注意这样的一些担忧。

words.definition_en: adj. requiring much time, effort, or attention
words.example_en: He described Morris as an excessively demanding and faultfinding tutor.
words.example_zh: 他将莫里斯描述为一个过度苛刻和挑剔的导师。

words.definition_en: n. the smallest part or particle imaginable
words.example_en: There's not a whit of truth in the statement.
words.example_zh: 这声明里没有丝毫的真实性。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a feeling of closeness and understanding that someone has for another person because of their similar qualities, ideas, or interests (2)n. likeness based on relationship or causal connection (3)n. liking for or an attraction to something
words.example_en: It's important that you share an affinity with your husband.
words.example_zh: 和丈夫有共同的爱好是十分重要的。

words.definition_en: n. a disordered psychic or behavioral state resulting from severe mental or emotional stress or physical injury
words.example_en: He confronted his childhood trauma and tried to exorcise the pain.
words.example_zh: 他正视自己儿时所受的创伤，努力消除心中的痛苦。

words.definition_en: v. to surprise or upset very much
words.example_en: They were stunned by the view from the summit.
words.example_zh: 在峰顶看到的景色使他们惊叹不已。

words.definition_en: adj. having a strong influence on ideas, works, events, etc., that come later
words.example_en: The reforms have been a seminal event in the history of the NHS.
words.example_zh: 这些改革已成为英国国民保健制度史上一件具有开创性的大事。

words.definition_en: adj. having or exhibiting self-importance
words.example_en: He was somewhat pompous and had a high opinion of his own capabilities.
words.example_zh: 他有些爱摆架子，并且高估自己的能力。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to say or declare openly (2)v. to believe in (a particular religion)
words.example_en: She still professes her innocence.
words.example_zh: 她仍然声称自己无辜。

words.definition_en: n. a standard for making a critical judgment
words.example_en: Exam results are not the only yardstick of a school's performance.
words.example_zh: 考试结果不是衡量学校水平的唯一标准。

words.definition_en: adj. not wavering or weakening
words.example_en: You will make it if you have unfaltering determination.
words.example_zh: 如果你有坚定的决心，你就一定会成功！

words.definition_en: adj. not able to be doubted
words.example_en: His legal position is unassailable.
words.example_zh: 他的合法地位是不容置疑的。

words.definition_en: v. to offset
words.example_en: You should add honey to counterbalance the acidity.
words.example_zh: 你应该加点蜂蜜来调和酸味。

words.definition_en: n. an original model on which something is patterned
words.example_en: Metal-wheeled chariots are the prototype of the tanks of modern warfare.
words.example_zh: 金属轮战车是现代战争中坦克的原型。

words.definition_en: n. a surrounding area or district
words.example_en: Crowds gathered in the vicinity of Trafalgar Square.
words.example_zh: 成群结队的人聚集在特拉法尔加广场周围。

words.definition_en: n. quickness of motion
words.example_en: Einstein's theory links energy with mass and velocity of light.
words.example_zh: 爱因斯坦的理论把能量同质量和光速联系起来.

words.definition_en: adj. accompanying especially in a subordinate or incidental way
words.example_en: Cultures that were better at trading saw a concomitant increase in their wealth.
words.example_zh: 比较擅长贸易的文明其财富也随之增长。

words.definition_en: n. a state of perplexity or doubt
words.example_en: The government appears to be in a quandary about what to do with so many people.
words.example_zh: 政府似乎陷入了左右为难的境地，不知道该拿这么多人怎么办。

words.definition_en: n. absence of order
words.example_en: Economic anarchy scourged the post-war world.
words.example_zh: 经济混乱使得战后的世界陷入水深火热之中。

words.definition_en: v. to completely suppress
words.example_en: The company increased its stake in the business, squelching rumours of a takeover bid.
words.example_zh: 该公司增加了其在生意中的股份，这样就压制住了被收购的谣言。

words.definition_en: adj. not usual or normal
words.example_en: Flying at 1,000 ft. he heard a peculiar noise from the rotors.
words.example_zh: 在1,000英尺的高度飞行时，他听到旋翼发出一种奇怪的噪音。

words.definition_en: n. an act of bidding farewell
words.example_en: He gave a touching valediction at graduation.
words.example_zh: 他在毕业典礼上发表了动人的告别辞。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to centralized control by an autocratic leader or hierarchy
words.example_en: In totalitarian societies, art exists for didactic purposes.
words.example_zh: 在极权社会中，艺术是为教诲目的存在的。

words.definition_en: adj. not modern or current
words.example_en: Romania badly needs aid to modernize its outmoded industries.
words.example_zh: 罗马尼亚迫切需要援助以对其过时的产业进行现代化。

words.definition_en: n. connection, link
words.example_en: The Prayer Book has provided a flexible enough nexus of beliefs to hold together the different church parties.
words.example_zh: 祈祷书为各信仰之间建起了足够灵活的联系纽带，以期将各个不同教派凝聚在一起。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to seem inferior
words.example_en: Such conduct will derogate from your reputation.
words.example_zh: 那样的行为有损你的名誉。

words.definition_en: v. to leave or get rid of quickly or without concern
words.example_en: The government declared that it did not dump radioactive waste at sea.
words.example_zh: 政府宣称并未将放射性废料倾倒在海里。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to ancient times
words.example_en: Though social philosophy is an archaic concept, it really is an emerging field of investigation.
words.example_zh: 可以说社会哲学是一个古老的概念又是一个新兴的研究领域。

words.definition_en: n. a cure or solution for any illness or problem
words.example_en: He said the introduction of market discipline to the economy was not a magic cure-all for its problems.
words.example_zh: 他说把市场规范引入该国经济，并不是解决其经济问题的灵丹妙药。

words.definition_en: adj. exhibiting mature qualities at an unusually early age
words.example_en: Margaret was always a precocious child.
words.example_zh: 玛格丽特一直是个早熟的孩子。

words.definition_en: (1)v. construct, manufacture (2)v. to make up for the purpose of deception
words.example_en: The evidence was totally fabricated.
words.example_zh: 这个证据纯属伪造。

words.definition_en: adj. of, relating to, or arranged in a hierarchy
words.example_en: Congress is a collegial and not a hierarchical body.
words.example_zh: 国会是一个学院式的机构,而不是一个等级制度的机构。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. willing to lie and trick people in order to get what is wanted (2)adj. not straight or direct
words.example_en: His devious nature was shown in half-lies and small dishonesties.
words.example_zh: 他不正直的天性表现在小谎话及细小的不诚实行为中。

words.definition_en: n. the act of treating people in a way that does not favor some over others
words.example_en: The new document will guarantee fairness for blacks in Georgia.
words.example_zh: 新文件将保证佐治亚州的黑人受到公平对待。

words.definition_en: adj. of, relating to, or arranged in a particular order or sequence
words.example_en: In this way the children are introduced to sequential learning.
words.example_zh: 这样孩子们就会循序渐进地学习。

words.definition_en: adj. firmly established by long persistence
words.example_en: It is hard for an inveterate smoker to give up tobacco.
words.example_zh: 要一位有多年烟瘾的烟民戒烟是困难的。

words.definition_en: adj. equal or similar to something in size, amount, or degree
words.example_en: He was given a job commensurate with his abilities.
words.example_zh: 他得到一份与他的能力相称的工作。

words.definition_en: v. to change or alter greatly and often with grotesque or humorous effect
words.example_en: Almost overnight, that sweet, little child had transmogrified into an antisocial monster.
words.example_zh: 几乎是一夜之间，那个可爱的小孩彻底变成了一个反社会的魔王。

words.definition_en: v. to increase in extent, volume, number, amount, intensity, or scope
words.example_en: The fighting escalated into a full-scale war.
words.example_zh: 这场交战逐步扩大为全面战争。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to an order of existence beyond the visible observable universe
words.example_en: Today, most people no longer believe in ghosts and witches. But these supernatural beings are still a part of Halloween.
words.example_zh: 在今天，很多人不再相信有幽灵和巫婆，但这些神奇的人物仍然是万圣节的一部分。

words.definition_en: adj. extremely excited
words.example_en: One overwrought member had to be restrained by friends.
words.example_zh: 一位成员过于激动，朋友们不得不进行拦阻。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. extremely good or excellent (2)adj. easy to notice especially because of being important or very good
words.example_en: Derartu is an outstanding athlete and deserved to win.
words.example_zh: 德拉图是位杰出的运动员，他理应获胜。

words.definition_en: n. time to come
words.example_en: He predicts the tremendous shadows which futurity casts upon the present.
words.example_zh: 他预测到未来给当今世界蒙上的巨大阴影。

words.definition_en: adj. existing in every part of something
words.example_en: It is knowledge directed to the fundamental and pervasive concerns of existence.
words.example_zh: 它要回答的是人类生存的根本、普遍的问题。

words.definition_en: v. to carry away with delight, wonder, or rapture
words.example_en: Her beauty entirely entrance him.
words.example_zh: 她的美丽完全使他出神了。

words.definition_en: adj. happening too soon or earlier than usual
words.example_en: His career was brought to a premature end by a succession of knee injuries.
words.example_zh: 他的职业生涯因连续的膝伤过早地结束了。

words.definition_en: adj. of or relating to a clerk or office worker
words.example_en: He was still attired in his clerical outfit.
words.example_zh: 他还穿着牧师的行头。

words.definition_en: adj. substantially real
words.example_en: The policy has not yet brought any tangible benefits.
words.example_zh: 这项政策还没有带来任何切实可行的好处。

words.definition_en: v. to commit to a goal or way of life
words.example_en: Bessie has dedicated her life to caring for others.
words.example_zh: 贝茜一生都致力于照顾他人。

words.definition_en: adj. very smart or clever
words.example_en: The ingenious boy made a radio set for himself.
words.example_zh: 那个聪明的男孩自己组装了一台收音机。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. showing approval (2)adj. marked by success
words.example_en: We could have done better under more favorable conditions.
words.example_zh: 在更有利的条件下我们还可以做得更好。

words.definition_en: v. to accept, support or approve of
words.example_en: Your father won't countenance you going abroad.
words.example_zh: 你父亲不会赞同你出国。

words.definition_en: v. to vary between opposing beliefs, feelings, or theories
words.example_en: He oscillated between optimism and despair.
words.example_zh: 他在乐观与绝望之间摇摆不定。

words.definition_en: v. to incite or rouse as if with a goad
words.example_en: His opposition goaded her into working even harder to succeed.
words.example_zh: 他的反对反而激励她更加努力地争取成功。

words.definition_en: adj. permitting or designed to permit spontaneous and unguided responses
words.example_en: Girls do better on open-ended tasks that require them to think for themselves.
words.example_zh: 女孩子更擅长需要独立思考的开放式任务。

words.definition_en: v. to promote the growth or development of
words.example_en: The rebels know the truth and seek to foment revolution.
words.example_zh: 那些叛乱者知道真相,并且想办法来挑起革命。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. belonging to the earliest period or state (2)adj. not spoiled, corrupted, or polluted
words.example_en: The car is in pristine condition.
words.example_zh: 这辆汽车是全新的。

words.definition_en: v. to plunder ruthlessly
words.example_en: The rebels went looting and pillaging.
words.example_zh: 叛乱者趁火打劫，掠夺财物。

words.definition_en: adj. causing feelings of anger and annoyance
words.example_en: The current situation is very frustrating for us.
words.example_zh: 当前的情形令我们十分灰心。

words.definition_en: n. an interruption in time or continuity
words.example_en: There was a hiatus in his acting life.
words.example_zh: 他的演艺生涯曾一度中断。

words.definition_en: adj. moving in an awkward or clumsy way
words.example_en: I thought him terribly ungainly when he danced.
words.example_zh: 我觉得他跳舞时显得极其笨拙。

words.definition_en: adj.
words.example_en: Being too large and all-encompassing is a common defect among state-owned enterprises.
words.example_zh: 过分追求大而全,是国企的一大通病。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to remove (something that is not wanted or needed) (2)v. to defeat and remove from a competition
words.example_en: I was eliminated from the 400 meters in the semi-finals.
words.example_zh: 我在400米半决赛中被淘汰。

words.definition_en: adj. vertical in position
words.example_en: He is a man of erect and soldierly bearing.
words.example_zh: 他是一个身姿挺拔、具有军人风度的男子。

words.definition_en: adj. full of lively activity
words.example_en: The bustling market stayed open until midnight.
words.example_zh: 熙熙攘攘的市场一直营业到午夜。

words.definition_en: n. a powerful often violent whirlpool sucking in objects within a given radius
words.example_en: Inside, she was a maelstrom of churning emotions.
words.example_zh: 她心中的情感似波涛汹涌，起伏不定。

words.definition_en: adj. not showing enough care and attention
words.example_en: I would be remiss if I did not do something about it.
words.example_zh: 如果我对此不做点儿什么，就是懈怠。

words.definition_en: v. to raise trivial and frivolous objection
words.example_en: Although the essay was flawless, the teacher kept caviling at the student's handwriting.
words.example_zh: 虽然文章无可挑剔，老师仍不断挑剔学生的书写。

words.definition_en: adj. beyond what is needed
words.example_en: She gave him a look that made words superfluous.
words.example_zh: 她看了他一眼，这已表明一切，无须多言了。

words.definition_en: adj. naively innocent and trusting
words.example_en: She and Dan were still dewy-eyed enough to think that they would get full compensation.
words.example_zh: 她和丹仍然非常天真，以为他们可以得到全额的补偿。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to cause to feel very excited or happy (2)n. a quivering or trembling caused by sudden excitement or emotion
words.example_en: It gave me a big thrill to meet my favorite author in person.
words.example_zh: 能见到我最喜欢的作者本人使我感到兴奋不已。

words.definition_en: v. to stir up
words.example_en: The Second World War kindled his enthusiasm for politics.
words.example_zh: 第二次世界大战激发了他的政治热情。

words.definition_en: n. malicious or defamatory gossip
words.example_en: There has been no hint of scandal during his time in office.
words.example_zh: 他在任期间没有任何丑闻。

words.definition_en: n. excessive or slavish admiration or flattery
words.example_en: Despite his performance at the Oscars ceremony and being caught up in all the glitz and adulation, Rahman is a low-key star.
words.example_zh: 尽管他在奥斯卡盛典上的表现及为奢华与恭维所包围，拉曼仍是个低调的明星。

words.definition_en: adj. always right and accurate
words.example_en: She has an unerring instinct for people's weak spots.
words.example_zh: 她能准确地把握他人的弱点。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to separate widely
words.example_en: The explosion scattered a flock of birds roosting in the trees.
words.example_zh: 爆炸声把栖息在树丛中的鸟群惊散了。

words.definition_en: adj. giving exact rules, directions, or instructions about how you should do something
words.example_en: The son of a mandarin has no prescriptive right to succeed his father.
words.example_zh: 高级官员的儿子没有法定的权力继承他的父亲。

words.definition_en: adj. tediously uniform or unvarying
words.example_en: It's monotonous work, like most factory jobs.
words.example_zh: 与工厂大部分工作一样，这份工作也很单调乏味。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to cause to happen or be created (2)v. to cause someone to have (a feeling or emotion)
words.example_en: Our challenge is to motivate those voters and inspire them to join our cause.
words.example_zh: 我们面临的挑战是如何调动那些选民的积极性并鼓励他们加入我们的事业。

words.definition_en: v. to give, transfer or deliver into the hands or control of another
words.example_en: The father consigned the child to his sister's care.
words.example_zh: 那位父亲把孩子托付给他妹妹照看。

words.definition_en: v. to fill or build up again
words.example_en: The government is trying to replenish supplies by airlifting food.
words.example_zh: 政府正试图通过空投食品补充供应。

words.definition_en: n. pain or suffering affecting the body, a bodily part, or the mind
words.example_en: Marie felt her distress ebbing away.
words.example_zh: 玛丽感到她的痛苦渐渐减退了。

words.definition_en: v. to present for acceptance
words.example_en: The army has not yet proffered an explanation of how and why the accident happened.
words.example_zh: 军队还没有对这次事件发生的经过和原因给出解释。

words.definition_en: n. an arbitrary constant whose value characterizes a member of a system
words.example_en: Each input parameter should have the variable name and its value.
words.example_zh: 各输入参数应该有自己的变量名和值。

words.definition_en: adj. deliberately not obeying people in authority or rules of behavior
words.example_en: I would feel rebellious against all life.
words.example_zh: 我觉得对所有的生活都有一种反抗的情绪。

words.definition_en: v. to obscure the existence or true state or character of
words.example_en: It is impossible to disguise the fact that finance is bad.
words.example_zh: 财政困难这一事实是无法隐瞒的。

words.definition_en: adj. having requisite or adequate ability or qualities
words.example_en: I was deemed to be a competent shorthand typist.
words.example_zh: 我被视为是一名能干的速记打字员。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. relating to a scientific experiment or to scientific experiments in general (2)adj. using a new way of doing or thinking about something
words.example_en: The experimental results show that the syntaxic method is effective.
words.example_zh: 实验结果表明该综合性方法效果良好。

words.definition_en: v. to check or control
words.example_en: we do need to act convincingly to curb future deficits.
words.example_zh: 我们需要采取令人信服的行动来控制将来的赤字。

words.definition_en: adj. having little value or importance
words.example_en: Outside California these difficulties may seem fairly trifling.
words.example_zh: 在别处，加利福尼亚的这些困难可能看起来相当微不足道。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by resolute fearlessness, fortitude, and endurance
words.example_en: Intrepid rally-drivers meet in Beijing for the start of the Peking to Paris Motor Challenge.
words.example_zh: 无畏的拉力赛选手相聚北京，参加北京到巴黎赛车挑战赛的开赛。

words.definition_en: v. to establish by legal and authoritative act
words.example_en: The king could not enact laws without the sanction of Parliament.
words.example_zh: 未经议会批准，国王不能制定法律。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing an ability to notice and understand things that are difficult or not obvious
words.example_en: It was very perspicacious of you to find the cause of the trouble so quickly.
words.example_zh: 你真是明察秋毫，问题的原因这麽快就找出来了。

words.definition_en: v. to make confused
words.example_en: Jokes about raccoon hunting in the book may bemuse international readers.
words.example_zh: 书中关于捕猎浣熊的笑话可能会让海外读者摸不着头脑。

words.definition_en: v. to fasten or restrain by or as if by a tether
words.example_en: The officer dismounted, tethering his horse to a tree.
words.example_zh: 这位军官下了马，把马拴在一棵树上。

words.definition_en: adj. awkward or inefficient in use or construction
words.example_en: He is still smarting over criticism of his victorious but clumsy performance.
words.example_zh: 别人批评他尽管最终胜出但表现笨拙，他为这样的批评感到闷闷不乐。

words.definition_en: v. to evade the point of an argument by caviling about words
words.example_en: He quibbled about the price.
words.example_zh: 他为价格争论。

words.definition_en: v. to offer for discussion or consideration
words.example_en: Zoologist Eugene Morton has propounded a general theory of the vocal sounds that animals make.
words.example_zh: 动物学家尤金·莫顿提出了一个有关动物发声的概括性理论。

words.definition_en: v. to bring into peril
words.example_en: Conservationists say the oil leak could imperil the long-term survival of the creatures.
words.example_zh: 自然保护协会人士认为石油泄露将长期威胁海龟的生存。

words.definition_en: adj. existing in a state of intellectual, moral, or social darkness
words.example_en: Listen to both sides and you will be enlightened; heed only one side and you will be benighted.
words.example_zh: 听则明，偏信则暗。

words.definition_en: v. to make someone feel less worried or frightened
words.example_en: They tried to reassure her, but she still felt anxious.
words.example_zh: 他们设法让她放心，可她还是焦虑不安。

words.definition_en: v. to stir to activity
words.example_en: The proposal would spark a storm of protest around the country.
words.example_zh: 这一提案将引发全国性的抗议浪潮。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not sincere (2)adj. humanly contrived often on a natural model
words.example_en: It was the first commercially available machine to employ artificial intelligence.
words.example_zh: 这是第一台作为商品出售的采用人工智能技术的机器。

words.definition_en: n. an angry and usually long speech or piece of writing that strongly criticizes someone or something
words.example_en: The book is a diatribe against the academic left.
words.example_zh: 该书对学界左派进行了抨击。

words.definition_en: n. the quality of a person who is completely honest
words.example_en: Her probity and integrity are beyond question.
words.example_zh: 她的诚实和正直是无可争辩的。

words.definition_en: v. to catch
words.example_en: Allied troops captured over 300 enemy soldiers.
words.example_zh: 盟军俘虏了300多名敌方士兵。

words.definition_en: adj. irregular, unusual
words.example_en: Jamie is an atypical English schoolboy.
words.example_zh: 杰米跟一般的英国男学生不太一样。

words.definition_en: adj. requiring immediate attention
words.example_en: It has becoming an exigent task to find a "new continent" of species resources.
words.example_zh: 寻找生物种质资源的“新大陆”已经成为了一个迫切的问题。

words.definition_en: n. a narrow opening or crack of considerable length and depth usually occurring from some breaking or parting
words.example_en: Just ahead of us there was a huge fissure.
words.example_zh: 离我们不远处,出现一个大裂缝。

words.definition_en: v. to free from or as if from a tether
words.example_en: Only better regulation can untether economies from the fickle fortunes of finance.
words.example_zh: 只有更好的监管，才能把经济体从金融业变幻无常的命运中解脱出来。

words.definition_en: n. a model or pattern for something that may be copied
words.example_en: He had become the paradigm of the successful man.
words.example_zh: 他已经成为成功人士的典范。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. incidental, indirect (2)adj. oblique in direction; slanting or deflected
words.example_en: He made only a glancing reference to the problem.
words.example_zh: 他只是顺带提了一下这个问题。

words.definition_en: v. to twist out of the true meaning or proportion
words.example_en: The media distorts reality and categorizes people as all good or all bad.
words.example_zh: 媒体会歪曲事实，将人说得不是完美无缺就是一无是处。

words.definition_en: v. to leave out
words.example_en: Did you omit some small but vital factor from one of your Columns?
words.example_zh: 是不是你在某一列中遗漏了一些微小却关键的因素？

words.definition_en: adj. brilliantly or showily bright, colorful, or impressive
words.example_en: The evening was crowned by her dazzling performance.
words.example_zh: 她那令人炫目的表演把晚会的气氛推向高潮。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by extreme calm, impassivity, and steadiness
words.example_en: He remained imperturbable in spite of the hysteria and panic all around him.
words.example_zh: 尽管他周围的人歇斯底里地惊慌失措，但他仍保持冷静。

words.definition_en: adj. impossible to deny
words.example_en: It is an undeniable fact that crime is increasing.
words.example_zh: 犯罪在增加是无可争辩的事实。

words.definition_en: v. to ignore in a deliberate and insulting way
words.example_en: He snubbed her in public and made her feel an idiot
words.example_zh: 他当众冷落她，让她觉得自己像一个白痴似的。

words.definition_en: adj. deeply felt
words.example_en: We would like to take this opportunity to convey our heartfelt condolences to the families of the victims.
words.example_zh: 我们谨此向受害人家属表达我们衷心的慰唁。

words.definition_en: n. a combination or mixture of things
words.example_en: His previous fusions of jazz, pop and African melodies have proved highly successful.
words.example_zh: 事实证明他先前融合了爵士乐、流行乐和非洲旋律的作品非常成功。

words.definition_en: v. to deprive of vital elements or resources, such as personnel, equipment or funding, for use elsewhere
words.example_en: Margins are eroding because new products cannibalize the market and customers of older products.
words.example_zh: 利润率也在下降，因为新产品侵占了老产品的市场和消费者。

words.definition_en: adj. alike or similar
words.example_en: I recall many discussions with her on these and kindred topics.
words.example_zh: 我回想起多次同她就这些问题及类似话题进行的讨论。

words.definition_en: adj. dull, boring, and ordinary
words.example_en: They were tired of the humdrum routine of their work.
words.example_zh: 他们厌倦了单调乏味的日常工作。

words.definition_en: v. to make a hole through
words.example_en: I refused to wear headphones because they can perforate your eardrums.
words.example_zh: 我不愿戴耳机，因为它可能会使鼓膜穿孔。

words.definition_en: adj. having no example or parallel
words.example_en: In recent years, with the rapid development of economic construction in our country, high-grade highway construction is evolving with unexampled speed.
words.example_zh: 近些年来，随着国民经济持续高速发展，高等级公路的建设正以前所未有的发展速度向前推进。

words.definition_en: v. to damage or ruin the force or effectiveness of
words.example_en: If he becomes the major opposition leader, he could hamstring a conservative-led coalition.
words.example_zh: 如果他成为主要反对党的领袖，可能会导致保守党领导的联合政府陷于瘫痪。

words.definition_en: n. a very small difference in color, tone, meaning, etc.
words.example_en: We can use our eyes and facial expressions to communicate virtually every subtle nuance of emotion.
words.example_zh: 我们可以用眼睛和面部表情来如实地传达情感上的每一丝细微差别。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to get secure usually lasting possession or control of (2)adj. protected from danger or harm
words.example_en: The team managed to secure a place in the finals.
words.example_zh: 球队成功赢得了一个决赛席位。

words.definition_en: v. to indulge oneself immoderately
words.example_en: I wanted only to wallow in my own grief.
words.example_zh: 我只想沉湎于自己的悲伤中。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by or calling for energy or stamina
words.example_en: Strenuous efforts had been made to improve conditions in the jail.
words.example_zh: 为改善那家监狱里的条件已做了艰苦的努力。

words.definition_en: adj. requiring maximum effort or ability
words.example_en: The most testing time is undoubtedly in the early months of your return to work.
words.example_zh: 最考验人的时候无疑是在你重返工作岗位的头几个月。

words.definition_en: adj. not willing to tell people about things
words.example_en: He was extremely reticent about his personal life.
words.example_zh: 他对自己的个人生活讳莫如深。

words.definition_en: v. to gain or regain the favor or goodwill of
words.example_en: Sacrifices were made to propitiate the gods.
words.example_zh: 人们供奉祭品以求神灵息怒。

words.definition_en: adj. not lasting long
words.example_en: In most cases, pain is transient.
words.example_zh: 大多数情况下，疼痛是短暂的。

words.definition_en: adj. without deliberation
words.example_en: Their headlong rush into expansion ended badly.
words.example_zh: 他们轻率地急于扩张，最终结局不佳。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to make fit, suitable, or congruous (2)v. to make room for
words.example_en: The runway of this airport is undergoing expansion to accommodate large planes.
words.example_zh: 这个机场的跑道正在扩建以适应大型飞机起落。

words.definition_en: adj. not original or special
words.example_en: For god sake, this is a canned soap opera.
words.example_zh: 上帝啊，这简直就是一出千篇一律的肥皂剧。

words.definition_en: v. to transfer (a sports team) to a lower ranking division
words.example_en: If Leigh lose, they'll be relegated.
words.example_zh: 如果利失球，他们将会降级。

words.definition_en: adj. notably or brilliantly outstanding because of dignity or achievements or actions
words.example_en: The composer was one of many illustrious visitors to the town.
words.example_zh: 那位作曲家是许多造访过这个城市的杰出人物之一。

words.definition_en: n. a name that someone uses instead of his or her real name
words.example_en: Both plays were published under the pseudonym of Philip Dayre.
words.example_zh: 两个剧本都是以菲利普·戴尔的笔名出版的。

words.definition_en: adj. not to be endured: intolerable
words.example_en: The heat and humidity were insufferable.
words.example_zh: 高温和潮湿让人难以忍受。

words.definition_en: v. to restrain from movement, progress, or action
words.example_en: He was manacled by the police.
words.example_zh: 他被警察上了镣铐。

words.definition_en: v. to sing in a low soft voice
words.example_en: He would much rather have been crooning in a smoky bar.
words.example_zh: 他宁愿在烟雾缭绕的酒吧里哼唱歌曲。

words.definition_en: adj. protracted excessively
words.example_en: he reviewer padded out his review with a lengthy biography of the author.
words.example_zh: 评论者在他的评论中添加了冗长的作者生平以拉长篇幅。

words.definition_en: adj. designed to be used once or only a limited number of times and then thrown away
words.example_en: It's estimated that around 80 per cent of babies wear disposable nappies.
words.example_zh: 据估计约 80% 的婴儿使用一次性尿布。

words.definition_en: adj. pretending to be morally better than other people
words.example_en: He writes smug, sanctimonious waffle.
words.example_zh: 他写的满篇都是自鸣得意、道貌岸然的废话。

words.definition_en: adj. having no relevant component removed or destroyed
words.example_en: The money was returned intact by its finder.
words.example_zh: 钱原封未动地被捡拾者送还。

words.definition_en: adj. very foolish or stupid
words.example_en: The president denounced the witless, nasty piece of journalism.
words.example_zh: 总统痛斥了那份极为肤浅、令人作呕的新闻报道。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing the ability or desire to do new and difficult things
words.example_en: He invented the Nobel Prize for enterprising scientists.
words.example_zh: 他为有进取心的科学家创设了诺贝尔奖。

words.definition_en: adj. showing strong and often angry feelings
words.example_en: One vehement critic is Michael Howard.
words.example_zh: 其中一位言辞激烈的评论家是迈克尔·霍华德。

words.definition_en: n. one that follows
words.example_en: He set out several principles that he hopes will guide his successors.
words.example_zh: 他制定出了几条原则,希望能指导其继任者。

words.definition_en: v. to proceed slowly or with difficulty
words.example_en: I wasn't badly hurt, but I injured my thigh and had to limp.
words.example_zh: 我伤得不重，但一条大腿伤到了，只好一瘸一拐地走。

words.definition_en: n. the act of retaining
words.example_en: The company needs to improve its training and retention of staff.
words.example_zh: 公司需要改进对员工的培训和留用工作。

words.definition_en: adj. self-proclaimed
words.example_en: Two of those arrested are said to be self-styled area commanders...
words.example_zh: 据说在那些被捕的人员中有两人自称是地区指挥官。

words.definition_en: adj. low in spirit
words.example_en: Barbara looked increasingly downcast as defeat loomed.
words.example_zh: 当败象逼近时，芭芭拉看上去越来越沮丧。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by or given to speech or writing that is given exaggerated importance by artificial or empty means
words.example_en: The once bombastic orator had himself lapsed into silence.
words.example_zh: 那个曾经夸夸其辞的演说家缄默不语。

words.definition_en: v. to move in a confused or disordered manner
words.example_en: They had jumbled it all up into a heap.
words.example_zh: 他们把所有的东西胡乱堆成了一堆。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. capable of becoming real (2)n. a chance or possibility that something will happen or exist in the future (3)n. an ability that someone has that can be developed to help that person become successful
words.example_en: The meeting has the potential to be a watershed event.
words.example_zh: 这次会议有可能成为一次分水岭事件。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by inactivity or repose
words.example_en: The rapid development of the economy turns the quiescent town into a noisy city.
words.example_zh: 经济的迅速发展使这个宁静的小镇成为闹市。

words.definition_en: adj. accustomed, used
words.example_en: He was wont to fall asleep after supper.
words.example_zh: 他习惯吃完晚饭就打盹。

words.definition_en: adj. not limited in any way
words.example_en: His ambition is unbounded.
words.example_zh: 他的野心毫无止境。

words.definition_en: v. to put into a state of agitated confusion
words.example_en: The unexpected guests flustered her.
words.example_zh: 不速之客的到来使她慌了神。

words.definition_en: adj. disposed to arouse or take part in or guilty of sedition
words.example_en: He distributed a seditious pamphlet called The Rights of Man.
words.example_zh: 他散发了名叫《人的权利》的带有煽动性的小册子。

words.definition_en: adj. not easily governed, managed, or directed
words.example_en: The economy still faces intractable problems.
words.example_zh: 经济仍然面临着一些棘手的问题。

words.definition_en: adj. regulated or determined in size or degree with reference to proportions
words.example_en: The value of a fraction is inversely proportional to that of the denominator.
words.example_zh: 分数值与分母值成反比例。

words.definition_en: n. the exact opposite of something or someone
words.example_en: I cannot vote for a candidate who stands for the antithesis of what I believe.
words.example_zh: 我不能选一个与我的信仰相反的候选人。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to engage in argument (2)v. to call into question
words.example_en: He disputed the allegations.
words.example_zh: 他对指控表示怀疑。

words.definition_en: n. the power to produce a desired result or effect
words.example_en: There is little information on the efficacy of this treatment.
words.example_zh: 关于此法的疗效目前所知甚少。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not interesting or exciting (2)adj. showing no emotion, concern, etc.
words.example_en: It tasted bland and insipid, like warmed cardboard.
words.example_zh: 这吃起来淡而无味，像加热过的纸板。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing an ability to think clearly
words.example_en: It lies in the blend of clearheaded logic and powerful intuition.
words.example_zh: 它是清晰的逻辑和强有力的直觉的融合。

words.definition_en: n. a calculated move: stratagem
words.example_en: He sees the proposal as more of a diplomatic gambit than a serious defense proposal.
words.example_zh: 在他看来，与其说这是一项精心筹划的国防动议，倒不如说是外交上的一着妙棋。

words.definition_en: v. to give evidence of displeasure or disapproval by or as if by facial expression
words.example_en: This practice is frowned upon as being wasteful
words.example_zh: 这种行为被认为是浪费而不获认可。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking originality, freshness, or novelty
words.example_en: The script is utterly banal. It is incredible that human minds can put such muck on to paper.
words.example_zh: 这个剧本是十足的平庸之作，难以想象有人会写出这样的垃圾。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. capable of adapting to varying conditions (2)adj. not real or sincere
words.example_en: TV game show hosts with their banal remarks and plastic smiles.
words.example_zh: 那些说话陈腐、笑容虚假的电视竞赛节目主持人。

words.definition_en: v. to encroach upon in a way that violates law or the rights of another
words.example_en: The film exploited his image and infringed his copyright.
words.example_zh: 这部电影使用了他的形象，侵犯了他的肖像权。

words.definition_en: v. to depart secretly and hide oneself
words.example_en: Unfortunately, his partners were crooks and absconded with the funds.
words.example_zh: 不幸的是，他的合伙人都是些骗子，已卷款逃之夭夭了。

words.definition_en: v. to be a sign of
words.example_en: This decision signified a radical change in their policies.
words.example_zh: 这个决定表明了他们的政策发生了根本的变化。

words.definition_en: adj. not firm or stiff
words.example_en: I picked up her wrist. It was limp and flaccid.
words.example_zh: 我抓起她绵软无力的手腕。

words.definition_en: adj. having a mutual or reciprocal relation
words.example_en: All things are interrelated and interact on each other.
words.example_zh: 一切事物都互相联系并相互作用。

words.definition_en: adj. quick to react or respond
words.example_en: Harriet was an easy, responsive little girl.
words.example_zh: 哈丽雅特是一个随和机灵的小女孩。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to have an effect (2)v. encroach, infringe
words.example_en: Your political opinions will necessarily impinge on your public life.
words.example_zh: 你的政治观点必然会影响你的社会生活。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to pay no attention to (2)v. to ignore or treat as unimportant
words.example_en: He disregarded the advice of his executives.
words.example_zh: 他对主管层的建议置若罔闻。

words.definition_en: adj. not kind or sympathetic toward other people
words.example_en: He was branded an unfeeling bully.
words.example_zh: 他被说成冷酷的恶棍。

words.definition_en: n. the degree or amount of strength or force that something has
words.example_en: Speech is made up of sound waves that vary in frequency and intensity.
words.example_zh: 语音是由不同频率和强度的声波组成的。

words.definition_en: n. an unfounded or mistaken impression or notion
words.example_en: Hallucination is common in patients who have suffered damage to the brain.
words.example_zh: 脑部受损的病人常会产生幻觉。

words.definition_en: adj. containing or giving large amounts of something
words.example_en: Rainfall is plentiful in the area.
words.example_zh: 这个地区雨量充足。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to subside
words.example_en: The government tried to appease discontented workers.
words.example_zh: 政府试图安抚不满的工人们。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. doing or requiring much sitting (2)adj. not migratory
words.example_en: People in sedentary jobs need to take exercise.
words.example_zh: 工作上要久坐的人需要做运动.

words.definition_en: (1)v. to make excessive claims for (2)v. to sell too much or too many
words.example_en: He has a tendency to oversell himself.
words.example_zh: 他爱自我吹嘘。

words.definition_en: adj. attracting attention or notice through unusual or conspicuous qualities
words.example_en: The most striking feature of those statistics is the high proportion of suicides.
words.example_zh: 那些统计数据最引人注目之处是其极高的自杀率。

words.definition_en: adj. flippant, impertinent
words.example_en: Don't be flip with me.
words.example_zh: 不要对我油嘴滑舌。

words.definition_en: v. to make free from guilt, responsibility, etc.
words.example_en: How can he be absolved of all responsibility for the accident?
words.example_zh: 他怎么能被免去所有的事故责任呢？

words.definition_en: adj. incapable of being fatigued
words.example_en: His indefatigable spirit helped him to cope with his illness
words.example_zh: 他不屈不挠的精神帮助他对抗病魔。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by capriciousness, recklessness, or foolishness
words.example_en: His fast-paced novels are full of bizarre situations and madcap antics.
words.example_zh: 他那些快节奏的小说里充满了怪诞的场景和荒唐的行为。

words.definition_en: adj. having a quality or characteristic that makes a person or thing different from others
words.example_en: Cooking with the lid on gives the food that distinctive smoky flavor.
words.example_zh: 盖上盖子做会让这种食物有一种熏制般的独特风味。

words.definition_en: adj. done in a secret way
words.example_en: He made a surreptitious entrance to the club through the little door in the brick wall.
words.example_zh: 他从砖墙的小门偷偷进入俱乐部。

words.definition_en: adj. not stopping or growing weaker
words.example_en: He watched her with unremitting attention.
words.example_zh: 他一直全神贯注地注视着她。

words.definition_en: n. abnormal dread of being in closed or narrow spaces
words.example_en: He experienced feelings of claustrophobia from being in a small place.
words.example_zh: 他呆在一个狭小的地方，体验到了幽闭恐惧症的感觉。

words.definition_en: adj. very serious or formal in manner
words.example_en: His solemn little face broke into smiles.
words.example_zh: 他严肃的小脸蛋上突然绽放出笑容。

words.definition_en: adj. more than is usual, necessary, or proper
words.example_en: Baking a whole cheese in pastry may seem extravagant.
words.example_zh: 烤点心时加一整块奶酪似乎太奢侈了。

words.definition_en: adj. not showing or having tact
words.example_en: His tactless words had always incurred his father’s deep displeasure.
words.example_zh: 他嘴笨，说话总是使他父亲感到十分不快。

words.definition_en: v. to support, strengthen, or defend
words.example_en: You need more facts to buttress up your argument.
words.example_zh: 你需要有更多的事实来支持你的论据。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. readily adaptable or responsive to new situations (2)adj. capable of being bent or folded without creases, cracks, or breaks
words.definition_zh: 能适应的，灵活的柔韧的
words.example_en: A supple horse able to perform the work asked for by his rider.
words.example_zh: 一匹灵活的马能够完成骑手要求的任务。

words.definition_en: v. to display conspicuously or abundantly
words.example_en: Nearby was a factory which exuded a pungent smell.
words.example_zh: 附近是一家散发出一种刺鼻气味的工厂。

words.definition_en: adj. spend time only with other members of the group and seem unfriendly towards people who are not in the group
words.example_en: Wenzhou Christians tend to be cliquish and enjoy speaking their dialect among themselves.
words.example_zh: 在教会里温州基督徒总喜欢群聚讲自己的家乡话。

words.definition_en: v. to declare or maintain with certainty
words.example_en: All entrants must warrant that their entry is entirely their own work.
words.example_zh: 所有参赛者必须保证其参赛作品完全是自己创作的。

words.definition_en: adj. familiar with something so that it seems normal or usual
words.example_en: The general is not accustomed to having his word questioned.
words.example_zh: 将军不习惯有人对他的命令提出质疑。

words.definition_en: n. a state of temporary inactivity
words.example_en: The question is in abeyance until we know more about it.
words.example_zh: 问题暂时搁置，直到我们了解更多有关情况再行研究。

words.definition_en: adj. expressing suffering or sadness
words.example_en: They lay on the firm sands, listening to the plaintive cry of the seagulls.
words.example_zh: 他们躺在硬实的沙地上，听着海鸥的哀鸣。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to think about or discuss something very carefully in order to make a decision (2)adj. done or said in a way that is planned or intended (3)adj. done or decided after careful thought
words.definition_zh: 深思熟虑故意的谨慎的
words.example_en: She deliberated over the decision for a long time before she made up her mind.
words.example_zh: 她慎重考虑了好久才下定决心作出这一决定。

words.definition_en: v. to move something or someone to another place nimbly and quickly
words.example_en: He whisked her across the dance floor.
words.example_zh: 他带着她飞快地穿过舞池。

words.definition_en: adj. capable of being easily impressed
words.example_en: The child is at an impressionable age.
words.example_zh: 这孩子正处于易受影响的年龄。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by an impassive matter-of-fact manner, style, or expression
words.example_en: Some people don't catch his deadpan humor, that makes it even funnier.
words.example_zh: 有些人不能了解他那种无表情的幽默,因此更有趣。

words.definition_en: v. to search out and publicly expose real or apparent misconduct of a prominent individual or business
words.example_en: The young reporter muckrakes at every opportunity.
words.example_zh: 这位年轻的记者不放过揭发丑闻的任何机会。

words.definition_en: v. excite, stimulate
words.example_en: A really good catalogue can also whet customers 'appetites for merchandise.
words.example_zh: 一份真正好的商品目录也可以激起顾客购买的欲望。

words.definition_en: n. a stock of needed materials or supplies
words.example_en: The department is responsible for the provision of residential care services.
words.example_zh: 该部门负责提供家庭护理服务。

words.definition_en: adj. very clever or skillful
words.example_en: Jamie is a remarkably adroit and determined politician.
words.example_zh: 杰米是一位异常精明果断的政治家。

words.definition_en: v. to assume or claim as true, existent, or necessary
words.example_en: They postulated a 500-year lifespan for a plastic container.
words.example_zh: 他们假定塑料容器的寿命为500年。
