words.definition_en: adj. extremely poor or bad
words.example_en: The general standard of racing was abysmal.
words.example_zh: 竞赛总体水平极糟。

words.definition_en: adj. engrossing, fascinating
words.example_en: I find snooker riveting though I don't play myself.
words.example_zh: 虽然我自己不打斯诺克，但是我觉得它挺令人着迷。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. showing that something important, especially something bad, may happen (2)adj. eliciting amazement or wonder
words.definition_zh: (1)预示重大（尤指不祥）事件的 (2)令人惊异的
words.example_en: The report contains numerous portentous references to a future environmental calamity.
words.example_zh: 报告里有很多地方预兆性地提到了将来的环境灾难。

words.definition_en: adj. refusing to do what other people want
words.example_en: Parts of the administration may be changing but others have been obdurate defenders of the status quo.
words.example_zh: 政府的一些部门可能正在作出改变，但是其他部门却执意维护现状。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. concerned only with the obvious or apparent (2)adj. lying close to the surface
words.example_en: This guy is a superficial yuppie with no intellect whatsoever.
words.example_zh: 这个家伙是个肤浅的雅皮士，没有什么头脑。

words.definition_en: adj. insufficient to pay all debts
words.example_en: The bank was declared insolvent.
words.example_zh: 银行被宣布破产。

words.definition_en: v. to make great or greater
words.example_en: An enterprise shall not aggrandize itself at the expense of environment.
words.example_zh: 企业不应以环境为代价增强自身的实力。

words.definition_en: adj. hopeful or romantic in a way that is not practical
words.example_en: He has always lived his life by a hopelessly quixotic code of honor.
words.example_zh: 他生活中一直遵循一种完全不切实际的堂吉诃德式的道德准则。

words.definition_en: v. to take, receive, or obtain especially from a specific source
words.example_en: The cause of the difference may derive from different accounting standards used.
words.example_zh: 导致账目出现差异的原因可能是采用了不同的会计准则。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. causing discussion, thought, argument, etc. (2)adj. serving or tending to provoke, excite, or stimulate
words.example_en: He has made a string of outspoken and sometimes provocative speeches in recent years.
words.example_zh: 他近些年发表了一连串直言不讳、有时带煽动性的演说。

words.definition_en: n. a group of organisms that use the same ecological resource in a similar way
words.example_en: The current contract between the Directors Guild of America and production companies ends in June.
words.example_zh: 美国导演协会和制片公司当前的合同将于六月份截止。

words.definition_en: adj. dismal, gloomy
words.example_en: Many of them remained sullen and resentful.
words.example_zh: 他们中有许多人还是闷闷不乐，怀恨在心。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. extremely important and necessary (2)adj. very basic
words.example_en: Money is not essential to happiness.
words.example_zh: 金钱对于幸福并非必不可少。

words.definition_en: n. a usually brief communication written for interoffice circulation
words.example_en: The delegation submitted a memorandum to the Commons on the blatant violations of basic human rights.
words.example_zh: 代表团向下院呈交了有关公然侵犯基本人权的备忘录。

words.definition_en: n. an ardent supporter
words.example_en: Her mother was a dedicated apostle of healthy eating.
words.example_zh: 她母亲是健康饮食矢志不渝的倡导者。

words.definition_en: v. to deprive of the benefit and protection of law
words.example_en: The German government has outlawed some fascist groups.
words.example_zh: 德国政府已经宣布一些法西斯团体为非法。

words.definition_en: v. to give up the use or enjoyment of
words.example_en: No one was prepared to forgo their lunch hour to attend the meeting.
words.example_zh: 谁都不愿意放弃午餐时间出席会议。

words.definition_en: adj. being the same
words.example_en: Nearly all the houses were identical.
words.example_zh: 几乎所有的房子都一模一样。

words.definition_en: n. one who takes part in dialogue or conversation
words.example_en: He was a more palatable interlocutor for the West than Mr Trump had been.
words.example_zh: 与特朗普相比，他是一个更容易被西方接受的对话者。

words.definition_en: adj. based on instinct or emotion rather than careful reasoning
words.example_en: His rejection of the proposal was a gut-level reaction rather than a reasoned judgment.
words.example_zh: 他拒绝该提议是出于本能反应，而非理性判断。

words.definition_en: adj. produced, growing, living, or occurring naturally in a particular region or environment
words.example_en: Not all indigenous regimes were willing to accept the supposed benefits of British trade.
words.example_zh: 并非所有当地的政权都愿意接受与英国贸易的所谓好处。

words.definition_en: v. to give off a sparkling or lustrous reflection of or as if of a moist or polished surface
words.example_en: Dewdrops glisten in the morning sun.
words.example_zh: 露珠在晨光下闪闪发光。

words.definition_en: n. a person who criticizes or opposes beliefs and practices that are widely accepted
words.example_en: Cage was an iconoclast. He refused to be bound by western musical traditions of harmony and structure.
words.example_zh: 凯奇是个反传统的人。他拒绝接受西方有关和声和结构的音乐传统的束缚。

words.definition_en: v. to tile to one side especially of a boat of ship
words.example_en: The ship listed again, and she was thrown back across the bunk.
words.example_zh: 船再次倾侧了，她又被甩回床铺的另一头。

words.definition_en: n. a poem, discourse, or utterance of a character in a drama that has the form of a monologue or gives the illusion of being a series of unspoken reflections
words.example_en: Hamlet's soliloquy is probably the most famous in English drama.
words.example_zh: 哈姆雷特的独白在英国戏剧中大概是最出名的了。

words.definition_en: adj. done with great force and energy
words.example_en: He was a vigorous, handsome young man.
words.example_zh: 他是个精力充沛、年轻帅气的小伙子。

words.definition_en: v. sully, soil
words.example_en: He has accused local people of trying to besmirch his reputation.
words.example_zh: 他指责当地人企图败坏他的名声。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. marked by futility or ineffectualness (2)adj. having or showing undue or excessive pride in one's appearance or achievements
words.example_en: All our efforts were in vain.
words.example_zh: 我们的所有努力都付诸东流了。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. violent and cruel in a way that hurts someone physically (2)adj. unpleasantly strong or severe
words.definition_zh: 凶险的，残酷的剧烈的，恶劣的
words.example_en: He suffered a vicious attack by a gang of white youths
words.example_zh: 他遭到一帮白人青年的残暴袭击。

words.definition_en: adj. done or made quickly
words.example_en: Burke cast a cursory glance at the menu, then flapped it shut.
words.example_zh: 伯克匆匆地瞥了一眼菜单，然后啪地合上了。

words.definition_en: v. to greet with enthusiastic approval
words.example_en: US magazines hailed her as the greatest rock'n'roll singer in the world.
words.example_zh: 美国杂志把她奉为世界上最伟大的摇滚歌手。

words.definition_en: adj. arousing or deserving hatred or repugnance
words.example_en: Herr Schmidt is certainly the most odious man I have ever met.
words.example_zh: 赫尔·施密特无疑是我见过的最让人作呕的人。

words.definition_en: adj. very reliable and trusted
words.example_en: He said all five were men of unimpeachable character.
words.example_zh: 他说这五个都是品格完美无缺的人。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by or showing reverence for deity and devotion to divine worship
words.example_en: The pious monk spent hours in prayer.
words.example_zh: 这位虔诚的僧侣花了数小时祈祷。

words.definition_en: adj. failing to take proper or normal care of something or someone
words.example_en: If the government is proved negligent, compensation will be payable.
words.example_zh: 如果证明是政府的疏忽，就应支付赔偿。

words.definition_en: n. the highest point of development or achievement
words.example_en: John Major has reached the pinnacle of British politics.
words.example_zh: 约翰·梅杰已攀上了英国政界的顶峰。

words.definition_en: adj. full of sadness or sorrow
words.example_en: He plays some passages so slowly that they become lugubrious.
words.example_zh: 有些段落他演奏得异常徐缓，听上去有些忧伤。

words.definition_en: adj. impressively direct and decisive (as in manner or presentation)
words.example_en: The narrative in book is ended by a pair of incisive essays.
words.example_zh: 这篇叙述文头尾部分都有深刻的短论。

words.definition_en: v. to avoid doing something that you are supposed to do
words.example_en: I do not shirk any responsibility on this matter.
words.example_zh: 在这件事上，我没有逃避任何责任。

words.definition_en: adj. causing horror or revulsion
words.example_en: She gave us a lurid description of the birth.
words.example_zh: 关于分娩，她向我们做了令人毛骨悚然的描述。

words.definition_en: adj. showing or done with great care and effort
words.example_en: They proved their theory by means of painstaking research.
words.example_zh: 他们证明的理论是经过苦心研究得来的。

words.definition_en: n. favoritism (as in appointment to a job) based on kinship
words.example_en: Cage changed his name to deflect accusations of nepotism.
words.example_zh: 凯奇改了名字以避免别人指责他搞裙带关系。

words.definition_en: adj. causing unrest, violence, or disturbance
words.example_en: The present international situation remains tense and turbulent.
words.example_zh: 当前的国际局势依然紧张动荡.

words.definition_en: v. to yield or grant typically by treaty
words.example_en: Germany and Japan need to cede to the emerging economies industries in which they are no longer competitive.
words.example_zh: 德国和日本应该对新兴经济体让出他们不在占有优势的行业。

words.definition_en: n. a hidden problem that makes something more complicated or difficult to do
words.example_en: After some technical hitches the show finally got under way.
words.example_zh: 一些小技术故障解决后，演出终于开始了。

words.definition_en: adj. done without any delay being purposely introduced
words.example_en: Death was instantaneous because both bullets hit the heart.
words.example_zh: 因为两颗子弹都击中了心脏，所以死亡是瞬间发生的。

words.definition_en: adj. inclined to ask questions
words.example_en: She is inquisitive about everyone's affairs.
words.example_zh: 她对每个人的事情都很好奇。

words.definition_en: v. to interpret wrongly
words.example_en: An outsider might misconstrue the nature of the relationship.
words.example_zh: 外人可能会误解这种关系的本质。

words.definition_en: v. to pretend or exaggerate incapacity or illness (as to avoid duty or work)
words.example_en: She was told by her doctor that she was malingering.
words.example_zh: 医生告诉她，她这是在装病。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to cover over completely (2)v. to overcome by superior force or numbers
words.example_en: The army was overwhelmed by the rebels.
words.example_zh: 军队被叛乱者完全击败了。

words.definition_en: adj. obsequiously submissive
words.example_en: She is expected to be subservient to her uncle.
words.example_zh: 她被要求对叔叔恭敬顺从。

words.definition_en: adj. tending to excite or inflame
words.example_en: The latest scheme is much smaller but a lot more incendiary.
words.example_zh: 最新的定居点计划规模小很多，然而却具有更大的煽动性。

words.definition_en: adj. reserved, shy
words.example_en: I'm still that shy, retiring little girl who was afraid to ask for sweets in the shop.
words.example_zh: 我仍然是那个羞怯孤僻，不敢在商店买糖果的小女孩。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. very small or too small in amount (2)adj. having little meaning, importance, or worth
words.example_en: His influence on this matter is paltry .
words.example_zh: 他对这件事的影响是微不足道的。

words.definition_en: n. dishonest or insincere behavior or speech that is meant to deceive someone
words.example_en: Emma saw his artifice, and returned to her first surmises.
words.example_zh: 爱玛看穿了他的伎俩后又回如最初的那样持怀疑态度。

words.definition_en: adj. designed or intended to teach people something
words.example_en: Most of the articles in the magazine are of a didactic nature.
words.example_zh: 杂志上大部分文章的本质都是说教性的。

words.definition_en: adj. happening or done very often
words.example_en: She has become a regular visitor to Houghton Hall.
words.example_zh: 她已成为霍顿府邸的一名常客。

words.definition_en: adj.
words.example_en: In the context of this chapter, Dore's comments strike a jarring note.
words.example_zh: 在这一章的语境中，多尔的评论显得很尖刻。

words.definition_en: adj. very small
words.example_en: She bought a diminutive toy for her daughter.
words.example_zh: 她给女儿买了个小巧可爱的玩具。

words.definition_en: n. something that shows what is coming
words.example_en: The cuckoo is a harbinger of spring.
words.example_zh: 布谷鸟预告春天的来临.

words.definition_en: (1)v. to grow well (2)v. to be very successful (3)v. to hold up and show in an excited or proud way
words.example_en: Few businesses are flourishing in the present economic climate.
words.example_zh: 在目前的经济气候下，很少有企业兴旺发达。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. arising unexpectedly (2)adj. newly formed or prominent
words.example_en: Bioengineering is an emergent branch of learning.
words.example_zh: 生物工程是一门新兴的学问。

words.definition_en: adj. not based on reason or evidence
words.example_en: It's difficult to say how likely such an arbitrary goal might be, but green leaders and others are optimistic.
words.example_zh: 很难去说这样一个武断的目标能有多靠谱，但环保领袖们和其他一些人却对此保持乐观。

words.definition_en: adj. not intended or planned
words.example_en: He became an unwitting accomplice in the crime.
words.example_zh: 他糊里糊涂地成了犯罪的帮凶。

words.definition_en: n. a strong natural liking for something that is usually bad
words.example_en: His proclivity for alcohol was almost as well-known as his poetry.
words.example_zh: 他的酗酒倾向几乎和他的诗作一样有名。

words.definition_en: adj. widely acclaimed and highly honored
words.example_en: She's a renowned landscape painter.
words.example_zh: 她是一位很有名的风景画画家。

words.definition_en: adj. made, done, or formed on or as if on the spur of the moment
words.example_en: The children put on an impromptu concert for the visitors.
words.example_zh: 孩子们为来访者即兴献上了一场音乐会。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to take an oblique course (2)v. to distort especially from a true value or symmetrical form
words.example_en: The ball skewed off at a right angle.
words.example_zh: 球呈直角偏离弹了出去。

words.definition_en: v. to scold or condemn vehemently and at length
words.example_en: Marion berated Joe for the noise he made.
words.example_zh: 玛丽昂严厉斥责乔吵吵闹闹。

words.definition_en: adj. not affected by personal or emotional involvement
words.definition_zh: 公正客观的，冷静客观的
words.example_en: We, as prosecutors, try to be dispassionate about the cases we bring.
words.example_zh: 作为检察官，我们对经手的案件会尽力做到不偏不倚。

words.definition_en: v. to influence or gently urge by caressing or flattering
words.example_en: He tried to coax the secret from me.
words.example_zh: 他试图哄我说出秘密。

words.definition_en: adj. showing or demanding excessive delicacy or care
words.definition_zh: 挑剔的，极其注重细节的
words.example_en: He was fastidious about his appearance.
words.example_zh: 他非常讲究仪表。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. clear and easy to read (2)adj. easy and enjoyable to read
words.definition_zh: 清晰易懂的可读性强的
words.example_en: This is an impeccably researched and very readable book.
words.example_zh: 这本书中的研究缜密透彻，可读性很强。

words.definition_en: v. to deny the existence or truth of
words.example_en: Alcohol negates the effects of the drug.
words.example_zh: 酒使药失去了效力。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. bitingly sarcastic (2)adj. tending or having the power to corrode
words.example_en: Sodium and sulphur are highly corrosive.
words.example_zh: 钠和硫磺腐蚀性很强。

words.definition_en: adj. not like talking about themselves or drawing attention to themselves
words.example_en: My teacher is a very quiet modest self-effacing person.
words.example_zh: 我的老师是一个很沉默谦虚不爱出风头的人。

words.definition_en: adj. full or loud in sound
words.example_en: The sonorous voice of the speaker echoed round the room.
words.example_zh: 那位演讲人洪亮的声音在室内回荡.

words.definition_en: (1)n. an outbreak of a disease that occurs over a wide geographic area and affects an exceptionally high proportion of the population (2)adj. occurring over a wide geographic area and affecting an exceptionally high proportion of the population
words.example_en: They feared a new cholera pandemic.
words.example_zh: 他们担心一种新的霍乱流行病。

words.definition_en: adj. removed or distant either physically or emotionally
words.example_en: He seemed aloof and detached.
words.example_zh: 他看起来冷淡且超然。

words.definition_en: adj. capable of devising ways and means
words.example_en: He was amazingly inventive and resourceful, and played a major role in my career.
words.example_zh: 他极具创造力，而且足智多谋，在我的职业生涯中起到了重要的作用。

words.definition_en: adj. caring only about making money
words.example_en: Mercenary men lust for wealth.
words.example_zh: 唯利是图的人贪求财富。

words.definition_en: adj. having the power to horrify
words.example_en: The film showed the most horrific murder scenes.
words.example_zh: 那部影片有极恐怖的谋杀场面。

words.definition_en: adj. causing, marked by, or expressing misery or grief
words.example_en: He used dolorous tone, chaotic style of painting showing extreme grief of our life.
words.example_zh: 他以忧伤的色调，纷乱的笔触，表达出人生的最大悲痛。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking power or strength
words.example_en: He blazed with impotent rage.
words.example_zh: 他勃然大怒，但于事无补。

words.definition_en: n. one that is eccentric
words.example_en: His mother and father thought Jim was a bit of an oddball too.
words.example_zh: 吉姆的父母也认为他有些古怪。

words.definition_en: n. good-natured easy friendliness
words.example_en: He was full of bonhomie.
words.example_zh: 他非常友善。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not named or identified (2)adj. lacking individuality, distinction, or recognizability
words.definition_zh: 无名的，匿名的无个性的
words.example_en: An anonymous caller told police what had happened.
words.example_zh: 有人打匿名电话向警方报告了所发生的事。

words.definition_en: v. to sing especially with a high or shaky voice
words.example_en: He warbled his way through the song.
words.example_zh: 整个歌曲他是用高颤音唱的。

words.definition_en: adj. having or showing sad thoughts and feelings about something that you want to have or do and especially about something that made you happy in the past
words.example_en: I can't help feeling slightly wistful about the perks I'm giving up.
words.example_zh: 我不禁对自己将要放弃的津贴感到有点儿不舍。

words.definition_en: adj. carelessly and foolishly spending money, time, etc.
words.example_en: Prodigal habits die hard.
words.example_zh: 大手大脚的习惯很难改掉。

words.definition_en: v. to get rid of
words.example_en: The crew jettisoned excess fuel and made an emergency landing.
words.example_zh: 机组人员丢弃了多余的燃料，紧急着陆。

words.definition_en: n. the use of few words to say something
words.example_en: Brevity is the soul of English.
words.example_zh: 简洁是英语的精华。

words.definition_en: (1)n. an expression of approval (2)v. to praise or honor
words.example_en: His new book received accolade from the papers.
words.example_zh: 他的新书受到报纸的热烈赞扬.

words.definition_en: adj. using more words than are needed
words.example_en: His writing is difficult and often verbose.
words.example_zh: 他的文章很晦涩，而且往往篇幅冗长。

words.definition_en: (1)v. speak evil of (2)adj. evil in nature, influence, or effect
words.example_en: Either the managers have been maligned or they are not telling the truth.
words.example_zh: 要么是经理们受到了污蔑，要么就是他们在撒谎。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. lacking dexterity or skill (2)adj. lacking the right proportions, size, or harmony of parts (3)adj. causing embarrassment (4)adj. not easy to handle or deal with
words.definition_zh: 笨拙的不合适的尴尬的棘手的
words.example_en: Amy made an awkward gesture with her hands.
words.example_zh: 艾米笨拙地做了个手势。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. able to change into many different forms or able to do many different things (2)adj. displaying great diversity or variety
words.definition_zh: 变化多端的多才多艺的
words.example_en: He is a protean stylist who can move from blues to ballads and grand symphony.
words.example_zh: 他是一位多才多艺的艺术家，能从布鲁斯转换到民谣以及雄壮的交响乐。

words.definition_en: v. to think carefully and deeply about something
words.example_en: As people grow older, they begin to ruminate on the uncertainty of life.
words.example_zh: 随着年龄的增长, 人们开始对生活的变化无常冥思苦想.

words.definition_en: v. to lose freshness, strength, or vitality
words.example_en: All color had faded from the sky.
words.example_zh: 天上的颜色都退去了。

words.definition_en: adj. present in all places at all times
words.example_en: These days the media are omnipresent.
words.example_zh: 现在新闻媒体无处不在。

words.definition_en: v. to give prominence to
words.example_en: Bright cushions accent the dark furniture.
words.example_zh: 鲜艳的靠垫衬托了深色家具。

words.definition_en: adj. having significant consequences
words.example_en: The report discusses a number of consequential matters that are yet to be decided.
words.example_zh: 这份报告讨论了许多有待决定的重大问题。

words.definition_en: v. to take the place or position of
words.example_en: Hand tools are relics of the past that have now been superseded by the machine.
words.example_zh: 手工工具是历史的遗物，现在已经被机器所取代。

words.definition_en: n. a businessperson of exceptional wealth, power, and influence
words.example_en: The tycoon is on the verge of bankruptcy.
words.example_zh: 那名大亨濒临破产的边缘。

words.definition_en: adj. disapproving
words.example_en: From frank's deprecatory remarks about the money "people" owed him, she had imagined the sums to be small.
words.example_zh: 从弗兰克不愿意提起“人们”欠他钱的态度来看，她一直以为这笔钱为数不多。

words.definition_en: n. any of the definable aspects that make up a subject (as of contemplation) or an object (as of consideration)
words.example_en: The caste system shapes nearly every facet of Indian life.
words.example_zh: 种姓制度几乎影响着印度生活的方方面面。

words.definition_en: v. to exist or happen at an earlier time than
words.example_en: His troubles predated the recession.
words.example_zh: 他的麻烦早在经济衰退前就出现了。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not knowing or caring about people and events in other places (2)adj. of, relating to, or coming from a province
words.example_en: Changsha is the provincial capital of Hunan.
words.example_zh: 湖南省的省会是长沙。

words.definition_en: n. the use of an unnecessarily large number of words to express an idea
words.example_en: He is a master at circumlocution.
words.example_zh: 他讲话很会兜圈子。

words.definition_en: n. polite actions and words
words.example_en: Staff members are trained to treat customers with civility at all times.
words.example_zh: 全体职员接受训练，以便在任何时候对顾客都以礼相待。

words.definition_en: adj. easily broken or destroyed
words.example_en: She lay in bed looking particularly frail.
words.example_zh: 她躺在床上，看上去特别虚弱。

words.definition_en: v. to choose not to do
words.example_en: The fans could not forbear crying out at the wonderful goal.
words.example_zh: 一记精彩的进球使球迷们禁不住叫喊起来。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not very important (2)adj. not included in the main part of society or of a group
words.example_en: The tribunals were established for the well-integrated members of society and not for marginal individuals.
words.example_zh: 这个特别法庭是为社会中的主流人群设立的，而不是为了无足轻重的小人物。

words.definition_en: v. to gain or attain usually by planned action or effort
words.example_en: Only with mass direct action will we obtain such change.
words.example_zh: 只有采取大规模直接行动，我们才会取得这样的改变。

words.definition_en: n. a new word, usage, or expression
words.example_en: The tech industry has a neologism: Gamification.
words.example_zh: 高新技术产业里有一个新词：游戏化。

words.definition_en: v. to reproach or challenge in a mocking or insulting manner
words.example_en: A gang taunted a disabled man.
words.example_zh: 一伙小混混嘲笑一位残疾人。

words.definition_en: v. to cause pain or suffering to
words.example_en: Italy has been afflicted by political corruption for decades...
words.example_zh: 几十年来意大利一直饱受政治腐败之苦。

words.definition_en: adj. big and heavy
words.example_en: Don had a hefty roll of flab overhanging his waistband.
words.example_zh: 唐的腰间沉甸甸地坠着一圈赘肉。

words.definition_en: adj. following in time, order, or place
words.example_en: Subsequent events confirmed our doubts.
words.example_zh: 后来发生的事证实了我们的怀疑。

words.definition_en: adj. able to become strong, happy, or successful again after a difficult situation or event
words.example_en: George Fraser was clearly a good soldier, calm and resilient.
words.example_zh: 乔治·弗雷泽显然是个好士兵，沉着冷静且适应力强。

words.definition_en: v. to satisfy (a need, a desire, etc.) fully or to excess
words.example_en: The dinner was enough to satiate the gourmets.
words.example_zh: 晚餐足以让这些美食家们大饱口福。

words.definition_en: v. to make clear and easier to understand
words.example_en: Our interesting teacher could illuminate almost any subject we studied.
words.example_zh: 我们这位有趣的老师几乎能解释清楚我们所学的所有科目.

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by or expressive of a concern with morality
words.example_en: To me moralistic films are just unbearable.
words.example_zh: 对我来说，说教电影简直难以忍受。

words.definition_en: adj. causing people to be offended
words.example_en: I find his theory objectionable in its racist undertones.
words.example_zh: 我觉得他理论中的种族主义含意令人厌恶。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to have particular meaning or importance for someone (2)v. to continue to produce a loud, clear, deep sound for a long time (3)v. to relate harmoniously
words.definition_zh: 对某人有重要性共振一致
words.example_en: What are the issues resonating with voters?
words.example_zh: 什么是投票者特别关心的问题？

words.definition_en: v. to thoroughly overwhelm and reduce to submission or passivity
words.example_en: Extra police were called in to quell the disturbances.
words.example_zh: 已调集了增援警力来平定骚乱。

words.definition_en: adj. tending to create a favorable impression
words.example_en: A prepossessing performer with a beautiful baritone, Murray is tall, blond and Midwestern-looking.
words.example_zh: 一个很吸引人的表演者和一个漂亮的男中音，莫利高大、金发碧眼，有着中西部人的外貌。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not precise or fixed in meaning: ambiguous, elusive (2)adj. not to be trusted
words.definition_zh: 难以抓住的，棘手的不可靠的
words.example_en: Actually the personality of a company is an odd and slippery thing.
words.example_zh: 事实上，企业个性是一种奇怪的而且靠不住的说法。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not false or artificial (2)adj. lacking art, knowledge, or skill
words.example_en: Her countenance and a few artless words fully conveyed all her gratitude and delight.
words.example_zh: 她的表情和寥寥几句质朴无华的语言已充分表达了她的感激与高兴。

words.definition_en: v. to criticize sharply
words.example_en: The president rebuked the House and Senate for not passing those bills within 100 days.
words.example_zh: 总统指责参众两院没有在100天之内通过那些法案。

words.definition_en: v. to check or diminish the activity or vigor
words.example_en: The government has been trying to dampen expectation of a quickeconomic recovery.
words.example_zh: 政府一直在设法抑制人们对经济迅速复苏的期望。

words.definition_en: (1)v. delay, defer (2)v. to extend forward or outward
words.example_en: The had certainly taken his time, even protracting the process.
words.example_zh: 他肯定是慢慢来的，甚至还拖延进程。

words.definition_en: n. something strange, droll, curious, or eccentric
words.definition_zh: 妙语，幽默滑稽奇怪的东西
words.example_en: He appears once again with a ready quip.
words.example_zh: 他再次出场妙语脱口而出。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. making provision for the future (2)adj. frugal, saving
words.example_en: They are provident statesmen.
words.example_zh: 他们是有远见的政治家。

words.definition_en: adj. relating to a group sharing cultural or national traditions
words.example_en: There are still several million ethnic Germans in Russia.
words.example_zh: 在俄罗斯仍有几百万德国族裔。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. pleasing to the mind or senses (2)adj. able to be accepted
words.example_en: To be idle sometimes is agreeable, but to be idle all the time might become monotonous.
words.example_zh: 一段时间空闲是令人愉快的，整日无所事事可能就单调乏味了。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to offend or insult (2)adj. very small in degree or amount
words.example_en: They felt slighted by not being adequately consulted.
words.example_zh: 没有充分征求他们的意见，这让他们感到被冷落了。

words.definition_en: adj. comfortably or conveniently spacious
words.example_en: Guestrooms are commodious and well-appointed.
words.example_zh: 客房宽敞舒适且陈设考究。

words.definition_en: v. to fill completely with something that permeates or pervades
words.example_en: The company had saturated the market for personal organizers (= so that no new buyers could be found) .
words.example_zh: 那家公司的产品已使电子记事簿市场饱和。

words.definition_en: n. an immature or inexperienced person
words.example_en: It turns out to be a fledgling mercenary to save them.
words.example_zh: 结果是一个新手雇佣兵去救他们。

words.definition_en: v. to be sparing or frugal
words.example_en: She never stints on the food at her parties.
words.example_zh: 她举办聚会吃的东西从不小气。

words.definition_en: adj. reduced to poverty
words.example_en: They were impoverished by a prolonged spell of unemployment.
words.example_zh: 他们因长期失业而一贫如洗。

words.definition_en: v. to struggle at close quarters with disorder and confusion
words.example_en: She scuffled with photographers as she left her hotel.
words.example_zh: 在离开所住的旅馆时，她和一些摄影记者发生了冲突。

words.definition_en: adj. giving warning
words.example_en: Some patients develop premonitory signs and symptoms, such as nervousness, sweating, and tachycardia.
words.example_zh: 病人可有先兆性症状，例如易于激动，出汗和心动过速。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to combine, unite, or coalesce
words.example_en: Bank of America merged with a rival bank.
words.example_zh: 美国银行和一家竞争银行合并了。

words.definition_en: v. to collect and often hide away a supply of
words.example_en: Because people expected prices to rise rapidly, they started to hoard goods.
words.example_zh: 由于人们预计价格会迅速上升，他们开始储藏商品。

words.definition_en: v. to overturn or overthrow from the foundation
words.example_en: The rebel army is attempting to subvert the government.
words.example_zh: 反叛军队企图颠覆政府统治。

words.definition_en: adj. standing or fixed in one place
words.example_en: Both your pictures are of static subjects.
words.example_zh: 你的两张照片拍的都是静止物体。

words.definition_en: n. a nervous or fearful feeling of uncertain agitation
words.example_en: I awaited the result of the football match with trepidation.
words.example_zh: 我忐忑不安地等着足球赛的结果。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. hard to find or capture (2)adj. hard to understand, define, or remember
words.example_en: Try to catch the elusive charm of the original in translation.
words.example_zh: 翻译时设法把握住原文中难以捉摸的风韵。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. highly developed and complex (2)adj. finely experienced and aware
words.example_en: They are cheating but are sophisticated enough to avoid detection.
words.example_zh: 他们在作弊，但因为手法老练没有被发现。

words.definition_en: n. a person who stimulates or annoys other people especially by persistent criticism
words.example_en: It still remains to be seen whether Buchanan will be just a gadfly or a real threat to the president.
words.example_zh: 布坎南只是故意搅局还是对总统构成真正的威胁，这还有待观察。

words.definition_en: v. reject or dismiss as contemptible or unworthy
words.example_en: Do not scorn your classmates.
words.example_zh: 不要鄙视你的同学。

words.definition_en: n. a privilege, gain, or profit incidental to regular salary or wages
words.example_en: Everybody is born with no perquisites.
words.example_zh: 每个人出生都没有特权。

words.definition_en: n. people who use violent or extreme methods
words.example_en: The country needs a strong intelligence service to counter espionage and foreign extremists.
words.example_zh: 该国需要强大的情报机构来打击间谍活动和外国极端分子。

words.definition_en: v. to prevent from doing something or to stop from happening
words.example_en: The accounting firm deliberately destroyed documents to thwart government investigators.
words.example_zh: 会计事务所故意毁坏文件，阻挠政府调查工作。

words.definition_en: n. a strong feeling of dislike or hatred
words.example_en: The wartime animus of the two nations subsided into mutual distrust when peace finally came.
words.example_zh: 当和平最终来临时这两个民族间战时的敌意消退为彼此的不信任。

words.definition_en: adj. of the greatest importance
words.example_en: This step is all-important for solving the problem.
words.example_zh: 这个步骤在解决此问题中至关重要。

words.definition_en: v. to cause of feeling of great wonder or surprise in
words.example_en: A matter of repeated occurrence like this will not astonish people.
words.example_zh: 这样司空见惯的事，不会使人吃惊。

words.definition_en: adj. very small
words.example_en: The film was shot in 17 days, a minuscule amount of time.
words.example_zh: 这部电影仅用短短的17天便拍摄完毕。

words.definition_en: v. to extract all the essential passages or portions from
words.definition_zh: 取出内脏，取出基本内容
words.example_en: It is not always necessary to gut the fish prior to freezing.
words.example_zh: 冷冻鱼之前并不总是需要先把内脏掏空。

words.definition_en: adj. very concerned about someone’s safety, health, or comfort
words.example_en: Knowing that the end was so near, she became rather solicitous.
words.example_zh: 她知道这一切即将结束, 反倒变得很有些放心不下了.

words.definition_en: adj. very fashionable
words.example_en: The place is as self-consciously trendy as they come.
words.example_zh: 这个地方非常有意识地追求时髦。

words.definition_en: n. the quality or state of being very exact, careful, or strict
words.example_en: The crime will be treated with the full rigor of the law.
words.example_zh: 这一罪行会严格依法审理。

words.definition_en: adj. genetically transmitted or transmittable from parent to offspring
words.example_en: In men, hair loss is hereditary.
words.example_zh: 男性脱发属于遗传。

words.definition_en: adj. disdainfully or skeptically humorous
words.example_en: Occasionally he made a humorously sardonic remark.
words.example_zh: 他时不时说一句幽默嘲讽的话。

words.definition_en: v. to publicly criticize in a very harsh way
words.example_en: She was pilloried in the press for her extravagant parties.
words.example_zh: 她因举办铺张的聚会而受到新闻界的严厉批评。

words.definition_en: adj. pleasant or amiable in person: attractive
words.definition_zh: 和蔼可亲的，平易近人的
words.example_en: Everyone knows that it's wise to be friendly and personable when in the company of the boss.
words.example_zh: 人人都知道，与老板相处时表现得友善而讨人喜欢是明智的。

words.definition_en: v. to behave in a manner conformable to what is right, proper, or expected
words.example_en: She comported herself with dignity throughout the hearing.
words.example_zh: 在整个听证过程中，她的举止始终端庄得体。

words.definition_en: adj. living or lasting for a short time
words.example_en: Chantal told Martine about her short-lived marriage.
words.example_zh: 尚塔尔跟马丁内谈起她短暂的婚姻。

words.definition_en: n. a church that is the official seat of a diocesan bishop
words.example_en: A prayer vigil is being held in the cathedral in memory of the bishop
words.example_zh: 为了纪念这位主教，教堂里正在举行守夜祈祷。

words.definition_en: n. a major change or period of change that causes a lot of conflict, confusion, anger, etc.
words.example_en: It was a violent political upheaval.
words.example_zh: 这是一场激烈的政治动乱。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by moderation
words.example_en: The Nile Valley keeps a temperate climate throughout the year.
words.example_zh: 尼罗河流域一年四季气候温和。

words.definition_en: adj. of, relating to, suggestive of, or characteristic of a totem or totemism
words.example_en: The religion of ancient Egypt emerged from the worship of tribal deities represented as totemic animals.
words.example_zh: 古埃及的宗教信仰产生于对以图腾动物为代表的部落神的膜拜。

words.definition_en: v. play down, de-emphasize
words.example_en: The environmental activist pleaded with the government not to soft-pedal industrial pollution.
words.example_zh: 环保活动人士请求政府不要淡化工业污染问题。

words.definition_en: n. the lowest point
words.example_en: The demand for this product will reach its nadir within two years.
words.example_zh: 对此产品的需求在两年内将达到最低点。

words.definition_en: v. to shorten by or as if by cutting off
words.example_en: He truncated a news item to fit the available space.
words.example_zh: 他删节一则新闻以适应报纸的篇幅。

words.definition_en: adj. lavishly productive
words.example_en: The vegetation is lush on the Hawaiian Islands.
words.example_zh: 夏威夷岛上的植被很葱翠。

words.definition_en: n. a typical or ideal example
words.example_en: Zhenjing GRE is the epitome of GRE training.
words.example_zh: 真经GRE是GRE培训的典型。

words.definition_en: adj. feeling bored or impatient while waiting for something to happen or change
words.example_en: The audience grew restive.
words.example_zh: 观众变得不耐烦了。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. made or produced first (2)adj. not like others
words.example_en: The original plan was to hold an indefinite stoppage.
words.example_zh: 最初的计划是无限期停工。

words.definition_en: v. to present and urge reasons in opposition
words.example_en: He remonstrated with the referee.
words.example_zh: 他向裁判抗议。

words.definition_en: adj. qualified to participate or be chosen
words.example_en: Mike was not eligible for the examination because he was over age.
words.example_zh: 迈克因年龄超限而没有资格参加考试。

words.definition_en: n. a hiding place especially for concealing and preserving provisions or implements
words.example_en: A huge arms cache was discovered by police.
words.example_zh: 警方发现了大批私藏的军火。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. complete or total (2)adj. not having the skills, knowledge, or experience needed to do a particular job or activity
words.definition_zh: 完全的，不受限制的无资格的
words.example_en: I gave her my unqualified support.
words.example_zh: 我全力支持她。

words.definition_en: v. to get rid of completely usually by killing off
words.example_en: Some people exterminate garden insects by spraying poison on the plants.
words.example_zh: 有些人在植物上喷撒毒剂以杀死花园内的昆虫。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. lacking confidence (2)adj. very careful about acting or speaking
words.definition_zh: 缺乏自信的言行谨慎的
words.example_en: He is diffident about expressing his opinions in the public.
words.example_zh: 他不敢在公开场合表达自己的意见。

words.definition_en: v. to delight in
words.example_en: She drank the wine slowly, savoring every drop.
words.example_zh: 她慢慢地喝着葡萄酒, 细细品味。

words.definition_en: adj. showing concern for the happiness and welfare of other people rather than for yourself
words.example_en: Nations are governed by self - interest, but they prefer to believe that their aims are altruistic.
words.example_zh: 各国都只考虑自己的利益, 却总愿意相信自己的目标是大公无私的。

words.definition_en: adj. falsely appearing to be fair, just, or right
words.example_en: It is unlikely that the Duke was convinced by such specious arguments.
words.example_zh: 公爵不太可能相信这种似是而非的论点。

words.definition_en: (1)v. to find an answer or solution to (2)v. to make a definite and serious decision to do something (3)n. a strong determination to do something
words.example_en: we must find a way to resolve these problems before it's too late.
words.example_zh: 我们必须及时找到解决这些问题的方法。

words.definition_en: v. to prove to be false or wrong
words.example_en: They accepted what he had said in default of any evidence to disprove it.
words.example_zh: 由于没有相反的证据,他们相信了他的话。

words.definition_en: adj. showing a readiness or desire to fight or argue
words.example_en: He is a pugnacious fighter.
words.example_zh: 他是个好斗的战士。

words.definition_en: adj. not willing to accept new or different ideas
words.example_en: You will never change their ideas because they are far too hidebound.
words.example_zh: 你无法改变他们的意思, 因为他们太墨守成规。

words.definition_en: n. one who expends resources foolishly and self-indulgently
words.example_en: Her father wouldn't let her marry a wastrel.
words.example_zh: 她的父亲不会让她嫁给一个败家子。

words.definition_en: adj. not enough or not good enough
words.example_en: Supplies of food and medicines are inadequate.
words.example_zh: 食物和药品供应不足。

words.definition_en: adj. having, revealing, or marked by a smug, ingratiating, and false earnestness or spirituality
words.example_en: The kind of unctuous tone that I've heard often at diplomatic parties.
words.example_zh: 我常在外交聚会上听到的那种虚与委蛇的腔调

words.definition_en: (1)adj. moving about aimlessly or irregularly (2)adj. behaving wrongly
words.example_en: His errant son at Dartmouth ran up debts of 250m.
words.example_zh: 他那行为出格的儿子在达特茅斯欠下了高达2亿5千万英镑的债务。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. not having enough of something for comfort or happiness (2)adj. having little thin
words.example_en: He could not support his family on his meager salary.
words.example_zh: 他靠微薄的工资无法养家。

words.definition_en: n. forecast, prognostication
words.example_en: If the cancer is caught early the prognosis is excellent.
words.example_zh: 如果癌症在早期发现，预后效果还是非常好的。

words.definition_en: n. the substitution of an agreeable or inoffensive expression for one that may offend or suggest something unpleasant
words.example_en: The term early retirement is nearly always a euphemism for redundancy nowadays.
words.example_zh: 现在提前退休几乎就是裁员的委婉说法。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to change direction
words.example_en: The war did not deflect him from the path he had long ago taken.
words.example_zh: 战争并没有使他放弃自己很久以前就选择的道路。

words.definition_en: v. to keep company
words.example_en: You should consort with your girlfriend and attend the opening ceremony.
words.example_zh: 你应该陪你的女友参加开幕式。

words.definition_en: (1)n. a strong feeling of happiness (2)v. to make very happy
words.example_en: Andrew roared with delight when he heard Rachel's nickname for the baby.
words.example_zh: 安德鲁听到雷切尔给婴儿起的绰号时，不禁高兴得大笑起来。

words.definition_en: v. to occur, fall, or come between points of time or events
words.example_en: The situation calmed down when police intervened
words.example_zh: 警方介入后，局势平息下来。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. causing fear, dread, or apprehension (2)adj. tending to inspire awe or wonder (3)adj. very difficult to deal with
words.definition_zh: 可怕的令人敬畏的难对付的
words.example_en: We have a formidable task ahead of us.
words.example_zh: 我们面临着一项艰巨的任务。

words.definition_en: adj. very clever and able to make intelligent
words.example_en: A canny investor would need to predict when the dollar will once more start tumbling.
words.example_zh: 精明的投资者需要预计到美元何时会再次大跌。

words.definition_en: (1)adj. of or relating to a separate person or thing (2)adj. distinguished by superiority (3)adj. departing from general usage or expectation
words.example_en: Cardinal Meschia was without doubt a singular character.
words.example_zh: 红衣主教梅斯基亚无疑是个怪人。

words.definition_en: n. adversary, opponent
words.example_en: You have exalted the right hand of his foes; you have made all his enemies rejoice.
words.example_zh: 你高举了他敌人的右手，你叫他一切的仇敌欢喜。

words.definition_en: adj. obstinate, cantankerous
words.example_en: She's so cussed that she always does the opposite of what you ask.
words.example_zh: 她这人真是牛脾气，总是与你的要求背道而驰。

words.definition_en: adj. not drawn upon or utilized
words.example_en: We must make use of our untapped reservoirs of talent.
words.example_zh: 我们必须利用我们尚未利用的人才。

words.definition_en: n. a stupid or unimaginative person
words.example_en: For years, teachers had figured him for a dullard.
words.example_zh: 多年来老师们都以为他是个笨蛋。

words.definition_en: v. to dry up
words.example_en: In a week, your skin will desiccate and you will munmmify.
words.example_zh: 一周之内你的皮肤会脱水变成木乃伊。

words.definition_en: adj. being on fire
words.example_en: The house ablaze is next door to me.
words.example_zh: 着火的那所房子就在我家隔壁.

words.definition_en: v. to have weight or effect
words.example_en: This would certainly militate against good bonding between the resin matrix and the glass.
words.example_zh: 这当然会影响树脂基体与玻璃间的良好粘结。

words.definition_en: n. a person who brings a legal action
words.example_en: The jury found against the plaintiff.
words.example_zh: 陪审团作出不利于原告的判决。

words.definition_en: n. the status or position of a star
words.example_en: She is on the escalator to stardom.
words.example_zh: 她青云直上地跻身于明星界。

words.definition_en: adv. off the right path or route
words.example_en: The hikers went astray in the fog.
words.example_zh: 徒步者在雾中迷了路。

words.definition_en: n. an unexpected, unearned, or sudden gain or advantage
words.example_en: They had received a $ 100,000 windfall and were agonizing about how to spend it.
words.example_zh: 他们得到了10万美元的意外之财，不知道该怎么花。

words.definition_en: n. the agency, function, or office of a deputy who acts as a substitute for another
words.example_en: Your proxy will need to sign the form on your behalf.
words.example_zh: 你的代理人将须代表你在表格上签字。

words.definition_en: adj. genetically or innately determined
words.example_en: Others think that the rules for what is 'musical' are hard-wired in our brains to some degree.
words.example_zh: 其他人认为，我们的大脑对什么东西是“悦耳的”在某种程度上有其内在的标准。

words.definition_en: adj. incapable of being corrected or amended
words.example_en: Maybe you will think that CHE incorrigible romanticist but I think that's why he is glamorous.
words.example_zh: 在某些方面来看,CHE或许有些浪漫得无可救药，但这也不就是他的魅力所在?

words.definition_en: adj. split apart
words.example_en: The upper surface of glacier is riven by crevasses.
words.example_zh: 冰川的上表面已裂成冰隙。

words.definition_en: n. the human body apart from the head, neck, arms, and legs
words.example_en: Torso more than a quarter of the site restricted activity, including tonic or should not rotate.
words.example_zh: 躯干四分之一以上部位活动受限, 包括强直或不能旋转。

words.definition_en: (1) v. to strike with or as if with a flail (2) v. to move, swing, or beat as if wielding a flail
words.example_en: Boxers flailing at each other in the ring.
words.example_zh: 在拳击场上互相猛击的拳手。

words.definition_en: adj. attracted to novelty
words.example_en: We need better teachers, not newfangled ideas about education.
words.example_zh: 我们需要更好的老师, 而不是新奇的教育想法。

words.definition_en: adj. having or relating to a frequency below the audibility range of the human ear
words.example_en: Another haunted and related natural phenomenon is known as frequency infrasonic sound waves.
words.example_zh: 另一种与闹鬼相关的自然现象是称作次声的低频声波。

words.definition_en: n. the amount of living matter
words.example_en: The weight of living organisms is called biomass.
words.example_zh: 生物有机体的重量称为生物量。

words.definition_en: (1) adj. ill humor (2) adj. connected with or suffering from dyspepsia
words.example_en: Sometimes dad was nice to us, but sometimes he was dyspeptic and nervy.
words.example_zh: 爸爸有时对我们很好，但有时他脾气暴躁，焦虑不安。

words.definition_en: n. a natural or artificial channel through which water flows
words.example_en: This river has a zigzagging watercourse and many branches.
words.example_zh: 这条河的河道曲折，汊流众多。

words.definition_en: n. self-possession or imperturbability especially under strain
words.example_en: He behaves throughout with a certain sang-froid.
words.example_zh: 他从头至尾都表现出一定的冷静沉着。

words.definition_en: n. a setting free from the charge of an offense by verdict, sentence, or other legal process
words.example_en: The jury brought in an acquittal.
words.example_zh: 陪审团宣告无罪。

words.definition_en: n. a standard method or procedure that is mindlessly adhered to or that minimizes individuality
words.example_en: The union's support had been in lockstep for years.
words.example_zh: 工会多年来始终步调一致地给予支持。

words.definition_en: v. to count, record, or list systematically
words.example_en: It took me ten hours to tabulate the results.
words.example_zh: 我花了十个小时把结果制成表格。

words.definition_en: adj. undaunted
words.example_en: Ms Politkovskaya was unfazed , making around 50 trips there, often for days at a time.
words.example_zh: 波利特科夫斯卡娅安之若素,她往返约50次, 常常一次停留数日。

words.definition_en: n. a theory holding that the self can know nothing but its own modifications and that the self is the only existent thing
words.example_en: The word you are trying to think of is solipsism.
words.example_zh: 你在想的一个名词叫唯我论。

words.definition_en: n. a minute or minor detail
words.example_en: Much of his early work is concerned with the minutiae of rural life.
words.example_zh: 他早期的许多作品关注的是农村生活中一些鸡毛蒜皮的琐事。

words.definition_en: adj. having discrete markings of different colors
words.example_en: Flowers on variegated branches yield offspring that have variegated foliage.
words.example_zh: 有斑驳的枝条上的花,产生有变异叶片的后代。

words.definition_en: n. an unhelpful, unkind, or harmful act
words.example_en: You could do yourself a grave disservice by revealing all to a potential rival.
words.example_zh: 将一切都透露给潜在的竞争对手只会给自己帮倒忙。

words.definition_en: n. pretentious nonsense
words.example_en: This is the claptrap that they have peddled many times before.
words.example_zh: 这是他们早就多次兜售给公众的场面话。

words.definition_en: n. a character in a story or play who opposes the hero
words.example_en: The king is represented as a villain in the play.
words.example_zh: 在这出剧中把国王刻画成一个反面人物。

words.definition_en: adj. tending to please or delight
words.example_en: Her winning ways were always enough to lure any man on.
words.example_zh: 她那迷人的举止足以使所有的男人为之神魂颠倒。

words.definition_en: n. a person who is unusually aware of and interested in new and unconventional patterns
words.example_en: In 1960 s England, the Vespa shuttled around the sharp, suit - wearing hipster group Known as the Mods.
words.example_zh: 1960年代的英国, 伟士牌在一群西装笔挺的时髦雅痞摩登一族间广为流行。

words.definition_en: adj. proved good, desirable, or feasible
words.example_en: Now you can apply tried and true principles to your own game.
words.example_zh: 现在你可以把这些久经验证的原则运用到自己的比赛中。

words.definition_en: adj. outmoded
words.example_en: People who express themselves through their clothes don't want to wear something viewed as passe.
words.example_zh: 那些通过穿着表达自己的人也不希望穿着被视为过时的衣服。

words.definition_en: adj. inadequate, insufficient
words.example_en: I am unequal to this position.
words.example_zh: 我胜任不了这个职位。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by bad temper, malevolence, or spite
words.example_en: No students like this splenetic teacher.
words.example_zh: 没有学生喜欢这个爱发脾气的老师。

words.definition_en: n. an agreement, combination, or group (as of companies) formed to undertake an enterprise beyond the resources of any one member
words.example_en: A consortium plans to build a natural-gas pipeline from Russia to supply eastern Germany.
words.example_zh: 一家财团计划修建一条从俄罗斯向德国东部供应天然气的管道。

words.definition_en: n. a formal or elegant letter
words.example_en: Her mother sends her a long epistle every week.
words.example_zh: 她母亲每周给她写一封长信。

words.definition_en: n. extravagant showiness
words.example_en: It was Fred's lust for glitz and glamour that was driving them apart.
words.example_zh: 弗雷德对浮华和美艳的欲望使他们分手。

words.definition_en: v. to make, declare, or prove obsolete or out-of-date
words.example_en: Your car would be superannuated early.
words.example_zh: 你的汽车早就该淘汰了。

words.definition_en: v. denounce
words.example_en: Thousands of readers denounced their bad behavior online.
words.example_zh: 成千上万的读者在网上谴责了他们的不良行为。

words.definition_en: adj. moving, occurring, or performed in a backward direction
words.example_en: he closure of the factory is a retrograde step.
words.example_zh: 工厂的关闭是一大退步。

words.definition_en: v. to move along unsteadily or with difficulty
words.example_en: I hurt my foot, and had to hobble home.
words.example_zh: 我的脚受了伤，只好一拐一拐地走回家。

words.definition_en: v. delimit
words.example_en: A special UN commission was formed to demarcate the border.
words.example_zh: 一个特别联合国委员会被设立以期划定边界。

words.definition_en: n. perception of the nature of an event after it has happened
words.example_en: What looks obvious in hindsight was not at all obvious at the time.
words.example_zh: 事后一目了然的在当时根本看不清。

words.definition_en: n. flamboyant, exaggerated, or sensational promotion or publicity
words.example_en: They announced, amid much ballyhoo, that they had made a breakthrough.
words.example_zh: 他们在一片喧嚣中宣布，他们取得突破性的进展。

words.definition_en: n. a hideous giant of fairy tales and folklore that feeds on human beings
words.example_en: The ogre was portrayed as an abortion.
words.example_zh: 民间传说里的吃人巨人给画成了畸形的怪物。

words.definition_en: n. the final result
words.example_en: The surprising upshot is that we have lots of good but not very happy employees.
words.example_zh: 令人意想不到的结果是，我们有许多优秀但却不快乐的雇员。

words.definition_en: n. a loud arrogant boaster
words.example_en: Modest by nature, she was no braggart, preferring to let her accomplishments speak for themselves.
words.example_zh: 她天性谦虚，从不自吹，喜欢以自己的成就来表明一切。

words.definition_en: v. to split into fragments, parts, or factions
words.example_en: The party began to splinter.
words.example_zh: 那个党开始分裂。

words.definition_en: v. to saturate with or subject thoroughly to (some strong or pervading influence)
words.example_en: This aesthetic movement known as Afrofuturism is steeped in African American musical tradition.
words.example_zh: 这种名为“非洲未来主义”的美学运动深深植根于非裔美国人的音乐传统。

words.definition_en: adj. having a thick or sticky consistency
words.example_en: Gases are much less viscous than liquids.
words.example_zh: 气体的粘滞性大大小于液体。

words.definition_en: n. an unpleasant person who grabs inconsiderately
words.example_en: The grabber was arrested by the police.
words.example_zh: 那个掠夺者被警察逮捕了。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking or indicating the lack of higher qualities of mind or spirit
words.example_en: He acted from base motives.
words.example_zh: 他的行为动机卑鄙。

words.definition_en: adj. morally despicable or abhorrent
words.example_en: Blaming parents is vile.
words.example_zh: 责怪父母是可耻的。

words.definition_en: adj. connected with a desire for pleasure
words.example_en: I don't like those who are sybaritic.
words.example_zh: 我不喜欢那些骄奢淫逸，贪图享乐的人。

words.definition_en: v. to give warnings or advice : make urgent appeals
words.example_en: Therefore I exhort you to confirm your love toward him.
words.example_zh: 所以我劝你们，要向他证实你们的爱。

words.definition_en: v. to provide for the payment of
words.example_en: My father has to defray my education.
words.example_zh: 我父亲得支付我的教育费用。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to correspond
words.example_en: Her list does not tally with mine.
words.example_zh: 她的单子与我的不相符。

words.definition_en: adj. easily irritated
words.example_en: A long-running dispute between two of Europe's most prickly neighbors is turning nasty.
words.example_zh: 欧洲两个最易动怒的邻国间一场旷日持久的争吵变得不堪入耳。

words.definition_en: adj. full of danger or risk
words.example_en: They had a parlous journey on stormy seas.
words.example_zh: 他们在风暴海洋上进行了一次危险的旅行。

words.definition_en: v. to destroy to the ground
words.example_en: The owners intend to raze the hotel and erect an office building on the site.
words.example_zh: 业主打算拆除酒店，并在原址建一座办公楼。

words.definition_en: adj. inspiring horror or repulsion
words.example_en: I heard stories of gruesome tortures in prisons.
words.example_zh: 我听说过监狱里令人毛骨悚然的刑讯。

words.definition_en: (1) adj. easily offended or disgusted (2) adj. excessively fastidious or scrupulous in conduct or belief
words.definition_zh: 易受惊的，易生气的过分谨慎的
words.example_en: This horror movie is definitely not for the squeamish.
words.example_zh: 这部恐怖片绝对不适合胆子小的人。

words.definition_en: v. bring about, cause
words.definition_zh: 造成（巨大的破坏或伤害）
words.example_en: These insects can wreak havoc on crops.
words.example_zh: 这些昆虫可严重危害农作物。

words.definition_en: n. wide and general destruction
words.example_en: The floods caused havoc throughout the area.
words.example_zh: 洪水给整个地区带来了灾害。

words.definition_en: phr. v. to make someone feel proud or conceited; to become swollen
words.example_en: He puffed himself up in front of the girls.
words.example_zh: 他在女孩们面前摆出一副自命不凡的样子。

words.definition_en: v. to form the basis or foundation of
words.example_en: It is our brains that are responsible for the complex social organization and the accumulation of technical, economic and scientific advances that, for better and worse, undergird modern civilization.
words.example_zh: 正是大脑使复杂的社会组织以及技术、经济和科学进步得以积累；无论好坏，正是这些支撑着现代文明。

words.definition_en: v. swindle
words.example_en: Don't try to con me! I'm not silly.
words.example_zh: 别想骗我，我不傻的。

words.definition_en: n. the act of weakening or exhausting by constant harassment, abuse, or attack
words.example_en: The rebels have declared a cease-fire in their war of attrition against the government.
words.example_zh: 在与政府的消耗战中，叛军已宣布停火。

words.definition_en: n. something that is base, trivial, or inferior
words.example_en: I go through phases where everything I write is just dross.
words.example_zh: 我经历过几个阶段，写什么都是垃圾。

words.definition_en: n. a short, memorable statement, especially one repeated in the media
words.definition_zh: （尤指媒体反复播放的）简短有力的话；精简言论
words.example_en: A sound bite is a brief, very quotable remark by a candidate for office that is repeated on radio and television news programs.
words.example_zh: 话语片段是指在电台和电视台的新闻节目中反复播放的候选人说过的有代表性的只言片语。

words.definition_en: adj. of, relating to, or bringing about redemption
words.example_en: Almost every story has a redemptive note of hope mixed in with levels of bleak despair.
words.example_zh: 几乎每则故事都有一丝希望，带来救赎，却也混杂着重重绝望，暗无天日。

words.definition_en: adj. friendly, brotherly
words.example_en: The two bump fists in a fraternal alliance.
words.example_zh: 他们两人像兄弟般地撞了撞拳头。

words.definition_en: v. to say or do again or again and again
words.example_en: It's very difficult to balance that need to be nimble and iterate quickly and the need to plan.
words.example_zh: 要想平衡灵活、快速迭代的需求与计划需求是非常困难的。

words.definition_en: v. to express sorrow audibly
words.example_en: Banshees wail and weep, but they never howl.
words.example_zh: 女妖会哀号哭泣，但她们从不嚎叫。

words.definition_en: v. to make a low whining plaintive or broken sound
words.example_en: The child was lost and began to whimper.
words.example_zh: 那孩子迷了路，抽抽搭搭地哭起来。

words.definition_en: n. a law enacted by the legislative branch of a government
words.example_en: Corporal punishment was banned by statute in 1987.
words.example_zh: 1987年通过的法令明文禁止体罚。

words.definition_en: v. to make dull or stupid
words.example_en: This hard task besotted me.
words.example_zh: 这个困难的任务让我显得很迷糊。

words.definition_en: adj. inclined to be merciful
words.example_en: The embrace of father is clement and mother's is warm.
words.example_zh: 爸爸的怀抱是宽厚的，妈妈的怀抱是温暖的。

words.definition_en: n. a cramping and oppressive lack of resources (such as money)
words.example_en: He was brought up in penury, without education.
words.example_zh: 他从小一贫如洗，没有受过教育。

words.definition_en: n. one that is annoying, unpleasant, or obnoxious
words.example_en: He could be a bit of a nuisance when he was drunk.
words.example_zh: 他喝醉时会是一个有点令人讨厌的人。

words.definition_en: (1) adj. highly enthusiastic and favorable (2) adj. producing light
words.definition_zh: 热情洋溢的，赞不绝口的发光的
words.example_en: He gave her a warm glowing smile.
words.example_zh: 他给了她一个热情洋溢的微笑。

words.definition_en: n. a joking or mocking remark
words.example_en: The remark was made half in jest.
words.example_zh: 这话是半开玩笑说出的。

words.definition_en: v. to include or place within something larger or more comprehensive
words.example_en: States collect taxes and subsume many of the responsibilities of governing from the county.
words.example_zh: 各州征收税款，并将郡县的许多治理职责纳入自身职权。

words.definition_en: adj. characterized by or peculiar to wisdom
words.example_en: Book is my hand-in-hand friend, she and I are together all along, and she loyally strings along with me and gives me ideaistic edification and a sapiential key.
words.example_zh: 书是我的亲密朋友，多年来它对我不离不弃，忠诚地陪伴守候在我身边，给了我思想的启迪和智慧的钥匙。

words.definition_en: n. time past and especially long past
words.example_en: The images provoked strong surges of nostalgia for the days of yore.
words.example_zh: 这些影像勾起了对昔日时光的深深怀念。

words.definition_en: n. a wicked act or thing
words.example_en: He thought of New York as a den of iniquity.
words.example_zh: 他把纽约视为罪恶的渊薮。

words.definition_en: adj. not gained by labor, service, or skill
words.example_en: The worst guilt is to accept an unearned guilt.
words.example_zh: 最严重的罪行是接受一个不劳而获的罪行。

words.definition_en: phrase. to have great power or influence over someone or something
words.definition_zh: 占支配地位；有巨大影响力
words.example_en: Rebel forces hold sway over much of the island.
words.example_zh: 该岛很大一部分控制在叛军手里。

words.definition_en: (1) adj. chief, principal (2) adj. mischievous, saucy
words.definition_zh: 主要的，首要的淘气的，调皮的
words.example_en: Unemployment was the arch cause of poverty.
words.example_zh: 失业是贫穷的主要原因。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by, arousing, or appealing to sexual desire
words.example_en: We read the gossip written about them with prurient interest.
words.example_zh: 我们怀着窥私猎奇的兴趣阅读关于他们的八卦。

words.definition_en: v. to attribute real identity to (a concept)
words.example_en: The mind has a tendency to hypostatize abstractions.
words.example_zh: 心灵有一种把抽象东西具体化的趋势。

words.definition_en: v. to come about as a natural growth, increase, or advantage
words.example_en: Interest will accrue if you keep your money in a savings account.
words.example_zh: 如果把钱存入储蓄账户，就会自然生息。

words.definition_en: adj. seeking solitude : retiring from society
words.example_en: All that neighbours knew about the reclusive man was that he had lived in the building for about 20 years.
words.example_zh: 关于那名隐居者邻居们所知道的就是他已经在那栋楼里住了大约二十年了。

words.definition_en: n. a warning enjoining one from certain acts or practices
words.example_en: A caveat here is appropriate.
words.example_zh: 这里的警告是适当的。

words.definition_en: adj. not to be modified or evaded
words.example_en: "Literary" fiction has no hard-and-fast definition.
words.example_zh: “文学”小说没有固定的定义。

words.definition_en: v. to disperse in an emulsion
words.example_en: Grime Reaper works fast to loosen and emulsify grease and grime for easy rinse.
words.example_zh: 格里姆收割机工程快速放松和乳化油脂和污垢，便于清洗。

words.definition_en: n. one that is stunning, amazing, or devastating
words.example_en: His resignation is a political bombshell.
words.example_zh: 他的辞职是一件政治上的爆炸性新闻。

words.definition_en: adj. overly sentimental
words.example_en: Although the writer meant for the book to serve as a kind of tribute to motherhood, the approach here is hardly a saccharine affair.
words.example_zh: 尽管作者想让这本书成为对母性的一种赞颂，但所用的方法却一点也不温情脉脉。

words.definition_en: v. to search about
words.example_en: The team is trying to ferret out missing details.
words.example_zh: 该团队正试图搜寻遗失的细节。

words.definition_en: (1) adj. being or represented on a small scale (2) n. a very small portrait or other painting (as on ivory or metal)
words.example_en: It looks like a miniature version of James Bond's car.
words.example_zh: 它看上去像一辆小型的詹姆斯•邦德的汽车。

words.definition_en: v. to put together in a makeshift way
words.example_en: I hate having builders botch up repairs on my house.
words.example_zh: 我痛恨建筑工人笨手笨脚地修理我的房子。

words.definition_en: v. to grope for or handle something clumsily or aimlessly
words.example_en: His awkwardness made him fumble with the key.
words.example_zh: 由于尴尬不安，他拿钥匙开锁时显得笨手笨脚。

words.definition_en: (1) v. to avoid an encounter with (2) n. an artful device to evade, deceive, or trick
words.example_en: These might help people dodge some of the health effects of being overweight.
words.example_zh: 这些可能会帮助人们规避肥胖对健康的某些影响。

words.definition_en: n. a gross error or mistake resulting usually from stupidity, ignorance, or carelessness
words.example_en: It had been a monumental blunder to give him the assignment.
words.example_zh: 分配给他这项任务是一个巨大的错误。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by or expressive of gloom
words.example_en: A morose France has fallen behind its competitors.
words.example_zh: 郁闷的法兰西人落在了竞争者的后面。

words.definition_en: n. an empty or deceptive act or pretense
words.example_en: Their whole marriage had been a charade-they had never loved each other.
words.example_zh: 他们的整个婚姻都是在做戏—他们从未相爱过。

words.definition_en: n. one that has recently or suddenly risen to an unaccustomed position of wealth or power and has not yet gained the prestige, dignity, or manner associated with it
words.example_en: Jackson was a southwestern parvenu who combined a sense of rough-hewn egalitarianism with the gentlemanly honor typical of his class.
words.example_zh: 杰克逊是一个来自南方的新贵，身上充满了属于他那个阶层的典型特征：融合了最初的平等主义和具有绅士派头的荣耀。

words.definition_en: n. one that is suggestive of or suited to an earlier time or style
words.example_en: Until fairly recently, hitchhiking was not an uncommon practice in the United States, but today's motorists are likely to see the occasional hitchhiker as a throwback, thinking, 'I didn't know people still did that!'
words.example_zh: 直到不久前，搭便车在美国还很常见；但如今的司机很可能把偶尔遇到的搭便车者视为旧时代的人，并想：‘我还以为已经没人这么做了！’

words.definition_en: n. one that has risen suddenly (as from a low position to wealth or power)
words.example_en: In each case it is difficult for an upstart to break in.
words.example_zh: 对于暴发户来说，想进入到以上任一行业都是相当困难的。

words.definition_en: n. use of a longer phrasing in place of a possible shorter form of expression
words.example_en: Generally speaking, periphrasis can be defined, as the substitution of longer, multi-word expressions for single words.
words.example_zh: 一般而言，迂回表达可以定义为以多词表达代替单个词的用法。

words.definition_en: adj. excessively embellished in style or language
words.example_en: Frankenstein was already turgid; Percy made it more so.
words.example_zh: 《弗兰肯斯坦》已经是夸张的，但珀西使它更夸张。

words.definition_en: adj. distinctly unpleasant or distasteful
words.example_en: Antioxidants stops fat from turning rancid.
words.example_zh: 抗氧化剂使脂肪停止腐烂发臭。

words.definition_en: (1) v. to look upon with disapproval or envy (2) v. to give or concede reluctantly or with displeasure
words.example_en: I certainly don't begrudge him the Nobel Prize.
words.example_zh: 我肯定不会妒忌他得了获诺贝尔奖。

words.definition_en: n. a mixture of various things
words.example_en: The show was a miscellany of song and dance.
words.example_zh: 那是有歌曲和舞蹈的混合表演。

words.definition_en: v. to prevent or check expression or activity of
words.example_en: The attacker then tried to throttle her with wire.
words.example_zh: 那名袭击者然后试图用电线勒死她。

words.definition_en: v. to stir up
words.example_en: He had slumped into a state of torpor from which nothing could rouse him.
words.example_zh: 他陷入了一种麻木状态，什么事都不能唤起他的兴趣。

words.definition_en: adj. in an extemporaneous manner
words.example_en: The artist’s apparently casual, improvisatory pictures are not the extempore renditions they may seem: her pictures are, in fact, the result of a highly disciplined style.
words.example_zh: 这位艺术家表面上随意、即兴创作的照片并不是即兴创作的：事实上，这些照片似乎是高度自律风格的结果。

words.definition_en: adj. not called for by the circumstances : not necessary, appropriate, or justified
words.example_en: There's too much crime and gratuitous violence on TV.
words.example_zh: 电视上充斥着过多的犯罪与无端的暴力。

words.definition_en: adj. foolishly adventurous and bold
words.example_en: When he tested an early vaccine on himself, some described the act as foolhardy.
words.example_zh: 当他在自己身上试验还处于初级阶段的疫苗时，有些人说他的行为是莽撞的。

words.definition_en: adj. having a rough uneven surface
words.example_en: We left the rough track and bumped our way over a rugged mountainous terrain.
words.example_zh: 我们驶离坎坷的小道，又颠簸过了崎岖多岩的山地。

words.definition_en: adj. tending to produce horror in a beholder
words.example_en: Police have made a macabre discovery.
words.example_zh: 警察有了个可怕的发现。

words.definition_en: n. a subordinate occupation pursued in addition to one's vocation especially for enjoyment
words.example_en: He was a printer by trade and naturalist by avocation.
words.example_zh: 他在印刷厂工作，同时是个博物学爱好者。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by an inclination to please or oblige
words.example_en: A good servant should be complaisant but not servile.
words.example_zh: 一个好的仆人应该是殷勤的，而不是卑屈的。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking in means of enforcement or coercion
words.definition_zh: 人微言轻的，不起作用的
words.example_en: In his view, the commission remains a toothless and ineffectual body.
words.example_zh: 在他看来，委员会仍然是个没有权威而且缺乏效率的机构。

words.definition_en: (1) adj. having a haughty overbearing manner (2) adj. elevated in character and spirit
words.example_en: Mr Erdogan has good reason for his lofty confidence.
words.example_zh: 埃尔多安先生有着傲慢狂妄的正当理由。

words.definition_en: n. something of monstrous size, power, or appearance
words.example_en: The city is a sprawling behemoth with no heart.
words.example_zh: 这座城市像缺失中心的庞然大物向外延伸。

words.definition_en: n. a collateral or derived branch, descendant, or member
words.example_en: Psychology began as a purely academic offshoot of natural philosophy.
words.example_zh: 心理学形成之初是作为自然哲学的一个纯学术分支。

words.definition_en: adj. of low intensity
words.example_en: The wedding will be a very low-key affair.
words.example_zh: 这场婚礼将非常低调。

words.definition_en: n. criticism for a fault
words.example_en: She raised her eyebrows in reproof.
words.example_zh: 她竖起眉毛，面露责备。

words.definition_en: n. preponderant influence or authority over others
words.example_en: Future global hegemony is at stake.
words.example_zh: 未来的全球霸权在面临关键时刻。

words.definition_en: n. pioneer
words.example_en: Vita was a trailblazer, a vigorous independent.
words.example_zh: 维塔是一位开拓者，一个精力充沛的独立派。

words.definition_en: v. to permeate or influence as if by dyeing
words.example_en: Keep smiling and that alone will imbue others with happiness and joy.
words.example_zh: 保持微笑，这都将灌输别人的幸福和喜悦。

words.definition_en: v. to diffuse through or penetrate something
words.example_en: Water will easily permeate a cotton dress.
words.example_zh: 水很容易渗透棉布衣服。

words.definition_en: v. to take part in or experience something along with others
words.example_en: They preferred not to partake in the social life of the town.
words.example_zh: 他们不想参加这个镇的社交活动。

words.definition_en: n. something of full width
words.example_en: Books influence the depth and breadth of life.
words.example_zh: 书籍影响生命的深度和广度。

words.definition_en: adj. having the agreement and consent of all
words.example_en: Editors were unanimous in their condemnation of the proposals.
words.example_zh: 编辑们一致谴责这些提议。

words.definition_en: phrase. even a very small amount; at all
words.example_en: He does not seem the least bit tractable.
words.example_zh: 他似乎一点儿也不温顺。

words.definition_en: v. to seize and hold in the custody of the law
words.example_en: We'll need to impound your car.
words.example_zh: 我们要扣留你的车辆。

words.definition_en: adj. marked by lofty or imposing dignity
words.example_en: The procession made its stately progress through the streets of the city.
words.example_zh: 游行的队伍缓慢而庄严地穿过城市的街道。

words.definition_en: v. to jut out
words.example_en: The bag bulged with books.
words.example_zh: 袋子里装满了书，鼓了起来。

words.definition_en: v. to use for gain
words.example_en: Now some experts are proposing that we should take advantage of the teen brain's keen sensitivity to the presence of friends and leverage it to improve education.
words.example_zh: 现在一些专家提出，我们应该利用青少年的大脑对朋友在场的敏锐感知，并借助它来改善教育。

words.definition_en: n. a smith who forges iron
words.example_en: The blacksmith was pelting away at the hot iron.
words.example_zh: 铁匠正在不停地敲打热铁。

words.definition_en: v. to elevate to divine status
words.example_en: We should not apotheosize anyone.
words.example_zh: 我们不应该神化任何人。

words.definition_en: adj. having a healthy reddish color
words.example_en: He had a naturally ruddy complexion, even more flushed now from dancing.
words.example_zh: 他的面色天生红润，现在跳了舞就更红了。

words.definition_en: n. a routine task or job
words.example_en: Exercise isn't a job or a chore.
words.example_zh: 锻炼身体不是一项工作或琐事。

words.definition_en: n. a young child usually between one and three years old
words.example_en: I had a toddler at home and two other children at school.
words.example_zh: 我有一个刚学步的孩子在家中，另外两个孩子在学校。

words.definition_en: adj. resembling or befitting a rude or insensitive person
words.example_en: He disgusted many with his boorish behavior.
words.example_zh: 他以其粗鲁的行为使许多人感到厌恶。

words.definition_en: v. to confer dignity upon
words.example_en: The mayor was there to dignify the celebrations.
words.example_zh: 市长的光临为庆祝活动增辉。

words.definition_en: n. the study of the essential nature of diseases and especially of the structural and functional changes produced by them
words.example_en: It involved research in genetics, plant breeding, plant pathology, entomology, agronomy, soil science, and cereal technology.
words.example_zh: 它涉及遗传学、植物育种、植物病理学、昆虫学、农学、土壤科学和谷物技术的研究。

words.definition_en: v. to throw or pour back or out from or as if from a cavity
words.example_en: Don't regurgitate the stuff given in the textbook.
words.example_zh: 不要照背课本中的东西。

words.definition_en: n. any of a family (Hominidae) of erect bipedal primate mammals that includes recent humans together with extinct ancestral and related forms and in some recent classifications the great apes (the orangutan, gorilla, chimpanzee, and bonobo)
words.example_en: Olduvai Gorge, an early hominid site in Tanzania, was found by a butterfly hunter who literally fell into its deep valley in 1911.
words.example_zh: 坦桑尼亚早期的原始人类遗址，奥杜瓦伊峡谷是1911年，一名蝴蝶猎人掉进峡谷深处时发现的。

words.definition_en: n. a newspaper that is about half the page size of an ordinary newspaper and that contains news in condensed form and much photographic matter
words.example_en: Tabloid newspapers love to dig up scandal.
words.example_zh: 通俗小报都热衷于刨丑闻。

words.definition_en: n. the history of a linguistic form (such as a word) shown by tracing its development since its earliest recorded occurrence in the language where it is found
words.example_en: The etymology of the name is obscure.
words.example_zh: 这个名字的词源尚不清楚。

words.definition_en: v. to give something to by way of compensation (as for a service rendered or damage incurred)
words.example_en: The company recompensed him for his losses.
words.example_zh: 公司补偿了他的损失。

words.definition_en: adj. extravagantly emotional
words.example_en: The rejection of their pay claim was given a less than rhapsodic reception by the miners.
words.example_zh: 矿工要求提高工资遭到拒绝而甚为恼火。

words.definition_en: n. party spirit especially when marked by dissension
words.example_en: There has been a substantial amount of factionalism within the movement.
words.example_zh: 该运动中已有大量的派系斗争了。

words.definition_en: n. a deliberate offense
words.example_en: It's an affront to human dignity to keep someone alive like this.
words.example_zh: 让一个人如此活着是对人类尊严的一种侮辱。

words.definition_en: v. to possess or exercise sovereign power
words.example_en: The gallant soldiers lost their lives so that peace might reign again.
words.example_zh: 英勇的士兵为了恢复和平而牺牲了他们的生命。

words.definition_en: v. to cover with a flood
words.example_en: If the dam breaks, it will inundate large parts of the town.
words.example_zh: 如果水坝坍塌，该城的大部分将被淹没。

words.definition_en: v. to place in proper rank or position
words.definition_zh: 安排；控制，组织；整理（思路、想法等)
words.example_en: Police were brought in to marshal the crowd.
words.example_zh: 警察奉命来维持秩序。

words.definition_en: n. the practice of spying or using spies to obtain information about the plans and activities especially of a foreign government or a competing company
words.example_en: Espionage is a capital offence in this country.
words.example_zh: 间谍活动在这个国家是死罪。

words.definition_en: v. to phrase or express in a specified manner
words.example_en: The letter was deliberately couched in very vague terms.
words.example_zh: 这封信故意写得含糊其辞。

words.definition_en: n. a level (as of achievement or intensity) regarded especially as a goal or standard
words.example_en: Whenever they reached their goal, they upped the ante, setting increasingly complex challenges for themselves.
words.example_zh: 每当他们达到了目标，他们就加高要求，为他们自己设置越来越复杂的挑战。

words.definition_en: v. to attack or overcome by aggressive argument
words.example_en: The aim was to bludgeon speculators and impress creditors.
words.example_zh: 目标是要打击投机倒把并让债权人留下深刻印象。

words.definition_en: n. something that serves as a temporary expedient
words.example_en: Sometimes, stopgap measures make the economy create long-term problems.
words.example_zh: 有时候，一些权宜之计会导致经济领域产生长期问题。

words.definition_en: n. trifle
words.example_en: Solving such life bagatelle will be one of fundamental trends that Internet develops henceforth.
words.example_zh: 解决这样的生活琐事，今后将成为互联网发展的重要趋势之一。

words.definition_en: n. one that bears the blame for others
words.example_en: I don't think I deserve to be made the scapegoat for a couple of bad results.
words.example_zh: 我认为我不该为出现的一些不良后果充当替罪羊。

words.definition_en: n. something feigned
words.example_en: His softness and consideration is probably a temporary feint.
words.example_zh: 他的温柔体贴可能只是一时的假象。

words.definition_en: v. to organize rigidly especially for the sake of regulation or control
words.example_en: Industrial research has not invariably been more regimented than academic science.
words.example_zh: 工业研究并不总是比学术科学更受管制。

words.definition_en: (1) v. to seek orders or votes (2) v. to ask people about sth in order to find out what they think about it
words.example_en: The party needs to canvass the uncommitted voters.
words.example_zh: 这个党需要向未表明态度的选民游说拉票。

words.definition_en: v. to deal out or seek to disseminate
words.example_en: They even set up their own news agency to peddle anti-isolationist propaganda.
words.example_zh: 他们甚至建立了自己的新闻机构以散布反孤立主义思想。

words.definition_en: v. to engage in nit-picking
words.example_en: Part of an editor's job is to nitpick writing flaws.
words.example_zh: 编辑的职责之一是仔细挑出写作瑕疵。

words.definition_en: n. the willful or malicious burning of property (such as a building) especially with criminal or fraudulent intent
words.example_en: She was the innocent victim of an arson attack.
words.example_zh: 她是一起纵火案的无辜受害者。

words.definition_en: adj. argumentative, rhetorical
words.example_en: Her corpus is also filled with forensic tensions.
words.example_zh: 她的文集同样充满了很多辩论的冲突。

words.definition_en: n. all the writings or works of a particular kind or on a particular subject
words.example_en: There is no final text, only an unfinished manuscript corpus.
words.example_zh: 他没有最终完稿，留下的只是一个未完成的手稿文集。

words.definition_en: n. a large party or celebration
words.example_en: Unfortunately, we can't go to the jamboree.
words.example_zh: 不幸地是，我们不能去庆祝会。

words.definition_en: v. to participate in a car pool
words.example_en: The day after, I'll carpool to save money.
words.example_zh: 后天，我可以拼车省钱。

words.definition_en: adj. having lost character, vitality, or strength
words.example_en: Effete heart needs a moment rest.
words.example_zh: 疲惫的心灵也需要片刻的停歇。

words.definition_en: v. to preserve or cherish as sacred
words.example_en: At least as important, the law appears to enshrine a right to collective bargaining.
words.example_zh: 至少同样重要的是，这项法律似乎确立了工人有集体谈判的权利。

words.definition_en: n. a widespread, indirect, or unforeseen effect of an act, action, or event
words.example_en: After being put out, service has received very good market repercussion.
words.example_zh: 服务推出后收到了非常好的市场反响。

words.definition_en: adj. consisting of, containing, or like sludge
words.example_en: His voice is not sludgy and slow, as it was in "Brokeback Mountain."
words.example_zh: 他的声音不似《断臂山》里的拖沓、低沉。

words.definition_en: n. arrogance, haughtiness
words.example_en: The intense vitality that had been so remarkable in the garage was converted into impressive hauteur.
words.example_zh: 先前她在车行里的那种旺盛的活力，转眼间变成了一种明显的骄傲情绪。

words.definition_en: adj. lacking in clarity or definition
words.example_en: The lettering is fuzzy and indistinct.
words.example_zh: 书写的文字模糊难认。

words.definition_en: adj. shut off from the light : dark, murky
words.example_en: Two doors along , a rubric stood out from the tenebrous gloom.
words.example_zh: 走过两扇门，黑洞洞的幽暗中显现出注目的标题。

words.definition_en: adj. having a good disposition
words.example_en: Well-disposed doctors check up on them, and several student volunteers play with the children.
words.example_zh: 关怀备至的医生为难民们做体检，一些学生志愿者陪着他们的孩子一起玩耍。

words.definition_en: v. to cause to spread out and affect a greater number or greater area : extend
words.example_en: Microwaves spread out as they propagate.
words.example_zh: 微波在传送过程中会扩散。

words.definition_en: n. a usually brief unit of action in a dramatic or literary work
words.example_en: The final episode will be shown next Sunday.
words.example_zh: 最后一集将于下周日播放。

words.definition_en: n. a person who serves for hire especially for purely mercenary motives
words.example_en: The salary of a hireling is 6 pounds per day.
words.example_zh: 雇工一天的工资是六镑。

words.definition_en: n. a person who plays pranks
words.example_en: Deepika is quite a prankster, often playing jokes on her friends.
words.example_zh: 迪皮卡是个顽皮的人，经常和她的朋友开玩笑。

words.definition_en: adj. worthy of esteem
words.example_en: He is an estimable and likeable young fellow.
words.example_zh: 他是一个难能可贵而又可爱的年轻人。

words.definition_en: adj. not adulterated : pure
words.example_en: Organic food is unadulterated food produced without artificial chemicals or pesticides.
words.example_zh: 有机食品是纯粹的不含人工化学制剂或杀虫剂的食物。

words.definition_en: ①adj. rapacious ②adj. very eager or greedy for food, satisfaction, or gratification
words.definition_zh: 贪婪的狼吞虎咽的，极其饥饿的
words.example_en: Quasars are perhaps the most ravenous and immortal beasts in the universe.
words.example_zh: 类星体或许是宇宙中最贪婪、最不朽的巨兽。

words.definition_en: v. to change from a fluid to a solid state by or as if by cold
words.example_en: The blood had started to congeal.
words.example_zh: 血液已经开始凝结。

words.definition_en: n. an allowable margin of freedom or variation : tolerance
words.example_en: How much leeway should parents give their children?
words.example_zh: 父母应该给孩子留多少自由的空间？

words.definition_en: n. a breaking apart or the state of being broken apart
words.example_en: The incidents have not yet caused a major rupture in the political ties between countries.
words.example_zh: 这些事件尚未造成各国间政治联系的严重破裂。

words.definition_en: phrase. by, of, or in itself or oneself or themselves
words.definition_zh: 就其本身而言；严格说来
words.example_en: I don't work out per se, but I'm very active physically.
words.example_zh: 严格说来，我并不专门健身，但我平时身体活动量很大。

words.definition_en: v. to set right : remedy
words.example_en: The court sought to redress the injustice.
words.example_zh: 法院试图纠正这一不公。

words.definition_en: n. a musical setting of the mass for the dead
words.example_en: I will sing a requiem for the land walkers.
words.example_zh: 我会给陆地上走的人唱首安魂曲。
